title: Add, Edit, and Delete Files in the Docusign Files Section of a Salesforce Record
heading_path: Add, Edit, and Delete Files in the Docusign Files Section of a Salesforce Record
text:
Learn how to add, edit, and delete files in the Docusign Files
section of a Salesforce record.

You can manage your Docusign files on Salesforce records with the
Docusign Files section.

You can add files by uploading documents to the Docusign Files section from
your device.

You can also add files from the Notes & Attachments section of a
Salesforce record.

You can use these options to add, edit, and delete files in the Docusign
Files section:

- [Generate a Document and Add it to Docusign Files](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=blu1586134216052.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add files to the Docusign Files component after generating them with Gen.")
- [Upload a File to Docusign Files](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=eto1586134371953.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to upload files from your device to the Docusign Files section of a Salesforce record.")
- [Download a Document from Docusign Files](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=tyz1586134372530.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to download documents from the Docusign Files section of a Salesforce record.")
- [Copy a File from Docusign Files to Notes & Attachments](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=vpk1586134372796.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to copy documents from Docusign Files to the Notes & Attachments section of a Salesforce record.")
- [Upload a New Version of a Document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=qsm1586134374562.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to upload a new version of a document in the Docusign Files section of a Salesforce record.")
- [Rename a File in the Docusign Files Component](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=hdb1586134375876.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to rename a file in the Docusign Files component of a Salesforce record.")
- [Delete a File from the Docusign Files Component](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=znk1586134376625.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to delete a file in the Docusign Files component of a Salesforce record.")

title: Generate an Invoice with Gen for Billing
heading_path: Generate an Invoice with Gen for Billing
text:
You can select a Docusign Gen for Billing custom button on a Salesforce record to
generate an invoice. The generated invoice is automatically saved to the Notes and
Attachments section of the Salesforce record.

title: Generate an Invoice with Gen for Billing
heading_path: Generate an Invoice with Gen for Billing
Before you begin
text:
Your administrator must grant Docusign
Generator permissions to your Docusign user.

You can select a Docusign Gen for Billing custom button on a Salesforce record to generate an
invoice. The generated invoice is automatically saved to the Notes and Attachments
section of the Salesforce record.

1. Find a Gen for Billing custom button:
   1. Log into Salesforce as a user with Docusign Gen
      Generator permissions.
   2. Open a Salesforce Invoice record.
   3. Select a Gen for Billing button on the page layout.

      Note: The button may be hidden under the arrow
      menu.![A screenshot displays an arrow menu opening to a button labeled Fresh Software Sales Contract.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/Select-Gen-custom-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYwNzQsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.FoEXe45jGBvBxonjFOpNb-AIM8JBmVGKOHPXsExxqaw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays an arrow menu opening to a button labeled Fresh Software Sales Contract.")

      The New Documents Generated and Saved page opens.
2. Optional Steps: Choose any optional steps:

   - Generated Documents: Uncheck the boxes of any
     documents that you don't want to send.
   - Preview: Select this link to see a preview of the
     generated document.
   - Download: Save a copy of the contract to your
     device.
   - **[Send for Signature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=tco1611350907305.html&_LANG=enus "You can send documents for eSignature using the built-in Docusign for Salesforce app in the Docusign Apps Launcher package.")**:
     Start the eSignature process.
   - **[Deliver By Email](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=iyu1615180590827.html&_LANG=enus "You can generate an invoice from a custom button and email it to recipients.")**: Send
     an email with these attachments to up to 5 recipients.
   - Done: Take no further action and close the
     page.

You have completed the process. You have generated a new
invoice and taken optional steps. The generated invoice displays in the record's Notes
and Attachments section.

title: Generate a Document and Add it to Docusign Files
heading_path: Generate a Document and Add it to Docusign Files
text:
Learn how to add files to the Docusign Files component after
generating them with Gen.

title: Generate a Document and Add it to Docusign Files
heading_path: Generate a Document and Add it to Docusign Files
Before you begin
text:
Your administrator must grant Docusign
Generator and Negotiator permissions to your Docusign user.

When you generate a new document with Gen, it is automatically saved in the Notes &
Attachments section of the record. You can add the document to the Docusign
Files component to enable these options for Docusign users:

- Preview
- Download
- Send for signature
- Send for internal approval
- Send for external review

Use this procedure to generate files and add them to the Docusign
Files component:

1. Log into Salesforce as a user with Docusign
   [Generator and Negotiator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate your Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.") permissions.
2. Open a Salesforce record (Account, Opportunity, and so on.)
3. Select a Gen button in the upper-right of the page.

   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/Select-Gen-custom-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYwNDIsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.PY-C0oKHUnLxZ7kbZ-mhgVnlIxlhvkKnWZWo3mF3oSE&_LANG=enus)

   Note: The button may be hidden under
   the arrow drop-down menu.

   The New Documents Generated and Saved page opens. The generated
   document is saved in the Notes & Attachments section of this record.
4. Select the Done button.

   The New Documents Generated and Saved page closes. The record page
   opens.
5. Select the Import button in the Docusign
   Files section of the page.

   The Import Files dialog box opens.
6. Select Choose from Salesforce Files. Complete the following
   steps in the Select a File dialog box that opens:
   1. Select the check the box next to a document. You can only import one file at a
      time.
   2. Select Import.

      The New Document Added dialog box opens.
7. Select Done.

   The New Document Added dialog box closes. The Docusign Files
   section refreshes and the new document displays.

You have completed the process. You have generated a new
document and uploaded it to the Docusign Files component. Users can view,
download, send for signature, send for external review, or send for internal approval.

title: Step 2: Add Recipients to Your Envelope (Envelope Template)
heading_path: Step 2: Add Recipients to Your Envelope (Envelope Template)
text:
Use this procedure to add recipients to the envelope. You can add recipients from your
Salesforce organization, from Docusign or by entering a name and email address.

You can add recipients from your Salesforce organization, from Docusign or
by entering a name and email address. [Follow these rules if you want to edit envelope template recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=uig1740079031042.html&_LANG=enus).

Use this procedure to add recipients to the envelope:

1. Select the Add Recipient button. The Add Recipient
   dialog box opens.
2. Add a recipient to your envelope:

   1. Select a recipient type from the left column:
      - From Salesforce: Enter the name of a
        Salesforce user or contact
      - By Name & Email: Enter any name and
        email address
      - Signing Group: Add a group of recipients
        from the connected Docusign account
   2. Action: Select a recipient action from the drop-down menu.
   3. (Optional) Select any option:
      - Delivery: [Send an envelope by email or
        SMS](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=psq1595994131623.html&_LANG=enus) (text message)

        Note:
        Allow SMS Delivery must be
        enabled in your account.
      - Add Role: Add a placeholder title (like
        Approver) that allows the sender to specify a name and email
        address later
      - Add Access Authentication
        - SMS/Phone: Send an access code to
          a recipient's mobile device to add an extra layer of
          security to the signing process
        - Access Code: Create a code that a
          recipient must enter to start the signing process
      - Add Private Message: Send a message to
        just this recipient or group
   4. Select the Select button.

   The Add Recipient dialog box closes. The recipient appears in the
   Recipients list.
3. (Optional):

   - Email Subject: Enter up to 100 characters of
     text.
   - Email Message: Enter up to 2,000 characters of
     text.
   - Reminders: Set the number of days between
     recipient reminders from the drop-down menu
   - Expiration: Enter the number of days before the
     envelope expires
4. Select the Next button in the upper-right of the page.

   The Docusign eSignature place fields page opens.

You have completed step two. Move to [Step 3](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xmx1596487118055.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Docusign Gen for Salesforce Overview (User)
heading_path: Docusign Gen for Salesforce Overview (User)
text:
Learn to use Docusign Gen to generate and send agreements for review
or signature.

Docusign Gen for Salesforce is an application in the Docusign
Apps Launcher for Salesforce package. Docusign Apps Launcher is
a unified bundle of Docusign apps: Docusign Gen, CLM,
Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce. Administrators configure and manage apps and
features from a centralized platform in Salesforce.

Docusign Gen for Salesforce is a document generation app. Your
administrator builds Gen templates and adds custom buttons to layouts. When you select a
button, Gen merges data from Salesforce records into your generated document. You can send
generated documents for review with Negotiate or send for signature with
eSignature for Salesforce.

This guide contains the following topics:

- [Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for
  Docusign Apps Launcher Apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=pay1650779719005.html&_LANG=enus "Learn the Docusign Apps Launcher requirements for browsers, operating systems, and devices.")
- [Activate Your Docusign User in
  Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate your Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.")
- [Enter a Verification Code to Access Docusign on a New Device](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=jtj1614664835837.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate a new device for use with your Docusign account.")
- [Enable Translations for Your Salesforce
  User](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=ioe1586134255116.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use Translation Workbench to let users choose their default Salesforce language.")
- [Generate Documents From Salesforce Records](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=ynf1688074694900.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to generate a new document from a Salesforce record using Docusign Gen for Salesforce.")

title: Upload a File to Docusign Files
heading_path: Upload a File to Docusign Files
text:
Learn how to upload files from your device to the Docusign Files
section of a Salesforce record.

Follow this procedure to upload files from your device to the Docusign
Files section of a Salesforce record.

Important: You must have Docusign Negotiate Negotiator permissions to use this
feature.

1. Open a Salesforce record (Account, Opportunity, and so on.)
2. Select Import in the Docusign Files section
   of the page.

   The Import Files dialog box opens.
3. Choose an import method:

   - Choose from Salesforce Files: Add a Salesforce document to
     Docusign Files:
     1. Select Choose from Salesforce Files. The Select a File
        dialog box opens.
     2. Select the check the box next to a document. **You can only import one file at
        a time.**
     3. Select Import. The New Document Added dialog box
        opens.
   - Upload from PC: Add a file from your device:
     1. Select Upload from PC. The Upload File dialog box
        opens.
     2. Select Upload. Follow the prompts to upload the file from
        your device.
     3. Select Import. The New Document Added dialog box
        opens.
4. (Optional) Send the document:

   - Send for External Review: Send to a recipient outside of your
     Salesforce organization
   - Send for Approval: Send to a recipient in your Salesforce
     organization
   - Send for Signature: Send to a recipient for signature
   - Done: Take no action and close the dialog box

   The file displays in the Docusign Files section of the
   Salesforce record when you refresh the page.

You have completed the process. You have added files to
the Docusign Files component. Any sending actions that you started are
in-progress.

title: Send a Document for Review or Approval with Negotiate
heading_path: Send a Document for Review or Approval with Negotiate
text:
Learn how to send a document for review or approval with Docusign
Negotiate.

You can manage your Docusign files on Salesforce records with the Docusign
Files section. You can take basic actions like adding, editing, and deleting
files.

You can also send files for internal review or external approval.

You can find these actions in the Share button ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/negotiateactionsarrow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYwODgsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.17EviLhpg3h7AqfDu6iNUxaqR5_Gw1vS5eEm5eouRP8&_LANG=enus) drop-down
menu.

This button is found to the right of any file in the Docusign Files section.

Here are options for sending content for review or approval from the Docusign Files section:

- [Create a Share Link to Send Docusign Files for Viewing, Downloading, and Editing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=ypi1586134378833.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to send a file from Docusign Files using a share link. This link lets people with access to your organization view, download, and edit your Docusign files.")
- [Use External Review to Send Docusign Files to Recipients Outside Your Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=gqt1586134379833.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to send a document for external review from the Docusign Files section of a Salesforce record.")
- [Use Send for Approval To Send Docusign Files to Users in Your Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=wkj1586134381776.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to send a document for internal approval from the Docusign Files section of a Salesforce record.")

Important: You must have [Docusign Negotiator permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=dzl1586134206658.html&bundleId=izj1586134369853&_LANG=enus) to send files for approval or review
with Negotiate.

title: Use External Review to Send Docusign Files to Recipients Outside Your Organization
heading_path: Use External Review to Send Docusign Files to Recipients Outside Your Organization
text:
Learn to send a document for external review from the Docusign Files section of a
Salesforce record.

Use this procedure to send a document for external review from the Docusign
Files section of a Salesforce record:

Note:

- Only customers of both Docusign Gen and Docusign Negotiate can send documents for
  external review
- You can only send a document for external review to a Salesforce contact

1. Select External Review from the drop-down menu.

   The Select Reviewer dialog box opens.
2. Complete the following in the Select Reviewer dialog box:

   1. Add a Reviewer: Enter the name or email address of a
      **Salesforce contact**.
   2. (Optional) Set a Due Date: Select this field and pick a date
      from the calendar.
   3. Select the Next button.

   The Personalize Your Message dialog box opens.
3. Complete the following in the Personalize Your Message dialog box:

   1. Subject: Keep or change the text of the email subject.
   2. Message: Keep or change the text of the email message.
   3. Select the Send button.

   The Personalize Your Message dialog box closes. A banner
   opens: Your document has been sent for review and copied to Docusign
   Files. The file appears in the Docusign Files section with a
   Pending Review status.

You have completed the process. You have sent the file
to a Salesforce contact. This contact can view, download, and edit this document.

title: Add New Currency Types to Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Add New Currency Types to Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn to add new currency types to your Salesforce organization.

You can add new currency types to your Salesforce organization. Adding currencies lets users
select different currency types when generating documents with Docusign Gen.

Example: Your Salesforce organization uses U.S.
Dollar as the default currency. Your users want to generate
documents with Euro as the currency. You add
Euro to your organization's currency types. Your Gen
users set the currency to Euro, and Docusign generates contracts with Euro as the
currency.

Note: You must add at least one currency to your organization to use
template-level [Gen date and
currency formatting](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=kqf1686095608923.html&_LANG=enus).

Use this procedure to add a new currency to your Salesforce organization:

1. Edit the Company Information page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYwODQsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.cnJzHmD1blq3v_tTIbNrNwBXUNx0718y5qm9gXJyQ0c&_LANG=enus) icon and
      choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Company Information in the
      Quick Find field.
   3. Select Edit.
2. Select the Activate Multiple Currencies check box and
   select Save.

   ![A screenshot displays the Activate Multiple Currencies box selected on the Salesforce Company Information page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Activate-Multiple-Currencies-box-selected-on-the-Salesforce-Company-Information-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYwODQsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.cnJzHmD1blq3v_tTIbNrNwBXUNx0718y5qm9gXJyQ0c&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Activate Multiple Currencies box selected on the Salesforce Company Information page.")

   The Company Information page opens.
3. Select Currency Setup and select
   New.

   The New Currency page opens.
4. Add a new currency:
   1. Currency Type: Select a new currency from the
      dropdown menu.
   2. Conversion Rate: Enter the conversion rate from
      your default currency to this currency. Example:
      .092.
   3. Decimal Places: Enter the number of digits
      displayed to the right of the decimal point. This setting overrides the
      decimal places setting on all currency fields for users with this
      currency. You can choose between 0 and 6 digits.
   4. Select Save.

You have completed the process. Users can change
the Currency field on a record to the new currency. Gen users can
select the new currency before generating documents.

title: Rename a File in the Docusign Files Component
heading_path: Rename a File in the Docusign Files Component
text:
Learn how to rename a file in the Docusign Files component of a
Salesforce record.

Use this procedure to rename a document in the Docusign Files section
of a Salesforce record:

1. Select Rename from the dropdown menu.

   The Rename Document dialog box opens.
2. Enter a new file name in the Document Name field.
3. Select Rename.

   A message displays: (document name) has been renamed. The
   Salesforce record page displays. The document has a New Version
   status button below it.

You have completed the process. You have renamed the
file.

title: Set the Currency and Locale Before Generating a Document
heading_path: Set the Currency and Locale Before Generating a Document
text: Learn to set the currency and locale of a document before you generate it.

title: Set the Currency and Locale Before Generating a Document
heading_path: Set the Currency and Locale Before Generating a Document
Before you begin
text:
- Your administrator must grant Docusign
  Generator
  permissions to your user
- Your administrator must enable the Generator Can Edit
  feature on the Gen template
- You must [set the default currency you want to
  use on the Salesforce record](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=xrh1686885796196.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to change the type of currency Salesforce displays on a record.")

You can set the currency and locale of a document before generating it from a record.
The following happens when you select a Gen button with Generator Can
Edit enabled:

- You set the date and currency formatting you want the generated document to
  display
- Gen creates a document using the formatting you selected
- Gen saves the generated document in Notes &
  Attachments

You can view or download the generated document or send it for signature.

Use this procedure to set the currency and locale of a document before generating it
from a record:

1. Select a Gen button on a Salesforce record.

   ![A screenshot of a Salesforce record displays a Gen custom button named Fresh Software Sales Contract under the arrow menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/Select-Gen-custom-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYwMjksInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.0Lu9RXrZOSh2WdDtWSwwC-sRvTzqKu7JYX-CqQxTOjg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a Salesforce record displays a Gen custom button named Fresh Software Sales Contract under the arrow menu.")

   The Date and Currency Format page opens.
2. (Optional): Customize the Date and Currency Format
   settings:
   1. Locale: Select a locale option from the dropdown
      menu:

      - Generator's Locale: Use the generating
        user's Salesforce locale for date formatting
      - Org Locale: Use the Salesforce
        organization's locale for date formatting
      - All Locales: Select a country or region
        to set date formatting
   2. Currency Format: Select a currency format from
      the dropdown menu:

      - **Display as symbol**: Example: **$123,456.78**
      - **Display as symbol (no decimals)**: Example:
        **$123,457**
      - **Display as ISO code**: Example: **USD 123,456.78**
      - **Display as ISO code (no decimals)**: Example: **USD
        123,457**
      - **Display without symbol and ISO code**: Example:
        **123,456.78**
3. Select Generate.

   The New Documents Generated & Saved dialog box
   opens. Salesforce saves the generated document in Notes &
   Attachments.
4. (Optional): Take action on the generated document:

   Note: Your administrator can disable the Send
   with Docusign button.

   - Preview: See a preview of the generated document
   - Download: Save a copy of the document to your
     device
   - [Send for Signature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=gsh1675832324336.html&_LANG=enus): Send to a recipient for signature
   - Done: Take no action and close the dialog
     box

You have completed the process. You have generated
a new contract and taken any optional steps. Salesforce saves the generated document in
the Notes & Attachments section of the record.

title: Change the Default Language for Gen, Negotiate, eSignature for Salesforce, and CLM
heading_path: Change the Default Language for Gen, Negotiate, eSignature for Salesforce, and CLM
text:
Learn to use Translation Workbench to let users choose their default Salesforce
language.

You can use Translation Workbench to let your users choose their default
Salesforce language. Docusign app content displays in the selected
language.

You can use the following options to enable language support in your Salesforce
organization:

- [Enable Translations for Your Salesforce Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ehh1586134268380.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Enable Translations for Your Salesforce User](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=yzs1586134269459.html&_LANG=enus)

title: Generate an Invoice with Gen for Billing and Email It to Recipients
heading_path: Generate an Invoice with Gen for Billing and Email It to Recipients
text: You can generate an invoice from a custom button and email it to recipients.

title: Generate an Invoice with Gen for Billing and Email It to Recipients
heading_path: Generate an Invoice with Gen for Billing and Email It to Recipients
Before you begin
text:
Your administrator must grant Docusign
Generator permissions to your Docusign user.

You can generate an invoice from a custom button and
email it to recipients.

Here's how to do that:

1. Find a Gen for Billing custom button:
   1. Log into Salesforce as a user with Docusign Gen
      Generator permissions.
   2. Open a Salesforce Invoice record.
   3. Select a Gen for Billing button on the page layout.

      Note: The button may be hidden under the arrow
      menu.![A screenshot displays an arrow menu opening to a button labeled Fresh Software Sales Contract.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/Select-Gen-custom-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYwNzcsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.i-RL99YGr4MW8yY7ZJFmmCyMDu9vareb5y3kU9bK1co&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays an arrow menu opening to a button labeled Fresh Software Sales Contract.")

      The New Documents Generated and Saved page opens.
2. Select Deliver by Email.

   The Deliver By Email dialog box opens.
3. Select Add Recipient.

   The Add Recipient dialog box opens.
4. Add a recipient:
   1. Name: Enter a name for the recipient.
   2. Email: Enter an Email address for the
      recipient.
   3. (Optional) Set as Bcc: Check the box to
      hide this recipient.
   4. Add: Save the recipient and close the dialog
      box.
   5. Add & New: Save the recipient and add
      another.
5. Optional Steps:

   - Email Subject: Edit or replace text in the subject field.
   - Email Message: Edit or replace text in the message field.
   - Preview: Select this link to see a preview of the generated
     document.
   - Download: Save a copy of the contract to your device.
6. Select the Send button.

You have completed the process. You have generated an
invoice, added recipients, and sent an email.

title: Enter a Verification Code to Access Docusign on a New Device
heading_path: Enter a Verification Code to Access Docusign on a New Device
text:
Learn how to activate a new device for use with your Docusign account.

You must enter a verification code to access your Docusign account on a new device. You must verify your device again if
you empty your browser cache.

Use this procedure to activate your Docusign account on a new device:

1. Enter Docusign credentials when prompted.

   The Login from a new device dialog box
   displays.
2. Select Email. Docusign sends a verification code to your
   email address.
3. Open the Verify a New Device email from Docusign. Check
   your junk mail folder if you do not see the message in your Inbox.
4. Copy the verification code from the email.
5. Enter the verification code in the Login from a new device
   dialog box.
6. Select Confirm.

You have completed the process. You can access your Docusign account on this device.

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Learn the Docusign Apps Launcher requirements for browsers, operating
systems, and devices.

You can use the Docusign for Salesforce apps with the following browsers,
operating systems, and devices:

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
Supported browsers
text:
Important: Salesforce and Docusign do not
support any version of Microsoft Internet Explorer.

| Browser | Supported version | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Google Chrome | Latest | n/a |
| Safari (Apple) | Latest | Desktop-only. Salesforce does not support the mobile version of Safari. Enabling third-party cookies is required. |
| Microsoft Edge | Latest | n/a |
| Mozilla Firefox | Latest | n/a |

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
Supported operating systems
text:
| Operating system | Version |
| --- | --- |
| Microsoft Windows | Windows 10 or newer |
| MacOS (Apple desktop) | 10.14 (Mojave) or newer |
| iOS (Apple mobile) | iOS 14.x or newer |
| Android OS | Android 8.0 or newer |

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
Salesforce mobile app requirements
text:
Required settings: Configure the following settings to use Docusign in the Salesforce mobile app:

- Enable third-party cookies in the [Salesforce mobile app settings](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.salesforce_app.htm&type=5).
- Turn off Include the instance name in Visualforce URLs when
  third-party cookies are blocked in Salesforce My
  Domain settings.

Approved Docusign apps: You can use the following Docusign apps with the Salesforce mobile app:

- Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
- Docusign Gen

Non-approved Docusign apps: You cannot use the following Docusign apps with the Salesforce mobile app:

- Docusign Negotiate
- Docusign CLM
- Docusign CLM Essentials

Approved envelope template types: You can only use the following
envelope template types in the Salesforce mobile app:

- [Quick send envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Send now envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus)

Mobile app tagging requirements: Meet the following requirements
to add fields to Docusign envelopes:

- Install the Docusign mobile app on your Android or iOS
  device.
- Turn off mobile app tagging when using an iPad.

For more information about Salesforce requirements, see the [Supported Browsers and Devices](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.getstart_browser_overview.htm&type=5) page on the
Salesforce support site.

title: Step 1: Add Documents or Templates (Send With DocuSign Button)
heading_path: Step 1: Add Documents or Templates (Send With DocuSign Button)
text:
Use this procedure to add documents or templates to a standard Send with DocuSign
envelope.

Use this procedure to add documents or templates to a
standard Send with DocuSign envelope.

1. Open a Salesforce record (Account, Opportunity, and so on.)
2. Select the Send with DocuSign button in the upper-right
   of the page.

   Note: It may be hidden in the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxMDgsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.K-Dz6zHBuVtFwPELnCIzdOdzMFJ84LPPXORrflV6woo&_LANG=enus) menu. ![Send with DocuSign button](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/Send-with-DocuSign-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxMDgsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.K-Dz6zHBuVtFwPELnCIzdOdzMFJ84LPPXORrflV6woo&_LANG=enus "Send with DocuSign button")

   The Documents page opens, displaying all documents
   from the Notes & Attachments section of the record.
3. (Optional) Choose any option to upload or arrange documents for your
   envelope:

   - Select the check box next to any Notes & Attachments documents you
     want to include
   - Upload: Select this button and follow the prompts
     to add a document from your device
   - Add from Salesforce: Select this button to add
     documents from Salesforce Files
   - Drag documents to change their order in the envelope![Drag files to reorder](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/Drag-files-to-reorder.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxMDgsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.K-Dz6zHBuVtFwPELnCIzdOdzMFJ84LPPXORrflV6woo&_LANG=enus "Drag files to reorder")
   - Select Template: Apply an existing DocuSign
     template to your envelope:
     1. Select the Select Template button. The
        Select DocuSign Template dialog box
        opens.This displays every template from your DocuSign account
        and all of your envelope templates.
     2. Select the check box next to any templates you want to use in
        this envelope.
     3. Select the Select button.![Select existing DocuSign templates](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/Select-existing-DocuSign-templates.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxMDgsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.K-Dz6zHBuVtFwPELnCIzdOdzMFJ84LPPXORrflV6woo&_LANG=enus "Select existing DocuSign templates")The Select DocuSign Template dialog
        box closes. All documents, recipients and signature fields from
        the selected templates are added to your envelope.
4. Select the Next button. The Recipients page opens.

You have completed step one. Move to [Step 2: Add Recipients to Your Envelope (Envelope Template)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=dfp1596477842847.html&_LANG=enus "Use this procedure to add recipients to the envelope. You can add recipients from your Salesforce organization, from Docusign or by entering a name and email address.").

title: Upload a New Version of a Document
heading_path: Upload a New Version of a Document
text:
Learn how to upload a new version of a document in the Docusign Files
section of a Salesforce record.

Use this procedure to upload a new version of a document in the Docusign
Files section of a Salesforce record:

1. Select Upload a New Version from the dropdown menu.

   The Upload a New Version dialog box opens.
2. Select Upload and follow the prompts to upload a file from your
   device.

   The uploaded file displays in the dialog box.
3. Select Upload.

   Note:
   Docusign keeps
   the original file name, even if the uploaded file name is different.

   The dialog box closes. A message displays: A new version of (document
   name) has been uploaded. The Salesforce record page displays. The document
   has a New Version status button below it.

You have completed the process. You have uploaded a new
version of the document.

title: Use a Gen for Billing Custom Button to Generate, Save, and Send an Invoice
heading_path: Use a Gen for Billing Custom Button to Generate, Save, and Send an Invoice
text:
Learn to generate an invoice with Gen for Billing and save, download, or send it for
signature.

You can generate a new invoice from a Salesforce record using a Gen for Billing button.

You select a Gen for Billing button on a record and Docusign generates an invoice using
data from the record. Gen saves the generated document in the record's Notes
& Attachments section. You can download the document or send it for
review or signature.

To use Gen for Billing, you need [Docusign Generator
permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=doi1628107496987.html&_LANG=enus) and access to the Salesforce Invoices
object.

Use these procedures to generate, save, and send invoices with Gen for Billing:

- [Generate an Invoice with Gen for Billing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=baw1614577855281.html&_LANG=enus "You can select a Docusign Gen for Billing custom button on a Salesforce record to generate an invoice. The generated invoice is automatically saved to the Notes and Attachments section of the Salesforce record.")
- [Generate an Invoice With Gen for Billing and Email It to
  Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=iyu1615180590827.html&_LANG=enus "You can generate an invoice from a custom button and email it to recipients.")

title: Send an Envelope Using a Standard Send With Docusign Template in Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Send an Envelope Using a Standard Send With Docusign Template in Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
You can send documents for eSignature using the built-in Docusign for Salesforce app in
the Docusign Apps Launcher package.

You can create and send an envelope using a standardSend with DocuSign button.

The envelope starts out blank,
with no pre-filled documents, recipients, or eSignature fields present.

The
envelope creation process has three stages:

- [Step 1: Add Documents or Templates (Send With DocuSign Button)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=pzf1617644386542.html&_LANG=enus "Use this procedure to add documents or templates to a standard Send with DocuSign envelope.")
- [Step 2: Add Recipients to Your Envelope (Envelope Template)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=dfp1596477842847.html&_LANG=enus "Use this procedure to add recipients to the envelope. You can add recipients from your Salesforce organization, from Docusign or by entering a name and email address.")
- [Step 3: Add Docusign and Salesforce Fields and Send Your Envelope (Send With Docusign Button)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=xmx1596487118055.html&_LANG=enus "The third step of the process involves placing Docusign and Salesforce fields into the document text.")

Use this procedure to create and send an envelope using a standard
Send with DocuSign button:

title: Download a Document from Docusign Files
heading_path: Download a Document from Docusign Files
text:
Learn how to download documents from the Docusign Files section of a
Salesforce record.

Use this procedure to download documents from the Docusign Files
section of a Salesforce record.

Select Download from the dropdown menu.

A banner opens: Successfully downloaded (file name).

You have completed the process. You have downloaded a
copy of the document from Docusign Files to your device.

title: Copy a File from Docusign Files to Notes & Attachments
heading_path: Copy a File from Docusign Files to Notes & Attachments
text:
Learn how to copy documents from Docusign Files to the Notes &
Attachments section of a Salesforce record.

Use this procedure to copy documents from Docusign Files to the
Notes & Attachments section of a Salesforce record.

Select Copy to Notes & Attachments from the dropdown
menu.

A dialog box displays: Document export in progress. Please check Notes
and Attachments section after some time. The dialog box closes. Another
dialog box displays: Document has been exported
successfully.

You have completed the process. You have copied a file
from Docusign Files to Salesforce Notes &
Attachments.

title: Use the One-Click Generation and Sending Experience
heading_path: Use the One-Click Generation and Sending Experience
text:
Learn to use a Gen and Send button to generate a document and send it for signature with
one click.

title: Use the One-Click Generation and Sending Experience
heading_path: Use the One-Click Generation and Sending Experience
Before you begin
text:
Your administrator must grant Docusign
Generator permissions to your Docusign user.

"Gen and Send" is an experience that lets you generate a document and send
it for signature with one click. Your administrator creates this experience by
linking a Gen template with an envelope template. You select the button and Docusign generates a document based on the record's data and sends it to envelope template
recipients.

Use this procedure to generate a document and send it for signature with a
Gen and Send button:

1. Open a Salesforce record containing a Gen and Send custom button. Your
   administrator decides the button name.
2. Select the Gen and Send button.

   Docusign generates a document based on the record's data and sends it to
   the envelope template recipients. A message opens: Sent for
   Signature.

You have completed the process. You have generated a document
based on that record’s data and sent it to envelope template recipients. When all
recipients complete the signing process, the finished contract saves to your Docusign account and the Salesforce record.

title: Use Send for Approval To Send Docusign Files to Users in Your Organization
heading_path: Use Send for Approval To Send Docusign Files to Users in Your Organization
text:
Learn how to send a document for internal approval from the Docusign Files section of a
Salesforce record.

Use this procedure to send a document for internal approval from the Docusign
Files section of a Salesforce record:

Note:

- Only customers of both Docusign Gen and Docusign Negotiate can send documents for
  internal approval
- You can only send internal approval requests to Salesforce users
- Internal approval recipients must have active Negotiator
  permissions to act on your request
- You can not send internal approval requests to Salesforce contacts or external email
  addresses

1. Select Internal Approval from the drop-down menu.

   The Select Approvers dialog box opens.
2. Complete the following in the Select Approvers dialog box:

   1. Add Approvers: Enter the name of any Salesforce user from
      your organization.
   2. (Optional) + Add Another Approver: Select this button to add
      more approvers.
   3. (Optional) Set Approval Order:
      1. Select the check box if you have more than one approver.
      2. Drag users up or down to create a new approval order.
         ![Change approval order](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/Change-approval-order.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxMDMsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.hJRGVi-IKAhttlq-soKf_rH3ysMtsWapa52aS_g_pz0&_LANG=enus "Change approval order")
   4. Select the Next button.

   The Personalize Your Message dialog box opens.
3. Complete the following in the Personalize Your Message dialog box:

   1. Subject: Keep or change the text of the email subject.
   2. Message: Keep or change the text of the email message.
   3. Select the Send button.

   The Personalize Your Message dialog box closes. A banner
   opens: Your document has been sent for approval and copied to Docusign
   Files. The file appears in the Docusign Files section with a
   Pending Approval status.

You have completed the process. You have sent the file
to a user from your Salesforce organization. This user can view, download, and edit this
document.

title: Generate a Contract and Send It for Signature
heading_path: Generate a Contract and Send It for Signature
text:
You can generate a document with Docusign Gen and then send it for signature with
Docusign eSignature for Salesforce.

title: Generate a Contract and Send It for Signature
heading_path: Generate a Contract and Send It for Signature
Before you begin
text:
Your administrator must grant Docusign
Generator permissions to your Docusign user.

You can generate a document with Docusign Gen for Salesforce and send it
for signature using Docusign eSignature for Salesforce.

There are two ways to send a generated document for signature:

Send with Docusign template
:   This is a three-stage sending experience built by Docusign. You add documents, recipients,
    and signature fields.

Docusign envelope template
:   This is a sending experience built by your administrator and linked to your
    Gen template. You may see documents, recipients, and signature fields in the
    envelope that have been pre-added by your administrator. You can add to or
    edit these elements.

Use this procedure to generate a document with Gen and send it for signature:

1. Open a Salesforce record.
2. Select a Gen button in the upper right.

   Note: Salesforce may hide
   the button under the arrow drop-down menu.

   ![Select Gen custom button](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/Select-Gen-custom-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYwMjQsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.QeSElQ2k4eNDgy0RIKdQGSuZg7kIIBZG5C55QnELW3Y&_LANG=enus "Select Gen custom button")

   The New Documents Generated & Saved dialog box
   opens. Salesforce saves the generated document in Notes &
   Attachments.
3. Select Send for Signature.
4. Follow the prompts to [complete and
   send your envelope](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=wxo1617594601811.html&_LANG=enus).

You have completed the process. You have generated a
document with Docusign Gen and sent it for signature with Docusign eSignature for
Salesforce.

title: Step 3: Add Docusign and Salesforce Fields and Send Your Envelope (Send With Docusign Button)
heading_path: Step 3: Add Docusign and Salesforce Fields and Send Your Envelope (Send With Docusign Button)
text:
The third step of the process involves placing Docusign and Salesforce fields into the
document text.

Follow this procedure to place Docusign and Salesforce fields
into the document text.

1. Add Docusign fields to the document:

   1. Select a Docusign field from the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/envelopetemplatestandardsfields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxMTMsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.C7L17Mbq5_0yDe3pxz6Fl4wJGN-u88bJ76_g4q1gpj4&_LANG=enus)
      Standard Fields menu in the left column.
   2. Drag the field to the document.![Add Docusign fields](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/Add-DocuSign-fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxMTMsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.C7L17Mbq5_0yDe3pxz6Fl4wJGN-u88bJ76_g4q1gpj4&_LANG=enus "Add Docusign fields")
2. Add Salesforce fields to the document:

   1. Select the Merge Fields button ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/envelopetemplatemergefields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxMTMsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.C7L17Mbq5_0yDe3pxz6Fl4wJGN-u88bJ76_g4q1gpj4&_LANG=enus) from the left
      column. The Salesforce Merge Fields menu opens.
   2. Select a field from the menu. You can expand arrows to find more fields, or enter text in
      the Search Fields field.
   3. Drag the field to the document text.![Add Salesforce fields](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/Add-Salesforce-fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxMTMsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.C7L17Mbq5_0yDe3pxz6Fl4wJGN-u88bJ76_g4q1gpj4&_LANG=enus "Add Salesforce fields")
3. (Optional) Choose any option:

   - Preview: Select this button to see a preview of
     the document.
   - Add fields for more recipients:
     1. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxMTMsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.C7L17Mbq5_0yDe3pxz6Fl4wJGN-u88bJ76_g4q1gpj4&_LANG=enus) next to the recipient name in the
        upper-left of the page.
     2. Select another recipient from the drop-down menu.
     3. Drag Docusign fields for this recipient to the document
        text.

        Note: Each recipient's fields are
        represented by a separate color (yellow, blue, and so
        on.)![Switch recipients](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/Switch-recipients.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxMTMsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.C7L17Mbq5_0yDe3pxz6Fl4wJGN-u88bJ76_g4q1gpj4&_LANG=enus "Switch recipients")
4. Select the Send button in the upper-right of the
   page.

You have completed the process. You have built and sent an
envelope.

title: Change the Currency Type of a Salesforce Record
heading_path: Change the Currency Type of a Salesforce Record
text:
Learn to change the type of currency Salesforce displays on a record.

Your Salesforce organization has a default currency format. However, your administrator can
add new currencies to your organization. Adding currencies lets you change the
default currency on a record.

Example: Your Salesforce organization uses U.S.
Dollar as the default currency, but your administrator has added
Euro. You change the Opportunity
Currency field of a French company to Euro.
The Amount field displays the value in Euros.

Note: If you want to [choose the currency format when generating documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=hhz1686691127617.html&_LANG=enus), you must change a record's currency first.

Use this procedure to change the currency type of a Salesforce record:

1. Select the Details tab of a record.
2. Select the pencil icon ![A screenshot of the pencil icon used to edit fields on a Salesforce record.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-pencil-icon-used-to-edit-fields-on-a-Salesforce-record.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYwMzIsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.oJl6bikv-sHkB_QIdrGJrOhaEFAWMzL-8K0Lm4AieE0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the pencil icon used to edit fields on a Salesforce record.") on the (object) Currency field.
3. Select a new currency type from the dropdown menu.
4. Select Save.

   The Amount field displays the new
   currency.

You have completed the process. You have changed the default
currency of a record.

title: Generate a Document with Docusign Gen
heading_path: Generate a Document with Docusign Gen
text:
Learn how to generate a document from a Salesforce record using Docusign Gen for
Salesforce.

title: Generate a Document with Docusign Gen
heading_path: Generate a Document with Docusign Gen
Before you begin
text:
Your administrator must grant Docusign
Generator permissions to your Docusign user.

You can use Docusign Gen to generate a document from Salesforce. The following happens when
you select a Gen button on a Salesforce record:

- Gen creates a document using data from the Salesforce record
- Gen saves the generated document in Notes & Attachments

You can view or download the generated document or send it for review or signature.

Important: You are the owner of the files you generate.
However, Salesforce displays a Docusign administrator as the Owner in
the record's Files related list. To find files you generated, visit Files >  Owned by Me.

Use this procedure to generate a document with Gen:

1. Select a Gen button on a Salesforce record. Your administrator chooses the button
   name.

   ![A screenshot of a Salesforce record displays a Gen custom button named Fresh Software Sales Contract under the arrow menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/Select-Gen-custom-button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYwMTksInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.hz0QixXqpwEG5zZq-PM33Zfz2pWV8w6K7n8X4uSdow0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of a Salesforce record displays a Gen custom button named Fresh Software Sales Contract under the arrow menu.")

   The New Documents Generated & Saved dialog box opens.
   Gen saves the generated document in Notes & Attachments.
2. (Optional): Take action on the generated document:

   Note: Your administrator can disable the Send with
   Docusign button.

   - Preview: See a preview of the generated document
   - Download: Save a copy of the document to your device
   - [Send for Signature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=gsh1675832324336.html&_LANG=enus): Send to a recipient for signature
   - Done: Take no action and close the dialog box

You have completed the process. You have generated a
new contract and taken any optional steps. Salesforce saves the generated document in the
Notes & Attachments section of the record.

title: Generate a Document from the Salesforce Mobile App
heading_path: Generate a Document from the Salesforce Mobile App
text: Learn to use the Salesforce mobile app to generate a document from a Salesforce record.

title: Generate a Document from the Salesforce Mobile App
heading_path: Generate a Document from the Salesforce Mobile App
Before you begin
text:
Your administrator must grant Docusign
Generator permissions to your Docusign user.

Docusign Gen for Salesforce lets you
generate documents from Salesforce records. Gen creates content by merging Docusign and Salesforce fields into uploaded documents.

You can
select a Gen custom button on a record and instantly generate contracts, invoices,
and more.

You can send this content for Docusign review or
signature.

You can use the Salesforce mobile app to
generate a document from a Salesforce record using Docusign Gen.

Use this procedure to send a Docusign envelope from the
Salesforce mobile app:

1. Open a Salesforce record.
2. Select a Docusign Gen custom button. The button may be hidden in the ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/Salesforce-More-button.jpg?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYwMzcsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.g4n79xiHbkE6N0G8t7udvZcIZfYVLbXZ3WvQIVbcU1g&_LANG=enus)
   More menu.

   A spinning icon displays progress. A message opens: New
   Documents Generated & Saved
3. Select an option:

   - Send for Signature: Create a Docusign envelope
     containing this document.
   - Done: Return to the record.

You have completed the process. You have generated a
document from a Salesforce record using Docusign Gen on the Salesforce mobile
app.

title: Activate Your Docusign for Salesforce User
heading_path: Activate Your Docusign for Salesforce User
text:
Learn how to activate your Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or
eSignature for Salesforce user.

Your administrator can create a Docusign for Salesforce user for you. You
can access approved Docusign apps in Salesforce with this user.
Administrators can create these types of Docusign for Salesforce
users:

- CLM: Access CLM files, folders, and features (workflow, document
  generation, review, and so on.)
- Gen: Generate contracts and send them for signature.
- Negotiate: Send Docusign files for external
  review, internal approval, or signature.
- eSignature: Create envelopes and send them for signature.

You receive an activation email from Docusign when your administrator
creates your user. Activate your user to access Docusign apps in
Salesforce.

Use this procedure to activate your Docusign user:

1. Open the Account Activation email from Docusign. Check your spam
   folder if you don't see the message in your inbox.
2. Select Activate.
3. Follow the activation prompts on the Docusign
   Log In page:

   - Existing
     Docusign
      users:
     Enter your Docusign credentials and select Accept
     Invitation.
   - New
     Docusign
      users:
     Create a Password and Security Question,
     and select Activate.
4. Complete your Docusign authentication in Salesforce:

   Important: Your Docusign user is not
   active until you complete these steps.

   1. Select a Docusign component or custom button on a record and
      select Authorize.

      The Docusign Log In dialog box opens.
   2. Enter your Docusign credentials or select Company Login to log in with SSO credentials.
   3. Select Log In.
   4. Select Accept.
   5. Select Allow Access.
5. Enter your Salesforce credentials.

   Note: Salesforce may require you to select
   Authorize and enter your credentials twice.

You have completed the process. Your Docusign user is active. You can use approved Docusign apps
in Salesforce.

title: Generate Documents From Salesforce Records
heading_path: Generate Documents From Salesforce Records
text:
Learn to generate a new document from a Salesforce record using Docusign Gen for
Salesforce.

You can use Docusign Gen to generate a document from Salesforce. You select a Gen custom button
on a record and instantly generate contracts, invoices, and more. Gen saves the
generated document in the record's Notes & Attachments. You can
download the document or send it for review or signature.

There are a number Gen experiences you can use. Each experience is based on a template built
and named by your administrator.

[Generate a Document with Docusign Gen](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=xtr1586134215865.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to generate a document from a Salesforce record using Docusign Gen for Salesforce.")
:   Generate a document from a record with the standard Gen experience and take no further
    action.

[Generate a Document and Send It for Signature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=wqx1612162988070.html&_LANG=enus "You can generate a document with Docusign Gen and then send it for signature with Docusign eSignature for Salesforce.")
:   Generate a document from a record with the standard Gen experience and send it for
    signature.

[Use the One-Click Generation and Sending Experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=wbn1686090714552.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use a Gen and Send button to generate a document and send it for signature with one click.")
:   The "Gen and Send" experience. With one click, Docusign generates a document and sends it to
    recipients from a linked envelope template.

[Set the Currency and Locale Before Generating a Document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=hhz1686691127617.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to set the currency and locale of a document before you generate it.")
:   Select the date, time, and currency formats before generating a document.

[Generate a Document From the Salesforce Mobile App](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=xuv1676000019535.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use the Salesforce mobile app to generate a document from a Salesforce record.")
:   Generate a document from a record on the Salesforce mobile app.

[Generate a Document and Add it to Docusign Files](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=blu1586134216052.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to add files to the Docusign Files component after generating them with Gen.")
:   Generate a document and add it to the Docusign Files component. You must be a
    Docusign
    Gen and Negotiate customer to
    use this option.

[Generate an Invoice with Gen for Billing](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=baw1614577855281.html&_LANG=enus "You can select a Docusign Gen for Billing custom button on a Salesforce record to generate an invoice. The generated invoice is automatically saved to the Notes and Attachments section of the Salesforce record.")
:   Generate an invoice using a Gen for Billing custom button.

[Generate an Invoice With Gen for Billing and Email It to Recipients](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=iyu1615180590827.html&_LANG=enus "You can generate an invoice from a custom button and email it to recipients.")
:   Generate an invoice using a Gen for Billing custom button and email the invoice
    to recipients.

title: Create a Share Link to Send Docusign Files for Viewing, Downloading, and Editing
heading_path: Create a Share Link to Send Docusign Files for Viewing, Downloading, and Editing
text:
Learn how to send a file from Docusign Files using a share link. This link lets people
with access to your organization view, download, and edit your Docusign files.

Use this procedure to create and send a share link for a document in the Docusign
Files section of a Salesforce record:

Note: Only users with permission to access
your Docusign Agreements can use this link to view, download, and edit shared
content.

1. Select Share Link from the drop-down menu.

   The Share Link dialog box opens.
2. Select the Copy Link button.

   The Copy Link button turns into a check mark with the words Link
   Copied! underneath.
3. Select the X button above the upper-right of the Share Link
   dialog box.

   The Share Link dialog box closes.

You have completed the process. You can send the copied
link to anyone with access to your Salesforce organization. Users with this link can view,
download, and edit this document.

title: Enable Translations for Your Salesforce User
heading_path: Enable Translations for Your Salesforce User
text:
Learn how to change the display language of all Docusign Apps Launcher
apps.

You can change the display language of all Docusign Apps Launcher apps by changing the
language settings for your Salesforce user.

1. Access the Salesforce Language & Time Zone settings:
   1. Select the Salesforce user icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/bpg1586134377111/page/images/salesforceusericon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYwMTAsInNoZWFmIjoiYnBnMTU4NjEzNDM3NzExMSJ9.6sGordmW3dDkqTkI4taA7F-kQQuZFjfBZGX_-HOG-AM&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Settings.
   2. Select Language & Time Zone.
2. Language: Select a language from the dropdown menu.
3. Select Save.

   A message displays: Your settings have been successfully
   saved.
4. Empty your browser cache and restart the browser.

You have completed the process. Docusign Apps Launcher content displays in the language that you
chose.

title: Delete a File from the Docusign Files Component
heading_path: Delete a File from the Docusign Files Component
text:
Learn how to delete a file in the Docusign Files component of a
Salesforce record.

Use this procedure to delete a file in the Docusign Files component of
a Salesforce record:

1. Select Delete from the dropdown menu.

   The Delete File dialog box opens.
2. Select Delete.

   A message displays: (document name) has been deleted. The
   Salesforce record page displays. The document is no longer in the Docusign Files section.

You have completed the process. You have deleted the
file.

title: Refresh the Products Provisioned to Your Docusign Account with the TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Refresh the Products Provisioned to Your Docusign Account with the TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn to activate and update Docusign for Salesforce apps and settings
using the Refresh Products feature.

Administrators can use the Docusign Apps Launcher
TroubleShooting tool to fix issues with Docusign apps. The TroubleShooting tool is available in Docusign Apps Launcher version 2.5 and higher. Salesforce
administrators with Docusign administrator permissions can use it.

You can activate and update apps and settings in your Salesforce organization using the
Refresh Products feature.

- Switch any newly added products like Docusign Gen from
  Pending to Active
- Update the Account Products Last Checked custom setting
- Keep account feature settings up to date

Follow this procedure to use the Refresh Products feature:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.4eUI-uL6WQGBnBshVYqwnutcI5fwbKceGEUXpVRp1h0&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting in the Label
   column.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/oas1598330401772.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.4eUI-uL6WQGBnBshVYqwnutcI5fwbKceGEUXpVRp1h0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.
4. Refresh Products: Select Execute.

   The page refreshes. A message opens: Successfully refreshed
   products on Docusign account.

You have completed the process. You have updated settings and
activated pending apps.

title: Use Manage App Policies to Allow or Prevent Docusign User Access
heading_path: Use Manage App Policies to Allow or Prevent Docusign User Access
text:
Learn how to use Manage App Policies to allow or prevent users
from authorizing their Docusign access.

New Docusign app users see an Authorize button when
they access Docusign content on a record. New users must enter
their Salesforce and Docusign credentials to access Docusign apps. Administrators can access the Manage App
Policies page to allow or prevent users from self-authorizing.

Use this procedure to manage your Docusign app policies:

1. Select Manage App Policies.

   ![A screenshot displays the Connected Apps OAuth Usage page with the Docusign Manage App Policies link highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Salesforce-Manage-App-Policies-link.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyOTEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.hiiVqRx7KHCPqGf89lpWBuQWsqrBGyDxSxcHv7I_Ghg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Connected Apps OAuth Usage page with the Docusign Manage App Policies link highlighted.")
2. Select Edit Policies.
3. Permitted Users: Select an option from the dropdown
   menu:

   - All users may self-authorize: Users may select the
     Authorize button to use Docusign apps.
   - Admin approved users are pre-authorized: Administrators assign Docusign app access to specific groups.

     Note: Enabling the Admin approved users are
     pre-authorized option denies access to all current
     users. Administrators use Salesforce profiles to assign
     access.
4. Select Save.

   The page closes. The Docusign Connected App Detail
   page displays.

You have completed the process. You have allowed or prevented
users from authorizing their Docusign access in your Salesforce
organization.

title: Add the Envelope Status Component to a Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Add the Envelope Status Component to a Salesforce Layout
text:
Learn to add the Docusign Envelope Status component to a Salesforce layout.

Docusign  Envelope Status is a component that you can add to your Salesforce layouts. The component displays the progress of all Docusign envelopes sent from a record. The component displays envelopes sent using the following methods:

- A Send with Docusign action
- An Envelope Template action
- [A Start with Docusign workflow button](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tskcae26f13-f2e5-4afd-85a7-145a70b12977.html&_LANG=enus)
- [An Agreement Desk for Salesforce request](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk151c1542-bdf4-4c8e-ba20-37affb7adc7e.html&_LANG=enus)
- [eSignature actions in Salesforce Flows](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dzi1705696883404.html&_LANG=enus)

Salesforce users with Docusign access can view and act on envelopes without logging on to Docusign.

Tip: This procedure is explained in this [short how-to video](https://docusign.gallery.video/watch?videoId=6250567558001).

Use this procedure to add the Docusign Envelope Status component to a Salesforce layout:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, open a record, select the gear icon ![A screenshot displays the a gear icon with a lightning bolt inside.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxODAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.TBTgekkcjDF26jnRPsl6kET8-6ft3j8tarTuVkVKtlI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the a gear icon with a lightning bolt inside.") and choose Edit Page from the dropdown menu.
2. Drag the Docusign Envelope Status component to the page layout on the right.

   ![A screen capture displays a cursor dragging the Docusign Envelope Status component to an Opportunity layout and saving the change.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Add-the-DocuSign-Envelope-Status-component.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxODAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.TBTgekkcjDF26jnRPsl6kET8-6ft3j8tarTuVkVKtlI&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays a cursor dragging the Docusign Envelope Status component to an Opportunity layout and saving the change.")
3. Activate the change to your layout:
   1. Select Activation.
   2. Select a tab to set your default options: Org Default, App Default, or App, Record Type, and Profile.
   3. Select Assign and choose options from the dialog box.
   4. Select Close.
4. Select Save.
5. Select the back arrow ![A screenshot displays the Salesforce Edit Page Back arrow icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-Back-arrow-on-the-Salesforce-Edit-Page-settings-bar.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxODAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.TBTgekkcjDF26jnRPsl6kET8-6ft3j8tarTuVkVKtlI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Salesforce Edit Page Back arrow icon.") to return to the record.

You have completed the process. You have added the Docusign Envelope Status component to the layout. Users with Docusign access can view and act on Docusign envelopes.

title: Set the Default Currency Formatting of Gen Data Fields
heading_path: Set the Default Currency Formatting of Gen Data Fields
text:
Learn how to set the default formatting of currency data fields.

You can set the default formatting of currency data fields. You can decide how Gen formats the
currency of generated agreements by setting defaults for these data fields.

Use this procedure to set the default currency formatting for a data field:

1. Select Options from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1MDcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.vg4I-jsYP404VKinws0KtNGqPmwP3F6bpBEvpVSMJsU&_LANG=enus) menu next to a
   currency field:

   ![A screenshot of the dropdown menu for a Gen data field displays the Options and Remove options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-dropdown-menu-for-a-Gen-data-field-displays-the-Options-and-Remove-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1MDcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.vg4I-jsYP404VKinws0KtNGqPmwP3F6bpBEvpVSMJsU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the dropdown menu for a Gen data field displays the Options and Remove options.")

   The Add Fields Options dialog box opens.
2. Currency Format: Select a currency format from the
   dropdown menu:

   Important: Set the Currency Format to
   Use Template Format to use template-level [Gen date and currency
   formatting](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=kqf1686095608923.html&_LANG=enus).

   - Use Template Format: Use the Gen template
     Date and Currency Format settings
   - Display as symbol: Example:
     $123,456.78
   - Display as symbol (no decimals): Example:
     $123,457
   - Display as ISO code: Example: USD
     123,456.78
   - Display as ISO code (no decimals): Example:
     USD 123,457
   - Display without symbol and ISO code: Example:
     123,456.78
3. (Optional): [Add
   matches or does not match
   logic to the field](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=pwd1586134341196.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use Salesforce record fields to trigger conditional logic that displays or hides content in your document.").
4. Select Save.
5. Select Save and Close.

You have completed the process. You have set the default
currency formatting of your Gen data field. Generated documents display the currency
formatting that you chose.

title: Step 7: Create a Custom Button or Finish the Template
heading_path: Step 7: Create a Custom Button or Finish the Template
text:
This step is optional. You can choose to create a custom button instead of generating
invoices automatically.

Use this procedure if you want to create a custom
button instead of generating invoices automatically. If you add a custom button,
Docusign generates invoices when a user selects the button.

Tip:
[The beginning of this video
explains this step](https://bcove.video/3mHgtcg).

Important: If
you have chosen to automate invoice generation, skip these steps and select
Save and Finish.

1. Button Label: Enter a descriptive name to let users know
   the purpose.
2. Page Layouts: Select the check box for each layout you
   want to update.
3. Select Update Layouts.
4. (Optional) Post Generation Actions: Configure e-signature
   options for this template:

   - Enable the Send for Signature button, so generators can send
     generated documents out for signature. This option shows
     or hides the Send for Signature button after
     users generate documents.
   - Launch automatically after generation. This
     option opens the standard Send with Docusign
     experience after a user generates a document. Users do not need to
     select the Send for Signature button after generating.
   - Select an Envelope Template to streamline sending for
     signature: Enter the name of an envelope template. This
     option opens the envelope template sending experience after document
     generation.

     Note: You can link a send
     now envelope template to enable a [one-click document
     generation and sending experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sjm1686076461925.html&_LANG=enus).
5. Select Save and Finish.

You have completed the entire process. You have built a
Docusign Gen for Billing template. Invoices generate automatically or when users select
the custom button on an invoice record.

title: Access the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Access the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn how to open the Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting tool in your Salesforce
organization.

title: Access the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Access the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
Before you begin
text:
You need Salesforce administrator and Docusign administrator
permissions.

Administrators can use the Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting tool to fix issues with Docusign apps. The TroubleShooting tool is available in Docusign Apps Launcher version 2.5 and higher. Salesforce
administrators with Docusign administrator permissions can use it.

Use this procedure to open the Docusign Apps Launcher
TroubleShooting tool:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzODksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.0kbYva7CX1vMfL32P2Xy-6MTzmi-7lQfKGEchGIRZhE&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting in the Label
