   column.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/oas1598330401772.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzODksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.0kbYva7CX1vMfL32P2Xy-6MTzmi-7lQfKGEchGIRZhE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.

You have completed the process. You have opened the Docusign Apps
Launcher TroubleShooting tool. You can use this tool to execute account-wide changes.

title: Create a Gen Template With the Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor
heading_path: Create a Gen Template With the Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor
text:
Learn to create a Gen template with the Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor.

You can create Gen templates using the Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor instead of Microsoft
Word. Docusign uses Agreement Document Format (ADF) for generated documents. ADF documents
automatically re-size for phones, tablets, and computers.

The following are the benefits of using the Agreement Cloud Editor to build Gen templates:

- Build and edit templates in the browser instead of using Microsoft Word
- Stay within Salesforce without logging into Docusign
- Import and edit existing Word documents
- Drag Salesforce and Docusign fields to your template without pasting
  XML code
- Add conditional logic and tables to your template

Use this procedure to create a Gen template with the Agreement Cloud Editor:

1. Open the Create a Template Configuration page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Go to
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYzNTcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.FNcw9LepI-mmTs5uR2GPehbfJAIBjmCGHnf9fOM0LRw&_LANG=enus) > View All >  Docusign Apps Launcher.
   2. Select Document Generation in the left column.
   3. Select Create a Template Configuration.

   The Create New Template dialog box opens.
2. Choose a name, data source, and editor for your template:
   1. Add Template Name: Enter a name for your template.
   2. Select Data Source: Select a Salesforce object to import
      data from.
   3. Select Template Editor: Select Docusign Agreement
      Cloud Editor.
   4. Select Create.

   The Create page opens.
3. Enter text or select Upload to create your template
   document.
4. Add Salesforce fields to your template document:
   1. Select the Salesforce Fields tab ![A screenshot displays an icon with two interlocking blue rectangles.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genacesalesforcefieldsicon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYzNTcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.FNcw9LepI-mmTs5uR2GPehbfJAIBjmCGHnf9fOM0LRw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays an icon with two interlocking blue rectangles.") in the left column.
   2. Drag a Salesforce field to the document or place the cursor in the text and select
      a field.![A screen capture displays a cursor dragging Salesforce fields to a document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/ACE-SFDC.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYzNTcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.FNcw9LepI-mmTs5uR2GPehbfJAIBjmCGHnf9fOM0LRw&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays a cursor dragging Salesforce fields to a document.")

   Note: The menu displays fields from your chosen data source and
   related objects. You can select up to four levels of child objects and two levels of
   Related Lists.
5. Add Docusign fields to your template document:
   1. Select the Docusign Fields tab ![A screenshot displays a blue rectangle icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210303-at-124311-AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYzNTcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.FNcw9LepI-mmTs5uR2GPehbfJAIBjmCGHnf9fOM0LRw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a blue rectangle icon.") in the left column.
   2. Drag a Docusign field to the document or place the cursor in the text and select a
      field.
   3. Select Next.

title: Create a Gen Template With the Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor
heading_path: Create a Gen Template With the Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor
text:
&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a blue rectangle icon.") in the left column.
   2. Drag a Docusign field to the document or place the cursor in the text and select a
      field.
   3. Select Next.

   The Deploy page opens.
6. Set File Naming Rules: Choose the naming convention used when
   users generate files from this template:
   1. File Name: Enter the beginning text for all generated file
      names. If you enter Fontara, the file name of each generated
      document begins with the word Fontara.
   2. File Suffix: [Choose the
      end text for all generated file names](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=nxb1586134245032.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the file name logic in the Gen template Upload Documents step."):

      - Main Data Source Name: Add your customer's name to the
        generated file name
      - Date: Add the document generation date to the generated
        file name
      - Main Data Source Name and Date: Add your customer's name
        and the generation date
7. Add Custom Button: Add a new custom button to your Salesforce
   layout:
   1. Button Label: Enter a descriptive name to demonstrate the
      purpose, like Generate NDA.
   2. Show button in: Select the check box for each layout that
      you want to update.
   3. Select Publish.

   A message displays: Template (name) buttons
   updated.

You have completed the process. You created an
Agreement Cloud Editor Gen template and added a custom button to a Salesforce layout. Approved
Gen users can generate contracts from Salesforce records.

title: Use the Docusign Status Tab to View the Status of an Envelope
heading_path: Use the Docusign Status Tab to View the Status of an Envelope
text:
Learn how to use the Docusign Status tab to view the status of your Docusign
envelopes.

Docusign envelopes are bundles of information sent to recipients for
electronic signature. Envelopes include documents, recipients, and Docusign and Salesforce fields.

The Docusign Status tab lets you view the status of your Docusign envelopes. Envelope status entries include these
details:

- Envelope #
- Docusign envelope ID
- Sender Name
- Sender Email
- Email Subject
- Status
- Owner
- Sent
- Expires
- Completed
- Declined or Voided Reason
- Time to Complete
- Created By
- Last Modified By
- Related Objects

Use this procedure to view the status details of a Docusign envelope in your
Salesforce organization:

1. Select the Docusign Status tab.
2. Select an envelope from the Envelope # column.

   The Docusign Envelope details page opens for that
   item.

You have completed the task. You viewed the status details of an
envelope sent from a Salesforce record.

title: Step 6: (Optional) Automatically Generate and Email Invoices
heading_path: Step 6: (Optional) Automatically Generate and Email Invoices
text:
This step is optional. You can choose to generate and email invoices automatically, instead of creating a custom button that your users select to generate invoices. This step is optional. Use this procedure to generate and email invoices automatically. If you choose to automate this task, invoices automatically generate when an invoice record stage changes to Posted. Invoices are saved in the Notes & Attachments section of the record. If you do not want to automate invoices, select the Next button on this page to create a custom button. Note: - You can only set up one automated invoice template at a time. If you try to set up another, a Skip button displays. Select it to move to step seven. - You can generate a maximum of 1,000 invoices per hour. Tip: [This step is explained in full at the beginning of this video](https://bcove.video/3uKM2F0). 1. Select the Set up automatic invoice PDF generation and save to parent invoice record check box. 2. (Optional) Send invoices by email: Select this check box to email the invoice to recipients. The Add Recipients section opens. Note: The Bill to Contact (Order) recipient is added by default. You can add up to four more recipients. 3. (Optional) Choose any steps from the Add Recipients section: - Add Recipient: Select this button and choose options from the dialog box that opens: - Record Fields: Dynamically add recipients from a record’s Bill To Contact or Created By field. - Name and Email: Add any recipient by entering a name and email address - Set as Bcc: Select the check box to hide this recipient from others - Add the recipient to the template: - Add: Save the recipient and close the dialog box - Add & New: Save the recipient and add more ![Add Recipient dialog box](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Add-Recipient-modal.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYzODYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.3DTH9t7IWe4T7BRARTx_FFG_ShE4csmc___f_8w1OXc&_LANG=enus "Add Recipient dialog box") - Message to All Recipients: - Email Subject: Edit text in the subject field. - Email Message: Edit text in the message field. Use the HTML tag
to include line breaks in your message. 4. Select Next. The Custom Button page opens. You have completed step six. Move to [Step 7: Create a Custom Button or Finish the Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=aup1614501330150.html&_LANG=enus "This step is optional. You can choose to create a custom button instead of generating invoices automatically.").

title: I Cannot Connect Docusign and Salesforce When the SpringCM File It App Is Installed
heading_path: I Cannot Connect Docusign and Salesforce When the SpringCM File It App Is Installed
text:
Learn to fix an organization that contains Docusign Apps Launcher and
the legacy SpringCM File It app.

I tried to connect Docusign and Salesforce. The connection failed.

My organization contains both Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce and
the legacy SpringCM File It app. I want to use both Docusign products in
the same organization.

title: I Cannot Connect Docusign and Salesforce When the SpringCM File It App Is Installed
heading_path: I Cannot Connect Docusign and Salesforce When the SpringCM File It App Is Installed
Resolution: Uninstall the Legacy SpringCM App or Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Cause: You cannot connect Salesforce to Docusign
when your organization contains Docusign Apps Launcher and the legacy
SpringCM File It app. Each Docusign product requires its own Salesforce
organization.

1. Choose one method for your existing Salesforce org:

   - Uninstall the legacy SpringCM File It app and install it in another Salesforce
     organization.
   - Uninstall Docusign Apps Launcher and install it in another
     Salesforce organization.
2. Follow the steps to [connect Salesforce and Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.").

   You have completed the process. You have created dedicated organizations for
   each Docusign product.

title: Gen Troubleshooting Guide
heading_path: Gen Troubleshooting Guide
text:
Learn about the topics that you can use to fix issues with your Gen for Salesforce
application.

This guide helps administrators troubleshoot issues with the Docusign Gen
for Salesforce application.

Gen is part of the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package of
apps that includes eSignature for Salesforce and CLM for Salesforce.

title: Link a Gen Template to an Envelope Template to Automate Contract Generation and Signing
heading_path: Link a Gen Template to an Envelope Template to Automate Contract Generation and Signing
text:
Learn how to link a Docusign Gen template to a Docusign envelope template to simplify
your contract process.

title: Link a Gen Template to an Envelope Template to Automate Contract Generation and Signing
heading_path: Link a Gen Template to an Envelope Template to Automate Contract Generation and Signing
Before you begin
text:
Install Docusign Apps Launcher version 5.3 or higher.

[Create a Docusign
envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uce1617376465289.html&_LANG=enus).

[Create a Gen template with Microsoft
Word](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=lig1586134209279.html&_LANG=enus).

Important:

- Use a Word-based Gen template. You cannot link an [Agreement Cloud
  Editor](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=bcp1586134298132.html&_LANG=enus)-based Gen template to an envelope template.
- You can only link templates built on the same Salesforce object. Linking a
  Gen template and envelope template built on Opportunity
  works. Linking an Opportunity-based Gen template with
  an Account-based envelope template does not work.
- You can only link the following types of envelope templates: Default,
  Quick Send, Place Fields, and
  Send Now.

You can link a Docusign Gen template to a Docusign
envelope template. When users generate an agreement with Gen and select
Send for Signature, your linked envelope template opens.

Linking templates simplifies your business process by automating contract generation and
signing.

Use this procedure to link a Gen template to an envelope template:

1. [Open the Create Button step as a
   Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=way1621663319299.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Envelope Templates: Enter the name of an envelope
   template that uses the same data source as this Gen template.
3. Select Save and Finish.

You have completed the process. You have linked a Gen template to
an envelope template. When users generate a document from a record, the linked envelope
template sending experience opens.

title: Add Conditional Logic to a Gen Template to Show or Hide Content
heading_path: Add Conditional Logic to a Gen Template to Show or Hide Content
text:
Learn to add conditional logic to your Word-based Gen template.

You can add conditional logic blocks to Gen template Anchor Text Tags. Conditional
logic creates "if-then" statements that add or exclude text from a generated
agreement.

You can use conditional logic to create one Word document that can generate different
content for different customers.

The following procedure demonstrates adding conditional logic to a customer
agreement:

1. Select Copy![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1ODUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.KC8VJXWQlL8Ay4KUS4EEjThc5C7qXPL-zaQy8sLMinY&_LANG=enus)
    next to a field and choose
   Options. ![A screenshot displays the description field with the copy menu open to options and remove.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-description-field-with-the-copy-menu-open-to-options-and-remove.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1ODUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.KC8VJXWQlL8Ay4KUS4EEjThc5C7qXPL-zaQy8sLMinY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the description field with the copy menu open to options and remove.")

   The Table Options dialog box opens.
2. Create match logic:
   1. Select Include other content if the conditions set for this
      field are met.
   2. matches: Keep this value.
   3. Enter New York in the text field.
   4. Select Save.![A screenshot displays the display options field with the values set to matches and New York.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Add-conditional-logic.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1ODUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.KC8VJXWQlL8Ay4KUS4EEjThc5C7qXPL-zaQy8sLMinY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the display options field with the values set to matches and New York.")

   The Billing State/Province anchor text changes to `<#
   <Conditional Select="/Opportunity/Account.BillingState" Match="New York"
   /> #> YOUR CONTENT HERE <#
   <EndConditional/> #>`
3. Select Copy next to the Billing
   State/Province anchor text.
4. Add the conditional anchor text to your Word document:
   1. Open the Gen template's Word document.
   2. Where you want the New York legal clause to display, paste
      `<# <Conditional
      Select="/Opportunity/Account.BillingState" Match="New York" />
      #>`.
   3. Replace the YOUR CONTENT HERE text with your New
      York legal clause text.
   4. Enter `<# <EndConditional/> #>` at the end
      of the New York legal clause text. ![A screenshot displays conditional logic tags surrounding legal text for New York customers.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Conditional-text.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1ODUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.KC8VJXWQlL8Ay4KUS4EEjThc5C7qXPL-zaQy8sLMinY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays conditional logic tags surrounding legal text for New York customers.")
5. [Re-upload the document to your Gen
   template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sqp1586134339354.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to update your Gen template Word documents.").
6. Select Save and Close and select
   Save on the Do you want to save
   changes? dialog box.

You have completed the process. You have added conditional logic
for specific customers. Generated agreements from New York customers display the New
York state legal clause.

title: Add Conditional Logic to a Gen Template to Show or Hide Content
heading_path: Add Conditional Logic to a Gen Template to Show or Hide Content
text:
Learn to add conditional logic to your Word-based Gen template.

You can add conditional logic blocks to Gen template Anchor Text Tags. Conditional
logic creates "if-then" statements that add or exclude text from a generated
agreement.

You can use conditional logic to create one Word document that can generate different
content for different customers.

The following procedure demonstrates adding conditional logic to a customer
agreement:

1. Select Copy![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzMDcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.pmKTHUexrvkvpbL0KYx_dJHQwHBT6WSa0vCn9ujzg4Y&_LANG=enus)

   Options. ![A screenshot displays the description field with the copy menu open to options and remove.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-description-field-with-the-copy-menu-open-to-options-and-remove.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzMDcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.pmKTHUexrvkvpbL0KYx_dJHQwHBT6WSa0vCn9ujzg4Y&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the description field with the copy menu open to options and remove.")

   The Table Options dialog box opens.
2. Create match logic:

   The Billing State/Province anchor text changes to `<#

   <EndConditional/> #>`
3. Select Copy next to the Billing
   State/Province anchor text.
4. Add the conditional anchor text to your Word document:

   2. Where you want the New York legal clause to display, paste

title: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
heading_path: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
text: Learn to connect your IL4 or MIL Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.

title: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
heading_path: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
Before you begin
text:
[Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.")

To use Docusign apps in your Salesforce organization, you must first connect
Docusign and Salesforce. You create this connection by
entering Salesforce and Docusign administrator credentials on the
Docusign Setup page. The Docusign
administrator used to connect accounts is called the integration user.

Note:

- Docusign recommends that you create a generic integration
  user. For more information, see this topic: [Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.").
- Docusign does not support connecting multiple Salesforce
  organizations to a single Docusign account.

Use this procedure to connect your IL4 or MIL Salesforce organization to a Docusign production account:

1. Update Session Settings as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxNTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9._6UQqoOmdoySt-FH88o-YKuIRAP2wmX-H2xhFKK7ZGs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside.") and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select `Session Settings` in the Quick
      Find field.
   3. Uncheck Lock sessions to the IP address from which they
      originated.
   4. Uncheck Lock sessions to the domain in which they were first
      used.![A screenshot displays two deselected options for Lock sessions.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-deselected-options-for-Lock-sessions-to-the-IP-address-and-Lock-sessions-to-the-domain.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxNTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9._6UQqoOmdoySt-FH88o-YKuIRAP2wmX-H2xhFKK7ZGs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays two deselected options for Lock sessions.")
   5. Select Save.
2. Complete the following from the Authorize page:
   1. Select Allow Access and choose
      Allow on the dialog box that displays.
   2. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that displays
      and select Log In.

title: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
heading_path: Connect an IL4 or MIL Salesforce Organization to Docusign
Before you begin
text:
horize page:
   1. Select Allow Access and choose
      Allow on the dialog box that displays.
   2. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that displays
      and select Log In.

   The Log in to Docusign page displays.
3. Complete the following steps on the Log in to Docusign
   page:
   1. Select Log in to Docusign. The Docusign Login dialog box displays.
   2. Select Advanced Options.
   3. Other Environment: Enter
      `USFED` in the field and select Log
      in.![A screenshot displays the Connect to Docusign page with Advanced Options and Other Environment set to USFED.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Other-Environment-field-with-USFED-entered-on-the-Connect-Docusign-to-Salesforce-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxNTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9._6UQqoOmdoySt-FH88o-YKuIRAP2wmX-H2xhFKK7ZGs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Connect to Docusign page with Advanced Options and Other Environment set to USFED.")
   4. Enter Docusign administrator credentials in the
      dialog box that displays and select Log in.
   5. Select Use Custom Domain in the Salesforce
      dialog box that displays.
   6. Enter your Salesforce domain in the Custom
      Domain field and select
      Continue.![A screenshot displays the Use Custom Domain field filled with an example domain.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Use-Custom-Domain-field-filled-with-an-example-domain.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxNTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9._6UQqoOmdoySt-FH88o-YKuIRAP2wmX-H2xhFKK7ZGs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Use Custom Domain field filled with an example domain.")
   7. Re-enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that
      displays and select Log In.

   The Select an account dialog box displays.
4. Select Account: Choose a Docusign
   account and select Next.

   The Docusign Setup page displays.
5. Edit the Salesforce Connect URL in your Docusign
   account:
   1. Log on to your Docusign account as an administrator
      and select Settings.
   2. Select Connect in the left column.
   3. Docusign Apps Launcher: Select
      Actions and choose
      Edit.
   4. ULR to Publish: Change
      act.mil in the URL to
      fed-act-event.

      - [Correct example](https://act-event.docusign.mil/listeners/connect-salesforce)
      - [Incorrect example](https://fed-act-event.docusign.com/listeners/connect-salesforce)
   5. Select SAVE CONFIGURATION.

You have completed the process. You have connected your
IL4 or MIL Salesforce organization to your Docusign account. You can
set up your Docusign Apps Launcher applications.

title: Delete a Gen Button
heading_path: Delete a Gen Button
text:
Learn to remove a Gen custom button from your Salesforce page layouts.

Use this procedure to remove a Gen custom button from
your Salesforce layouts:

1. Access the Buttons, Links, and Actions page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0NTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.4N8rfJSSF4hk73r6zO5F051d2WqhXe-sbh-3DMlIOV4&_LANG=enus) icon and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Object Manager in the Quick Find
      field.
   3. Select an object (Account, Opportunity, and so on) in the left navigation
      bar.
   4. Select Buttons, Links, and Actions.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0NTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.4N8rfJSSF4hk73r6zO5F051d2WqhXe-sbh-3DMlIOV4&_LANG=enus) next to a custom button and
   choose Delete from the dropdown menu.

   ![A screenshot displays the Buttons, Links, and Actions page with two custom button links.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/gendeletecustombutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0NTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.4N8rfJSSF4hk73r6zO5F051d2WqhXe-sbh-3DMlIOV4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Buttons, Links, and Actions page with two custom button links.")

   A dialog box displays: Are you sure?.
3. Select Delete. The page refreshes with the button no longer
   displayed.

   Note: Salesforce may display an error:
   Your request to delete this custom button or link can not be completed
   because this button or link is being used by another feature. Remove the
   button from all Salesforce layouts.

You have completed the process. You have removed the
button from Salesforce layouts.

title: Use the Docusign Setup Page to Administer Your Docusign Gen Account
heading_path: Use the Docusign Setup Page to Administer Your Docusign Gen Account
text:
Learn how to use the Docusign Setup page to manage Gen and eSignature
users, components, templates, and more.

Docusign Setup is the main landing page for managing Gen and
eSignature for Salesforce users, components, templates, and more.

You access this page by [opening the Docusign Apps Launcher package in your Salesforce
organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to open the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package in your organization.").

![A screenshot displays Docusign Setup page options like eSignature, document generation, User Management, and Components.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-Docusign-Setup-page-options-like-eSignature-Document-Generation-User-Management-and-Components.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyMDEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.42zf39IyiztfoViPPFO9-gJIWhDMs2XhryUd-k2d8wM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Docusign Setup page options like eSignature, document generation, User Management, and Components.")

The Docusign Setup page displays the following resources:

- Home: Manage and configure the Gen and eSignature for
  Salesforce apps.
- [eSignature](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xsw1586134361444.html&_LANG=enus): Configure your eSignature for Salesforce
  templates, buttons, and settings.
- [Document Generation](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zgw1621457735347.html&_LANG=enus): Create, edit, and delete Docusign Gen templates from this page.
- [User Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=zbp1629859390541.html&_LANG=enus): Add and manage Docusign Gen and eSignature users.
- [Components](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=xkm1586134206424.html&_LANG=enus): Customize layouts and experiences by adding
  custom components.
- Help: Learn how to get the most from Docusign
  Apps Launcher:
  - [Support Center](https://support.docusign.com/s/?language=en_US): Customers learn to use and
    troubleshoot Docusign products.
  - [Community](https://support.docusign.com/forum): Docusign customers
    ask and answer questions.
  - [Docusign University](https://dsucustomers.docusign.com/page/all-courses): Docusign customers build expertise.

title: Learn About Adding Child Objects to a Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Adding Child Objects to a Gen Template
text:
Learn about adding up to four levels of child objects and two levels of related lists.

You can add child objects to your Gen template. Child objects are objects related to a parent
object. For example, opportunities are child objects of accounts.

You can add child objects with up to four levels of relation to the parent, and two levels of
related lists. The following are examples of possible combinations:

Table 1. Child Relation Combinations

| Object Relation | Example |
| --- | --- |
| Root | Opportunity |
| 1 : 1 | Opportunity >  Account |
| 1 : 1 : 1 | Opportunity >  Account >  Parent Account |
| 1 : 1 : 1 : 1 | Opportunity  >  Account >  Parent Account >  Owner |
| 1 : Many | Opportunity >  Opportunity Contact Roles |
| 1 : Many : 1 | Opportunity >  Opportunity Contact Roles >  Contact |
| 1 : Many : 1 : 1 | Opportunity >  Opportunity Contact Roles >  Contact >  Reports To |
| 1 : Many : Many | Account  >  Contacts >  Cases Note: This example starts from the Account object. |
| 1 : Many : Many : 1 | Account  >  Contacts >  Cases >  Owner Note: This example starts from the Account object. |

The following image displays a sample Gen template:![A screenshot displays a Gen template with child relation fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-a-Gen-template-with-child-relation-fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1MjEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.S72cDlG3OWaoynQTrJiZ5nb3INS9ebD_T4Z314IC2kk&_LANG=enus)

title: "Failed to Generate Lightning Component: Cannot Create a New Component with the Namespace: [Custom Object Name]" Displays When I Try to Add a Gen Button
heading_path: "Failed to Generate Lightning Component: Cannot Create a New Component with the Namespace: [Custom Object Name]" Displays When I Try to Add a Gen Button
text:
Learn to add a Gen button to a custom object layout when the Gen Custom Button page
fails.

I used a custom object as my Data Source when building a Gen template. When I got to the
Create Button step, an error displayed:

"Failed to generate lightning component: Cannot create a new component with the
namespace: [Custom Object name]. Only components in the same namespace as the organization
can be created through the API."

I cannot add the Gen button to a layout.

title: "Failed to Generate Lightning Component: Cannot Create a New Component with the Namespace: [Custom Object Name]" Displays When I Try to Add a Gen Button
heading_path: "Failed to Generate Lightning Component: Cannot Create a New Component with the Namespace: [Custom Object Name]" Displays When I Try to Add a Gen Button
Resolution 1: Add a Custom Button to a Layout
text:
Cause: Docusign cannot add a button to a layout
when administrators choose a custom Salesforce object as the Main Data Source. An error
displays in the Create Button step. In many cases, Docusign creates the
button, despite this error. The administrator must use Salesforce controls to add the
button to a layout.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Open the Object Manager page as an administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.OB7G3PCa3voFXutqei0ae9Beq5h73jbbb28VzMQrO20&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Object Manager in the Quick Find field.

   The Object Manager page displays.
2. Select a Salesforce object and choose Page Layouts on the
   page that opens.
3. Add the Gen button to a layout:
   1. Page Layout Name: Select a page layout.
   2. Select Buttons.
   3. Drag the Gen button to the Custom Buttons section.
   4. Select Save.

      ![A screenshot displays a button being dragged into the Opportunity Detail section of a page layout.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/gendetailpageaddbutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.OB7G3PCa3voFXutqei0ae9Beq5h73jbbb28VzMQrO20&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a button being dragged into the Opportunity Detail section of a page layout.")

   You have completed the process. You have added the Gen button to a
   layout.

title: "Failed to Generate Lightning Component: Cannot Create a New Component with the Namespace: [Custom Object Name]" Displays When I Try to Add a Gen Button
heading_path: "Failed to Generate Lightning Component: Cannot Create a New Component with the Namespace: [Custom Object Name]" Displays When I Try to Add a Gen Button
Resolution 2: Add a Detail Page Button to a Layout
text:
Cause: Docusign cannot add a button to a layout
when administrators choose a custom Salesforce object as the Main Data Source. An error
displays in the Create Button step. In many cases, Docusign creates the
button, despite this error. The administrator must use Salesforce controls to add the
button to a layout.

You can use code to create a Detail Page Button instead.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Open the Buttons, Links and Actions page as an administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.OB7G3PCa3voFXutqei0ae9Beq5h73jbbb28VzMQrO20&_LANG=enus) and choose Setup.
   3. Enter and select Object Manager in the Quick Find field.
   4. Select a Salesforce object.
   5. Select Buttons, Links and Actions.

   The Buttons, Links and Actions page displays.
2. Create a new button:
   1. Select New Button or Link .
   2. Label: Enter a name for your Gen button.
   3. Name: Keep the default.
   4. Display Type: Select Detail Page
      Button.
   5. Behavior: Keep or edit the default.
   6. Content Source: Keep URL.
   7. Paste the following content in the field:
      `{!URLFOR('/apex/dfsle__gendocumentgenerator', null, [sId =
      Opportunity.Id, templateId = 'a0J6g000000nMjzEAE', recordId = Opportunity.Id,
      recordName = Opportunity.Name, title = 'Generate MSA'])}`

      Note: Replace the object name with your object, the
      templateID with your Gen template Id, and enter a title.
   8. Select Save.

      ![A screenshot displays the custom button or link page with custom Gen template text entered.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/gendetailpagebutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.OB7G3PCa3voFXutqei0ae9Beq5h73jbbb28VzMQrO20&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the custom button or link page with custom Gen template text entered.")
3. A dialog box displays: The new custom button will not be displayed to users until
   you add it to the page layout. Select OK.
4. Select Page Layouts on the page that opens.
5. Add the Gen button to a layout:
   1. Page Layout Name: Select a page layout.
   2. Select Buttons.
   3. Drag the Gen button to the Custom Buttons section.
   4. Select Save.

      ![A screenshot displays a button being dragged into the Opportunity Detail section of a page layout.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/gendetailpageaddbutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.OB7G3PCa3voFXutqei0ae9Beq5h73jbbb28VzMQrO20&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a button being dragged into the Opportunity Detail section of a page layout.")

   You have completed the process. You have added the Gen Detail Page Button to
   the layout.

title: Step 3 (Optional): Remove Apps Launcher Custom Buttons from Your Layouts
heading_path: Step 3 (Optional): Remove Apps Launcher Custom Buttons from Your Layouts
text:
Learn to remove Docusign Apps Launcher custom buttons from your
Salesforce layouts.

You can uninstall Apps Launcher without removing your Docusign custom
buttons. But consider removing these buttons to maintain a clean layout and avoid
confusing your users.

Use this procedure to remove all Apps Launcher custom buttons from your Salesforce
layouts:

1. Open the Buttons, Links, and Actions page as an
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1MjksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.E-PggmRD-RRjpgLsfJ-6O0CrWAeYzHK9fb9uIwqyERU&_LANG=enus) and select
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Object Manager in the Quick
      Find field.
   3. Select an object (Account, Opportunity, and so on.)
   4. Select Buttons, Links, and Actions.

      The Buttons, Links, and Actions page displays.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1MjksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.E-PggmRD-RRjpgLsfJ-6O0CrWAeYzHK9fb9uIwqyERU&_LANG=enus) next to a Docusign button and choose
   Delete from the dropdown menu.![A screenshot displays the arrow menu with the delete button highlighted for a custom button.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/gendeletecustombutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1MjksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.E-PggmRD-RRjpgLsfJ-6O0CrWAeYzHK9fb9uIwqyERU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the arrow menu with the delete button highlighted for a custom button.")

   A dialog box displays: Are you sure?
3. Select Delete.

   The page refreshes with the button no longer displayed.

   Note: You may see:
   Your request to delete this custom button or link can not be
   completed because this button or link is being used by another
   feature. Open the object's page layout and remove the button
   there.
4. Repeat this entire procedure for each layout that contains a Docusign Apps Launcher button.

You have completed step three of the process. You have
removed all Docusign Apps Launcher custom buttons from your Salesforce layouts. Move to
[Step 4: Uninstall Apps Launcher from Your organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ump1623741000284.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package from your Salesforce organization.").

title: Remove and Close Docusign Users
heading_path: Remove and Close Docusign Users
text:
Learn to remove access and close Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature
users.

You can use the Remove and Close command to revoke a Salesforce user's
access to Docusign.

- Remove and Close removes a Salesforce user's access to
  Docusign apps and the connected Docusign account. This command does not delete
  Salesforce users.
- You can not remove and close your user while logged on.
- You can not remove and close the only eSignature administrator user.

Use this procedure to remove Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature access and close Docusign users in the connected account:

1. Select the check box next to each user you want to remove and close.
2. Select Remove and Close from the Add
   Users
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/CLM-Files-Lightning-action-arrow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyNTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.3o_TTssvm3UY2XCohOJclNloaQrLKF4D97Xrcc7ORdc&_LANG=enus) drop-down menu.
3. Select Remove.

You have completed the process. You removed a Salesforce user's
access to the Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce apps. You also
closed (removed) the user from the connected Docusign account. The user remains in your
Salesforce organization.

title: Download Agreement Cloud Editor Documents as Word Files
heading_path: Download Agreement Cloud Editor Documents as Word Files
text:
Learn how to download Agreement Document Format Gen contracts as Microsoft Word
documents.

[Agreement Cloud
Editor](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=bcp1586134298132.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create a Gen template with the Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor.") templates create documents in ADF (Agreement Document Format.) ADF is
an HTML-based document type that you can create and edit on a Salesforce page.

You
cannot send ADF Gen documents for internal approval or external review from a
browser. To send ADF documents for review or approval, you can first download them
as Microsoft Word documents.

Use this procedure to download ADF Gen contracts
as Word documents:

1. Select an Agreement Cloud Editor Gen button on a Salesforce record.

   The generated document displays in the browser.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzMzMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.p3t6nI4TFCk5lsIjWxYjiVPuHQGC3GfANu7gku4VQF0&_LANG=enus) next to the Send for
   Signature button.
3. Select Download as Word File.![A screenshot displays the Download as Word File option under the Send for Signature menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/gendownloadasword.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzMzMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.p3t6nI4TFCk5lsIjWxYjiVPuHQGC3GfANu7gku4VQF0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Download as Word File option under the Send for Signature menu.")

   Docusign saves a copy of the document to your device
   in Microsoft Word format.

You have completed the process. You have downloaded a Microsoft
Word copy of the ADF contract. You can send this for internal approval, external review,
or use it to compare changes with a previous version.

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
text:
Learn how to use table column XML tags in Word-based Gen templates to import Salesforce
data into a generated agreement.

You can use table column XML tags in Word-based Gen templates to import Salesforce data into a
generated agreement.

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Table Column tag
text:
The Table Column tag is represented by the `<TableColumn>` XML
tag. You use a Table Column tag in a table that you add to your Word-based Gen
template. When users generate agreements, Table Column tags dynamically add a column
for each unique piece of record data.

Table Column tags are useful when creating tables for products whose prices change.
The `<TableColumn>` tag adds a new column each time prices
change.

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Table Column tag example
text:
You add a Table Column tag to your Gen template. A user generates an agreement from a
Salesforce record that has five product categories. Docusign adds
five table columns; one for each product category.

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Using Table Column tags in Gen templates
text:
You create custom tables in Word by copying tags from a Gen template and pasting them
after a table column header. Use the following guidelines when creating Table Column
tags:

- Add Table Column tags to the first row of a table.
- The Select attribute adds a new column in the table for each match that it
  finds.
- The Select attribute adds columns in order of the values that it finds on a
  record.
- You can place any merge tag in a table column.
- You can add multiple Table Column tags in a table.
- You can nest Table Column tags. Copy a table that contains a Table Column tag
  and paste it inside the cell of another table.
- A single table cannot contain both Table Row and Table Column tags.
- You can use a maximum of 63 columns in a Microsoft Word table.

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Table Column attributes
text:
You can use the following attributes in <TableColumn> tags:

| Attribute | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Select | XPath used to select data from an XML document |
| Tag | Can be any String value |
| TagRef | Used with a Tag attribute placed on a Repeat, TableRow, or TableColumn tag |
| TrackName | Specific to Term Sync |

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Suppress Table Column tag
text:
The Suppress Table Column tag is represented by the
`<SuppressTableColumn>` XML tag. You use a Suppress Table
Column tag in a table that you add to your Word-based Gen template. When users
generate agreements, Suppress Table Column tags dynamically hide columns based on
record data. Suppress Table Column tags are useful when a record has no data in a
field. You can also use Suppress Table Column tags to hide data from certain
recipients.

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Suppress Table Column example
text:
You add a Suppress Table Column tag to your Gen template. A user generates an
agreement from a Salesforce record containing a Discount
column. The Salesforce record does not contain discounted products. Docusign does not add the Discount column to the generated
agreement.

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Using Suppress Table Column tags in Gen templates
text:
You create custom tables in Word by copying tags from a Gen template and pasting them
after a table column header. Use the following guidelines when creating Suppress
Table Column tags:

- <SuppressTableColumn> removes an entire column from a table.
- <SuppressTableColumn> attributes are identical to Conditional tag
  attributes.
- Docusign removes the entire table if the column to be
  suppressed is the only one on the record.

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Suppress Table Column attributes
text:
You can use the following attributes in <SuppressTableColumn> tags:

| Attribute | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Select | XPath used to select data from an XML document |
| Match | Table column is suppressed (removed) if the data returned matches this value |
| NotMatch | Table column is suppressed (removed) if the data returned does not match this value |
| Test | Conditional option based on Boolean logic |
| TagRef | Used with a Tag attribute placed on a Repeat, Table Row, or Table Column tag |

title: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Use Table Column XML Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template to Import Salesforce Data
Use Suppress Table Column with Table Row Select
text:
You can use Suppress Table Column tags with Table Row Select tags to build a conditional
table. Using these tags together is useful if you want to suppress a column when a
field is null or empty. You can also combine these tags to suppress a column when a
check box field is selected in a table row.

| Name | Type | Quantity | List Price | Discount | Discount % | Unit Price | Total Price |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| **<# <TableRow Select=" /Opportunity// Opportunity LineItems" /> #><# <Content Select=". /Name"/> #>** | **<# <Content Select=" ./Product2. Family" /> #>** | **<# <Content Select=" ./Quantity" /> #>** | **<# <Content Select= "./ListPrice "/> #>** | **<# < SuppressTableColumn Test=”count (//OpportunityLineItems/ Discount[text() !=’0,00%’])=0 “/>#><# <Content Select=". /Discount[text()!=’ 0,00%’]" Optional ="true"/> #>** | **Number of oppties <# <Content Select ="count (//OpportunityLineItems) "/> #> --<# <Content Select="count (//OpportunityLineItems/ Discount[text()= ’0,00%’])"/> #>--<# <Content Select="count (//OpportunityLineItems /Discount [text()!=’0,00%’])"/> #>** | **<# <Content Select=". /UnitPrice \_\_c"/> #>** | **<# <Content Select=". /TotalPrice" /> #>** |

title: I See a Blank Screen When I Try to Grant Gen Permissions
heading_path: I See a Blank Screen When I Try to Grant Gen Permissions
text:
Learn to enable Cross-Origin Resource Sharing to prevent blank screens when granting Gen
permissions.

I am a Salesforce and Docusign administrator, but I can't grant Gen
permissions to users.

When I try to grant Generator permissions, I see a white screen with no controls.

title: I See a Blank Screen When I Try to Grant Gen Permissions
heading_path: I See a Blank Screen When I Try to Grant Gen Permissions
Resolution: Enable Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS)
text:
Cause: You have not enabled [Cross-Origin Resource Sharing](https://help.salesforce.com/articleView?id=extend_code_cors.htm&type=5) in your Salesforce
organization.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Open the Session Settings page as an
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NjEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.m3LSajUcKUt0l_JYkzH53xSFs4T8_H4rH3OiYOVgGec&_LANG=enus) and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Session Settings in the
      Quick Find field.

   The Session Settings page displays.
2. Select Add Domain.
3. [Paste your Salesforce Visualforce
   domain in the Domain field](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.pages.meta/pages/pages_quick_start_external_iframe.htm).
4. Select Save.

   You have completed the process. Non-integration users can grant
   permissions to Docusign apps in your Salesforce
   organization.

title: Create a Document Rule in a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Create a Document Rule in a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn to create a document rule to enable conditional document generation in a Gen
template:

Use this procedure to set up a document rule to enable conditional document generation in a
Gen template:

1. Select the Upload Documents arrow. The arrow has no text
   until you select it.
2. Select + Add Rule.

   The Document Rule dialog box opens.
3. Create a document rule:
   1. Enter rule name: Enter a descriptive name for
      the rule.
   2. APPLY: Select an option from the dropdown
      menu:

      - All: Docusign triggers
        the rule if a record matches all conditions.
      - Any: Docusign triggers
        the rule if a record matches at least one condition.
   3. IF: Add a condition to your rule:

      1. Select an Option: Choose a Salesforce
         field to start the condition:
      2. Equals: Choose
         Equals or Does Not
         Equal from the dropdown menu.
      3. Enter conditional value: Enter a value
         for the condition. Example: You start a condition with Billing Country >  Equals and enter Brazil to finish
         the condition.
   4. (Optional)
      Duplicate Row:
      Select the copy ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Copy-Row-Button.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1ODAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.zL-WU2cAwipw8tWtSae5urd7RdI4twVlU6V0p1GunXs&_LANG=enus) button to copy a
      condition.
   5. (Optional)
      + or if: Select the
      link to add conditions.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Document-Rule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1ODAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.zL-WU2cAwipw8tWtSae5urd7RdI4twVlU6V0p1GunXs&_LANG=enus)
4. Select Save.

You have completed the process. You built a document rule that
selects the correct template based on record conditions.

title: A Red "OK" Error Displays When I Try to Access Any Docusign Component
heading_path: A Red "OK" Error Displays When I Try to Access Any Docusign Component
text:
Learn to upgrade Docusign Apps Launcher to avoid seeing an error when
accessing components.

I can't access any Docusign components on my layouts.

A red OK error displays when I select any component on a record. This
includes any of the following components:

- Docusign Files
- Docusign Envelope Status
- Docusign CLM Files
- Docusign CLM Obligations
- Docusign CLM Tasks

title: A Red "OK" Error Displays When I Try to Access Any Docusign Component
heading_path: A Red "OK" Error Displays When I Try to Access Any Docusign Component
Resolution: Upgrade to the Latest Version of Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Cause: Your organization has an older version of Docusign Apps Launcher installed. Upgrade to the latest
version.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Check your Docusign Apps Launcher version number:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0ODQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Y1mD8HTfhRPe5B519TWHBR_s3XfiftbQbCx99J3XmUU&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Installed Packages in
      the Quick Find field.
   3. Check your Version Number.
2. Choose an upgrade path:

   - If your version is higher than 6.0, [upgrade to
     the latest Apps Launcher version](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=nok1698959626724.html&_LANG=enus).
   - If your version is 6.0 or lower, [use this process to upgrade
     to the latest version](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/DocuSign-Apps-Launcher-Is-there-a-recommended-upgrade-path-for-an-older-version-of-the-package).

   You have completed the process. You have upgraded your older Docusign Apps Launcher package. You can open Docusign components on Salesforce records.

title: Edit the Docusign App Permissions of Salesforce Users
heading_path: Edit the Docusign App Permissions of Salesforce Users
text:
Learn to edit the permissions of Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce
users.

You can use the Edit Permissions
command to change the Docusign app permissions of Salesforce users.

Use this
procedure to edit the Docusign app permissions of Gen, CLM Negotiate, or eSignature for
Salesforce users:

1. Select the check box next to each user you want to edit.
2. Select Edit Permissions from the Add
   Users
   ![A screenshot displays a downward-facing triangle icon in a square.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/CLM-Files-Lightning-action-arrow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyNDgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.hjVLNuaFH1Bgh4eH10rrbok25oalYV7PUPCSVAobdk0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a downward-facing triangle icon in a square.") drop-down menu.
3. Change access from the Edit Permissions dialog box that
   opens:

   1. eSignature
      - Administrator: Administrators can create
        templates and manage all Docusign users in Salesforce.
      - User: Users can send documents for
        signature.
   2. CLM
      - Administrator: Administrators can access
        everything in CLM and manage connections with Salesforce. They
        can't manage Docusign users in Salesforce.
      - User: Users can access workflows and
        view, edit, and upload documents.
      - Viewer: Viewers can only preview and
        upload documents.
      - No Access: Users have no access to the
        CLM app
   3. Gen
      - Generator: Generators can create new
        documents and templates.
      - No Access: Users have no access to the
        Gen app.
   4. Negotiate
      - Negotiator: Negotiators can collaborate
        with signers and viewers.
      - No Access: Users have no access to the
        Negotiate app.
   5. (Optional) Update permissions for any existing
      users: Select to update a user's permissions in the
      connected Docusign account.
4. Select Apply.

You have completed the process. You edited the permissions of Gen,
CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce users.

title: Use the Docusign Gen Tab of the CLM Document Generation Page
heading_path: Use the Docusign Gen Tab of the CLM Document Generation Page
text:
Learn to use the Docusign Gen tab of the CLM Document Generation
page.

When you go to Docusign Setup >  Document Generation, you see the following tabs:

- CLM: Links and information about CLM Document Generation
  features.
- Docusign Gen: Links and information about how to create,
  edit, and delete Docusign Gen templates.

This guide demonstrates how to access and use the Docusign Gen
tab:

1. Select the Docusign Gen tab:

   ![A screenshot displays the Docusign Gen tab of the CLM Document Generation page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/CLM-DocuSign-Gen-tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgyNzksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.2Ecz0k1XCI3DRCzrZ6ODbLCN1iwGsKTFONGvU3g2uLc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Docusign Gen tab of the CLM Document Generation page.")
2. Access any of these links or buttons:

   - Learn More: Select this button to open Docusign
     Gen guide information.
   - Create a Template Configuration: Open the Docusign Gen template configuration builder.
     - [Create a Gen template with Microsoft
       Word](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=lig1586134209279.html&_LANG=enus)
     - [Create a Gen template with the Docusign Agreement Cloud
       Editor](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=bcp1586134298132.html&_LANG=enus)
     - [Create a Gen for Billing Template to Generate
       Invoices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ldc1610646513281.html&_LANG=enus)
     - [Create a Gen Template using the Salesforce
       Person Account Object](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ird1636867371186.html&_LANG=enus)
   - Quick Guide: Select this link to open Docusign
     Gen overview information.

You have completed the process. You have explored the features of
the Docusign Gen tab.

title: Use SOQL Code to Filter and Sort Gen Data
heading_path: Use SOQL Code to Filter and Sort Gen Data
text:
Learn to add Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) code to filter and sort date in Gen
templates.

Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) searches your organization’s Salesforce data for
specific information. You can [insert Salesforce
SOQL code into the TableRow fields of Gen templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=mrr1588510303522.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about inserting Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) code into Gen templates.").

Using SOQL code in tables creates custom rules for filtering and sorting data. You
can only use sorting and filtering with child relationship fields.

Use this procedure to add SOQL code to Gen templates:

1. [Edit the Gen template Add Fields
   page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=was1621662565775.html&_LANG=enus):
2. Select the Copy
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1MzUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.nh53yHPL8-9BYdjL4dVsYGeynQ-vNnG1af5uWXkPxTY&_LANG=enus) arrow
   next to a child object and choose Options from the
   dropdown menu.![A screenshot displays a Gen template field with the options and remove items visible.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-a-Gen-template-field-with-the-options-and-remove-items-visible.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1MzUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.nh53yHPL8-9BYdjL4dVsYGeynQ-vNnG1af5uWXkPxTY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Gen template field with the options and remove items visible.")

   The Table Options dialog box opens.
3. Enter the Salesforce SOQL code for any of these options:
4. Select Save.

   The Table Options dialog box closes.
5. Select Finish.

   A dialog box displays: Do you want to save
   changes?.
6. Select Save.

You have completed the process. You have inserted Salesforce code
to sort or filter child object data in your Gen table. Follow this link to [see the exception to these SOQL rules.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ndk1614892150271.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the scenario where sorting works differently than the standard SOL sorting rules.")

title: Add Docusign Buttons, Links, or Actions to a Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Add Docusign Buttons, Links, or Actions to a Salesforce Layout
text:
Learn to add Docusign buttons, links, or actions to your Salesforce
layouts.

You can add Docusign buttons, links, or actions to your Salesforce
layouts. Your users can access Docusign features with these controls. For
example, when you add a Gen button to a layout, your users can select it to generate an
agreement.

Use this procedure to add Docusign buttons, links, or actions to a
layout:

1. Access a page layout as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the gear icon ![Salesforce gear icon used to access Setup in the header.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxODUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.x7l4qMryLVRrPNxcHsw_g3LJpjBmkSUekKGQ9rJyZsQ&_LANG=enus "Salesforce gear icon used to access Setup in the header.") and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select `Object Manager` in the Quick Find
      field.
2. Select a Salesforce object from the Label column.
3. Select Page Layouts.
4. Select a layout from the Page Layout Name column.
5. Drag a button, link, or action from the menu to the layout.

   ![Salesforce page layout editor showing a custom button being dragged from the menu to the page layout canvas.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/dragtocanvas.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxODUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.x7l4qMryLVRrPNxcHsw_g3LJpjBmkSUekKGQ9rJyZsQ&_LANG=enus "Salesforce page layout editor showing a custom button being dragged from the menu to the page layout canvas.")
6. Select Save.

You have completed the process. You have added a
button, link, or action to a layout. The items you added display on every record of that
object type.

title: User Management for Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce
heading_path: User Management for Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce
text:
Learn to add Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature users and manage permissions
on the user management page.

Docusign Apps Launcher is a package of Docusign applications you can use in Salesforce. The
Apps Launcher package contains the Docusign
eSignature,
CLM, Gen, and
Negotiate applications. Each Salesforce user needing to use
these apps must have a Docusign user. Administrators create Docusign users and grant
permissions on the Docusign user management page.

The following is a list of apps and permission types administrators can assign:

Docusign Gen
:   Generator: Generators can generate contracts and send
    them for signature.

Docusign CLM
:   Administrator: Administrators can access everything in
    CLM and manage connections with Salesforce. They can't manage Docusign users in
    Salesforce.
:   User: Users can access workflows and view, edit, and
    upload documents.
:   Viewer: Viewers can only preview and upload
    documents.

Docusign eSignature
:   Administrator: Administrators create templates and manage
    all Docusign users in Salesforce.
:   User: Users can send documents for signature.

Docusign Negotiate
:   Negotiator: Negotiators can send content for review and
    signature.

You can take the following actions on the Docusign User Management page:

- [Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users
  in Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zbp1629859390541.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to grant Docusign app access to your Salesforce users.")
- [Create Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or
  eSignature Users in Your Salesforce Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=uwf1586134275195.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature users on the user management page.")
- [Check the Activation Status of a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce User](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=hak1586134277505.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to check the activation status of a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.")
- [Search for Docusign Users in the Docusign CLM
  User Management Page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=keb1628113418793.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use the search feature to find Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature users.")
- [Edit the Permissions of Gen, Negotiate, or
  eSignature for Salesforce Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=doi1628107496987.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to edit the permissions of Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce users.")
- [Remove Gen, Negotiate, and eSignature Access
  From a Salesforce User in the User Management Page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=tha1628110439784.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature access from Salesforce users.")
- [Remove Access and Close Docusign Gen,
  Negotiate, and eSignature Users in the Docusign User Management
  Page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=cmn1628112474213.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove access and close Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature users.")

You can take the following actions to help your Docusign for Salesforce users:

title: User Management for Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce
heading_path: User Management for Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce
text:
cmn1628112474213.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove access and close Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature users.")

You can take the following actions to help your Docusign for Salesforce users:

- [Resend the Activation Email for a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce User](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=liv1586134278039.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to resend the activation email to a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.")
- [Reset the Password of a Docusign CLM, Gen,

- ["You Are Not the Owner of the
  Envelope" Displays When I Try to Remind, View, Correct, or Void an
