title: Enable Translations for Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Enable Translations for Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn how to use Translation Workbench to enable choosing a default Salesforce
language.

Use this procedure to enable Translation Workbench in your organization and let users
choose their default Salesforce language.

1. Access Translation Settings as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYzMDEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.UbS5HGo78XIg_T0v0b1yBijvo6b3o4NX4E88zab79PU&_LANG=enus) icon and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Translation Settings in the Quick Find
      field.
2. Select Enable on the Translation Settings page.

   ![A screenshot of the Translation Settings page displays the Enable button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Enable-Salesforce-Translation-settings.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYzMDEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.UbS5HGo78XIg_T0v0b1yBijvo6b3o4NX4E88zab79PU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Translation Settings page displays the Enable button highlighted.")

   The Translation Workbench page opens. A message displays: The
   Translation Workbench is currently enabled for your
   organization.
3. (Optional): Select Add to add additional
   languages to the organization.

You have completed the process. Users in your
Salesforce org can switch languages and view Docusign Apps Launcher content in any of the
enabled languages.

title: The “Select an Account” Page Prevents Me From Connecting Docusign and Salesforce
heading_path: The “Select an Account” Page Prevents Me From Connecting Docusign and Salesforce
text:
Learn to fix this account connection issue.

I cannot connect Docusign to my Salesforce organization. The "Select an
Account" page returns every time. I do not see an error message.

title: The “Select an Account” Page Prevents Me From Connecting Docusign and Salesforce
heading_path: The “Select an Account” Page Prevents Me From Connecting Docusign and Salesforce
Resolution: Use Credentials That Are Only Connected to One Docusign Account
text:
Cause: Your integration user's email address is connected to
more than one Docusign account.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Access the Docusign Setup page as a Salesforce
   administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Select Log in to Docusign.
3. Enter Docusign administrator credentials.

   Important: Ensure that the email address of this
   administrator is only connected to one Docusign
   account.
4. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials.
5. Select an Account: Choose a Docusign account and
   select Next.
6. Authorize: Select Allow
   Access and enter Salesforce administrator
   credentials.

   A message displays: Successfully connected and authorized to
   Docusign.

   You have completed the process. You have connected your Docusign
   account to your Salesforce organization.

title: The “Select an Account” Page Prevents Me From Connecting Docusign and Salesforce
heading_path: The “Select an Account” Page Prevents Me From Connecting Docusign and Salesforce
text:
Learn to fix this account connection issue.

I cannot connect Docusign to my Salesforce organization. The "Select an
Account" page returns every time. I do not see an error message.

title: The “Select an Account” Page Prevents Me From Connecting Docusign and Salesforce
heading_path: The “Select an Account” Page Prevents Me From Connecting Docusign and Salesforce
Resolution: Use Credentials That Are Only Connected to One Docusign Account
text:
Cause: Your integration user's email address is connected to
more than one Docusign account.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Access the Docusign Setup page as a Salesforce
   administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Select Log in to Docusign.
3. Enter Docusign administrator credentials.

   Important: Ensure that the email address of this
   administrator is only connected to one Docusign
   account.
4. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials.
5. Select an Account: Choose a Docusign account and
   select Next.
6. Authorize: Select Allow
   Access and enter Salesforce administrator
   credentials.

   A message displays: Successfully connected and authorized to
   Docusign.

   You have completed the process. You have connected your Docusign
   account to your Salesforce organization.

title: Add a Table to a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Add a Table to a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn to create Gen template tables that add Salesforce data to agreements.

You can create a table in a Microsoft Word-based Gen template to import Salesforce data to
a generated agreement. You build tables by pasting tags from your Gen template into your
Word table.

Use this procedure to add a table to your Word-based Gen template:

1. Select a document name.

   The document preview page opens in a new tab or window.
2. Select Download and open the document in Microsoft Word.
3. Add a table to your Word document:
   1. Insert a table in Word, using at least two rows and one column.
   2. In the top row, enter a name for each column.
   3. Enter `<# <TableRow Select="/` in the first cell of the second
      row.![A screenshot displays a table row with <# <TableRow Select="/ under the description column.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/tablefirstrow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1NjMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.WfgvgKvHR8hRLexM9zmnRIm69TIWoYA4uumMGKIgXTs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a table row with <# <TableRow Select=\"/ under the description column.")
   4. On the Gen template Add Fields page, select
      Copy next to the anchor text you want in the first row of
      your table.![A screenshot displays the Gen template description field with the copy button highlighted next to the anchor text.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Gen-template-description-field-with-the-copy-button-highlighted-next-to-the-anchor-text.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1NjMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.WfgvgKvHR8hRLexM9zmnRIm69TIWoYA4uumMGKIgXTs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Gen template description field with the copy button highlighted next to the anchor text.")
   5. Paste the text directly after the Table Row text in your Word document.![A screenshot displays the Word table description field with the full line of anchor text added.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/tablerowfulltag.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1NjMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.WfgvgKvHR8hRLexM9zmnRIm69TIWoYA4uumMGKIgXTs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Word table description field with the full line of anchor text added.")
   6. Copy the remaining data fields from the Gen template and paste them into the
      appropriate table cells.![A screenshot displays four Word table rows with full lines of anchor text added.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/remainingtags.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1NjMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.WfgvgKvHR8hRLexM9zmnRIm69TIWoYA4uumMGKIgXTs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays four Word table rows with full lines of anchor text added.")
   7. Save the Word document and return to the Gen Upload
      Documents page.

You have completed the process. You have added a table
with XML tags to the Gen Word template. Additional rows are dynamically added to the table,
based on the number of related Salesforce fields.

title: Use the Docusign Logs Tab to View Docusign Errors
heading_path: Use the Docusign Logs Tab to View Docusign Errors
text:
Learn to use the Docusign Logs tab for viewing Docusign error messages in your Salesforce organization.

The Docusign Logs tab opens a page that displays all Docusign error messages. These include all
DEBUG, INFO,
WARN, and ERROR logs.

Log entries include these details:

- Message
- Severity
- Version
- Created By
- Docusign entity ID
- Salesforce source ID
- Transaction ID
- Class
- Method

Use this procedure to view the Docusign Logs tab in your Salesforce
organization:

1. Select the Docusign Logs tab.

   The Docusign Logs page opens.
2. Select a log from the Docusign Log column.

   The Docusign log details page opens for that item.

You have completed the task. You viewed the details of an error
from your Docusign account.

title: Open Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Open Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn to open the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package in
your organization.

Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce is a package you install from the
Salesforce AppExchange. The package contains the following applications:

- Docusign CLM for Salesforce
- Docusign CLM Essentials for Salesforce
- Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
- Docusign Gen for Salesforce
- Docusign Negotiate for Salesforce
- Docusign eSignature Apex Toolkit for Salesforce

When you install one of these apps in your organization, Docusign
installs all apps. Depending on the apps you purchase, you see a custom Docusign administrator dashboard when you open Apps Launcher.

Docusign Gen customers see administrator controls for
Gen and eSignature.

Docusign CLM customers see administrator controls for
CLM, Gen, and eSignature.

CLM Essentials customers see controls for CLM
Essentials, Gen, and eSignature.

Use this procedure to access Docusign Apps Launcher in your Salesforce
organization:

1. Select the App Launcher grid ![A screenshot displays a square icon made of nine colored dots](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/applaunchergrid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxODgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.oCPZUf7meisI5thrIge_IbyGSvucmiMt3ZuGukZjqGs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a square icon made of nine colored dots") and select View All.
2. Select Docusign Apps Launcher.

You have completed the process. You have opened all
default Docusign Apps Launcher tabs and the Docusign
Setup page.

title: "The User Is Not the Owner of the Lock" Displays When I Edit a Gen Template or Envelope Template
heading_path: "The User Is Not the Owner of the Lock" Displays When I Edit a Gen Template or Envelope Template
text:
Learn to fix the "User is not the Owner of the Lock" error when editing a Gen or
envelope template.

I tried to edit a Docusign Gen or envelope template. An error displayed:
The user is not the owner of the lock. The template is locked by
another user or in another application.

I cannot edit the template or save changes.

title: "The User Is Not the Owner of the Lock" Displays When I Edit a Gen Template or Envelope Template
heading_path: "The User Is Not the Owner of the Lock" Displays When I Edit a Gen Template or Envelope Template
Resolution: Re-Login to Salesforce or Empty Your Browser Cache
text:
Cause: You closed the browser tab while editing a template
without selecting Save and Close. You opened another
browser tab and tried to edit the template you closed.

1. Select the Salesforce user icon and select Log
   Out from the dropdown menu.
2. Log back in to Salesforce.
3. Try to edit your Gen template again.
4. If the error displays, empty your browser cache and re-login.

   You have completed the process. You have removed the template
   lock. You can edit or save changes to the Gen template or envelope
   template.

title: Step 3: Paste Anchor Text Fields Into Your Word Document
heading_path: Step 3: Paste Anchor Text Fields Into Your Word Document
text:
Learn to paste your Salesforce anchor text fields into a Microsoft Word document.

Use this procedure to paste your Salesforce anchor
text fields into a Microsoft Word document.

Tip:
[This video explains the step](https://players.brightcove.net/6118377982001/default_default/index.html?videoId=6248882103001).

1. Select Copy next to the first anchor text field.
2. Paste the anchor text field in the correct spot of your Word template.

   Note: In this example, the
   administrator pastes the Name field where the customer name
   should display. If a user generates a contract for Acme, Co., that

title: Learn About Using the Default Docusign for Salesforce Tabs
heading_path: Learn About Using the Default Docusign for Salesforce Tabs
text:
Learn to find and use the default Docusign Apps Launcher tabs.

A set of tabs displays when you open Docusign Apps Launcher in your Salesforce
organization. These tabs display functions commonly needed by a Docusign for Salesforce administrator.

![A screenshot displays the default Docusign tabs: Setup, Gen Templates, Envelope Templates, Logs, Envelopes, and Status.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-default-Docusign-for-Salesforce-tabs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxOTcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.nEGvzd2RnjlZxa1ky964rgqxzL4IQp2oN9gg5N9qLeM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the default Docusign tabs: Setup, Gen Templates, Envelope Templates, Logs, Envelopes, and Status.")

You can access these tabs by [opening Docusign Apps Launcher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus).

[Docusign Setup](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=cay1654320876587.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Docusign Setup is the landing page for administering your Docusign account. You can manage Docusign
    users, components, templates, and more. Depending on the apps you purchased, you
    see a custom Docusign administrator dashboard.
    Gen customers see different administrator controls than
    CLM customers.

[Docusign Gen Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=fsc1646690056619.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Create, edit, and delete Docusign Gen templates from this
    page. Users can generate agreements by selecting a Gen template button on a
    Salesforce record.

[Docusign Envelope Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=scr1646715662157.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Create, edit, and delete Docusign envelope templates from this
    page. Users can send envelopes for signature by selecting an envelope template
    button on a record.

[Docusign Logs](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=epu1646716431620.html&_LANG=enus)
:   View the DEBUG, INFO, WARN, and ERROR logs of envelopes sent from Salesforce
    records.

[Docusign Envelopes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=nbq1646756159307.html&_LANG=enus)
:   View the details of all Docusign envelopes sent from
    Salesforce records.

[Docusign Status](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=bek1646762309217.html&_LANG=enus)
:   View the status (Sent, Completed, and so on) of all Docusign
    envelopes sent from Salesforce records.

Select any tab to view the page.

title: Access the Docusign Gen Templates Tab to Build and Edit Gen Templates
heading_path: Access the Docusign Gen Templates Tab to Build and Edit Gen Templates
text:
Learn to access the Docusign Gen Templates tab in Salesforce.

Gen templates allow users to select a button on a record to instantly generate a contract. Use this procedure to access the Docusign Gen Templates tab in your Salesforce organization:

1. Select the Docusign Gen Templates tab.
2. Make any selection:

   - [New](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zgw1621457735347.html&_LANG=enus): Create a new Gen template.
   - [Change Owner](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sut1646711991124.html&_LANG=enus): Change the owner of a Gen template.
   - [Import](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=juw1586134252760.html&_LANG=enus): Import up to 50,000 records at a time.
   - [Rename](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=gww1586134337518.html&_LANG=enus): Change the name of a Gen template.
   - [Edit](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=xcs1646713533209.html&_LANG=enus): Add or edit documents, Salesforce fields, and Docusign fields in a Gen template.
   - [Delete](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=mbd1586134342525.html&_LANG=enus): Delete a Gen template.

   You have completed the task. You accessed the Docusign Gen Templates tab. From this page, you can create and edit Gen Templates.

title: Add a Generate Agreement with Docusign Action to a Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Add a Generate Agreement with Docusign Action to a Salesforce Flow
text:
Learn to add a Generate agreement with Docusign action to your
Salesforce Flow to trigger document generation.

title: Add a Generate Agreement with Docusign Action to a Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Add a Generate Agreement with Docusign Action to a Salesforce Flow
Before you begin
text:
Install Docusign Apps Launcher version 6.12 or higher.

[Create a Gen template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=lig1586134209279.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a Gen template using a Microsoft Word document.").

Important: The current API limitation is 100 generations per 10 minutes. We recommend running scheduled flows no more
than once every 10 minutes to avoid hitting rate limits. If you want to increase your
limit, [contact your Docusign representative](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/How-do-I-contact-DocuSign-Customer-Support) about options.

You can build a Generate agreement with Docusign action to add document
generation capabilities to a Salesforce Flow. You can use the Generate agreement
with Docusign action to trigger a Docusign Gen
template. The Gen template generates a document from a record.

Example: You build a Record-Triggered Flow and link it to a Docusign Gen template. You configure the Flow to trigger document
generation when a record's stage changes to Negotiation/Review.
A user changes the stage of the Burlington Textiles opportunity
to Negotiation/Review. Salesforce triggers the Docusign Gen
template linked to your Flow. Docusign generates a document using
data from the record.

Use this procedure to add a Generate agreement with Docusign action to a
Salesforce Flow:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, create or edit a Salesforce Flow.
2. Add a Generate agreement with Docusign action:
   1. Locate the point in your Flow where you want to add your action.
   2. Hover over the circle icon and select the plus +
      icon that displays.![A screen capture displays the Add Element plus icon in a Salesforce Flow.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screen-capture-displays-the-Add-Element-plus-icon-in-a-Salesforce-Flow.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNzEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.f4UMRkYzv_KPay_c3wBt8LUbOkq_pXjJICgBZcgwNvU&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the Add Element plus icon in a Salesforce Flow.")
   3. Choose Action from the Add
      Element dialog box.
   4. Add Element: Enter and select
      Generate agreement with Docusign in the search field.

title: Add a Generate Agreement with Docusign Action to a Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Add a Generate Agreement with Docusign Action to a Salesforce Flow
Before you begin
text:
Add Element plus icon in a Salesforce Flow.")
   3. Choose Action from the Add
      Element dialog box.
   4. Add Element: Enter and select
      Generate agreement with Docusign in the search field.

   The Generate agreement with Docusign dialog box opens.
3. Add a label and API name for your action:
   1. Label: Enter Generate agreement with
      Docusign.
   2. API Name: Keep or change the default name.
4. Source Id: Choose the ID of the object your Flow is
   built on. For example, if your Flow is built on Opportunity, choose: Triggering Opportunity >  Opportunity ID.
5. Template ID: Add the ID of the Gen template used in the
   Flow.
   1. Select the Docusign Gen Templates tab.
   2. Select the Gen template that you want to use in the Flow.
   3. Copy the alpha-numeric template ID at the end of the URL. Do not copy
      the slash marks.![A screenshot displays the Gen template ID highlighted in the envelope template URL.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Gen-template-ID-highlighted-in-the-envelope-template-URL-1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNzEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.f4UMRkYzv_KPay_c3wBt8LUbOkq_pXjJICgBZcgwNvU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Gen template ID highlighted in the envelope template URL.")
   4. Paste the ID (without slash marks) in the Template
      ID field.
   5. Select Save.![A screenshot displays a Generate agreement with Docusign action with Source ID and Template ID highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Generate_agreement_with_Docusign_flow_action.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNzEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.f4UMRkYzv_KPay_c3wBt8LUbOkq_pXjJICgBZcgwNvU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Generate agreement with Docusign action with Source ID and Template ID highlighted.")
6. Select Save > Activate.

   A message displays: Your automation was
   activated.

You have completed the process. You added a Generate
agreement with Docusign action to your Salesforce Flow. When conditions
are met, Flow uses Docusign to generate an agreement.

title: Add Users to the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: Add Users to the Docusign Login Permission Set
text:
Learn to use the Docusign Login permission set to give Docusign access to your Salesforce users.

The Docusign Login permission set helps you manage Docusign access for your Salesforce users.

Important:

- The Docusign Login permission set is available in Docusign Apps Launcher v3.5 and higher.
- When you add an existing Docusign user to this permission set, Docusign imports the user's permissions.
- Administrators can use the Docusign Login permission set with their
  single sign-on (SSO) identity provider and Docusign
  just-in-time provisioning. Just-in-time provisioning lets users create their
  own Docusign accounts in Salesforce by entering their SSO
  credentials.

Use this procedure to add users to the Docusign Login permission set:

1. Select Docusign Login.
2. Select Manage Assignments.
3. Select Add Assignments.
4. Select the check box next to each user that you want to add.
5. Select Assign.
6. Select Done.

You have completed the process. You have added one or
more users to the Docusign Login permission set.

title: Enable Standard Docusign CLM Federated Security
heading_path: Enable Standard Docusign CLM Federated Security
text:
Learn how to enable standard Federated Security in your Docusign CLM
account.

Federated Security uses a standard mapping between Salesforce and Docusign security settings:

Table 1. Standard Federated Security Mapping

| Salesforce Security Access | CLM Security Access |
| --- | --- |
| Read | View |
| Edit | View and Edit |
| Delete | View, Edit, and Delete |

Users with Read access on a Salesforce record are given
View access to the CLM content on that page.

Users with Salesforce Edit access are given View
and Edit access in CLM.

Users with Salesforce Delete access are given View,
Edit and Delete access in CLM.

Important:

- Having No Access rights in Docusign CLM prevents
  Salesforce users from seeing CLM content
- Salesforce users must have at least View rights to
  all Salesforce folders in the CLM account
- Docusign recommends that all Salesforce users are given **View, Edit, and Delete**
  access to CLM folders. Federated security defaults to the most restrictive
  access. So a user with **Delete** access in Salesforce and **View**
  access in CLM would only have View access in CLM. Giving all Salesforce
  users **View, Edit, and Delete** access lets administrators manage all
  security through Salesforce.
- If a user logs in to CLM with the CLM username linked to their Salesforce
  user, Federated Security is respected.
- Creating separate CLM credentials (not tied to a Salesforce user) is not recommended. It
  circumvents the Federated Security model.

Use this procedure to enable standard Federated Security mapping in your Docusign CLM
account:

1. Log in to your Docusign CLM account as an administrator.
2. Select the Admin tab.
3. Select Connected Apps at the bottom of the left
   navigation pane.
4. Select the Manage link in the Salesforce tile.
5. Check the Enable federated security box under the
   Security header.

   ![Federated Security enabled.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Federated-Security-enabled.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1NjUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.UwFZ4TaF95eXVwoOLjJr6t6ARolNmvTIkvHyDpb7QAg&_LANG=enus)
6. (Optional):

   - New object mapping: Set custom security
     mappings.
   - Cache time: Change how often Docusign CLM syncs
     with Salesforce.
7. Select the Save button on the bottom of the page.

The process is complete. Salesforce Federated Security is enabled
on your CLM account. Salesforce users have access to Docusign CLM content security based
on their Salesforce Profile or Role security. No separate security model is needed for
Salesforce users in your CLM account.

title: Edit the Name of a Gen Template
heading_path: Edit the Name of a Gen Template
text:
Learn to change the name of a Gen template.

Use this procedure to change the name of a Gen
template:

1. Select Docusign Gen Templates.
2. Select the Name field.
3. Select the Edit
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210520-at-12321-AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0NDAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.wtQijewZ7uDDfiviuRclLxAC5ilCTTLJLIaJHFoBIJw&_LANG=enus) button.![A screenshot displays the Gen template name next to the edit icon.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210520-at-11822-AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0NDAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.wtQijewZ7uDDfiviuRclLxAC5ilCTTLJLIaJHFoBIJw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Gen template name next to the edit icon.")
4. Enter a new name for the template.
5. Press Enter on your keyboard.
6. Select Save.

You have completed the process. You have changed the
name of a Gen template.

title: Step 4: Upload the Word Document to Your Gen Template
heading_path: Step 4: Upload the Word Document to Your Gen Template
text:
Learn to upload your Word template to use in your Docusign Gen template.

Use this procedure to upload your Word template to
use in your Docusign Gen template.

Tip:
[This video explains the step](https://players.brightcove.net/6118377982001/default_default/index.html?videoId=6248887178001).

1. Select Next at the bottom of the Add Fields page.
2. Select Upload Files. Follow the prompts to upload the Word
   template from your device.
3. Complete the following:
   1. Generated File Name: Enter text for the beginning of all
      generated document names.
   2. [File Suffix](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=nxb1586134245032.html&_LANG=enus): Set the second part of the document name:

      - Main Data Source Name: Your customer's name
      - Date: The date a user generated the document
      - Main Data Source Name and Date: Your customer's name and
        the document generation date

      1. File Format: Keep Microsoft Word
         or select PDF as the output file type.
4. (Optional):

   - Document Rule: Select + Add Rule to
     add conditional logic to document creation.
   - Upload Files: Select this button to add additional Word
     documents to the template.
   - Generate all documents in this configuration with one click:
     Generate all uploaded documents when a user selects a Gen button.

     Note: You can use Generate
     all when you have uploaded more than one document. If the box is
     unchecked, generators select which documents to include.
5. Select Next.

You have completed step four. Move to [Step 5: Preview Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=fwi1586134297224.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view a preview of what a generated document will look like.").

5. Select Next.

You have completed step four. Move to [Step 5: Preview Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=fwi1586134297224.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view a preview of what a generated document will look like.").

title: Step 4: Upload the Word Document to Your Gen Template
heading_path: Step 4: Upload the Word Document to Your Gen Template
text:
Learn to upload your Word template to use in your Docusign Gen template.

Use this procedure to upload your Word template to
use in your Docusign Gen template.

Tip:
[This video explains the step](https://players.brightcove.net/6118377982001/default_default/index.html?videoId=6248887178001).

1. Select Next at the bottom of the Add Fields page.
2. Select Upload Files. Follow the prompts to upload the Word
   template from your device.
3. Complete the following:
   1. Generated File Name: Enter text for the beginning of all
      generated document names.
   2. [File Suffix](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=nxb1586134245032.html&_LANG=enus): Set the second part of the document name:

      - Main Data Source Name: Your customer's name
      - Date: The date a user generated the document
      - Main Data Source Name and Date: Your customer's name and
        the document generation date

      1. File Format: Keep Microsoft Word
         or select PDF as the output file type.
4. (Optional):

   - Document Rule: Select + Add Rule to
     add conditional logic to document creation.
   - Upload Files: Select this button to add additional Word
     documents to the template.
   - Generate all documents in this configuration with one click:
     Generate all uploaded documents when a user selects a Gen button.

     Note: You can use Generate
     all when you have uploaded more than one document. If the box is
     unchecked, generators select which documents to include.
5. Select Next.

You have completed step four. Move to [Step 5: Preview Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=fwi1586134297224.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view a preview of what a generated document will look like.").

title: Set the Default Date and Time (Locale) Formatting of Gen Data Fields
heading_path: Set the Default Date and Time (Locale) Formatting of Gen Data Fields
text:
Learn how to set the default formatting of date and time (locale) data
fields.

You can set the default formatting of date and time (locale) data fields. You can decide how
Gen formats the date and time of generated agreements by setting defaults for these
data fields.

Use this procedure to set the default date and time formatting for a data field:

1. Select Options from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1MTIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.nGdKHONG2RJr4eI3K0ASJUOSOsLjo1UwnQmx1WfuagM&_LANG=enus) menu next to a date
   field:

   ![A screenshot of the dropdown menu for the Gen Created Date field displays the Options and Remove options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-dropdown-menu-for-the-Gen-Created-Date-field-displays-the-Options-and-Remove-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1MTIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.nGdKHONG2RJr4eI3K0ASJUOSOsLjo1UwnQmx1WfuagM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the dropdown menu for the Gen Created Date field displays the Options and Remove options.")

   The Add Fields Options dialog box
   opens.
2. Date Format: Select a date format from the dropdown
   menu, or choose a custom option:

   Important: Set the Date Format to
   Use Template Format to use template-level [Gen date and currency
   formatting](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=kqf1686095608923.html&_LANG=enus).

   - Use Template Format: Use the Gen template
     Date and Currency Format settings
   - Locale Default: Use the Salesforce organization's
     locale for date formatting
   - Custom: Enter a custom date format
3. Time Format: Select a time format from the dropdown
   menu, or choose a custom option:

   - Locale Default: Use the Salesforce organization's
     locale for time formatting
   - Custom: Enter a custom time format
4. (Optional): [Add
   matches or does not match
   logic to the field](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=pwd1586134341196.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use Salesforce record fields to trigger conditional logic that displays or hides content in your document.").
5. Select Save.
6. Select Save and Close.

You have completed the process. You have set your Gen
data field's default date and time formatting. Generated documents display the date and
time formatting that you chose.

title: Add New Currency Types to Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Add New Currency Types to Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn to add new currency types to your Salesforce organization.

You can add new currency types to your Salesforce organization. Adding currencies lets users
select different currency types when generating documents with Docusign Gen.

Example: Your Salesforce organization uses U.S.
Dollar as the default currency. Your users want to generate
documents with Euro as the currency. You add
Euro to your organization's currency types. Your Gen
users set the currency to Euro, and Docusign generates contracts with Euro as the
currency.

Note: You must add at least one currency to your organization to use
template-level [Gen date and
currency formatting](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=kqf1686095608923.html&_LANG=enus).

Use this procedure to add a new currency to your Salesforce organization:

1. Edit the Company Information page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYzMDcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.us4BIRNoQdI3RyYkwe8P2lAi0fje1-gbxLd1TD_AkFA&_LANG=enus) icon and
      choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Company Information in the
      Quick Find field.
   3. Select Edit.
2. Select the Activate Multiple Currencies check box and
   select Save.

   ![A screenshot displays the Activate Multiple Currencies box selected on the Salesforce Company Information page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Activate-Multiple-Currencies-box-selected-on-the-Salesforce-Company-Information-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYzMDcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.us4BIRNoQdI3RyYkwe8P2lAi0fje1-gbxLd1TD_AkFA&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Activate Multiple Currencies box selected on the Salesforce Company Information page.")

   The Company Information page opens.
3. Select Currency Setup and select
   New.

   The New Currency page opens.
4. Add a new currency:
   1. Currency Type: Select a new currency from the
      dropdown menu.
   2. Conversion Rate: Enter the conversion rate from
      your default currency to this currency. Example:
      .092.
   3. Decimal Places: Enter the number of digits
      displayed to the right of the decimal point. This setting overrides the
      decimal places setting on all currency fields for users with this
      currency. You can choose between 0 and 6 digits.
   4. Select Save.

You have completed the process. Users can change
the Currency field on a record to the new currency. Gen users can
select the new currency before generating documents.

title: Check the Activation Status of a Docusign for Salesforce User
heading_path: Check the Activation Status of a Docusign for Salesforce User
text:
Learn how to check the activation status of a Docusign CLM, Gen,
Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.

Newly-created Docusign users must select an activation link before using Docusign apps in
Salesforce. You can check the activation status of newly-created users on the Docusign User
Management page.

Use this procedure to check the activation status of a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or
eSignature for Salesforce user:

Check the Status column:

- Active: The user has activated their Docusign account
- Pending: The user has not activated their Docusign account

Tip: Pending
users should check their junk mail folder if they cannot find their Docusign activation email.

You have completed the process. You have verified the
activation status of your Docusign Users. Pending users must [activate their Docusign user accounts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus).

title: "Unable to Download the ContentVersion from Salesforce for the ID” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: "Unable to Download the ContentVersion from Salesforce for the ID” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
text:
Learn to fix the "Unable to download the ContentVersion from Salesforce for the ID"
error.

When users try to generate agreements, an error displays: Unable to download the
ContentVersion from Salesforce for the ID.

title: "Unable to Download the ContentVersion from Salesforce for the ID” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: "Unable to Download the ContentVersion from Salesforce for the ID” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
Resolution 1: Create a Test Word Document for Your Template
text:
Cause: Your Gen template's Word document contains Salesforce
merge fields that are not present in your Salesforce object.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Select the Docusign Gen Templates tab in
   Salesforce.
2. Follow these instructions to [create
   a new Gen template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=lig1586134209279.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a Gen template using a Microsoft Word document.").

   Note: Do not add Salesforce fields on the Add Fields
   step. Upload a blank Word document on the Upload
   Documents step.
3. Select the Gen button on a record.

   You have completed the process. If an agreement generates, the
   connection between Docusign and Salesforce is working.
   Create a new Word template from a blank Word document. Only add merge
   fields to your Word document that are present in your Salesforce
   objects.

title: "Unable to Download the ContentVersion from Salesforce for the ID” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: "Unable to Download the ContentVersion from Salesforce for the ID” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
Resolution 2: Upgrade Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Cause: Your organization has an older version of Docusign Apps Launcher installed. Upgrade to the latest
version.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Check your Docusign Apps Launcher version number:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NzcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.LmF5bbm8et8aCXtmnSXb6IH8cp8Fi06Xds7Mql0-PCg&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Installed Packages in
      the Quick Find field.
   3. Check your Version Number.
2. Choose an upgrade path:
   1. If your version is higher than 6.0, [upgrade to
      the latest Apps Launcher version](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=nok1698959626724.html&_LANG=enus) .
   2. If your version is 6.0 or lower, [use this process to upgrade
      to the latest version](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/DocuSign-Apps-Launcher-Is-there-a-recommended-upgrade-path-for-an-older-version-of-the-package).

      You have completed the process. You have upgraded your older Docusign Apps Launcher package. You can open Docusign components on Salesforce records.

title: "Unable to Download the ContentVersion from Salesforce for the ID” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: "Unable to Download the ContentVersion from Salesforce for the ID” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
Resolution 3: Use the TroubleShooting Tool to Disconnect and Reconnect Docusign
text:
Cause: Your Docusign account is connected
to more than one Salesforce organization.

This is common when administrators create or refresh a Salesforce sandbox that is
connected to a Docusign account. The error displays when a
user selects a Gen button.

1. Open the [Docusign Apps
   Launcher TroubleShooting page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=rag1597638195677.html&_LANG=enus "Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting is a Salesforce page that allows administrators to troubleshoot and resolve issues with these Docusign apps.") in Salesforce.
2. Disconnect Account and Preserve User Settings:
   Select Execute.

   A message displays: **Successfully logged out from Docusign account.** The Troubleshooting page
   displays the message: **No Docusign Account
   Connected.**
3. Follow these instructions to [connect your Docusign
   account.](https://docs.docusign.com/supportdocs/dal-gen-admin-guide/Content/connect-salesforce-and-docusign.htm#zfp1586134208988).

   Important: Only connect a Docusign account
   that is not connected to another Salesforce organization.

   You have completed the process. You have disconnected your Docusign account from your Salesforce organization and
   reconnected. Users can generate agreements with Gen.

title: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
heading_path: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
text:
Learn to fix the issue that prevents linking an envelope template and Gen
template.

I built a Gen template and tried to link it to an envelope template. On the Create
Button page, I searched in the Envelope
Templates field. The envelope template that I wanted to link did not
display in the results.

I built the Gen template and envelope templates on the Opportunity object, so I should be able
to link them.

title: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
heading_path: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
Resolution 1: Switch to a New Source Object and Switch Back
text:
Cause: Docusign is not reading the Gen
template data source correctly.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Open the Gen template Select
   Source page as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ktj1621660808301.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Select a different object from the Salesforce
   Object dropdown menu and select
   Next.
3. Select Back.
4. Select the original object from the Salesforce
   Object dropdown menu and select Save and
   Close.
5. [Open the Gen template Create
   Button page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=way1621663319299.html&_LANG=enus).
6. Envelope Templates: Search for and select your
   envelope template.
7. Select Save and Close.

   You have completed the process. You have reset the source object
   in your Gen template. You can link the envelope template in the
   Create Button step.

title: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
heading_path: I Cannot Link an Envelope Template to a Gen Template
Resolution 2: Use the Developer Console to Fix the Error
text:
Cause: Docusign is not reading the Gen
template data source correctly.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Open the Salesforce Developer
   Console](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=platform.code_dev_console.htm&type=5).
2. Update the dfsle\_SourceObjectc on an affected
   dfsleGenTemplate\_c to the correct Application
   Programming Interface (API) value.

   You have completed the process. You used the Developer Console
   to reset the source object in your Gen template. You can link the
   envelope template in the Create Button
   step.

title: Preview an Agreement Cloud Editor Gen Template
heading_path: Preview an Agreement Cloud Editor Gen Template
text:
Learn how to see what a document generated from your template looks like.

When you add Docusign and
Salesforce fields to your Gen template, you can check your work using the Preview feature.
Selecting the Preview On button merges a Salesforce record with your
template. You see a preview of a generated document.

1. Open the Gen template Create tab as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzMzgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XoO_SlUPEpz09C1hv3oNnGfTdC-GHnzHOMVzWzLNfo8&_LANG=enus) and go to View All >  Docusign Apps Launcher.
   2. Select the Docusign Gen Templates tab.
   3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzMzgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XoO_SlUPEpz09C1hv3oNnGfTdC-GHnzHOMVzWzLNfo8&_LANG=enus) next to a template and choose
      Edit from the dropdown menu.

   The Gen template page opens to the Create tab.
2. Select the Preview Off switch in the upper left of the page.

   The Choose a Record to Preview dialog box opens.
3. Record: Enter or select the name of a Salesforce record.
4. Select Preview.

   The template document opens, displaying the merged Salesforce
   content.
5. **(Optional)**:

   - Show smart content: Highlight all text that was imported from
     Salesforce fields
   - X: Clear this record and select a different record to
     preview![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genacepreviewsmartcontent.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzMzgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XoO_SlUPEpz09C1hv3oNnGfTdC-GHnzHOMVzWzLNfo8&_LANG=enus)
6. Select Preview On to close the preview and return to the
   Template Configurations page.
7. Select Next in the upper right of the page.

   A message opens: Template (your template name)
   updated.

You have completed the process. You have previewed a
document generated from your Agreement Cloud Editor template.

title: Download Previous Versions of Docusign CLM, Gen, and eSignature for Salesforce
heading_path: Download Previous Versions of Docusign CLM, Gen, and eSignature for Salesforce
text:
Find links to previous versions of the Docusign Apps Launcher package
of apps.

Docusign Apps Launcher is a package of apps that is available on the
Salesforce AppExchange. When you install any Apps Launcher app, Docusign installs the following:

- Docusign
   CLM for Salesforce
- Docusign
   Gen for Salesforce
- Docusign
   eSignature for Salesforce

The AppExchange does not provide links to previous Docusign Apps Launcher
versions. You can download earlier versions of the package on this page.

title: Download Previous Versions of Docusign CLM, Gen, and eSignature for Salesforce
heading_path: Download Previous Versions of Docusign CLM, Gen, and eSignature for Salesforce
Previous Docusign Apps launcher downloads and release notes
text:

title: Step 6: Create a Person Account Button and Add it to the Account Layout
heading_path: Step 6: Create a Person Account Button and Add it to the Account Layout
text:
Learn to add a Gen custom button to a Salesforce Account layout.

You can not add a custom button to the Person Account layout in this
step. However, you must add a custom button to at least one account layout to save
and finish your Gen template. After saving the template, you must then remove the
button from your Account layout. You can only add the Gen button to
thePerson Account layout after you have followed these
steps.

Tip:
[The beginning of this video
explains this step](https://bcove.video/3mHgtcg).

1. Button Label: Enter a descriptive name to let users know
   the purpose.
2. Page Layouts: Select the check box for one Account
   layout.

   Note: Selecting one check
   box prevents removing the button from multiple layouts later.
3. Select Update Layouts.
4. (Optional) Post Generation Actions: Configure e-signature
   options for this template:

   - Enable the Send for Signature button, so generators can send
     generated documents out for signature. This option shows
     or hides the Send for Signature button after
     users generate documents.
   - Launch automatically after generation. This
     option opens the standard Send with Docusign
     experience after a user generates a document. Users do not need to
     select the Send for Signature button after generating.
   - Select an Envelope Template to streamline sending for
     signature: Enter the name of an envelope template. This
     option opens the envelope template sending experience after document
     generation.

     Note: You can link a send
     now envelope template to enable a [one-click document
     generation and sending experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sjm1686076461925.html&_LANG=enus).
5. Select Save and Finish.

You have completed step six. Move to [Step 7: Remove the Person Account Custom Button from the Accounts Layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ynj1637170143807.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to to remove the custom button from the Salesforce Account layout.").

title: Use Does Not Match Conditional Logic in a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Use Does Not Match Conditional Logic in a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn to create "does not match" conditional logic to add or remove text when a
Salesforce field is not present.

Use this procedure to create "does not match" logic. Does not
match logic adds or removes text when a Salesforce field is not present.

1. Select the Add Fields arrow. The arrow has no text until you
   select it.
2. Select Copy![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1OTIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.QsyYqvRBOoSFLMUtiIc4ywIJ7Ir_ayO0LXMuGnmDWsg&_LANG=enus)
    next to a field and choose
   Options. ![A screenshot displays the description field with the copy menu open to options and remove.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genconditionaloptionsdropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1OTIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.QsyYqvRBOoSFLMUtiIc4ywIJ7Ir_ayO0LXMuGnmDWsg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the description field with the copy menu open to options and remove.")

   The Table Options dialog box opens.
3. Create does not match logic:
   1. Select Include other content if the conditions set for this field are
      met.
   2. Select does not match.
   3. Enter keywords to avoid matching. For example, use does not match >  Closed Lost to avoid generating agreements for opportunities you did not win.![A screenshot displays the display options field with the values set to does not match and closed lost.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-display-options-field-with-the-values-set-to-does-not-match-and-closed-lost.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1OTIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.QsyYqvRBOoSFLMUtiIc4ywIJ7Ir_ayO0LXMuGnmDWsg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the display options field with the values set to does not match and closed lost.")
   4. Select Save.

   The Add Fields page opens.
4. Select Copy next to the anchor text tag you updated.
5. Add the conditional logic to your Gen template:
   1. Open the Word document that you are using in your Gen template.
   2. Paste the copied anchor text into the document.
   3. Add any custom text to the tag. If you use Welcome back, valued
      customer! as custom text, the tag that results is:`<#
      <Conditional Select="/Opportunity/Description" NotMatch="New Customer" />
      #> Welcome back, valued customer! <# <EndConditional/>
      #>`
6. Select Finish when you are done editing.

   Note: You can create more advanced
   conditional logic than match and does not match. See the Conditionals topics in [Gen Advanced Features](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=pui1586134216490.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use advanced actions when configuring your Gen templates.") for more information.

You have completed the process. You added
does not match conditional logic to your Gen template.

title: A "Not Found" Error Displays When I Use the Gen Agreement Cloud Editor
heading_path: A "Not Found" Error Displays When I Use the Gen Agreement Cloud Editor
text:
Learn to fix the "Not Found" error that displays when you create an Agreement Cloud
Editor Gen template.

I cannot build a Gen template with the Agreement Cloud Editor.

A Not Found error displayed when I selected
Next on the Create page.

title: A "Not Found" Error Displays When I Use the Gen Agreement Cloud Editor
heading_path: A "Not Found" Error Displays When I Use the Gen Agreement Cloud Editor
Resolution: Contact Docusign Support
text:
Cause: The Agreement Cloud Editor feature is not turned on
in your Docusign account.

Agreement Cloud Editor is a paid feature and may add cost to your Docusign plan.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Contact Docusign support](https://support.docusign.com/s/contactSupport).
2. Add the Agreement Cloud Editor feature to your
   Docusign account.

   You have completed the process. You can build Gen templates with
   the Agreement Cloud Editor.

title: Customize Agreement Cloud Editor Template Text
heading_path: Customize Agreement Cloud Editor Template Text
text:
You can customize Agreement Cloud Editor template documents to change font size and
style, add sections of conditional content and add tables.

You can customize Agreement Cloud Editor template documents in the following ways:

- Change font size and style
- Add sections of conditional content
- Add tables

Changes made in the template display in generated documents. You can customize text by
using the toolbar displayed above the document:

![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genacetoolbar.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzMzYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9._VBcWCF--5QtB7Y68iB5KSpH4A-8SFNkjCcLbTYRHlE&_LANG=enus)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | Undo the last action taken |
|  | Redo the last action taken |
|  | Format the text as Header, Sub Header, or Normal Text |
|  | Resize the selected text |
|  | Make the selected text bold |
|  | Italicize the selected text |
|  | Underline the selected text |
|  | Change the font color |
|  | Align the text at the left of the page |
|  | Align text at center of page |
|  | Align the text at the right of the page |
|  | Change line spacing |
|  | Add a bulleted list |
|  | Add a numbered list |
|  | Add a table |
|  | Add an image |
|  | Add a file |

title: Change the Default Language for Gen, Negotiate, eSignature for Salesforce, and CLM

title: Step 2: Create Docusign and Salesforce Anchor Text Fields
heading_path: Step 2: Create Docusign and Salesforce Anchor Text Fields
text:
Learn to create Docusign and Salesforce anchor text fields for your Gen
template.

Use this procedure to create Docusign and Salesforce
anchor text fields for your Gen template. You can add child objects from up to four levels of
relation to the parent Salesforce object. You can add fields from up to two levels of related
lists.

Tip:
[This video explains the step](https://bcove.video/3e1VOM3).

1. Select + Add in the Salesforce Fields section.
2. Select a Salesforce field from the Select an Option dropdown
   menu.

   ![A screen capture displays adding Salesforce fields to the Anchor Text section of the Gen Add Data Fields step.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Gen-Add-Data-Fields.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYzMzYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9._ch8D1uCUOSj0i8lLTBp9RiLxEKv8qQQlIo0QVU18W0&_LANG=enus)
3. (Optional): Customize the Date and Currency Format settings:

   Important: To use Date and Currency
   Format, you must set [currency
   fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=any1686940255489.html&_LANG=enus) and [date fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=gyv1686944628002.html&_LANG=enus) to Use Template Format.

   1. Locale: Select a locale from the dropdown menu:

      - Generator's Locale: Use the generating user's Salesforce
        locale for date formatting
      - Org Locale: Use the Salesforce organization's locale for
        date formatting
      - All Locales: Select a country or region to set date
        formatting
   2. Currency Format: Select a currency format from the dropdown
      menu:

      - Display as symbol: Example:
        $123,456.78
      - Display as symbol (no decimals): Example:
        $123,457
      - Display as ISO code: Example: USD
        123,456.78
      - Display as ISO code (no decimals): Example:
        USD 123,457
      - Display without symbol and ISO code: Example:
        123,456.78
   3. Generator Can Edit: This check box lets users choose the
      date and currency format before generating documents.
4. Select + Add Signer at the bottom of the page.
5. Signature: Select a Docusign field from the
   dropdown menu.
6. (Optional): Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYzMzYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9._ch8D1uCUOSj0i8lLTBp9RiLxEKv8qQQlIo0QVU18W0&_LANG=enus) to the far right of any tag:
   1. Remove: Delete a field
   2. Options: [Use conditional logic
      to show or hide text](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=nnl1586134232740.html&_LANG=enus) based on Salesforce record fields

You have completed step two. Move to [Step 3: Paste Anchor Text Fields Into Your Word Document](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/ffk1586134297621.html "Learn to paste your Salesforce anchor text fields into a Microsoft Word document.").

title: Create a Gen Template using the Person Account Object
heading_path: Create a Gen Template using the Person Account Object
text:
Learn how to build a Gen template using the Salesforce Person Account
object.

Person Account is a part of the Account object.
Person Account objects store information about people by
combining certain account and contact fields into one record. The Person
Account object is available in Salesforce Business, Professional,
Enterprise, Performance, Unlimited, and Developer editions.

Note: Before you can use Person accounts, you must enable
them in your Salesforce organization. Visit the Salesforce help site to learn to
enable Person accounts.

You can build Person Account Gen templates using this procedure:

[Step 1: Add a Name, Template Editor, and Select Account as the Data Source](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=kdb1636868236978.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to set the main template details: template name, description, and Salesforce data source (Account.)")

[Step 2: Create Docusign and Salesforce Anchor Text Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=irc1586134297835.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Docusign and Salesforce anchor text fields for your Gen template.")

[Step 3: Paste Anchor Text Fields Into Your Word Document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ffk1586134297621.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to paste your Salesforce anchor text fields into a Microsoft Word document.")

[Step 4: Upload the Word Document to Your Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=gxl1586134297397.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to upload your Word template to use in your Docusign Gen template.")

[Step 5: Preview Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=fwi1586134297224.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view a preview of what a generated document will look like.")

[Step 6: Create a Person Account Button and Add it to the Account Layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=hki1636868684641.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a Gen custom button to a Salesforce Account layout.")

[Step 7: Remove the Person Account Custom Button from the Accounts Layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ynj1637170143807.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to to remove the custom button from the Salesforce Account layout.")

[Step 8: Add Your Template Button to the Salesforce Person Account Layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=yxb1637170224921.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a custom button to the Salesforce Person Account layout.")

title: Add the Docusign Files Component to a Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Add the Docusign Files Component to a Salesforce Layout
text:
Learn to add the Docusign Files component to a Salesforce page
layout.

Docusign Files is a component that displays all Docusign files associated with a record. Users can interact with Docusign
content without leaving Salesforce. You can add the Docusign Files
component to a Salesforce layout.

[Follow this how-to video](https://docusign.gallery.video/watch?videoId=6307338125112) or use this procedure to add the Docusign Files to a layout:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, open a record, select the gear icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxNzUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.f12QpFGKfemGiUf2vSgzuFd0NObT_uXwPx3C5Qn8SEU&_LANG=enus) and choose
   Edit Page from the dropdown menu.
2. Drag the Docusign Files component to the page layout.

   ![A screen capture displays the Docusign Files component being dragged to the Opportunity page layout.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Add-DocuSign-Files-component.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxNzUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.f12QpFGKfemGiUf2vSgzuFd0NObT_uXwPx3C5Qn8SEU&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the Docusign Files component being dragged to the Opportunity page layout.")
3. Activate the change to your layout:
   1. Select Activate.
   2. Select a tab to set your default options: Org Default,
      App Default, or App, Record Type, and
      Profile.
   3. Select Assign and choose options from the dialog box.
   4. Select Close.
4. Select Save.
5. Select the back arrow ![A screenshot of the Back arrow on the Salesforce Edit Page settings bar](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-Back-arrow-on-the-Salesforce-Edit-Page-settings-bar.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxNzUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.f12QpFGKfemGiUf2vSgzuFd0NObT_uXwPx3C5Qn8SEU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Back arrow on the Salesforce Edit Page settings bar") to return to the record.

You have completed the process. You have added the Docusign Files component to a Salesforce layout. Users can interact with Docusign files on Salesforce records.

title: Learn About Adding Document Rules to a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Adding Document Rules to a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn about using document rules to set conditional logic in Gen templates.

You can use document rules in a Gen template to enable conditional document
generation. Your Gen templates can generate different agreement types based on
conditions present in Salesforce records. Conditional generation is useful when a
Gen template has multiple versions of a contract.

title: Learn About Adding Document Rules to a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Adding Document Rules to a Word-Based Gen Template
Document Rule example
text:
Fontara has a Gen template named Global Sales Contract. The administrator
adds three different contract versions to the Gen template: English, French, and
Brazilian Portuguese.

The administrator builds a document rule in the Global Sales Contract template using
this logic:

- IF: Billing Country >  Equals >  Brazil
- THEN: Generate Brazilian Portuguese Contract

A user selects the Global Sales Contract button on a Brazilian customer's record. Docusign generates a Brazilian Portuguese-language agreement from
the record. Without the document rule, the user must manually select the Portuguese
template before generating.

title: Learn About Adding Document Rules to a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Adding Document Rules to a Word-Based Gen Template
Document rule fields
text:
Administrators can use the following Salesforce fields to create a document rule:

- BOOLEAN (using true or false)
- CURRENCY
- DATE
- DOUBLE
- EMAIL
- PERCENT
- PHONE
- PICKLIST
- STRING
- URL

title: Learn About Adding Document Rules to a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Adding Document Rules to a Word-Based Gen Template
Notes on document rules
text:
- You cannot use commas in number field rule values (CURRENCY, DOUBLE, PERCENT,
  and so on.) Example: Use 9000 instead of
  9,000.
- Do not use quotation marks in string field rule values (STRING, PICKLIST, URL,
  and EMAIL.)
- Do not use quotation marks for Boolean field rule values
  (true and false.)
- Do not use quotation marks for date field rule values that follow the YYYY-MM-DD
  format.
- You can use Date Literals in Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) syntax.
  Example: LAST\_WEEK, LAST\_n\_DAYS:n, and so on. Follow this link for more
  information about [entering conditional date values](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.soql_sosl.meta/soql_sosl/sforce_api_calls_soql_select_dateformats.htm).
- Use double field rule values when using decimals for large numbers. Example: Use
  9000000000.00 to search for
  9000000000.)
- Checkbox field rule values are Boolean.
  - Selected check boxes equal true (or True or TRUE.)
  - Unselected check boxes equal false (or False or FALSE.)
- Use the currency prefix before multi-currency field rule values. Example: Use
  USD9000.00 for $9000.00.
- You can duplicate any condition with a field value, whether or not it has a
  conditional value.
- You cannot save a document rule until you add a rule name and conditions for all
  fields.

title: The Generate Agreement with Docusign Action Failed in My Salesforce Flow

title: Add Filters to a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Add Filters to a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn to use XML code to build custom tables in Word that filter Salesforce
data.

You can use XML code to build custom tables in Word that filter Salesforce data for
Gen agreements. You can use filtering to remove unwanted data from a record and
import only the results you want.

Use this procedure to build a table that filters product types to import only certain
results:

1. Select the Copy button next to the Type anchor
   text.![A screenshot displays the copy button highlighted next to a Gen template anchor text field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-copy-button-highlighted-next-to-a-Gen-template-anchor-text-field.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzMDAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.ZL9nHyiIfbGPXHly_cLeoVxX-__nPlFkn3YiwZHqe1Q&_LANG=enus)
2. Create an empty table in your Word template and paste the
   Type anchor text into the first cell of the table:

   Note: This field merges every Type
   value from a Salesforce record into the table. Because you only want to see
   the Software type, edit the tag.

   |  |
   | --- |
   | Type: |
   | **`<# <Content Select="./Type__c"/> #>`** |
3. Edit the existing anchor text by adding `[text()='Software']`
   into the text after the tag name `Type__c`.

   Note: Replace the word
   Software with your Salesforce product
   type.

   The updated field reads: `<# <Content
   Select="./Type__c[text()='Software']"/>
   #>`
4. Edit the text to fit Microsoft Word table formatting:
   1. Remove `<# <Content Select="./` at the beginning
      of the tag.
   2. Replace that text with `<# <TableRow
      Select="/Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems/`

   The updated field reads: `<# <TableRow
   Select="/Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems/Type__c[text()='Software']" />
   #>`
5. Copy and paste the field into the first row of your table:

   |  |
   | --- |
   | Type: |
   | **`<# <TableRow Select="/Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems/Type__c[text()='Software']" /> #>`** |
6. Add the Content Select text to the end of your field
   text. The table uses this text to import Salesforce data.

   Important: Paste
   the Content Select text directly at the end of the
   TableRow text, with no spaces between.

   |  |
   | --- |
   | Type: |
   | `<# <TableRow Select="/Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems/Type__c[text()='Software']" /> #><# <Content Select="./Type__c"/> #>` |

   When a user generates an agreement from a Salesforce record, the data in
   the table looks like this:

   |  |
   | --- |
   | Type: |
   | Software |

   Note:
   - This example shows a 1 : Many path: `<# <TableRow
     Select="//Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems/Type[text() =
     ‘Software’ or text() = 'Services']/.." /> #>`
   - To access the Product level from the Opportunity, use the
     `//Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems/Product`
     path. 1 : Many : 1 paths can be difficult to
     use because certain parts of the syntax expression must change.

You have completed the process. You have built a Gen table in
Microsoft Word to filter the results of a Salesforce record.

title: Change the Docusign Template Used in a Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Change the Docusign Template Used in a Salesforce Flow
text:
Learn to change the Docusign template or Salesforce object connected
to your Flow.

title: Change the Docusign Template Used in a Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Change the Docusign Template Used in a Salesforce Flow
Before you begin
text:
Install Docusign Apps Launcher version 6.12 or higher.

Note: The current API limitation is 100 generations per 10
minutes. If you want to increase your limit, [contact your Docusign representative](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/How-do-I-contact-DocuSign-Customer-Support) about
options.

You can edit a Flow to change the Docusign template or Salesforce object used in your Flow.
Use this procedure to edit a Salesforce Flow:

1. Open a Flow as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNzQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.EON03Fgh491u3Qo-Frd47PpLGytOIM0wz4Cj1S58MkE&_LANG=enus) in the upper right and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Flows in the Quick Find
      field.
   3. Select a Flow from the All Flows list.
2. Edit the Docusign action in your Flow:
   1. Select your Docusign
      Apex Action
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-blue-and-white-Apex-Action-icon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNzQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.EON03Fgh491u3Qo-Frd47PpLGytOIM0wz4Cj1S58MkE&_LANG=enus).

      The action panel opens.
   2. Enter a new Docusign template ID or Salesforce
      Source Id.
   3. Select Done.
3. Select Save As New Version when you have completed editing your
   Flow.

   The Save as dialog box opens.
4. Select Save.
5. Select Activate.

   A message displays: Your automation was
   activated..

You have completed the process. You changed the template ID or
Salesforce object ID in your Flow.

title: Gen XML Toolbox: Learn to Use Custom XML in Your Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Gen XML Toolbox: Learn to Use Custom XML in Your Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn how to use custom XML to add tables, filter data, group data, add conditional
logic, and more.

You can use custom XML in your template to add tables, filter and group data, add conditional
logic, and more. XML tags in Word-based Gen templates merge Docusign and Salesforce fields into a generated agreement.

Gen merge tags (<Content> , <TableRow>, <Conditional>, and so on) use XPath
to read extracted XML data. XPath is an XML query language.

You can also build custom XML/XPath tags in your Word template to add tables, filter data,
group data, add conditional logic, and more.

title: Gen XML Toolbox: Learn to Use Custom XML in Your Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Gen XML Toolbox: Learn to Use Custom XML in Your Word-Based Gen Template
How Docusign uses XML in Gen
text:
You start building a Word-based Gen template. You create a Word document containing
Docusign and Salesforce fields that you pasted from the Gen
template.

You upload the Word document to the Gen template and add a Gen button to your
Salesforce layouts.

A user selects the Gen button on a record. Docusign uses an API
call to send Docusign and Salesforce XML data from your template
to a Docusign server.

The Docusign server creates the finished contract by merging the
XML data with the template document. Docusign sends the finished
agreement back to Salesforce.

title: Gen XML Toolbox: Learn to Use Custom XML in Your Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Gen XML Toolbox: Learn to Use Custom XML in Your Word-Based Gen Template
Using XML in Gen templates
text:
- The tags that you create in Gen and place in a Word or PDF document always
  reference Salesforce XML nodes.
- You can reference a single node by using array syntax for expressions that
  evaluate to multiple nodes. Example:
  //Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems[1]/Description
- Gen currently only supports XPath 1.0.
- Docusign does not support XSLT-specific functions.
- You can concatenate strings with XPath.
  Example: concat(//Opportunity/Account.Name, ' - ',
  //Opportunity/Name)
- You can use all [merge tags from Docusign CLM and
  Legacy Docusign CLM (SpringCM)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=uqj1643324072491&topicId=gjv1576610127258.html&_LANG=enus) in Gen
  templates.
