title: Enter a Verification Code to Access Docusign on a New Device
heading_path: Enter a Verification Code to Access Docusign on a New Device
text:
Learn how to activate a new device for use with your Docusign account.

You must enter a verification code to access your Docusign account on a new device. You must verify your device again if
you empty your browser cache.

Use this procedure to activate your Docusign account on a new device:

1. Enter Docusign credentials when prompted.

   The Login from a new device dialog box
   displays.
2. Select Email. Docusign sends a verification code to your
   email address.
3. Open the Verify a New Device email from Docusign. Check
   your junk mail folder if you do not see the message in your Inbox.
4. Copy the verification code from the email.
5. Enter the verification code in the Login from a new device
   dialog box.
6. Select Confirm.

You have completed the process. You can access your Docusign account on this device.

title: Import Custom Objects for Gen
heading_path: Import Custom Objects for Gen
text:
Learn about importing custom objects to your Salesforce organization.

You use existing Salesforce data to build Docusign Gen templates. You can also import data
from other sources and add it to your Salesforce objects like Accounts and Opportunities. When
you have imported this data, you can use it to build a Gen template. You can only import data
into one Salesforce object at a time. You can import up to 50,000 records at a time.

You can take the following actions when importing data:

- Add new records to Salesforce.
- Update existing Salesforce records
- Add new records and update existing records at the same time.

Follow this link to learn more about [importing data to your Salesforce organization from other sources](https://salesforce.vidyard.com/watch/ARIjWm2qrDkJVJxEhReFug).

title: Learn About Rebuilding Your Docusign Connection After Changes to Your Salesforce Domain Name
heading_path: Learn About Rebuilding Your Docusign Connection After Changes to Your Salesforce Domain Name
text:
Learn to rebuild the connection between Docusign and Salesforce after domain name
changes.

All Salesforce organizations have a unique domain name called My Domain.
With My Domain, you can include your organization name in your URLs.

Example:
`https://mycompany.my.salesforce.com`.

You can set up a custom login page and policy, offer single sign-on, and enable social
account logins. My Domain also enables working in multiple Salesforce organizations in
the same browser at the same time.

You can change your domain name. The following are reasons for changing your domain name:

- You want to update your brand.
- You want to use [Enhanced Domains](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.domain_name_enhanced.htm&type=5).
- You want to enable partitioned domains.

When you change your My Domain name, your Salesforce-hosted URLs also change. You must
rebuild the connection to your Docusign account.

You can rebuild your connection to Docusign after a domain change with the
Sync Domains tool.

Important: The Sync Domains process may
take up to 72 hours to complete. Enable Redirect previous My Domain URLs to
your current My Domain in your Salesforce organization. Enabling
redirects during this time period prevents errors with your Docusign integration.

The rebuilding procedure differs depending on the Docusign apps that you
use:

- [Docusign Gen, Negotiate, eSignature, and
  eSignature Apex Toolkit customers](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ppq1681966676237.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to fix domain integration issues using the Sync Domains feature.")
- [Docusign CLM and CLM Essentials for Salesforce
  customers](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ynp1680122164220.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to fix domain integration issues using the Sync Domains feature. Sync Domains fixes issues with Enhanced Domains and any changes that you make to your Salesforce domain.")

title: Use Group Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Use Group Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn how to use group tags to gather a cluster of related data. Group tags can filter a
large amount of data to get targeted results.

Use this procedure to build Group tags and add them to your Word-based Gen template:

Example: You have a catalog in your Salesforce organization with multiple
product types. Your catalog is grouped by software,
service, and hardware product
categories. You want to generate contracts that show products grouped by type. You
add Group tags to your Gen template.

When your users generate agreements, Docusign groups the available
products by type.

Use this procedure to add Group tags to your Gen template:

1. Select Copy next to the Opportunity Product
   Name anchor text.![A screenshot displays the Copy button highlighted next to the OpportunityProductName tag.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Copy-OpportunityProductName-tag.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzMjYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.IK1MYnZGen_Rl6w515A1OSZ-m6AiORbP0J4S38mwFlU&_LANG=enus)
2. Add a table with XML tags for product name and category to your Word document:
   1. Open the Gen template Add Documents page and
      select your Word document.

      The document preview page opens.
   2. Select Download and open the document in
      Microsoft Word.
   3. Create an empty, two-column table in your Gen Word template.
   4. Enter Name in the column 1 header and
      Category in the column 2 header.
   5. Paste the Name anchor text that you copied from
      Gen into the cell below the Name column:

      |  |  |
      | --- | --- |
      | Name | Category |
      | `<# <Content Select="./Name"/> #>` |  |
   6. Enter `<# <TableRow Select="./GroupItems/Item" />
      #>` at the start, to fit the formatting recognized by a Word
      table. The updated field reads: `<# <TableRow
      Select="./GroupItems/Item" /> #><# <Content Select="./Name"
      /> #>`

      Note: In the following example, replace the word
      `Item` with the list you are using. For example,
      `<# <TableRow Select="./GroupItems/OpportunityLineItems" />
      #>`

      |  |  |
      | --- | --- |
      | Name | Category |
      | `<# <TableRow Select="./GroupItems/Item" /> #><# <Content Select="./Name" /> #>` |  |
   7. Return to the Gen template and select Copy next
      to the Category tag.
   8. Paste the Category anchor text into the cell below
      the Category column:

      | **Name** | **Category** |
      | --- | --- |
      | `<# <TableRow Select="./GroupItems/Item" /> #><# <Content Select="./Name" /> #>` | `<# <Content Select="./Category"/> #>` |
3. Enter the Group Select tags just above your table in the
   Word document:
   1. `<# <Group Select ="/Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems"
      By="./Category" /> #>`
   2. Here are all `<# <Content Select="./GroupName" />
      #>` items:
4. Add the **`<# <EndGroup/> #>`** tag just below the
   table in your Word document.

   Important: The
   finished table with header and footer tags displays as follows:

   `<# <Group Select ="/Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems"
   By="./Category" /> #>`

   Here are all `<# <Content Select="./GroupName" />
   #>` items:

   | Name | Category |
   | --- | --- |
   | `<# <TableRow Select="./GroupItems/Item" /> #><# <Content Select="./Name” /> #>` | `<# <Content Select="./Category"/> #>` |

   `<# <EndGroup/> #>`
5. [Re-upload the document to your Gen
   template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sqp1586134339354.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to update your Gen template Word documents.").
6. Select Save and Close and select
   Save on the Do you want to save
   changes? dialog box.

   You have added your table with Group tags. The tags you added perform
   the following functions:

title: Use Group Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Use Group Tags in a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
").
6. Select Save and Close and select
   Save on the Do you want to save
   changes? dialog box.

   You have added your table with Group tags. The tags you added perform
   the following functions:

   | Tag | Purpose |
   | --- | --- |
   | `/Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems` | Finds all of your Opportunity Line Items on a record. |
   | `By="./Category"` | Filters Opportunity Line Items by category. |
   | `<# <Content Select="./GroupName" /> #>` | Required for any Group by tag type. **Do not customize this tag or it will not work correctly**. |
   | `<# <TableRow Select="./GroupItems/Item" /> #>` | Required for any Group by tag type. |
   | `<# <EndGroup/> #>` | Ends the group tag. Must be placed outside the table. |

   When a user generates an agreement from a Salesforce record, the tables
   display as follows: ![A screenshot displays the generated output of the Group tables that you built in this process.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Group-tag-generated-output.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzMjYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.IK1MYnZGen_Rl6w515A1OSZ-m6AiORbP0J4S38mwFlU&_LANG=enus)

You have completed the process. You have added a table in your
Word document using Group XML tags. When users generate agreements with this Gen
template, Docusign generates separate tables for each product
type.

title: Configure Salesforce Settings to Simplify Docusign App Administration
heading_path: Configure Salesforce Settings to Simplify Docusign App Administration
text:
Learn how to update settings in your Salesforce organization to ease the management and
use of your Docusign apps.

You can update settings in your Salesforce organization to ease the management and use of your
Docusign apps.

You can use the following settings to help Docusign apps work in your Salesforce
organization:

- [Salesforce Connected App Security Changes](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=refbdaee1ac-b187-4df9-94f2-a094f9f47ef2.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the actions that you must take to use Docusign apps after Salesforce security changes.")
- [Install and Configure the Docusign Connected App](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=tsk6312c996-938f-4f24-b11f-9da2f7887493.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to install and configure the Docusign connected app for your Salesforce organization.")
- [Add Docusign IP Ranges to Salesforce to Prevent Errors](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=lip1592574174158.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add Docusign IP ranges to your Salesforce organization's Trusted IP Ranges.")
- [Use Manage App Policies to Allow or Prevent Docusign User Access](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=akc1598285785361.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use Manage App Policies to allow or prevent users from authorizing their Docusign access.")
- [Understanding Docusign Permission Sets in Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=xxm1623778416524.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the types and functions of Docusign Apps Launcher permission sets.")
- [Change the Default Language for Gen, Negotiate, eSignature for Salesforce, and CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ioe1586134255116.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use Translation Workbench to let users choose their default Salesforce language.")
- [Add New Currency Types to Your Salesforce
  Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=gzh1686882277803.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add new currency types to your Salesforce organization.")
- [Change the Currency Type of a Salesforce
  Record](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=xrh1686885796196.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to change the type of currency Salesforce displays on a record.")
- [Federated Security for Docusign CLM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=plx1597758869161.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about how you can apply Salesforce security settings to your CLM content using Federated Security.")
- [Use Hyperforce with Docusign Apps Launcher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=swo1740519895843.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add domains to your allowlist so that Docusign apps work with your Hyperforce organization.")

title: Step 1: Add a Name, Template Editor, and Select Account as the Data Source
heading_path: Step 1: Add a Name, Template Editor, and Select Account as the Data Source
text:
Learn to set the main template details: template name, description, and Salesforce data
source (Account.)

Use this procedure to decide the name, template editing
tool, and select Account as the data source.

Important:
Person
Account does not appear in the list of available objects in this
step. You must select the Account object here to use the
Person Account object later in the process.

1. Select Configure at the far right.
2. Select Create a Template Configuration.
3. Complete the following tasks:
   1. Add Template Name: Enter a name for your
      template.
   2. (Optional) Add Template Description: Add details
      about the template.
   3. Select Data Source: Select
      Account from the drop-down menu.
   4. Select Template Editor: Select
      Microsoft Word.
   5. Select Create.

You have completed step one. Move to [Step 2: Create Docusign and Salesforce Anchor Text Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=irc1586134297835.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Docusign and Salesforce anchor text fields for your Gen template.").

title: Search for Docusign Users on the Docusign User Management Page
heading_path: Search for Docusign Users on the Docusign User Management Page
text:
Learn to use the search feature to find Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature users.

You can use the Search by name or email field to find Gen,
CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature users.

Use this procedure to search for Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature users:

1. Search by name or email: Enter a name or email address
   in the field.

   ![Gen, Negotiate, and eSignature user management search](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Gen-Negotiate-and-eSignature-user-management-search.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyNDMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.ATeSR5pq_8IiYlJTDDE6g7OolltEDmQpcoyLJH889Vc&_LANG=enus "Gen, Negotiate, and eSignature user management search")

   Results update automatically as you enter characters.
2. Select ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Clear-results.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyNDMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.ATeSR5pq_8IiYlJTDDE6g7OolltEDmQpcoyLJH889Vc&_LANG=enus)
   to clear your search.

You have completed the process. You have used the search field on
the User Management page to find Docusign users.

title: Add Currency and Locale Defaults to Your Gen Template
heading_path: Add Currency and Locale Defaults to Your Gen Template
text: Learn to add a default currency and locale to your Gen template.

title: Add Currency and Locale Defaults to Your Gen Template
heading_path: Add Currency and Locale Defaults to Your Gen Template
Before you begin
text:
- Install Docusign Apps Launcher to version 5.4 or higher
- [Add New Currency Types to Your Salesforce
  Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=gzh1686882277803.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add new currency types to your Salesforce organization.")

You can add a default currency and locale to your Gen template. By adding default
currency and locale fields, you decide how Gen formats the date, time, and currency
formats displayed in generated agreements.

You can customize the following settings:

- Locale: Set the date formatting of all documents generated
  from this template.
- Currency: Set the currency formatting of all documents
  generated from this template.
- Generator Can Edit: Users set the currency and locale
  before generating documents.

Use this procedure to edit the currency and locale settings of your Gen template:

1. [Edit the Gen template Add Fields page as a
   Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=was1621662565775.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to access the Add Fields step in a Word-based Gen template.").
2. [Set the format of all currency fields to Use
   Template Format](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=any1686940255489.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set the default formatting of currency data fields.").
3. [Set the format of all date and time (locale)
   fields to Use Template Format](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=gyv1686944628002.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set the default formatting of date and time (locale) data fields.").

   Important: Set currency and locale field defaults to
   Use Template Format, or Gen ignores
   Date and Currency Format settings.
4. Set Date and Currency Format defaults:
   1. Locale: Select a locale option from the dropdown
      menu:

      - Generator's Locale: Use the generating
        user's Salesforce locale for date formatting
      - Org Locale: Use the Salesforce
        organization's locale for date formatting
      - All Locales: Select a country or region
        to set date formatting
   2. Currency Format: Select a currency format from
      the dropdown menu:

      - Display as symbol: Example:
        $123,456.78
      - Display as symbol (no decimals): Example:
        $123,457
      - Display as ISO code: Example:
        USD 123,456.78
      - Display as ISO code (no decimals):
        Example: USD 123,457
      - Display without symbol and ISO code:
        Example: 123,456.78
   3. Generator Can Edit: Users can [choose a currency and locale
      format before generating](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=hhz1686691127617.html&_LANG=enus).
5. Select Next.
6. Select Save and Close.

   You have completed the process. You have customized the currency and locale settings
   of your Gen template.

title: Access the Gen Template Select Source Step
heading_path: Access the Gen Template Select Source Step
text:
Learn to access the Select Source step in a Word-based Gen template.

You can view or change the Salesforce data source your Gen template is built on.

Warning: Changing your Gen template data source deletes all of
your template's Salesforce fields. Docusign fields remain
unchanged.Docusign recommends creating a new template, rather
than changing the data source of an existing template.

Use this procedure to access the Select Source step of a Gen Word template:

1. Edit the Gen template Select Source page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0NzEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.QTyfX8qGDmPrWSJnhO4nk2RHCCFqrdN-b_6rIQJcbbM&_LANG=enus) and choose View
      All.
   2. Select Docusign Apps Launcher.
   3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0NzEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.QTyfX8qGDmPrWSJnhO4nk2RHCCFqrdN-b_6rIQJcbbM&_LANG=enus) next to a Gen template and choose
      Edit from the dropdown menu.

   The Gen template creation page opens.
2. Select the Select Source arrow. The arrow has no text
   until selected.

You have completed the process. You have opened the
Select Source step of a Gen template.

title: Create a Generate and Send Experience for Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Create a Generate and Send Experience for Salesforce Flow
text:
Learn to create a Salesforce Flow action that generates an agreement and sends it for
Docusign eSignature.

title: Create a Generate and Send Experience for Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Create a Generate and Send Experience for Salesforce Flow
Before you begin
text:
Install Docusign Apps Launcher version 6.12 or higher.

[Create a Docusign
envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uce1617376465289.html&_LANG=enus).

[Create a Gen template with Microsoft
Word](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=lig1586134209279.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a Gen template using a Microsoft Word document.").

[Link a Gen template to an envelope
template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=bwo1620419687966.html&_LANG=enus).

Note: The current API limitation is 100 generations per 10 minutes.
If you want to increase your limit, [contact your Docusign representative](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/How-do-I-contact-DocuSign-Customer-Support) about options.

You can link Docusign eSignature and Gen templates to create a
generate and send experience in Salesforce Flow. When you create a "Gen and Send"
experience, Salesforce Flow can generate an agreement and send it for signature.

Gen and Send experiences simplify your business process by automating contract
generation and signing.

Note:
Docusign does not support using the
Sign Now option when you add an In Person
Signer to a Flow.

Use this procedure to create a Gen and Send experience in Salesforce Flow:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, create or edit a Salesforce Flow.
2. Open the Add Element dialog box:
   1. Locate the point in your Flow where you want to add your action.
   2. Move the cursor to the circle icon and select the plus
      + icon that displays.![A screen capture displays the Add Element plus icon in a Salesforce Flow.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screen-capture-displays-the-Add-Element-plus-icon-in-a-Salesforce-Flow.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNzYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.BOiljy7oQfdSlujicv2yYsqYuB8DceW3dnpbu_ckL6o&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the Add Element plus icon in a Salesforce Flow.")
   3. Add Element: Enter and select
      Generate agreement with Docusign
      in the search field.
3. Add a label and API name for your action:
   1. Label: Enter
       Docusign Generate & Send
      .
   2. API Name: Keep or change the default name.
4. Source Id: Choose the ID of the object your Flow is
   built on. For example, if your Flow is built on Opportunity, choose: Triggering Opportunity >  Opportunity ID.
5. Template ID: Add the ID of the Gen template used in the
   Flow.
   1. Select the Docusign Gen
      Templates
      tab.
   2. Select the Gen template that you want to use in your Flow.

title: Create a Generate and Send Experience for Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Create a Generate and Send Experience for Salesforce Flow
Before you begin
text:
tunity ID.
5. Template ID: Add the ID of the Gen template used in the
   Flow.
   1. Select the Docusign Gen
      Templates
      tab.
   2. Select the Gen template that you want to use in your Flow.

      Note: You must choose a Gen template that you linked to an envelope template.
   3. Copy the alpha-numeric template ID at the end of the URL. Do not copy
      the slash marks.![A screenshot displays the Gen template ID highlighted in the envelope template URL.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Gen-template-ID-highlighted-in-the-envelope-template-URL-1.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNzYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.BOiljy7oQfdSlujicv2yYsqYuB8DceW3dnpbu_ckL6o&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Gen template ID highlighted in the envelope template URL.")
   4. Paste the ID (without slash marks) in the Template
      ID field.
   5. Select Done.![A screenshot displays the New Action dialog box with a Gen template ID and Account ID entered.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Generate_agreement_with_Docusign_flow_action.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNzYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.BOiljy7oQfdSlujicv2yYsqYuB8DceW3dnpbu_ckL6o&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the New Action dialog box with a Gen template ID and Account ID entered.")
6. Select Save >  Activate.

   A message displays: Your automation was
   activated.

You have completed the process. You created a Gen and
Send Salesforce Flow. When conditions are met, Flow uses Docusign to generate an agreement and send it for signature.

title: Step 6: Add a Gen Custom Button to Your Salesforce Layout
heading_path: Step 6: Add a Gen Custom Button to Your Salesforce Layout
text:
Learn to add a Gen custom button to your Salesforce layouts.

Use this procedure to add a Gen custom button to your Salesforce layout:

Tip:
[The beginning of this video explains
this step](https://bcove.video/3mHgtcg).

1. Button Label: Enter a descriptive name to let users know the
   purpose.
2. Page Layouts: Select the check box for each layout you want to
   update.
3. Select Update Layouts.
4. (Optional) Post Generation Actions: Configure e-signature options
   for this template:

   - Enable the Send for Signature button, so generators can send generated
     documents out for signature. This option shows or hides the
     Send for Signature button after users generate documents.
   - Launch automatically after generation. This option opens the
     standard Send with Docusign experience after a user generates a
     document. Users do not need to select the Send for Signature button after
     generating.
   - Select an Envelope Template to streamline sending for
     signature: Enter the name of an envelope template. This option opens the
     envelope template sending experience after document generation.

     Note: You can link a send now envelope template
     to enable a [one-click document generation and sending
     experience](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sjm1686076461925.html&_LANG=enus).
5. Select Save and Finish.

You have completed the entire process. You built a
Docusign Gen for Salesforce template and added a custom button to Salesforce page layouts.
Approved Gen users can generate contracts from a Salesforce record to save or send for
signature.

title: Create a Gen for Billing Template to Generate Invoices
heading_path: Create a Gen for Billing Template to Generate Invoices
text:
Learn to create Gen for Billing templates to generate invoices from a Salesforce record
automatically or from a custom button.

You can configure Gen for Billing templates to generate invoices from a Salesforce record
automatically or from a custom button.

Important:

- You must have a [Salesforce Billing account](https://www.salesforce.com/products/sales-cloud/tools/cpq-software/) to create Gen for Billing
  templates.
- You must use the Invoice object when creating Gen for Billing
  templates.
- You can only create a Gen for Billing template with Microsoft Word. You cannot use the
  Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor.
- You can only create one automated template at a time.

Use this procedure to build a Gen for Billing template:

[Step 1: Add a Name, Data Source, and Template Editor](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=vgg1614494891133.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to set the main template details: template name, description, and Salesforce data source (Invoice.)")

[Step 2: Create Docusign and Salesforce Anchor Text Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=irc1586134297835.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Docusign and Salesforce anchor text fields for your Gen template.")

[Step 3: Paste Anchor Text Fields Into Your Word Document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ffk1586134297621.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to paste your Salesforce anchor text fields into a Microsoft Word document.")

[Step 4: Upload the Word Document to Your Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=gxl1586134297397.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to upload your Word template to use in your Docusign Gen template.")

[Step 5: Preview Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=fwi1586134297224.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view a preview of what a generated document will look like.")

[Step 6: (Optional) Automatically Generate and Email Invoices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=bhg1614497261479.html&_LANG=enus "This step is optional. You can choose to generate and email invoices automatically, instead of creating a custom button that your users select to generate invoices.")

[Step 7: Create a Custom Button or Finish the Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=aup1614501330150.html&_LANG=enus "This step is optional. You can choose to create a custom button instead of generating invoices automatically.")

title: Create a Gen Template with Microsoft Word
heading_path: Create a Gen Template with Microsoft Word
text:
Learn to build a Gen template using a Microsoft Word document.

You can build Docusign Gen templates to automate your contract
generation process. Gen templates create agreements by merging Docusign and Salesforce fields to a Word document that you upload. When users select the Gen
button, Docusign generates an agreement based on the record data.

Important: To create a template, [grant Generator status to your Docusign user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=doi1628107496987.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to edit the permissions of Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce users.").

Use this procedure to build a Gen template with Microsoft Word:

[Step 1: Choose a Name, Data Source, and Template
Editor](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=vxk1586134289075.html&_LANG=enus "Use this procedure to decide the name, Salesforce data source, and editing tool for the template.")

[Step 2: Create Docusign and
Salesforce Anchor Text Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=irc1586134297835.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Docusign and Salesforce anchor text fields for your Gen template.")

[Step 3: Paste Anchor Text Fields Into Your Word
Document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ffk1586134297621.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to paste your Salesforce anchor text fields into a Microsoft Word document.")

[Step 4: Upload the Word Document to Your Gen
Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=gxl1586134297397.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to upload your Word template to use in your Docusign Gen template.")

[Step 5: Preview Your Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=fwi1586134297224.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to view a preview of what a generated document will look like.")

[Step 6: Add a Gen Custom Button to Your Salesforce
Layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ldc1586134295945.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a Gen custom button to your Salesforce layouts.")

title: Add Docusign IP Ranges to Salesforce to Prevent Errors
heading_path: Add Docusign IP Ranges to Salesforce to Prevent Errors
text:
Learn to add Docusign IP ranges to your Salesforce organization's
Trusted IP Ranges.

You can add all Docusign IP ranges to your Salesforce
organization's Trusted IP Ranges.

Adding IP ranges ensures that all Docusign apps function without
issue in your Salesforce organization.

For more information, view the
following Salesforce documentation: [Set Trusted IP Ranges for Your Organization](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.security_networkaccess.htm&type=5).

Use
this procedure to add Docusign IP ranges to your Salesforce
organization's Trusted IP Ranges.

Note: Also use this procedure if your organization specifies [IP ranges on user profiles](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=platform.login_ip_ranges.htm&type=5).

1. Access the Network Access page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyODcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Aq48Vr2JBHWLuE32qrRWvfgzgI4XMC2aXospE6Vk1hc&_LANG=enus)and
      choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Network Access in the Quick
      Find field.

   ![A screenshot of the Salesforce Network Access page displays Trusted IP Ranges.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Salesforce-Network-Access-Settings.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyODcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Aq48Vr2JBHWLuE32qrRWvfgzgI4XMC2aXospE6Vk1hc&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Salesforce Network Access page displays Trusted IP Ranges.")
2. Add IP ranges from the Docusign Trust Center to
   your Trusted IP Ranges:

   - [eSignature for Salesforce ranges](https://www.docusign.com/trust/security/esignature)
   - [Gen and CLM for Salesforce ranges](https://www.docusign.com/trust/security/clm)
3. (Optional) [Add Salesforce IP ranges](https://ip-ranges.salesforce.com/ip-ranges.json) if your Salesforce organization is built on [Hyperforce code](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000388902&type=1).
4. Select Save.

You have completed the process. You have added Docusign IP ranges
to your Salesforce organization's Trusted IP Ranges section.

title: Resend the Activation Email for a Docusign for Salesforce User
heading_path: Resend the Activation Email for a Docusign for Salesforce User
text:
Learn how to resend the activation email to a Docusign CLM, Gen,
Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.

When you create a Docusign user in Salesforce, Docusign sends an activation email. The user
selects a link in the email and activates their Docusign user. If the user cannot find their
original activation email, you can send them another.

Use this procedure to resend a Docusign activation email to a Salesforce user:

1. Access the Docusign
   Users page as an administrator:
   1. Log on to your Docusign account.
   2. Select Settings at the top of the page.
   3. Select Users in the left column.
2. Select Actions next to a user and choose Resend
   Invitation from the dropdown menu.
3. (Optional) Access Code: Add a security code in the dialog box
   that opens. You must send the access code to the user.
4. Select OK.

You have completed the process. You have sent a new
activation email to the inactive user.

title: Add Gen and Send Functionality to Your Gen Template
heading_path: Add Gen and Send Functionality to Your Gen Template
text: Learn to add Gen and Send functionality to your Gen template.

title: Add Gen and Send Functionality to Your Gen Template
heading_path: Add Gen and Send Functionality to Your Gen Template
Before you begin
text:
Install Docusign Apps Launcher version 5.3 or higher.

[Create a Docusign
envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uce1617376465289.html&_LANG=enus).

[Create a Gen template with Microsoft
Word](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=lig1586134209279.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a Gen template using a Microsoft Word document.").

Important:

- Use a Word-based Gen template. You cannot link an [Agreement Cloud
  Editor](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=bcp1586134298132.html&_LANG=enus)-based Gen template to an envelope template.
- You can only link templates built on the same Salesforce object. Linking a
  Gen template and envelope template built on Opportunity
  works. Linking an Opportunity-based Gen template with
  an Account-based envelope template does not work.
- You can only link the following types of envelope templates: Default,
  Quick Send, Place Fields, and
  Send Now.

When you build a Gen and Send experience, your users can generate and send an
agreement with one click. When users generate an agreement, the linked envelope
template sending experience immediately opens. When all recipients complete the
signing process, the finished contract is saved to your Docusign
account and the Salesforce record.

Use this procedure to add Gen and Send functionality to your Gen template:

1. Add Gen and Send functionality to your Gen template:
   1. Select Enable the Send for Signature button, so generators
      can send generated documents out for signature.
   2. Select Launch automatically after
      generation.
   3. Select an Envelope Template to streamline sending for
      signature: Enter and select the name of an envelope
      template. ![A screenshot shows Launch automatically after generation selected and the Fontara NDA envelope template linked.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-shows-Launch-automatically-after-generation-selected-and-the-Fontara-Mobile-Sales-envelope-template-linked.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgyNjYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.ltmy0KsTK2skx12WOEwjQ_KZxozTQaG0JNgTuaGEstg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot shows Launch automatically after generation selected and the Fontara NDA envelope template linked.")
2. Select Save and Finish.

   The Create Button page closes.

You have completed the process. You have linked your Gen template
to an envelope template. When users generate an agreement, the linked envelope template
sending experience opens.

title: "Unable To Check Completion of Document Generation Argument Is Undefined" Displays When I Attempt To Preview
heading_path: "Unable To Check Completion of Document Generation Argument Is Undefined" Displays When I Attempt To Preview
text:
Learn to fix the error: "Unable to check completion of document generation. Argument is
undefined."

When I try to create a Gen template, I see an error in the Preview step.

The error displayed is: "Unable to check completion of document generation. Argument is
undefined."

title: "Unable To Check Completion of Document Generation Argument Is Undefined" Displays When I Attempt To Preview
heading_path: "Unable To Check Completion of Document Generation Argument Is Undefined" Displays When I Attempt To Preview
Resolution: Activate Your Expired Gen Trial as a Paid App
text:
Cause: Your 30-day Gen trial has expired.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

Contact your Docusign sales representative to activate Gen as a
paid application.

You have completed the process. You have activated your expired Gen
trial.

title: Enable or Disable the Gen Send for Signature Button
heading_path: Enable or Disable the Gen Send for Signature Button
text:
Learn to enable or disable the Send for Signature button that opens after users generate
agreements.

title: Enable or Disable the Gen Send for Signature Button
heading_path: Enable or Disable the Gen Send for Signature Button
Before you begin
text:
Install Docusign Apps Launcher v 5.3 or
higher.

When users generate agreements with Gen, a dialog box opens with a Send for
Signature button. Users can select this button to send generated
agreements for signature. You can choose to hide the Send for
Signature button if you don't want users to send generated
agreements. You can also choose to show the button if hidden.

Note: This feature is not a global setting for all Gen templates. You can
enable or disable any Gen template's Send for Signature
button.

Use this procedure to enable or disable the Gen Send for Signature button:

1. Enable or disable the Send for Signature button:

   - Enable: Select the Enable the Send for
     Signature button, so generators can send generated documents out for
     signature check box. Users see the Send for Signature
     button after generating an agreement.
   - Disable: Uncheck the Enable the Send for
     Signature button, so generators can send generated documents out for
     signature check box. Users do not see a Send for
     Signature button after generating an agreement.
2. Select Save and Finish.

You have completed the process. You have enabled or disabled the
Send for Signature button that displays after users generate
agreements.

title: Gen Word Template Options: Select Source Step
heading_path: Gen Word Template Options: Select Source Step
text:
Learn how to change the data source of a Gen template.

The Select Source step has only one customizable option: the Salesforce
data source. The data source is the Salesforce object that you select at the start of
Gen template creation. After you have chosen your data source, you can go back and
change it in the Select Source step.

This guide demonstrates the options that you can use in the Select Source step of Gen template
creation: ﻿

- [Access the Gen Word template Select Source
  step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ktj1621660808301.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to access the Select Source step in a Word-based Gen template.")
- [Change the Salesforce data source of your
  Word-based Gen template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=uxx1586134338952.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to change the data source of a Gen template.")

title: Delete a Gen Template
heading_path: Delete a Gen Template
text:
Learn to delete a Gen template.

When you delete a Gen template, remove that
template's buttons from your Salesforce layouts. Use this procedure to delete a Gen
template:

1. Select Docusign Gen Templates.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0NDYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.inF-Q2GVKaK96PoMm3HPSpOkEeTXsUxU2v-s_no5MxI&_LANG=enus) next to a Gen template and choose Delete
   from the dropdown menu.

   A dialog box displays: Are you sure you want to delete this Docusign
   Gen Template?
3. Select Delete.

   A dialog box displays: Docusign Gen Template was
   deleted.

You have completed the task. You have deleted a Gen
template. Remove this template's buttons from your Salesforce layouts.

title: Learn About Using Docusign Gen With Docusign CLM
heading_path: Learn About Using Docusign Gen With Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about using Docusign Gen with Docusign
CLM.

The Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps contains CLM, Gen, Negotiate, and
eSignature for Salesforce.

CLM customers can build templates with the CLM Document Generation tool
and the Docusign Gen application. Each application has different
capabilities that you can use when building CLM document generation templates.

Important: CLM customers cannot downgrade to a Gen-only
license or assign Gen-only permissions.

title: Learn About Using Docusign Gen With Docusign CLM
heading_path: Learn About Using Docusign Gen With Docusign CLM
When should I use Gen?
text:
- For simple use cases, like billing and invoices
- When you have upgraded from Gen to CLM and want to continue using your Gen
  templates
- When you want to use Gen for Salesforce Billing
- When you do not want generated documents saved in CLM folders

title: Learn About Using Docusign Gen With Docusign CLM
heading_path: Learn About Using Docusign Gen With Docusign CLM
When should I use CLM Document Generation?
text:
- For advanced use cases
- When you want generated documents saved in CLM folders
- When you want to use generated documents in CLM advanced workflows

title: Learn About Using Docusign Gen With Docusign CLM
heading_path: Learn About Using Docusign Gen With Docusign CLM
What can CLM users do in Gen?
text:
- Use the CLM Document Generation page on the
  Docusign Setup tab
- Create, add, edit, and delete Gen templates
- Access both their Gen templates and their CLM Document Generation templates
- Add Gen custom buttons to Salesforce page layouts
- Generate documents using a Gen custom button in Salesforce

title: Learn About Using SOQL-Based Table Filtering and Sorting in Gen
heading_path: Learn About Using SOQL-Based Table Filtering and Sorting in Gen
text:
Learn about inserting Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) code into Gen
templates.

Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) searches your organization’s Salesforce data
for specific information. You can insert
Salesforce SOQL code into the TableRow fields of Gen templates .

Using SOQL code in tables creates custom rules for filtering and sorting data. You
can only use sorting and filtering with child relationship fields.

Example: An Account has two contacts, each with two cases (four cases
total for that account.) A user generates an agreement for that account, setting a
limit of one for each case. Note: Limit controls the number
of results a child relationship field can add to a generated agreement.

Result: Docusign adds one Case to the generated
agreement, even though more than one contact is present.

title: Learn About Using SOQL-Based Table Filtering and Sorting in Gen
heading_path: Learn About Using SOQL-Based Table Filtering and Sorting in Gen
SOQL for Gen use cases
text:
Note: The asterisk indicates where the option to filter/sort
exists.

| Object Relation | Example |
| --- | --- |
| 1 : Many\* | Opportunity >  Opportunity Contact Roles\* |
| 1 : Many\* : 1 | Opportunity >  Opportunity Contact Roles\* >  Contact |
| 1 : Many\* : 1 : 1 | Opportunity >  Opportunity Contact Roles\* >  Contact >  Reports To |
| 1 : Many\* : Many\* | Account  >  Contacts\* >  Cases\* >  Owner Note: This example starts from the Account object. |

title: Learn About Using SOQL-Based Table Filtering and Sorting in Gen
heading_path: Learn About Using SOQL-Based Table Filtering and Sorting in Gen
SOQL for Gen Fields
text:
- [SOQL Filtering (WHERE)](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.soql_sosl.meta/soql_sosl/sforce_api_calls_soql_select_conditionexpression.htm): Enter the Salesforce SOQL
  code to filter results by a certain field.
- [SOQL Sorting (ORDER BY)](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.soql_sosl.meta/soql_sosl/sforce_api_calls_soql_select_orderby.htm): Enter the Salesforce SOQL
  code to sort results by a certain field.
- [SOQL Sorting (LIMIT)](https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.soql_sosl.meta/soql_sosl/sforce_api_calls_soql_select_limit.htm): Enter the number of results
  that you want, from 1 through 2000.

![A screenshot displays the Gen table options filtering, sorting, and limit fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Gen-table-options-filtering-sorting-and-limit-fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.xlYih-G3l29iHUzLZcTpPPCSIW7-p9Vy3gZh8BTnRSE&_LANG=enus)

title: Upload Documents, Change File Names, or Add Rules to a Gen Template
heading_path: Upload Documents, Change File Names, or Add Rules to a Gen Template
text:
Learn to edit the generated file name, add files, or add document rules to your Gen
template.

Use this procedure to edit the generated file name, add files,
or add document rules to your Gen template:

1. Change any file name settings:

   - Generated File Name: Edit the text for the beginning of all
     generated document names.
   - File Suffix: Set the second part of all generated document
     names.
   - File Format: Choose Microsoft Word or PDF as the file type
     for all generated documents.
2. Upload Files: Add documents to the Gen template.
3. + Add Rule: [Create a Document Rule in a Word-Based Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=djo1614894478713.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create a document rule to enable conditional document generation in a Gen template:").
4. Bulk Document Creation: Select Generate all documents
   in this configuration with one click to merge all template documents into a
   single generated document.
5. Select Save and Close and select Save on
   the Do you want to save changes? dialog box.

You have completed the process. You have changed one or
more components of your Gen template on the Upload Documents page.

title: Use the Docusign Envelopes Tab to View the Details of an Envelope
heading_path: Use the Docusign Envelopes Tab to View the Details of an Envelope
text:
Learn how to view the details of envelopes sent from your Salesforce
records.

Docusign envelopes are bundles of information sent to recipients for
electronic signature. These include documents, recipients, and Docusign and Salesforce fields. You can use the Docusign
Envelopes page to view the details of envelopes sent from Salesforce
records.

Envelope entries include these details:

- Docusign Envelope name
- Email Subject
- Owner
- Salesforce Source ID
- Email Message
- Docusign envelope ID
- Sent status
- Remind signers via email
- Send reminder in N day(s)
- Remind every N day(s) until complete
- Expire/void Envelope
- Expire/void envelope in N day(s)
- Warn of expiration in N day(s) prior
- Created By
- Last Modified By

Use this procedure to view the details of a Docusign envelope in your
Salesforce organization:

1. Select the Docusign Envelopes tab.

   The Docusign Envelope page opens.
2. Select an envelope from the Docusign Envelope
   column.

   The Docusign Envelope details page opens for that
   item.

You have completed the task. You have viewed the details of
an envelope sent from a Salesforce record.

title: Customize a Dynamic Table in an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
heading_path: Customize a Dynamic Table in an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
text:
Learn to customize dynamic table content in an Agreement Cloud Editor
template.

You can customize dynamic table content in an Agreement Cloud Editor template.

You can use the following actions:

- Change font size and style
- Add rows
- Add fill colors to cells
- Add, remove, or reorder Salesforce tags
- Delete rows or cells

When you create a new table with the Agreement Cloud Editor, the table toolbar displays
below the table.

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | Add a row above |
|  | Add a row below |
|  | Add, remove, or reorder Salesforce tags |
|  | Add color to a cell |
|  | Delete a selected row or cell |

title: Clear User Settings Using the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Clear User Settings Using the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn to fix the CUSTOM\_SETTINGS\_LIMIT\_EXCEEDED issue with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.

Administrators can use the Docusign Apps Launcher
TroubleShooting tool to fix issues with Docusign apps. The TroubleShooting tool is available in Docusign Apps Launcher version 2.5 and higher. Salesforce
administrators with Docusign administrator permissions can use it.

Salesforce Experience Cloud users sometimes see this error message when sending
envelopes: CUSTOM\_SETTINGS\_LIMIT\_EXCEEDED.

Users with too many stored access tokens see this error message. Access tokens can be stored
in custom settings without users or administrators knowing. Excessive access tokens
can violate Salesforce storage limits and prevent Experience Cloud users from
sending Docusign envelopes.

Administrators can use the Clear User Settings feature of the
TroubleShooting tool to delete access tokens for all users. Clear User
Settings deletes cached access tokens to free up storage.

Follow this procedure to use the Clear User Settings feature:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzOTgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.9tqhDi59JLDVYYaSIMq4wqaJlH6jnEIqxdWQaWu324c&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting in the Label
   column.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/oas1598330401772.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzOTgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.9tqhDi59JLDVYYaSIMq4wqaJlH6jnEIqxdWQaWu324c&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview. The TroubleShooting page opens.
4. Clear User Settings: Select
   Execute.

   A message opens: Successfully enqueued job. Check Apex Batch Jobs
   page and Docusign Logs for results.

You have completed the process. You have cleared the cached access
tokens. Salesforce Experience Cloud users can send envelopes again.

title: Exception for Gen Template SOQL Sorting Rules
heading_path: Exception for Gen Template SOQL Sorting Rules
text:
Learn about the scenario where sorting works differently than the standard SOL sorting
rules.

Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) searches your organization’s Salesforce data
for specific information. You can insert Salesforce SOQL code into the TableRow
fields of Gen templates.

Using SOQL code in tables creates custom rules for filtering and sorting data. You
can only use sorting and filtering with child relationship fields.

There is one scenario where sorting works differently than [standard SOQL sorting](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=mrr1588510303522.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about inserting Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) code into Gen templates.").

title: Exception for Gen Template SOQL Sorting Rules
heading_path: Exception for Gen Template SOQL Sorting Rules
Example
text:
An administrator wants to add SOQL code for a Gen field with an Account >  Contacts >  Cases >  Status relationship:

![A screenshot displays a Gen template field with an Account, Contacts, Cases, Status relationship.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-a-Gen-template-field-with-an-Account-Contacts-Cases-Status-relationship.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1MzcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.t2BIAesPBs2iw0AltE0FLU_oIu5XRg2RFblVf3lLbj0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Gen template field with an Account, Contacts, Cases, Status relationship.")

The Cases data field has an ORDER BY
value of Status DESC NULLS LAST:

![A screenshot displays the table options dialog box with Status desc nulls last in the order by field.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-table-options-dialog-box-with-Status-desc-nulls-last-in-the-order-by-field.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1MzcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.t2BIAesPBs2iw0AltE0FLU_oIu5XRg2RFblVf3lLbj0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the table options dialog box with Status desc nulls last in the order by field.")

title: Exception for Gen Template SOQL Sorting Rules
heading_path: Exception for Gen Template SOQL Sorting Rules
Expected result
text:
Docusign sorts the output by Status, not
ID.

title: Exception for Gen Template SOQL Sorting Rules
heading_path: Exception for Gen Template SOQL Sorting Rules
Actual result
text:
Docusign sorts the output by ID and not
Status.

![A screenshot displays SOQL results sorted by ID instead of Status.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genwordsortoutput.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1MzcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.t2BIAesPBs2iw0AltE0FLU_oIu5XRg2RFblVf3lLbj0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays SOQL results sorted by ID instead of Status.")

There is no workaround for this scenario in the Gen user interface. You can rearrange
the generated content in Microsoft Word tables.

title: Change the Font Size and Style of a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Change the Font Size and Style of a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
You can change the font size and style of the Word documents used in Gen templates.

You can change the font size and style of Word
documents used in Gen templates. To make changes, open the documents in Microsoft Word. When
you are done making changes, re-upload the documents to the Gen template.

1. Select a document name.

   The document preview page opens in a new tab or window.
2. Update your Word document:
   1. Select Download.
   2. Open the document and use Microsoft Word to update the font or style.![A screenshot displays Gen anchor text fields in bold font.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/tagstylechanges.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1NjAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.m_8rWQISXeXK0zhmk7A6t0lz9hM8xwFRpIwX7jxhgNE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Gen anchor text fields in bold font.")
   3. Save the changes in Word and return to the Gen Upload
      Documents page.
3. [Re-upload the document to your Gen
   template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sqp1586134339354.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to update your Gen template Word documents.").
4. Select Save and Close and select Save on
   the Do you want to save changes? dialog box.

You have completed the process. Generated agreements
display the changes that you made in Microsoft Word.

title: Learn About Adding Conditional Logic to a Gen Template Word Document
heading_path: Learn About Adding Conditional Logic to a Gen Template Word Document
text:
Learn about adding conditional logic blocks to Gen template Word documents.

You can add conditional logic blocks to Gen template Anchor Text Tags. Conditional logic
creates "if-then" statements that add or exclude text from a generated agreement.

You can use conditional logic to create one Word document that can generate different content
for different customers. For example, you can add conditional logic that hides new customer
language when generating existing customer agreements.

In the Gen template interface, you use document to add conditional logic in a Gen template.
You can only add Match or Does Not Match conditions
in the Gen template user interface.

To use more advanced logic, you can add conditional logic to Microsoft Word documents used in
Gen templates. You can enter text in the document, paste data fields from Salesforce, or a
combination of both.

title: Learn About Adding Conditional Logic to a Gen Template Word Document
heading_path: Learn About Adding Conditional Logic to a Gen Template Word Document
Conditional logic example
text:
Your customers from the state of New York have special legal restrictions on the product
you make. You create a Gen template for all of your customer agreements. You add conditional
logic to your Gen template for your New York customers. This logic displays New York legal
clauses when a record's Billing State/Province field equals New York.
This logic hides the New York legal clauses when the Billing
State/Province field does not contain New York: ![A screenshot displays conditional logic tags surrounding legal text for New York customers.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Conditional-text.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzMDIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.EA0mTmD5_3I5Ib9lFveipHIfbA5ulT63JMfK7Ufn75A&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays conditional logic tags surrounding legal text for New York customers.")

title: Learn About Adding Conditional Logic to a Gen Template Word Document
heading_path: Learn About Adding Conditional Logic to a Gen Template Word Document
Sample conditional logic blocks
text:
- Match: Conditional logic that only displays text for new
  customers:

  `<# <Conditional Select=""/Opportunity/Type""
  Match=""New Customer"" /> #> Welcome, new
  customer! <# <Content Select=""/Opportunity/Payment_Terms__c""
  /> #> your payment terms <# <EndConditional /> #>`
- Does Not Match: Conditional logic that only displays text for
  existing customers:

  `<# <Conditional Select="/Opportunity/Type"
  NotMatch="New Customer" /> #> Hello, valued
  customer! <# <Content Select=""/Opportunity/Payment_Terms__c""
  /> #> your payment terms <# <EndConditional /> #>`

title: Learn About Adding Conditional Logic to a Gen Template Word Document
heading_path: Learn About Adding Conditional Logic to a Gen Template Word Document
Using conditional logic in Gen templates
text:
Use the following guidelines when using conditional logic in Word documents:

- You always start conditional logic blocks with a
  <Conditional> tag and end with an
  <EndConditional> tag.
- You can place <Conditional> and
  <EndConditional> tags on the same line, or split them over
  multiple lines of text.
- You can create a simple string comparison by using the
  <Conditional> tag with Select &
  Match/NotMatch logic.
- You can create complex boolean (true/false) expressions in conditional blocks by using
  <Conditional> with Select & Test
  attributes.
- You can add conditional logic to a Gen template by downloading the template's Word
  document. You then add the text to the document and [re-upload the document to the Gen template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sqp1586134339354.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to update your Gen template Word documents.").

title: Upgrade Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Upgrade Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn how to upgrade the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce
organization.

Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce is a package that includes the following
Docusign applications:

- [Docusign eSignature for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N30000001taX4EAI&tab=e)
- [Docusign CLM for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N30000004e3VUEAY&tab=e)
- [Docusign Gen for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N3A00000FeGX6UAN&tab=e)

When you install any of these apps, Docusign installs the complete Docusign Apps
Launcher package. Docusign regularly releases updated versions of the Apps Launcher
package that include product enhancements and bug fixes. If you have an older
version of Apps Launcher installed, you can upgrade the package by visiting the
Salesforce AppExchange.

Use this procedure to upgrade the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your
Salesforce organization:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, choose a Docusign app from the
   AppExchange:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxNDAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.hhfCvmKdKQFysufs4FxEf1k3SslV55eu7vZDeEUylSQ&_LANG=enus) and choose
      View All.
   2. Select Visit AppExchange >  Go to AppExchange.
   3. Enter `Docusign
      eSignature`,`Gen`, or
      `CLM` in the search field.
   4. Select an app and choose Get it Now when the app
      page displays.
2. Log in to the AppExchange with Salesforce administrator credentials:

   Note: Enter production credentials even if installing in a
   sandbox organization. These credentials are used to access the
   AppExchange.

   1. Select Log In on the LET'S GET
      STARTED page.
   2. Select Salesforce on the Hello
      Trailblazer! dialog box that displays.
   3. Enter Salesforce production administrator credentials on the page that
      displays and select Log In.

      The Where do you want to install this
      package? dialog box displays.
3. Confirm where to install the package:
   1. Install in This Org: Select Install
      Here.
   2. Select I have read and agree to the terms and
      conditions.
   3. Select Confirm and Install.

      The page refreshes. The Salesforce login page displays.
4. Install the Apps Launcher package:
   1. Enter production or sandbox administrator credentials and select
      Log In.
   2. Select Install for All Users.

      Note: If you want to choose Install for Admins Only, or Install
      for Specific Profiles, see this topic about [how Salesforce applies app permissions](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.distribution_installing_packages.htm&type=5).
   3. Select Upgrade.
   4. Select Done when the page refreshes.

You have completed the process. You upgraded the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps. Your Salesforce organization
contains the latest version of Docusign eSignature for Salesforce,
CLM, CLM Essentials, and Gen. You receive an email notification when Salesforce
completes the installation.

title: Learn About Using Gen Template Filtering to Import Only the Data You Need
heading_path: Learn About Using Gen Template Filtering to Import Only the Data You Need
text:
Learn about using XML code to build custom tables in Word that filter Salesforce data.

You can use XML code to build custom tables in Word that filter Salesforce data for
Gen agreements. You can use filtering to remove unwanted data from a record and
import only the results you want.

title: Learn About Using Gen Template Filtering to Import Only the Data You Need
heading_path: Learn About Using Gen Template Filtering to Import Only the Data You Need
Filter Tag example
text:
You have a catalog with multiple product types. You want to generate agreements from
Salesforce records that only display a certain product type. You have a
Type picklist with values for Software, Services, and
Hardware.

You build a Gen template with Microsoft Word. You add a Word table with custom XML
filtering to import only Software results.

title: Learn About Using Gen Template Filtering to Import Only the Data You Need
heading_path: Learn About Using Gen Template Filtering to Import Only the Data You Need
Sample Filter tag expressions
text:
The following are sample expressions for filter tags:

- Only display results when one field matches certain values:

  `<#
  <TableRow Select="//Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems/Type[text() =
  ‘Software’ or text() = 'Services']/.." /> #>`
- Only display results when two fields match certain values:

  `<#
  <TableRow Select="/Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems[contains
  (./Product_Family__c,'Services') and ./Product_Rating__c/text() = '5']"
  /> #>`
- Only display results when a currency field isn’t negative:

  `<#
  <TableRow Select="//Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems/
  UnitPrice[not(contains(.,'-'))]/.." /> #>`

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
text:
Learn how the Docusign Login permission set helps manage Docusign user access in Salesforce.

The Docusign Login permission set helps you manage Docusign access for your Salesforce users. Users with Docusign Login membership can authorize Docusign
app access themselves. This permission set lets you bypass the Docusign User Management tool and manage user activation with
Salesforce controls.

Important:

- The Docusign Login permission set is available in Docusign Apps Launcher v3.5 and higher.
- When you add an existing Docusign user to this permission set, Docusign imports the user's permissions.
- Administrators can use the Docusign Login permission set with their
  single sign-on (SSO) identity provider and Docusign
  just-in-time provisioning. Just-in-time provisioning lets users create their
  own Docusign accounts in Salesforce by entering their SSO
  credentials.

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
When to use the Docusign Login permission set:
text:
Use the Docusign Login permission set in the following
scenarios:

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
Administrator wants to use Salesforce controls
text:
A Salesforce administrator feels more comfortable using settings for permission sets than the
Docusign User Management tool. The administrator uses the Docusign Login permission set to give Docusign
app access to Salesforce users.

Note: This method only works for Salesforce users who already have an
active Docusign user in the connected Docusign
account.

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
Administrator disconnects or refreshes a Salesforce organization
text:
An administrator refreshes their Salesforce organization or disconnects and reconnects from
their Docusign account. Docusign deletes all
users not added to the Docusign Login permission set, and the administrator must
re-add them. Docusign
Docusign preserves all
users added to the Docusign Login permission set. The
administrator does not need to re-add these users after the disconnection or
refresh.

Note: Salesforce does not preserve Docusign users when you uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package.
Salesforce deletes the Docusign Login permission set when you
uninstall the package.

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
Users already exist and have permissions in the connected Docusign account
text:
A Docusign administrator has already created users and assigned permissions
in the connected Docusign account. The administrator adds
Salesforce users to the Docusign Login permission set. The users
do not receive activation emails. They activate their Docusign
access directly on Salesforce records by selecting a Docusign
component or custom button.

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
Users log on with single sign-on (SSO)
text:
An organization uses a single sign-on (SSO) provider for user logins. The administrator adds
Salesforce users to the Docusign Login permission set. Users do
not receive an activation email. Users select a Docusign component
or button on a record and use SSO credentials to authorize Docusign access.

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
When to use Docusign User Management:
text: Use Docusign User Management in the following scenarios:

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
Users do not exist in the connected Docusign account
text:
An administrator wants to add Docusign app access to a user that does not
exist in the connected Docusign account. In this case, the
administrator cannot use Docusign Login and must use User
Management.

title: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: User Access Management in Salesforce With the Docusign Login Permission Set
Users exist in the connected Docusign account but lack permissions
text:
An administrator wants to add Docusign app access to an existing user from
the connected Docusign account. The existing Docusign user lacks needed permissions, like
Generator or eSignature User. In
this case, the administrator cannot use Docusign Login and must
use User Management.

title: Use the Disconnect Link to Disconnect and Reconnect Docusign and Salesforce
heading_path: Use the Disconnect Link to Disconnect and Reconnect Docusign and Salesforce
text:
Use this procedure to disconnect and reconnect your Docusign account
from your Salesforce organization.

Occasionally, you must disconnect and reconnect your Docusign
connection. The following are reasons to disconnect and reconnect:

- You want to connect your Salesforce organization to a different Docusign account
- An administrator deleted your Docusign integration user
- [Salesforce applied enhanced domains to your organization](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000393816&type=1)

Warning:
Docusign recommends that you
[disconnect your Docusign account
and preserve user settings with the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting tool](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=oks1610512362945.html&_LANG=enus). This option preserves your Docusign users and settings. After reconnecting, all Docusign users and permissions remain.

If you want to disconnect and reconnect without preserving user settings, you can
use the Disconnect link on the Docusign Setup page.
After reconnecting, you must rebuild all Docusign users and settings.

Use this procedure to disconnect and reconnect without preserving users and settings:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, [open Docusign Apps Launcher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Select the Docusign Setup tab.
3. Select Disconnect. ![A screenshot displays the Disconnect link highlighted on the Docusign Setup page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Disconnect-link-highlighted-on-the-Docusign-Setup-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1NDMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.HlUVHeTFdq3_cbTANwUvaexeX4nU-bH482kLBMeIDRQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Disconnect link highlighted on the Docusign Setup page.")

   The Disconnect Account and Remove Users dialog box displays.
4. Select Disconnect.

   Warning: Disconnecting Docusign from
   Salesforce disables Docusign for all Salesforce users. Disconnecting
   also deletes all Docusign user configurations. When you reconnect, you
   must rebuild all users and settings.

   The page refreshes. The Connect Docusign to Salesforce page
   displays.
5. [Reconnect Docusign and
   Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.").

You have completed the process. You have updated the
connection between your Salesforce organization and your Docusign account.

title: I Want to Know More About How Gen Template Naming Conventions Work
heading_path: I Want to Know More About How Gen Template Naming Conventions Work
text:
Learn about the file name logic in the Gen template Upload Documents step.

I am building a Gen template and am confused by the File Suffix
section of the Upload Documents step.

I want to know more about the following terms:

- Generated File Name
- File Suffix
- File Format

title: I Want to Know More About How Gen Template Naming Conventions Work
heading_path: I Want to Know More About How Gen Template Naming Conventions Work
Resolution: Use the Following Example to Understand Gen Template File Naming Conventions
text:
Cause: Some confusion exists about what Gen template file naming
terms mean.

Docusign uses naming conventions to make unique file names for
agreements generated by the same template.

Standard agreement names only include your organization name and the file format, like
.DOCX, or .PDF. You can use naming conventions to add the following information to your
agreement names:

- Main Data Source Name
- Date
- Main Data Source Name and Date
- Microsoft Word
- PDF

This custom information makes it easier to see when your agreements were created and
from what object.

Use this example to understand naming conventions better:

1. Build a Gen template and advance to the Upload Documents
   step.
2. Configure the following settings on your Gen template's Upload
   Documents step:
   1. Generated File Name: Enter Fresh Software
      NDA.
   2. File Suffix: Select Main Data Source Name and
      Date.
   3. File Format: Keep Microsoft
      Word.

      ![A screenshot displays the Upload Your Word Templates page with File Suffix highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genfilesuffix.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NTMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.jApsmUW60o9_ifbMKyQ1VrUIxHmdunsS5jckkSB__r4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Upload Your Word Templates page with File Suffix highlighted.")
3. Finish building your Gen template and add a custom button to the layout.

   You have completed the process.

   A user generates an agreement for an Opportunity named Edge
   Communications on February 7, 2025. The generated
   agreement name is Fresh Software NDA\_Edge
   Communications\_1-11-2020.docx.

Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1NDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.CGast1LQpH__x6pCbSh1vwBpa3NquZybBz_k3g-k7Q4&_LANG=enus) next to a Gen template and choose
