title: Access the Gen Word Template Upload Documents Step
heading_path: Access the Gen Word Template Upload Documents Step
text:
Learn to access the Upload Documents step in a Gen Word template.

Follow this procedure to access the Upload Documents step of a Gen Word template:

Edit the Gen template Upload Documents page as a
Salesforce administrator:

1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/applaunchergrid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgyODMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.poq4cNruPQgUiz6LA0J_xsOrHWQ2zLAMcQe5kpkhlRs&_LANG=enus) and choose View

Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgyODMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.poq4cNruPQgUiz6LA0J_xsOrHWQ2zLAMcQe5kpkhlRs&_LANG=enus) next to a Gen template and choose

title: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
heading_path: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
text:
Learn to use group tags in Word-based Gen templates to merge Salesforce data into
tables.

You can use the Group tag to create separate tables for each product grouped under a
main category.

title: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
heading_path: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
Group tag
text:
The Group tag is represented by the `<Group>` XML tag, paired
with the `<EndGroup>` tag.

You can use Group tags to create templates that adjust when your organization adds
and removes products and services.

title: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
heading_path: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
Group tag example
text:
You create a Salesforce category of Vehicles with line items
for cars, trucks, and
vans.

You add the `<Group>` tag to your Gen template to create separate tables
for cars, trucks, and vans in generated agreements.

Your organization stops selling vans, but starts selling motorcycles and minivans.
When users generate agreements, the `<Group>` tag removes the
vans table, and only displays tables for motorcycles and minivans.

title: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
heading_path: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
Using Table Row tags in Gen templates
text:
Use the following guidelines when creating Group tags:

- Group tag data use an XPath selector.
- Docusign adds output using the context of the selected
  data.
- Place content to be repeated between the `<Group>` and
  `<EndGroup>` tags.
- You can place any merge tag in a Group tag.
- The Select attribute uses an XPath selector to determine
  what data to select.
- The By attribute specifies how to categorize results of
  the Select attribute.

title: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
heading_path: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
Supported Group tag XPath
text:
You can use the following XPath in Group tags:

- `<GroupName>` : This tag adds the name of the group
  specified by the By attribute.
- `<GroupItems>`: This tag adds all group items specified by
  the By attribute.

title: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
heading_path: Learn About Using Group Tags in Gen to Merge Salesforce Data Into Tables
Group tag attributes
text:
You can use the following attributes in Group tags:

| Attributes | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Select | XPath that selects data from an XML document |
| By | XPath that specifies how to categorize the results from the Select attribute |

title: Reset the Password for a Docusign for Salesforce User
heading_path: Reset the Password for a Docusign for Salesforce User
text:
Learn to reset the password of a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for
Salesforce user.

Use this procedure to reset the password of a Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature
for Salesforce user:

1. Access the Docusign
   Users page as an administrator:
   1. Log on to your Docusign account.
   2. Select Settings at the top of the page.
   3. Select Users in the left column.
2. Select Actions next to a user and choose Reset
   Password from the dropdown menu.

   A message opens: A reset password email has been sent to (user
   name).

You have completed the process. You sent a password
reset link to the user's email address.

title: I Cannot Uninstall the Docusign Gen Application
heading_path: I Cannot Uninstall the Docusign Gen Application
text:
Learn to remove the Gen application by uninstalling Docusign Apps
Launcher and all dependencies.

I am trying to uninstall the Gen application. I cannot find it in Installed Packages.

title: I Cannot Uninstall the Docusign Gen Application
heading_path: I Cannot Uninstall the Docusign Gen Application
Resolution: Uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher Package
text:
Cause: Docusign Gen is not a standalone app. It
does not display in the Installed Packages list.

To uninstall Gen, uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package. Remove
all dependencies before uninstalling.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Remove users from Docusign
   Apps Launcher permission sets](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=pnw1623705527946.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove all users from Docusign permission sets.").
2. [Remove Docusign Apps Launcher
   components from Salesforce layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=uxu1623708462949.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove all Docusign Apps Launcher components from Salesforce layouts.").
3. [Remove Docusign Apps Launcher
   custom buttons from your Salesforce layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=cif1623740064281.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove Docusign Apps Launcher custom buttons from your Salesforce layouts.").
4. [Uninstall the Docusign Apps
   Launcher package from your Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ump1623741000284.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package from your Salesforce organization.").

   You have completed the process. You have removed the Docusign Apps Launcher package and all dependencies.

title: Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce
organization.

Docusign Apps Launcher for
Salesforce is a package that includes the following Docusign apps:

- [Docusign eSignature for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N30000001taX4EAI&tab=e)
- [Docusign CLM for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N30000004e3VUEAY&tab=e)
- [Docusign Gen for Salesforce](https://appexchange.salesforce.com/appxListingDetail?listingId=a0N3A00000FeGX6UAN&tab=e)

When you install any of these apps, Docusign installs the complete Docusign Apps Launcher package.

Use this procedure to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce
organization:

1. As a Salesforce administrator, choose a Docusign app from the
   AppExchange:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxMzcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.2zf0ksJhuvREuHwb_2KLagrlP6ln03VZeSw1FIb4rqE&_LANG=enus) and choose View
      All.
   2. Select Visit AppExchange >  Go to AppExchange.
   3. Enter `Docusign eSignature`, `Gen`, or
      `CLM` in the search field.
   4. Select an app and choose Get it Now when the app page
      displays.
2. Log in to the AppExchange with Salesforce administrator credentials:

   Note: Enter production credentials even if installing in a sandbox
   organization. These credentials are used to access the AppExchange.

   1. Select Log In on the LET'S GET STARTED
      page.
   2. Select Salesforce on the Hello
      Trailblazer! dialog box that displays.
   3. Enter Salesforce production administrator credentials on the page that displays and
      select Log In.

      The Where do you want to install this package? dialog
      box displays.
3. Choose where to install the package:
   1. Select Install in Production or Install in
      Sandbox.
   2. Select I have read and agree to the terms and
      conditions.
   3. Select Confirm and Install.

      The page refreshes. The Salesforce login page displays.
4. Install the Apps Launcher package:
   1. Enter production or sandbox administrator credentials and select Log
      In.
   2. Select Install for All
      Users.

      Note: If you want to choose ​Install for Admins Only, or ​Install for Specific Profiles​, see this topic about [how Salesforce applies app permissions](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.distribution_installing_packages.htm&type=5).
   3. Select Install or Upgrade.
   4. Select Done when the page refreshes.

You have completed the process. You have installed the
Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps. This package includes Docusign eSignature, CLM, and Gen. You receive an email notification when
Salesforce completes the installation.

title: Disconnect Your Docusign Account and Preserve User Settings with the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Disconnect Your Docusign Account and Preserve User Settings with the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn how to disconnect Docusign from Salesforce while preserving user
settings with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.

Occasionally you must disconnect and reconnect your Docusign connection. The following are reasons to disconnect and
reconnect:

- You want to connect your Salesforce organization to a different Docusign account
- An administrator deleted your Docusign integration
  user
- Salesforce applied [enhanced domains](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000393816&type=1) to your organization

You can use the Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting tool to reset your
integration safely.

Follow this procedure to use the Disconnect Account and Preserve User
Settings feature:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzOTEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.ytyiAuXFd0dSfYXk6DQ1XOhxKCQEzqyNkDAqNi1g5rM&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/TroubleShooting-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzOTEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.ytyiAuXFd0dSfYXk6DQ1XOhxKCQEzqyNkDAqNi1g5rM&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.

   The TroubleShooting page opens.
4. Disconnect Account and Preserve User Settings: Select
   Execute.

   A message opens: Successfully logged out from Docusign account. The
   Troubleshooting page displays the message: No Docusign Account
   connected.

You have completed the process. You have disconnected your Docusign account from your Salesforce organization. You can [re-connect Salesforce and Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus) or
connect to a different Docusign account.

title: Remove Users From the Docusign Login Permission Set
heading_path: Remove Users From the Docusign Login Permission Set
text:
Learn to remove users from the Docusign Login permission
set.

The Docusign
 Login permission set helps you manage Docusign access for your Salesforce users. Users with Docusign Login membership can authorize Docusign
app access themselves.

Use this procedure to remove users from the Docusign Login permission set:

1. Select Docusign Login.
2. Select Manage Assignments.
3. Select the check box next to each user that you want to remove.
4. Select Remove Assignments.

   The Are you sure you want to remove these users from this
   permission set? dialog box opens.
5. Select OK.
6. Select Done.

You have completed the process. You have removed one or
more users from the Docusign Login permission set.

title: Docusign Creates Gen Templates That I Didn't Save
heading_path: Docusign Creates Gen Templates That I Didn't Save
text:
Learn to delete unwanted Gen templates.

I started building a Gen template but decided I didn't need it. I closed the browser tab
without saving the template.

When I opened the Gen Templates page again, Docusign had saved the
template that I discarded.

title: Docusign Creates Gen Templates That I Didn't Save
heading_path: Docusign Creates Gen Templates That I Didn't Save
Resolution: Delete Unwanted Gen Templates
text:
Cause: Docusign auto-saves your Gen templates to
ensure that you don't lose work. Even if you close a template without selecting
Save and Close
Docusign saves it.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Select Docusign Gen Templates.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NDYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.lLBXgsXd6t0tB3_3QpeTCFEXx1SlyuRwLVvg3wzEm8Y&_LANG=enus) next to a Gen template and choose Delete
   from the dropdown menu.

   A dialog box displays: Are you sure you want to delete this
   Docusign Gen Template?
3. Select Delete.

   A dialog box displays: Docusign Gen Template was
   deleted.

   You have completed the task. You have deleted a Gen template. Remove this
   template's buttons from your Salesforce layouts.

title: Getting Started With Gen
heading_path: Getting Started With Gen
text:

organization:

Components give your users a space to view and interact with Docusign content on Salesforce records.")

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Learn the Docusign Apps Launcher requirements for browsers, operating
systems, and devices.

You can use the Docusign for Salesforce apps with the following browsers,
operating systems, and devices:

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
Supported browsers
text:
Important: Salesforce and Docusign do not
support any version of Microsoft Internet Explorer.

| Browser | Supported version | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Google Chrome | Latest | n/a |
| Safari (Apple) | Latest | Desktop-only. Salesforce does not support the mobile version of Safari. Enabling third-party cookies is required. |
| Microsoft Edge | Latest | n/a |
| Mozilla Firefox | Latest | n/a |

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
Supported operating systems
text:
| Operating system | Version |
| --- | --- |
| Microsoft Windows | Windows 10 or newer |
| MacOS (Apple desktop) | 10.14 (Mojave) or newer |
| iOS (Apple mobile) | iOS 14.x or newer |
| Android OS | Android 8.0 or newer |

title: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Supported Devices, Operating Systems, and Browsers for Docusign Apps Launcher
Salesforce mobile app requirements
text:
Required settings: Configure the following settings to use Docusign in the Salesforce mobile app:

- Enable third-party cookies in the [Salesforce mobile app settings](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.salesforce_app.htm&type=5).
- Turn off Include the instance name in Visualforce URLs when
  third-party cookies are blocked in Salesforce My
  Domain settings.

Approved Docusign apps: You can use the following Docusign apps with the Salesforce mobile app:

- Docusign eSignature for Salesforce
- Docusign Gen

Non-approved Docusign apps: You cannot use the following Docusign apps with the Salesforce mobile app:

- Docusign Negotiate
- Docusign CLM
- Docusign CLM Essentials

Approved envelope template types: You can only use the following
envelope template types in the Salesforce mobile app:

- [Quick send envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Send now envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus)

Mobile app tagging requirements: Meet the following requirements
to add fields to Docusign envelopes:

- Install the Docusign mobile app on your Android or iOS
  device.
- Turn off mobile app tagging when using an iPad.

For more information about Salesforce requirements, see the [Supported Browsers and Devices](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.getstart_browser_overview.htm&type=5) page on the
Salesforce support site.

title: "Failed to Update One or More Metadata Items" Displays When I Create a Gen for Billing Custom Button
heading_path: "Failed to Update One or More Metadata Items" Displays When I Create a Gen for Billing Custom Button
text:
Learn to fix the "Failed to update one or more metadata items" error.

I created a new Gen for Billing template and selected Save and
Close before creating the custom button.

I opened the template later and added a button name.

When I selected Update Layouts, an error displayed:
Failed to update one or more metadata items. I cannot add
the button.

title: "Failed to Update One or More Metadata Items" Displays When I Create a Gen for Billing Custom Button
heading_path: "Failed to Update One or More Metadata Items" Displays When I Create a Gen for Billing Custom Button
Resolution: Manually Add the Gen for Billing Custom Button to the Invoices Layout
text:
Cause: Saving a Gen for Billing template without creating a
button causes a failure. Add the button through the Salesforce interface.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Access the Invoices Page Layout as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NjYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.c-7EKlxCRt7JNxHO26zMDfhzM77v3yRg843GlW0vHUk&_LANG=enus) and select
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Object Manager in the
      Quick Find field.
   3. Select Invoices from the Label
      column.
   4. Select Page Layouts.

      The Page Layouts page opens.
2. Select the Buttons menu.
3. Drag the Gen for Billing custom button to the Custom Buttons
   menu.
4. Select Save.![A screenshot displays the Send with Docusign button being dragged to the custom buttons menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/dragtocanvas.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NjYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.c-7EKlxCRt7JNxHO26zMDfhzM77v3yRg843GlW0vHUk&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Send with Docusign button being dragged to the custom buttons menu.")

   You have completed the process. You have added the Gen for
   Billing custom button to the Invoices layout.

title: Docusign Fails to Connect to Salesforce When I Enter My Docusign Administrator Credentials
heading_path: Docusign Fails to Connect to Salesforce When I Enter My Docusign Administrator Credentials
text:
Learn to reset a browser session to connect Docusign and
Salesforce.

I am unable to finish connecting my Docusign account to my Salesforce
organization.

I enter Docusign administrator credentials, but the connection process
fails.

title: Docusign Fails to Connect to Salesforce When I Enter My Docusign Administrator Credentials
heading_path: Docusign Fails to Connect to Salesforce When I Enter My Docusign Administrator Credentials
Resolution: Clear the Docusign Login Session in Your Browser
text:
Cause: The connection process can fail if you are logged on with
different Docusign credentials on another browser tab.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Clear your browser cache.
2. Log in to Salesforce as an administrator.
3. [Open Docusign Apps Launcher as
   an administrator.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to open the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package in your organization.")
4. Follow these steps to [connect Salesforce and Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=pkr1586134231912.html&_LANG=enus).

   You have completed the process. You have cleared your browser cache and
   connected your Docusign account with your Salesforce
   organization.

title: Federated Security for Docusign CLM
heading_path: Federated Security for Docusign CLM
text:
Learn about how you can apply Salesforce security settings to your CLM content using
Federated Security.

There are two Docusign CLM apps that you can use in Salesforce:

- Docusign CLM: The current app that is part of the Docusign
  Apps Launcher package of apps
- Docusign Document Management: The legacy CLM app, formerly
  known as SpringCM File It

This article applies to the Docusign CLM app.

See this page for legacy app instructions: [Federated Security for Docusign Document Management (SpringCM File
It)](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=dsa1643667928464&topicId=fmk1599229525077.html&_LANG=enus).

You can use Federated Security to apply Salesforce security to content in your CLM
account. Federated Security lets you manage CLM security from Salesforce without needing
to set separate permissions.

Federated Security applies Salesforce Profile or
Role security to all CLM content. When you update security
settings in Salesforce, CLM content security also updates.

You can find Federated Security settings on the CLM Admin page.

Warning:

- Do not refresh a Salesforce organization with Federated Security applied
- Do not connect a Salesforce organization to a different CLM account when
  Federated Security is applied

[You must follow these steps before you refresh or
reconnect](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ytt1602710410891.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about best practices for refreshing a Salesforce organization with Federated Security enabled."). Failure to do so destroys all CLM file security, and you must
rebuild the entire CLM directory.

Note: Federated Security cannot be applied to CLM reports because you can
only run reports in your CLM account.

**Example**: A user has no access to the
Finance folder in Salesforce. That user has
View access to the Finance folder in
CLM. The user runs a report in the CLM account. The user sees documents from the
Finance folder in the report results. Federated security does not apply because you
cannot run the CLM report in Salesforce.

title: Add Child Objects to a Gen Template
heading_path: Add Child Objects to a Gen Template
text:
Learn to add child objects in the Gen Data Fields step.

Use this procedure to add child objects to parent objects in the Gen Data Fields step:

1. Select + Add under a field.
2. Select the Select an Option dropdown menu.
3. Select a child object. Child objects display in the following lists:

   - Lookup Relationships: Choose fields from objects linked to
     the parent object.
   - Related Lists: Choose from fields related to the parent
     object.
4. Select + Add to add more levels of objects.![A screenshot displays the relation between child objects and parent objects on a Gen template.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-relation-between-child-objects-and-parent-objects-on-a-Gen-template.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1MjUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.U8vpXmC5cRYusz39eoYIUFTI-co7MuwLujyGZtqoeqE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the relation between child objects and parent objects on a Gen template.")
5. Select Finish and Save when you are
   finished adding fields.

You have completed the process. You have added child
object fields with up to four levels of relation to parent objects. Paste the anchor text from
these data fields into your Microsoft Word template.

title: Step 1: Remove Users from Apps Launcher Permission Sets
heading_path: Step 1: Remove Users from Apps Launcher Permission Sets
text:
Learn to remove all users from Docusign permission sets.

You are required to remove at least one user from the following permission sets:

- Docusign Administrator
- Docusign CLM Admin

You may be required to remove users from [other Docusign Apps Launcher
permission sets](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=xxm1623778416524.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the types and functions of Docusign Apps Launcher permission sets.").

1. Open the Permission Sets page as an administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1MjIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.zho-yIdFcoH-wngtz-rO0i0huE6MibpabAnfa1TeCPo&_LANG=enus) and select
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Permission Sets in the Quick
      Find field.

   The Permission Sets page opens.
2. Remove all users from the Docusign Administrator
   permission set:
   1. Select Manage Assignments.
   2. Select the Action check box to select all
      users.
   3. Select Remove Assignments.![A screenshot displays the Action check box and the remove assignments button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/daluninstallremoveassignments.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1MjIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.zho-yIdFcoH-wngtz-rO0i0huE6MibpabAnfa1TeCPo&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Action check box and the remove assignments button highlighted.")

      A dialog box displays: Are you sure you want to remove
      these users from this permission set?.
   4. Select OK.

      The dialog box closes. The Docusign
      Administrator permission set page opens.
3. Select Done.
4. Repeat this procedure for the Docusign CLM Admin
   permission set.
5. Repeat this procedure for any other Docusign permission sets
   containing users.

You have completed step one of the process. You have
removed all users from Docusign Apps Launcher permission sets. Move to
[Step 2: Remove Apps Launcher Components from Your Layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=uxu1623708462949.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove all Docusign Apps Launcher components from Salesforce layouts.").

title: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your Gen or eSignature Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
heading_path: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your Gen or eSignature Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
text: Learn to fix domain integration issues using the Sync Domains feature.

title: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your Gen or eSignature Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
heading_path: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your Gen or eSignature Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
Before you begin
text:
[Install Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce
v5.2](https://login.salesforce.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04t3u000002wLcp)

You can change your Salesforce domain name to update your brand, adopt [Enhanced Domains](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.domain_name_enhanced.htm&type=5), or enable partitioned domains. When
your My Domain name changes, your Salesforce-hosted URLs also change. You must
rebuild the connection to your Docusign account.

The Sync Domains feature of the Docusign
TroubleShooting tool rebuilds your connection to Docusign after a
domain change.

Use this procedure to rebuild your Gen, Negotiate,
eSignature, or eSignature Apex Toolkit
integration with the Sync Domains feature:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MTAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.MUUpJrizxkckhMEn04WNyipwRVBTJ-Kgf2S3VsQxoII&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/TroubleShooting-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MTAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.MUUpJrizxkckhMEn04WNyipwRVBTJ-Kgf2S3VsQxoII&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.

   The TroubleShooting page opens.
4. Sync Domains: Select Execute.

   The page refreshes. A message displays: Successfully synced domains on
   Docusign account.

You have completed the process. You have rebuilt the
Gen, Negotiate,
eSignature, or eSignature Apex Toolkit
integration with your Salesforce organization.

title: Docusign Gen for Salesforce Administrator Overview
heading_path: Docusign Gen for Salesforce Administrator Overview
text:
Learn about how you can use and configure Docusign Gen in your
Salesforce organization.

Docusign Apps Launcher is a unified package of apps:

- CLM for Salesforce
- CLM Essentials for Salesforce
- eSignature for Salesforce
- Gen for Salesforce

These apps work together to get your users from contract generation to review to signature
with speed and efficiency.

This guide covers administration of the Docusign Gen for Salesforce app.
Administrators can use Gen to create templates for document generation. Gen templates merge
Docusign and Salesforce fields into contracts to create agreements.

You can add Gen template custom buttons to Salesforce object layouts. Salesforce users with
Gen access can select a Gen custom button on a record and instantly generate agreements. Users
save generated agreements to Salesforce records and send them for signature.

title: Start a Docusign Gen 30-Day Trial
heading_path: Start a Docusign Gen 30-Day Trial
text:
Learn how to activate a free 30-day trial of the Docusign Gen
application.

title: Start a Docusign Gen 30-Day Trial
heading_path: Start a Docusign Gen 30-Day Trial
Before you begin
text:
You must have the following to start a Gen trial:

- Salesforce administrator credentials
- Docusign administrator credentials
- [Docusign Apps Launcher installed](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.")
- [Connection to a Docusign account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.")

You can activate a free 30-day trial of the Docusign Gen for Salesforce
application. The trial has all the capabilities of the fully licensed Gen product.
You can start a Gen trial without contacting Docusign sales.

Note: Each Gen user must also have an eSignature user in your Docusign account. So if you're licensed for 100 Docusign eSignature users, you can add 100 trial Gen users.

Use this procedure to start your 30-day free trial of Docusign Gen for
Salesforce.

1. Select Document Generation in the left column.
2. Select Try it out.
3. Select Get Started.

   The dialog box closes. A message opens: Your Docusign Gen
   trial has started.
4. Select Allow Access.
5. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials.

   A message opens: Successfully connected and authorized to
   Docusign.

You have completed the process. You have activated your Gen trial.
You have 30 days to evaluate Gen at no cost. [Contact Docusign
Sales](https://www.docusign.com/contact-sales) to continue using Gen after 30 days.

title: Use Filter and Conditional Tags Together in a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Use Filter and Conditional Tags Together in a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn to build tables to import conditional content from Salesforce
records.

You can build tables to import content from Salesforce records.

Filter tags only import certain fields from a Salesforce record.

Conditional tags display or hide blocks of text depending on
Salesforce field data.

You can use these two types of tags together to create custom Gen templates.

Example
: You want to create tables that add
products by category on your generated agreements. You create specific tables for each
category. Each table has tags that filter all line items in the product table, returning
only the ones you need. You add the tables to your Gen template.

Use this procedure to add a table with Filter and Conditional tags:

1. Select a document name.

   The document preview page opens in a new tab or window.
2. Add a table with custom XML to your Word template:
   1. Select Download.
   2. Open the Word document and go to where you want the table.
   3. Insert a table with a header, at least one row, and four columns.
   4. In the top row, enter a name for each column.
   5. Enter `<# <TableRow Select="/` in the first cell of the first
      row.

      |  |  |  |
      | --- | --- | --- |
      | Product Name | Product Code | Price |
      | **`<# <TableRow Select="/`** |  |  |
   6. Create a tag that imports Product Name content and
      suppresses content that does not match Services. Place this
      text directly after the <# <TableRow Select="/ tag.

      |  |  |  |
      | --- | --- | --- |
      | Product Name | Product Code | Price |
      | **`<# <TableRow Select="./Quote_Line_Items"/>#><#<SuppressTableRow Select="./Product_Category" NotMatch='Services'/>#><#<Content Select="./Product_Name" Optional="true />#>`** |  |  |
   7. Create a tag in the Product Code column that imports
      Product Code data from the Product
      Name field.

      |  |  |  |
      | --- | --- | --- |
      | Product Name | Product Code | Price |
      | **`<# <TableRow Select="./Quote_Line_Items"/>#><#<SuppressTableRow Select="./Product_Category" NotMatch='Services'/>#><#<Content Select="./Product_Name" Optional="true />#>`** | **`<#<Content Select="./Product_Code" Optional="true />#>`** |  |
   8. Create a tag for the Price column that imports items from
      the Line Net Total  column. Add the /mo
      tag at the end for services that match the Monthly Billing Unit
      code.

      |  |  |  |
      | --- | --- | --- |
      | Product Name | Product Code | Price |
      | **`<# <TableRow Select="./Quote_Line_Items"/>#><#<SuppressTableRow Select="./Product_Category" NotMatch='Services'/>#><#<Content Select="./Product_Name" Optional="true />#>`** | **`<#<Content Select="./Product_Code" Optional="true />#>`** | **`<#<Content Select="./Line_Net_Total" Optional="true />#><#<Conditional Select="./Billing_Unit" Match='Monthly" />#>/mo<#<EndConditional/>#>`** |
   9. Save the changes in Word and return to the Gen Upload
      Documents page.
3. [Re-upload the document to your Gen
   template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sqp1586134339354.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to update your Gen template Word documents.").
4. Select Save and Close and select Save on
   the Do you want to save changes? dialog box.

   You have added the table to your Gen template. When users generate an agreement,
   Gen only imports the Services content. Gen also adds Product Name,
   Product Code, and Line Net Total content
   with mo at the end. ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/tableresult.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzMTcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.6W4-yy5cLaIShKIPLMeeT6Do_Lu3a5dKL8JO2XkDPOs&_LANG=enus)

title: Use Filter and Conditional Tags Together in a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Use Filter and Conditional Tags Together in a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
en=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzMTcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.6W4-yy5cLaIShKIPLMeeT6Do_Lu3a5dKL8JO2XkDPOs&_LANG=enus)

You have completed the process. You have added a new
table to your Gen template using filter and conditional tags. Generated agreements display or
hide content based on the tag values.

title: Gen Template Advanced Options: Microsoft Word
heading_path: Gen Template Advanced Options: Microsoft Word
text:
Learn to use advanced actions when configuring your Gen templates.

You can use advanced options to customize your Gen templates. Adding tables,
conditional logic and button management are all features of the Gen app.

- [Gen Word template options: Select Source
  step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=lxs1621489455284.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to change the data source of a Gen template.")
- [Gen Word template options: Add Fields step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=tlx1621488778096.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the options that you can use in the Add Fields step of the Gen template creation process.")
- [Gen Word template options: Upload Documents
  step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=xbc1621487025657.html&_LANG=enus "This guide demonstrates the options that you can use in the Upload Documents step of the Gen template creation process.")
- [Gen Word template options: Create Button
  step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=jhk1621491026561.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about linking Docusign envelope templates to Gen templates, and disabling the Send for Signature button.")
- [Gen XML toolbox: Use custom XML in your Gen
  template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=jte1621489098984.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to use custom XML to add tables, filter data, group data, add conditional logic, and more.")
- [CLM and Gen compatibility](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=mor1615480798834.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about using Docusign Gen with Docusign CLM.")
- [Use the CLM tab of the CLM Document Generation
  page](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=tiy1615491633933.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use the CLM tab of the CLM Document Generation page.")
- [Gen for Salesforce Flow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=udy1726512459295.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build Generate agreement with Docusign actions for Salesforce Flow.")

title: Use Conditional Logic in a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Use Conditional Logic in a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn to use Salesforce record fields to trigger conditional logic that displays or
hides content in your document.

You can use conditional logic in Gen data fields to display or hide language in your
generated document.

Example: You add conditional logic on a Billing
Address field to display specific legal language to California customers. You
generate a document from a record with a California address. The generated document contains
a special legal clause for California customers.

Use this procedure to create conditional logic for a Gen data field:

1. Select Options from the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0OTgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.kyAth5C8RB6p8izQeywlfCp-nR1e47Lohdm0pKzcSp0&_LANG=enus) dropdown menu.

   ![A screenshot of the dropdown menu for a Gen data field displays the Options and Remove options.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-dropdown-menu-for-a-Gen-data-field-displays-the-Options-and-Remove-options.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0OTgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.kyAth5C8RB6p8izQeywlfCp-nR1e47Lohdm0pKzcSp0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the dropdown menu for a Gen data field displays the Options and Remove options.")
2. Select Include other content if the conditions set for this field are
   met.
3. Choose an option from the dropdown menu:

   - matches
   - does not match
4. Enter keywords to match or not match in the text field.

   Note: See the following topic for more
   information about [match/not match
   logic](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=nnl1586134232740.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about adding conditional logic blocks to Gen template Word documents.").
5. Select Save. ![A screenshot displays the Data Field Options dialog box with the Include other content option selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Data-Field-Options-dialog-box-with-the-Include-other-content-option-selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0OTgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.kyAth5C8RB6p8izQeywlfCp-nR1e47Lohdm0pKzcSp0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Data Field Options dialog box with the Include other content option selected.")
6. Select Save and Close.

You have completed the process. You have added
conditional logic to your Gen data field. Text is displayed or hidden based on keywords in the
record.

title: Add a Dynamic Table to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
heading_path: Add a Dynamic Table to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
text:
Learn to add a dynamic table to an Agreement Cloud Editor template.

You can add a dynamic table to an Agreement Cloud Editor
template. Dynamic tables automatically add rows and columns based on the amount of data on a
Salesforce record.

Use this procedure to add a dynamic table to an Agreement Cloud Editor
Gen template:

1. Open the Gen template Create tab as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Zn3D9L85kE9N5D79O4sIQO_V9e1npJRAjFvDy5RGLh8&_LANG=enus) and go to View All >  Docusign Apps Launcher.
   2. Select the Docusign Gen Templates tab.
   3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Zn3D9L85kE9N5D79O4sIQO_V9e1npJRAjFvDy5RGLh8&_LANG=enus) next to a template and choose
      Edit.

   The Gen template page opens to the Create tab.
2. Place the cursor where you want to add a table.
3. Select the Salesforce Fields tab ![A screenshot displays two interlocking blue rectangle icons.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genacesalesforcefieldsicon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Zn3D9L85kE9N5D79O4sIQO_V9e1npJRAjFvDy5RGLh8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays two interlocking blue rectangle icons.") to the left of the Docusign Fields menu.

   The Salesforce Fields menu opens.
4. Search for and select the Opportunity Line Items field.

   ![A screenshot displays the Opportunity Line Items field displayed in the Salesforce Fields section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genacelineitemsfield.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Zn3D9L85kE9N5D79O4sIQO_V9e1npJRAjFvDy5RGLh8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Opportunity Line Items field displayed in the Salesforce Fields section.")
5. Create your dynamic table:
   1. Drag items from the Fields menu to the Selected
      Columns section.![A screenshot displays a Salesforce field moving to the Selected Columns section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genacelineitemsadd.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Zn3D9L85kE9N5D79O4sIQO_V9e1npJRAjFvDy5RGLh8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a Salesforce field moving to the Selected Columns section.")
   2. (Optional) Select X to remove a column.
   3. (Optional) Drag columns to change their order.

      ![A screenshot displays a column moving lower in the Selected Columns section.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genacelineitemsdrag.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Zn3D9L85kE9N5D79O4sIQO_V9e1npJRAjFvDy5RGLh8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a column moving lower in the Selected Columns section.")
   4. Select Apply.

title: Add a Dynamic Table to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
heading_path: Add a Dynamic Table to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
text:
xNTgzNTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Zn3D9L85kE9N5D79O4sIQO_V9e1npJRAjFvDy5RGLh8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a column moving lower in the Selected Columns section.")
   4. Select Apply.

   A dynamic table with Salesforce merge fields displays in your template.
   Example:![A screenshot displays a dynamic table with Salesforce line items.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-a-dynamic-table-with-Salesforce-line-items.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Zn3D9L85kE9N5D79O4sIQO_V9e1npJRAjFvDy5RGLh8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a dynamic table with Salesforce line items.")
6. Select Next and Publish.

   A message displays: Template (your template name)
   updated.

You have completed the process. You have added a
dynamic table to your Gen template. The dynamic table automatically adds rows and columns
based on the amount of data on a Salesforce record. All text in the table matches the font
size and style of the template.

title: The Document Generator User Does Not Display as the Owner in Salesforce Files
heading_path: The Document Generator User Does Not Display as the Owner in Salesforce Files
text:
Learn to find the Salesforce owner of documents generated with Docusign Gen.

Users do not display as the owner of documents they generate with Gen. When users view a
record's Files related list, they see the Docusign integration
user listed as the owner.

Users have trouble finding documents that they generated and are unable to determine
who generated them.

title: The Document Generator User Does Not Display as the Owner in Salesforce Files
heading_path: The Document Generator User Does Not Display as the Owner in Salesforce Files
Resolution: View the Document Owner in Salesforce Files
text:
Cause: Docusign uses your integration
user's credentials to authenticate document generation.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Open Salesforce Files:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1MDQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.ho_GzUPvAAfrDalDFtaAbeJ-F99Ijq_xESkn1nM0Pn8&_LANG=enus).
   2. Enter and select Files in the search
      field.

   The Files Owned by Me page opens.
2. Select Owned by Me.

   You have completed the process. The list displays all documents
   that you have generated with Gen.

title: Make a Standard Salesforce User Your Integration User
heading_path: Make a Standard Salesforce User Your Integration User
text:
Learn to make a standard Salesforce user your Docusign integration
user.

Connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account to use Docusign Apps Launcher in Salesforce.

To create the connection, you must enter Docusign and Salesforce
credentials on the Docusign Setup page. The connecting
Salesforce user is called the integration user.

If you don't want to use a Salesforce administrator as your integration user, you can use
standard credentials with some modifications.

Use this procedure to make a standard Salesforce user your integration user:

1. Clone a standard Salesforce user profile and name it Docusign
   Integration User.
2. Add permissions to the Integration User profile:
   1. Open the new profile and select Edit.
   2. Administrative Permissions: Select the following
      options:

      - Manage Users
      - API Enabled
      - View Setup and Configuration
      - Modify Metadata Through Metadata API Functions
      - Customize Application
      - Manage Custom Permissions
   3. Standard Object Permissions and Custom
      Object Permissions:

      - View All: Select for each object Apps
        Launcher must read.
      - Modify All: Select for each object Apps
        Launcher must update.

      Note: These settings grant access to your Salesforce organization's
      ownership hierarchy. These permissions do not grant unrestricted
      access to all data on these objects. You can also restrict access to
      specific data points using [Field Level
      Security](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=ind.fsc_admin_configure_field_level_security.htm&type=5) or [Sharing
      Settings](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=ind.cg_task_update_sharing_settings.htm&type=5).
   4. Open Object Manager and set Field Level
      Security for all fields Apps Launcher must read or
      update. These settings grant access to features like document generation
      or workflow automation:

      - Visible: Select for each object Apps
        Launcher must read.
      - Read-Only: Deselect for each object Apps
        Launcher must update, including all Docusign
        objects.![A screenshot displays field-level security settings for the Docusign Integration User profile.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-fieldlevel-security-settings-for-the-Docusign-Integration-User-profile.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1MzgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.FFpZj3ya6iJdjuJUtUxndnQSWcAgQKlgY4Ap2MADK38&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays field-level security settings for the Docusign Integration User profile.")
   5. Select Save.
3. Create a standard Salesforce user to act as your integration user.

   Note: Create generic credentials not tied to a person, like
   docusign-integration-user@yourorg.com. View this
   topic for more information about [integration user best practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.").
4. Set Docusign access for your Salesforce integration
   user:

   1. Log on to your Docusign account as an
      administrator.
   2. Create a Docusign administrator using your Salesforce integration user
      credentials.
5. Add your integration user to the Docusign Integration User
   profile.
6. Add your integration user to the Docusign Administrator
   permission set.
7. [Connect your Docusign
   account to your Salesforce organization using the Docusign
   and Salesforce integration user credentials](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.").

title: Make a Standard Salesforce User Your Integration User
heading_path: Make a Standard Salesforce User Your Integration User
text:
credentials](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.").

You have completed the process. You made a standard Salesforce
user your integration user and connected Docusign and Salesforce. This
integration user maintains the connection between accounts without requiring
unrestricted administrator permissions.

title: Connect a Docusign Production Account to Salesforce
heading_path: Connect a Docusign Production Account to Salesforce
text:
Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign
production account.

title: Connect a Docusign Production Account to Salesforce
heading_path: Connect a Docusign Production Account to Salesforce
Before you begin
text:
[Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.")

To use Docusign applications, you must connect a Docusign
account to your Salesforce organization. You create this connection by entering
Salesforce and Docusign administrator credentials on the
Docusign Setup page. The Docusign
administrator used to connect accounts is called the integration user.

Note:

- Docusign recommends that you create a generic integration
  user. For more information, see this topic: [Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.").
- Docusign does not support connecting multiple Salesforce
  organizations to a single Docusign account.

Use this procedure to connect your Salesforce organization to a Docusign
production account:

1. Complete the following from the Authorize page:
   1. Select Allow Access and choose
      Allow on the dialog box that displays.
   2. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that
      displays and select Log In.

   The Log in to Docusign page displays.
2. Complete the following steps on the Log in to Docusign
   page:
   1. Select Log in to Docusign. The Docusign Login
      dialog box displays.
   2. Enter Docusign administrator credentials in the
      dialog box that displays and select Log in.
   3. Re-enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that
      displays and select Log In.

   The Select an account dialog box displays.
3. Select Account: Choose a Docusign
   account and select Next.

   The Docusign Setup page displays.

You have completed the process. You have connected your
Salesforce organization to your Docusign production account. You can
set up your Docusign Apps Launcher applications.

title: Add Child Objects to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
heading_path: Add Child Objects to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
text:
Learn to add up to four levels of child objects and two levels of related
lists.

Agreement Cloud Editor templates can contain up to four levels
of child objects and two levels of related lists. You can use the following combinations of
relation:

| Relation type | Object path |
| --- | --- |
| Root | Opportunity |
| 1: 1 | Opportunity >  Account |
| 1: 1: 1 | Opportunity >  Account >  Parent Account |
| 1: 1: 1: 1 | Opportunity >  Account >  Parent Account >  Owner |
| 1: Many | Opportunity >  Opportunity Contact Roles |
| 1: Many: 1 | Opportunity >  Opportunity Contact Roles >  Contact |
| 1: Many: 1: 1 | Opportunity >  Opportunity Contact Roles >  Contact >  Reports To |
| 1: Many: Many | Account >  Contacts >  Cases |
| 1: Many: Many: 1 | Account >  Contacts >  Cases >  Owner |

Use this procedure to add child relations to parent objects in a Gen
template:

1. Open the Gen template Create tab as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNDEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.ORtQt_UcgbdbZ-GjcfZLtOZHX1I-b7qmGqsCc4okRos&_LANG=enus) and go to View All >  Docusign Apps Launcher.
   2. Select the Docusign Gen Templates tab.
   3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNDEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.ORtQt_UcgbdbZ-GjcfZLtOZHX1I-b7qmGqsCc4okRos&_LANG=enus) next to a template and choose
      Edit.

   The Gen template page opens to the Create tab.
2. Select the  Salesforce Fields
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genacesalesforcefieldsicon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNDEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.ORtQt_UcgbdbZ-GjcfZLtOZHX1I-b7qmGqsCc4okRos&_LANG=enus) tab.
3. Expand arrows to display child objects.

   ![A screenshot displays a tree of Salesforce objects starting with Account and expanding to Account Name.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genace4levels.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNDEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.ORtQt_UcgbdbZ-GjcfZLtOZHX1I-b7qmGqsCc4okRos&_LANG=enus)
4. Drag a child object field to the document text.

You have completed the process. You have added child object
fields to a Gen template.

title: Fix Account-Wide Issues Using the Docusign Apps Launcher Troubleshooting Page
heading_path: Fix Account-Wide Issues Using the Docusign Apps Launcher Troubleshooting Page
text:
Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting is a Salesforce page that
allows administrators to troubleshoot and resolve issues with these Docusign apps.

Docusign Apps Launcher is a package of apps that you install in your Salesforce organization.
These apps include Docusign Gen, Negotiate, CLM, CLM Essentials, and eSignature for
Salesforce.

Docusign Apps Launcher TroubleShooting is a tool that lets
administrators troubleshoot and resolve issues with Docusign apps. The page is available
in Docusign Apps Launcher version 2.5 and higher. Salesforce administrators with
Docusign administrator permissions can use it.

The TroubleShooting page helps you resolve common administration issues. You can fix complex
problems by selecting the Execute button next to a task.

Here are the functions available on the Troubleshooting page:

[Disconnect Account and Preserve User Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=oks1610512362945.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disconnect Docusign from Salesforce while preserving user settings with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.")
:   Disconnect your Docusign account from Salesforce without deleting your Docusign
    users.

[Disconnect Account and Remove Users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=vli1610514116162.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disconnect a Docusign account and remove users with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.")
:   Disconnect your Docusign account and remove all Docusign users and settings from
    Salesforce.

[Clear User Settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ncl1610479844444.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to fix the CUSTOM_SETTINGS_LIMIT_EXCEEDED issue with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.")
:   Delete cached user access tokens to free up Salesforce storage space.

[Folder Permissions](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sqj1610517836441.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to reset folder permissions for Docusign content with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.")
:   Trigger a root folder permissions update. This update resets user permissions for all
    content.

[Refresh Products](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=agy1623993092688.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to activate and update Docusign for Salesforce apps and settings using the Refresh Products feature.")
:   Activate and update apps and settings connected to your Salesforce organization.

[Sync Domains](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ynp1680122164220.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to fix domain integration issues using the Sync Domains feature. Sync Domains fixes issues with Enhanced Domains and any changes that you make to your Salesforce domain.")
:   Resolve errors related to Enhanced Domains or domain changes that you make.

[Manage CLM Integration User](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=yyh1698871133582.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Reset the token used by your Docusign CLM integration user. This feature is only available
    for Docusign CLM for Salesforce accounts.

title: Edit Salesforce Data Fields in a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Edit Salesforce Data Fields in a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn to edit data fields in your Gen template configuration.

Use this procedure to edit data fields in your Gen
template configuration:

1. Select the Add Fields arrow. The arrow displays no text until
   selected.
2. Select the dropdown arrows next to a field. ![A screenshot displays the main data source page with field and anchor text fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-main-data-source-page-with-field-and-anchor-text-fields.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0OTMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.1by7MFFQ78gAIA8CbfDbGA8Cc37SuYGjfHh9KhuePV8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the main data source page with field and anchor text fields.")
3. Select a Field from the dropdown menu.
4. Select Finish when you are done making changes.

   Important: Update all
   changed tags from this template's Word document and re-upload the document.

You have completed the first step of the process. You
have changed the tags. Proceed to step two to [edit this
template's Word document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sqp1586134339354.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to update your Gen template Word documents.").

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
text:
Learn about the actions that you must take to use Docusign apps after
Salesforce security changes.

Important: You are not required to make any changes if
you have taken the following steps:

- Installed the Docusign connected app.
- Set the OAuth policy to Admin-approved users are pre-authorized.

  Note: Changing policy revokes existing
  user tokens. Existing users must re-authorize.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
What is changing with Salesforce connected app security?
text:
Starting in September 2025, Salesforce began enforcing [restrictions for uninstalled connected apps](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=005132365&type=1). This usage
restriction blocks access to uninstalled connected apps. If you have not installed
the Docusign connected app and assigned permissions, your users
cannot access Docusign apps.

As part of this change, Salesforce created the Approve uninstalled connected apps user permission (defaulted to System
Administrator standard profile.)

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
How do these security changes impact Docusign integrations?
text:
Connected apps installed before the security change continue to function
without interruption. Take action only if you did not install the connected app
before the changes.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
What is an uninstalled connected app?
text:
An [uninstalled connected app](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.connected_app_how_to_install.htm&type=5) is an application in your
organization that has not been installed by a System Administrator. Organizations
integrated with Docusign contain an uninstalled connected app
named DocuSign on the Connected Apps OAuth
Usage page. By default, this application is not installed, and must
be manually installed by a System Administrator. ![A screenshot displays the install button highlighted on the Docusign connected app OAuth Usage page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A_screenshot_displays_the_install_button_highlighted_on_the_Docusign_connected_app_OAuth_Usage_page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyNzksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.vsJ-kzlvkfxXKfiEJz3Ty6qI0QGcBsnembXUcednlzw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the install button highlighted on the Docusign connected app OAuth Usage page.")

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
Which Docusign managed packages contain the connected app?
text:
The following Docusign for Salesforce packages contain the Docusign connected app:

- Docusign Apps Launcher (Namespace: dfsle)
- Docusign for Salesforce (Legacy Namespace: dsfs)
- FileIt (Legacy Namespace: SpringCMEos)

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
How are new users impacted?
text:
If you have not installed the app before the security change, your new
users cannot access the Docusign connected app. Users see the
authorization button, but a Salesforce OAuth error displays after they enter their
Docusign credentials: We can't authorize you because
of an OAuth error. For more information, contact your Salesforce
administrator.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
How are existing users impacted?
text:
If you never installed the app, existing users can access it if they meet the
following criteria:

- The user has previously authorized the app.
- The app isn’t using the [OAuth 2.0 device flow](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.remoteaccess_oauth_device_flow.htm&language=en_US&type=5).

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
Does Docusign Apps Launcher use OAuth 2.0 device flow?
text: No. Docusign Apps Launcher does not use OAuth 2.0 device flow.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
What actions should I take?
text:
[Install the connected app to grant Docusign
for Salesforce access to new users](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/Install-the-DocuSign-Apps-Launcher-to-Manage-Connected-Apps-in-Salesforce-Setup).

Note: Salesforce
and Docusign recommend selecting Admin-approved
users are pre-authorized. Each user must enter credentials to
gain Docusign for Salesforce app access if you select
All users may self-authorize.

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
What happens when I change the connected app policy?
text:
Make the following changes when you update the Permitted Users
setting to Admin approved users are pre-authorized:

Administrators
:   - All Apps Launcher apps: Add all necessary
      profiles and permission sets to the Docusign
      connected app. An error message displays for users not included
      in these profiles and permission sets: User is not
      admin approved to access this app.
    - CLM for Salesforce: [Disconnect your account and
      preserve user settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=oks1610512362945.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disconnect Docusign from Salesforce while preserving user settings with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.") if you use CLM Document
      Generation. Otherwise, a Salesforce session is
      invalid error displays when users try to generate
      agreements.

Existing users
:   Authorized users must re-authorize. Steps to re-authorize vary for each
    installed package:

    - Docusign Apps Launcher: [Activate your Docusign
      for Salesforce user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qob1643667997351&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html).
    - Docusign Connect for Salesforce (Legacy):
      [Reconfigure your login with Docusign Connect](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=gab1637077302276&topicId=tsk6312c996-938f-4f24-b11f-9da2f7887493.html). There is no
      option to authorize within the legacy Docusign
      for Salesforce integration.
    - FileIt (Legacy SpringCM): Take no action.
      Existing users are already authorized to use SpringCM FileIt. If
      users must re-authorize, use these procedures:
      - [Configure FileIt app security
        settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=dsa1643667928464&topicId=qgu1583188662524.html).
      - [Connect Salesforce to
        SpringCM](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=dsa1643667928464&topicId=btt1576610138405.html).

title: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
heading_path: Salesforce Connected App Security Changes
How do I resolve connected app errors?
text:
User is not admin approved to access this app
:   Resolution: Install and configure the Docusign
    connected app. Add the missing profile or permission set if you have
    already installed the app.

You have not granted or have revoked your consent to be impersonated by Docusign Apps Launcher
:   Resolution: Install the Docusign connected app. [Refresh the user's consent flow](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/DAL-Error-You-have-not-granted-or-have-revoked-your-consent-to-be-impersonated-by-DocuSign-Apps-Launcher) if you have
    already installed the app.

We can't authorize you because of an OAuth error. For more information, contact your Salesforce administrator
:   Resolution 1: Install the Docusign connected app. Add access to missing profiles
    or permission sets on the Connected App Detail page
    if you have already installed the app.
:   Resolution 2: Install the Docusign connected app. Go to Setup >  Connected Apps OAuth Usage and choose All users may
    self-authorize for the Docusign
    connected app.

Salesforce session is invalid
:   Resolution 1: Install the Docusign connected app. [Disconnect and reconnect your account and
    preserve user settings](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=oks1610512362945.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disconnect Docusign from Salesforce while preserving user settings with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.") after changing OAuth policy to
    All users may self-authorize.
:   Resolution 2: Enable Allow
    Visualforce pages to access APIs if either
    Restrict Access to APIs with Connected
    Apps or Restrict Customers and Partners
    from Access APIs is enabled.

title: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
heading_path: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
text:
Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.

The integration user is a Docusign administrator that connects your Docusign account to your Salesforce organization. When you connect accounts, you must enter Docusign and Salesforce administrator credentials. When your accounts are connected, users can access Docusign functions like sending agreements for signature.

Docusign uses OAuth 2.0 for communicating between Salesforce and Docusign. These tokens continue to operate unless an administrator chooses to break them or force an expiration.

Follow these guidelines to avoid issues with your integration:

Create dedicated integration users
:   Create generic integration administrators for your Docusign account and your Salesforce organization. Ensure that these generic credentials aren't tied to a person. For example, you can create a user named integration-user@yourcompany.com. Creating generic integration users protects your integration when administrators leave or change roles. Deactivating your integration user deletes custom Docusign fields and templates created by that user. So it is best to create an integration user that you will never deactivate.

Enable password login for at least one Docusign administrator
:   If you enforce SSO login for your integration user, [enable
    password login for at least one Docusign administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=rrf1583359212854&topicId=fji1583359148678.html&_LANG=enus). Your Docusign integration breaks if your SSO setup experiences
    an outage and no administrator can log in with a password.

Utilize a compatible Salesforce user license
:   Your Docusign for Salesforce integration user must be a member of the Docusign Administrator permission set. Since you cannot add [Salesforce Integration Users](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=release-notes.rn_api_integration_license.htm&release=242&type=5) to permission sets, you cannot use this license type as your integration user. Docusign recommends that you use a Salesforce [Standard User](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.users_license_types_available.htm&type=5) license when creating your Docusign integration user.

Note: Docusign does not support connecting multiple Salesforce organizations to a single Docusign account.

The following is a list of integration user scenarios:

title: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
heading_path: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
Actions that break your integration
text:
You close (deactivate) the Docusign integration user
:   - Docusign deletes all envelope templates
    - Docusign send buttons do not work
    - Docusign deletes custom merge fields created by the Docusign integration user
:   Before closing your Docusign integration user, create a template in your Docusign account and re-save each custom merge field.

(Gen and CLM only) When you deactivate your Salesforce integration user
:   Your Gen and CLM for Salesforce functions break. The Docusign eSignature for Salesforce app continues to function without issue.

You enable multi-factor authentication (MFA) for your Salesforce integration user
:   A Salesforce integration user needs the following access:

    - Salesforce System Administrator access
    - [Enable MFA for API logins](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus) for
      the System Administrator profile set to
      False

title: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
heading_path: Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices
Actions that do not break your integration
text:
You change the Docusign integration user password
:   Envelope templates, custom merge fields, and Docusign send buttons continue to work.
:   Only enter the Docusign integration user's password for the initial authorization.

You change the Salesforce integration user password
:   Envelope templates, custom merge fields, and Docusign send buttons continue to work.
:   Only enter the Salesforce integration user's password for the initial authorization.

You deactivate the Salesforce integration user
:   Envelope templates, custom merge fields, and Docusign send buttons continue to work.
:   Important:

    - Deactivating an integration user that's used for both Docusign Apps Launcher **and** legacy eSignature for Salesforce breaks the legacy app.
    - Deactivating the Salesforce integration user breaks your Docusign Gen and CLM for Salesforce functionality.
    - If the deactivated administrator is also a system sender, Apex toolkit flows relying on the system sender break. Add a new active user as the system sender to prevent this.

You replace the integration user
:   Envelope templates, custom merge fields, and Docusign send buttons continue to work.

    Note: Closing (deactivating) the Docusign integration user before adding a new one breaks the integration.

You enable single sign-on (SSO) for the Docusign integration user
:   Envelope templates, custom merge fields, and Docusign send buttons continue to work.
:   Enabling SSO in the Docusign account does not interfere with communications between Salesforce and Docusign.

title: "You Must Have Docusign Generator Permissions to Perform This Operation" Displays When I Try to Create a Gen Template
heading_path: "You Must Have Docusign Generator Permissions to Perform This Operation" Displays When I Try to Create a Gen Template
text:
Learn to fix the "You must have Docusign generator permissions to
perform this operation" error.

I am a Docusign administrator, but I cannot create a Gen template. When I
try, an error message displays: You must have Docusign generator permissions to
perform this operation.

title: "You Must Have Docusign Generator Permissions to Perform This Operation" Displays When I Try to Create a Gen Template
heading_path: "You Must Have Docusign Generator Permissions to Perform This Operation" Displays When I Try to Create a Gen Template
