Resolution: Grant Generator Permissions to Administrator User
text:
Cause: You have not granted Generator permissions to your
administrator user. Without Generator permissions, you cannot perform administrative
actions in Gen, including adding new users and creating templates.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

[Grant Generator permissions to your
user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=dzl1586134206658.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature users and manage permissions on the user management page.").

You have completed the process. You have granted Generator permissions to
your administrator user. You can create templates and manage Docusign users.

title: Clone a Gen Template
heading_path: Clone a Gen Template
text:
Learn to clone a Gen template to create a new template that is similar to an existing
one.

You can copy or "clone" a Docusign
Gen template. This is helpful if you want to create a new template that is similar to an
existing one.

Use this procedure to clone a Gen template:

1. Access the Docusign Gen Templates page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.IkTnBtX1VBrwxZ5xzUFDNw2Eb7LF126SBjdVqnS0T08&_LANG=enus) and choose View
      All.
   2. Select Docusign Apps Launcher.
   3. Select Docusign Gen Templates.
2. Select the name of a Gen template.
3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.IkTnBtX1VBrwxZ5xzUFDNw2Eb7LF126SBjdVqnS0T08&_LANG=enus) next to the
   Gen template and choose Clone from the dropdown menu.

   ![A screenshot displays the Clone option selected from the Edit a Gen template dropdown menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Clone-a-Gen-template.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.IkTnBtX1VBrwxZ5xzUFDNw2Eb7LF126SBjdVqnS0T08&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Clone option selected from the Edit a Gen template dropdown menu.")

   The page refreshes. The cloned copy is now the active template on the page. The
   template displays (copy 1) at the end of the name.

You have completed the process. You have cloned a Gen
template. Follow the instructions [to edit a Gen
template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ywu1586134208345.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to edit, delete, or change ownership for a Gen template or custom button.") to make any changes.

title: Access the Docusign Envelope Templates Tab to Build and Edit Envelope Templates
heading_path: Access the Docusign Envelope Templates Tab to Build and Edit Envelope Templates
text:
Learn to access the Docusign Envelope Templates tab in
Salesforce.

Docusign Envelope Templates allow users to select a button on a record to
launch an administrator-built sending experience.

Use this procedure to access the Docusign Envelope Templates tab in
your Salesforce organization:

1. Select the Docusign Envelope Templates tab.
2. Make any selection:

   - [New](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=gou1591745811909.html&_LANG=enus): Create a new envelope template.
   - [Change Owner](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ain1596147068228.html&_LANG=enus): Change the owner of an envelope template.
   - [Import](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=juw1586134252760.html&_LANG=enus): Import your data into Salesforce. You can import up to 50,000 records at a time.
   - [Rename](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=bzu1596146928480.html&_LANG=enus): Change the name of an envelope template.
   - [Edit](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xow1596147003565.html&_LANG=enus): Add or edit documents, recipients, Salesforce fields, Docusign fields, and envelope template options.
   - [Delete](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=yzr1596147036291.html&_LANG=enus): Delete an envelope template.

   You have completed the task. You accessed the Docusign Envelope
   Templates tab. From this page, you can create and edit Envelope
   Templates.

title: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
text:
Learn to fix issues with Gen when the “There Were Some Problems with Your Document
Generation” error displays.

The “There Were Some Problems With Your Document Generation” error displays for many
reasons. Use the following resolutions to fix this issue:

title: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
Resolution 1: Check If the User Is Active
text:
Cause: If a Gen user has not activated their account, the document
generation process fails. To have Active status, users must create a Docusign password, and authorize the Gen app upon first use.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Open Docusign Apps Launcher as
   an administrator.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to open the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package in your organization.")
2. Select User Management in the left column.
3. Check the Status column.
4. Inform Pending users that they must [activate their user accounts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate your Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.").

   You have completed the process. When the user completes activation, they can
   generate agreements from Salesforce records.

title: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
Resolution 2: Add Generator Permissions to the User
text:
Cause: If an Apps Launcher user cannot generate agreements, add
Generator permissions to their user.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Open Docusign Apps Launcher as
   an administrator.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to open the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package in your organization.")
2. Select User Management in the left column.
3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XusU4q-iONegYhIjbSYb4FaiQrEDzr3gYq5f5pO79dQ&_LANG=enus) next
   to the user and choose Edit Permissions from the dropdown menu.
4. Select Generator from the Docusign Gen
   dropdown menu.
5. Select Apply.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-edit-permissions-page-with-generator-selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XusU4q-iONegYhIjbSYb4FaiQrEDzr3gYq5f5pO79dQ&_LANG=enus)

   A message displays: Successfully edited permissions for (user
   name).

   You have completed the process. Docusign sends the user
   [a new activation email](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate your Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.").

title: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
Resolution 3: Reset the Existing User's Password
text:
Cause: If a user has an invalid Docusign password,
Gen actions will fail.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Log in to the connected Docusign account](https://account.docusign.com/#/username) as an
   administrator.
2. Go to Admin >  Users.
3. Select Actions next to the user's name and choose
   Reset Password.

   ![A screenshot displays the reset password option selected on the actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genresetpassword.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XusU4q-iONegYhIjbSYb4FaiQrEDzr3gYq5f5pO79dQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the reset password option selected on the actions menu.")

   A message displays: A reset password email has been sent to (user
   name).

   You have completed the process. Docusign sends the user
   [a new activation email](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate your Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.").

title: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
Resolution 4: Restore System Administrator Status to Your Integration User
text:
Cause: The Salesforce administrator user that you use to connect Docusign and Salesforce is called the integration user. Follow this link
for more information: [Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.")

If you revoke the administrator status of your integration user, the integration fails
and Gen does not work.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. As an administrator, select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XusU4q-iONegYhIjbSYb4FaiQrEDzr3gYq5f5pO79dQ&_LANG=enus) and choose
   Setup.
2. Restore System Administrator status to the installing Salesforce user. Or use
   different Salesforce administrator credentials to [reconnect the accounts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.").

   You have completed the process. You have restored the access of the
   integration user.

title: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
Resolution 5: Grant Customize Application Permissions
text:
Cause: Salesforce users require Customize
Application permissions to read custom user settings. Add Docusign Apps Launcher access to Gen users' Custom Setting
Definitions.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. As an administrator, select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XusU4q-iONegYhIjbSYb4FaiQrEDzr3gYq5f5pO79dQ&_LANG=enus) and choose
   Setup.
2. Enter and select Profiles or Permission
   Sets in the Quick Find field.
3. Select a profile or permission set.
4. Hover the cursor over Enabled Custom Setting Definitions
   Access and select the Edit button that
   displays.

   ![A screenshot displays the edit button highlighted for the enabled custom settings definitions access link.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/gencustomsettingdefedit.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XusU4q-iONegYhIjbSYb4FaiQrEDzr3gYq5f5pO79dQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the edit button highlighted for the enabled custom settings definitions access link.")
5. Select Docusign Apps Launcher from Available Custom Settings
   Definitions and select the Add arrow.

   The Docusign Apps Launcher definition moves to the Enabled
   Custom Setting Definitions column.
6. Select the Save.

   ![A screenshot displays the Docusign apps launcher highlighted in the enabled custom settings definitions access column.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/gencustomsettingdefadd.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XusU4q-iONegYhIjbSYb4FaiQrEDzr3gYq5f5pO79dQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Docusign apps launcher highlighted in the enabled custom settings definitions access column.")

   You have completed the process. You have granted Docusign
   Apps Launcher access to the profile or permission set.

title: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
Resolution 6: Check Your Salesforce Storage
text:
Cause: Gen fails if you don't have enough storage available in your
Salesforce organization. When users generate agreements, document creation fails at the
Preview step. One of the following error messages display:

- There were some problems with your document generation. Unable to generate
  the following files: <file name>
- Unable to create a new ContentVersion for the file name : <file name>
  and SfContentDocumentId.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. As an administrator, select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XusU4q-iONegYhIjbSYb4FaiQrEDzr3gYq5f5pO79dQ&_LANG=enus) and choose
   Setup.
2. Enter and select Storage Usage in the Quick Find field.
3. Check the Storage Type section.

   Note: If the Percent Used column adds up to
   100%, you are out of storage.

   You have completed the process. You have checked the storage capacity of
   your Salesforce organization. If there is not enough storage, delete data or contact
   your Salesforce representative to purchase additional storage.

title: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
Resolution 7: Paste Your Template Content Into a New Word Document
text:
Cause: Gen templates sometimes have hidden formatting in their Word
documents, like macros or hidden XML data. These hidden elements can prevent the template
from generating agreements. Pasting all text from your Word document into a new,
unformatted Word document fixes issues with hidden formatting.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Edit the Gen template Upload Documents page as a Salesforce
   administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=nyj1621662912687.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Select a document name.

   The document preview page opens in a new tab or window.
3. Select Download and open the document in the Microsoft Word
   program.
4. Paste the template's text into a blank Word document:
   1. Copy all document content from your Gen template's Word document.
   2. Create a new blank Word document and paste the copied content.
   3. Save the new Word document to your device. Use the same file name as the
      original Gen template document.
5. Return to the Gen Upload Documents page and select
   X next to the old document version.![A screenshot of the Upload Your Word Templates section displays the Generated File Name, File Suffix, and File Format fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/deletewordtemplate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XusU4q-iONegYhIjbSYb4FaiQrEDzr3gYq5f5pO79dQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload Your Word Templates section displays the Generated File Name, File Suffix, and File Format fields.")

   A message displays: File was successfully
   deleted.
6. Select Upload Files and follow the prompts to upload the new
   version from your device.

   A message displays: File was successfully uploaded.
7. Select Save and Close.
8. Select Finish and choose Save in the
   Do you want to save changes? dialog box.

   You have completed the process. Your old Gen template has been replaced with
   the new template, which has all the same content, but no hidden formatting.

title: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
Resolution 8: Save Your Word Document in the Correct Format
text:
Cause: You can only use .PDF or .DOCX file types in Gen templates.
If a Gen template document is saved as .DOC or another unsupported format, the template
fails.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Edit the Gen template Upload Documents page as a Salesforce
   administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=nyj1621662912687.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Select a document name.

   The document preview page opens in a new tab or window.
3. Select Download and open the document in the Microsoft Word
   program.
4. Open your device's Downloads folder and verify that the file extension is
   .DOCX.
5. If the file is in a different format, open it and save a copy in .DOCX
   format.
6. Return to the Gen Upload Documents page and select
   X next to the old document version.
   ![A screenshot of the Upload Your Word Templates section displays the Generated File Name, File Suffix, and File Format fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/deletewordtemplate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XusU4q-iONegYhIjbSYb4FaiQrEDzr3gYq5f5pO79dQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload Your Word Templates section displays the Generated File Name, File Suffix, and File Format fields.")

   A message displays: File was successfully
   deleted.
7. Select Upload Files and follow the prompts to upload the new
   version from your device.

   A message displays: File was successfully uploaded.
8. Select Save and Close.
9. Select Finish and choose Save in the
   Do you want to save changes? dialog box.

   You have completed the process. You have replaced your old Gen template with
   a .DOCX version.

title: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
Resolution 9: Resolve All Unresolved Tracked Changes
text:
Cause: Document generation fails if your Gen template's Word
document has unresolved tracked changes.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Edit the Gen template Upload Documents page as a Salesforce
   administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=nyj1621662912687.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Select a document name.

   The document preview page opens in a new tab or window.
3. Select Download, open the document in Microsoft Word, and
   select the Review tab.
4. Select Accept and choose Accept All
   Changes.

   ![A screenshot displays the Microsoft Word review tab with accept all changes highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/accepttrackedchanges.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XusU4q-iONegYhIjbSYb4FaiQrEDzr3gYq5f5pO79dQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Microsoft Word review tab with accept all changes highlighted.")
5. Save the changes and return to the Gen Upload Documents
   page.
6. Return to the Gen Upload Documents page and select
   X next to the old document version.![A screenshot of the Upload Your Word Templates section displays the Generated File Name, File Suffix, and File Format fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/deletewordtemplate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XusU4q-iONegYhIjbSYb4FaiQrEDzr3gYq5f5pO79dQ&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload Your Word Templates section displays the Generated File Name, File Suffix, and File Format fields.")

   A message displays: File was successfully
   deleted.
7. Select Upload Files and follow the prompts to upload the new
   version from your device.

   A message displays: File was successfully uploaded.
8. Select Save and Close.
9. Select Finish and choose Save in the
   Do you want to save changes? dialog box.

   You have completed the process. You have saved tracked changes and
   re-uploaded the Word document to your Gen template.

title: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
Resolution 10: Test Configuration Using a Simple Template
text:
Cause: You may have a problem with your Gen template or a template
Word document.

Create a simple Word document with only a few merge tags to test your Gen integration. If
your simple template works, you know that your Gen integration is working. If the simple
template fails, you must examine your integration. If the simple template works, check
your failing Gen template for issues.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Open Docusign Apps Launcher as
   an administrator.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to open the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package in your organization.")
2. Select Docusign Gen Templates.
3. Follow these instructions to [create a new Gen
   template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=lig1586134209279.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a Gen template using a Microsoft Word document.").

   Note: When creating the new
   template, only add two or three basic Anchor Text tags to your Word document.
4. Test the new template by generating an agreement.

   You have completed the process. If the new, simple Gen template successfully
   generates an agreement, troubleshoot the original Word document. Check for hidden
   formatting, Macros, unsaved tracked changes or other problems. If the simple Gen
   template also fails, check for issues with the Gen integration. See if the template
   contains deleted Salesforce fields.

title: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
Resolution 11: Disconnect Docusign Administrator Credentials from Other Accounts
text:
Cause: Gen fails if you use one set of Docusign
administrator credentials to connect multiple Salesforce organizations. For example, if
you use one set of Docusign credentials to connect to a Salesforce
sandbox and a Salesforce production organization. The connection conflict causes problems
with document generation. Disconnect from one organization and re-connect with different
Docusign administrator credentials.

Example: You use Docusign administrator
credentials to connect to Salesforce Org A. You use the same credentials to connect with
Salesforce Org B.

Result: The most-recently-connected organization operates normally.
All other integrations fail. The Salesforce Org A integration fails and the Org B
integration operates normally.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Log in to the Salesforce organization requiring a new integration user.
2. [Use the Disconnect Link to Disconnect and Reconnect Docusign and Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ntr1586134210309.html&_LANG=enus "Use this procedure to disconnect and reconnect your Docusign account from your Salesforce organization.")

   You have completed the process. You have reconnected your Docusign account to the desired org.

title: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
heading_path: “There Were Some Problems with Your Document Generation” Displays When I Generate an Agreement
Resolution 12: Use Production Administrator Credentials for Your Integration User
text:
Cause: Gen fails if you use Docusign Demo
administrator credentials for your integration user. An error displays in the Document
Preview step of the template creation process: There were some problems with
your document generation.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Open Docusign Apps Launcher as
   an administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to open the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package in your organization.").
2. [Disconnect Your Docusign Account and Preserve User Settings with the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=oks1610512362945.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disconnect Docusign from Salesforce while preserving user settings with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool.").
3. Log out of Salesforce.
4. Clear your browser cache and log back in to Salesforce.
5. Follow the instructions to [Connect Salesforce
   and Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account."), using Docusign production
   administrator credentials.

   You have completed the process. You have reconnected your Docusign account to prevent Gen template creation errors in the Preview step.

title: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn about using XPath/XML to customize your Word-based Gen template.

You can use XML to create advanced logic in Gen Word templates that is not available
in the Gen user interface.

The following are commonly-used XML expressions that you can add to your
template:

- Concatenate strings
- Add a comma-separated related list
- Add a comma-separated, concatenated list of fields
- Merge the current date into a document
- Merge owner information
- Insert an image

title: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
Comma-separated related list
text:
```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | <# <Repeat Select="//Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems" /> #> |
|  | <# <Content Select="./Name" /> #><# <Conditional Test="count(./preceding-sibling::*) |
|  | +1 < (count(//Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems) - 1)" /> #>, |
|  | <# <EndConditional /> #><# <Conditional Test="count(./preceding-sibling::*)+1 = |
|  | (count(//Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems) - 1)" /> #>, and <# <EndConditional /> #> |
|  | <# <EndRepeat /> #> |
```

title: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
Concatenate strings
text:
```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | <# <Content Select="concat(//Opportunity/Account.Name, ' - ', //Opportunity/Name)" /> #> |
```

title: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
Comma-separated, concatenated list of fields
text:
```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | <# <Repeat Select="//Field_1__c[text() != ''] | //Field_2__c[text() != ''] | |
|  | //Field_3__c[text() != '']" /> #><# <Content Select="." /> #><# <Conditional Test=" |
|  | (count(./preceding-sibling::Field_1__c[text() != ''] | ./preceding-sibling::Field_2__c[text() |
|  | != ''] | ./preceding-sibling::Field_3__c[text() != '']) + 1) < (count(//Field_1__c[text() != ''] | |
|  | //Field_2__c[text() != ''] | //Field_3__c[text() != '']) - 1)" /> #>, <# <EndConditional /> #> |
|  | <# <Conditional Test="(count(./preceding-sibling::Field_1__c[text() != ''] | ./preceding-sibling:: |
|  | Field_2__c[text() != ''] | ./preceding-sibling::Field_3__c[text() != '']) + 1) = |
|  | (count(//Field_1__c[text() != ''] | //Field_2__c[text() != ''] | //Field_3__c[text() != '']) - 1)" /> #>, |
|  | and <# <EndConditional /> #><# <EndRepeat /> #> |
```

title: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
Merge the current date into a document
text:
Add the TODAY() formula field to the Salesforce parent object
(Account, Opportunity, and so on.)

Note: You can hide this from page
layouts.

title: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
Merge owner information
text:
Add a formula field like Owner.FirstName&"
"&Owner.LastName to the Salesforce parent object (Account,
Opportunity, and so on.)

Note: You can hide this from page
layouts.

title: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
Insert an image
text:
Use this example to insert an image into a document:

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | <# <Image Select="string('https://<<IMAGE ADDRESS>>.png')" Height="2" Width="6" Optional="true"/> #> |
```

title: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Learn About Using XML to Customize a Word-Based Gen Template
Store an image in a Salesforce field with a URL
text:
```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | <# <Image Select="/Opportunity/MainCompetitors__c " Height="2" Width="4" |
|  | Optional="true"/> #> |
```

Note: Use inches for the Height and Width
numbers. The URL, Height, and Width specifications here are examples. Replace these
with dimensions from your own images.

title: Create a One-Click Generation and Sending Experience
heading_path: Create a One-Click Generation and Sending Experience
text:
Learn to build a one-click generation and sending experience for generating a contract
and sending it for signature.

title: Create a One-Click Generation and Sending Experience
heading_path: Create a One-Click Generation and Sending Experience
Before you begin
text:
- Install Docusign Apps Launcher version 5.3 or higher
- [Create a Docusign
  envelope template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uce1617376465289.html&_LANG=enus)
- [Create a Gen template with Microsoft
  Word](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=lig1586134209279.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a Gen template using a Microsoft Word document.")

You can build a combined document generation and sending experience. A
generation and sending experience lets your users generate a document and send it
for signature with one click.

Example: As an administrator, you create a Docusign envelope template. You create a Gen template and link to
your envelope template on the Gen template Create Button page. You add a Gen custom
button to a layout.

A user visits a record and selects the Gen button. Docusign
generates an agreement with that record’s data and sends it to all envelope template
recipients.

When all recipients complete the signing process, Docusign saves the agreement to your Docusign account and Salesforce
record.

Note: You can only link templates built on the same Salesforce
object. Linking a Gen template and envelope template built on
Opportunity works. Linking an
Opportunity-based Gen template with an
Account-based envelope template does not work.

Use this procedure to build a generation and sending experience:

1. Change the sending experience of your envelope template to send
   now:
   1. [Access the envelope template Options page as a
      Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=ete1620167625547.html&_LANG=enus).
   2. Sending Experience: Select Send
      Now.
   3. Select Next.
   4. Select Save and Close.
2. Link your envelope template to your Gen template:
   1. [Open the Create Custom
      Button page as a Salesforce
      administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=way1621663319299.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to access the Create Button step of a Microsoft Word-based Gen template.").
   2. Select Launch automatically after
      generation.
   3. Select an envelope template to streamline sending for
      signature: Enter and select the name of a
      send now envelope template.![A screenshot shows Launch automatically after generation selected and the Fontara Mobile NDA template linked.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-shows-Launch-automatically-after-generation-selected-and-the-Fontara-Mobile-Sales-envelope-template-linked.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0MzMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Q1zUyXf6YEG6PDSQWEc8viQMfKs2S7EA4_xooyyWcTI&_LANG=enus "A screenshot shows Launch automatically after generation selected and the Fontara Mobile NDA template linked.")
   4. Select Save and Finish.

   The Create Button page closes.

You have completed the process. You have linked your Gen template
to a send now envelope template. When users select the custom
button, Docusign generates a document and sends it to the envelope
template recipients.

title: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
heading_path: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
text:
Learn to fix the "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID"
error.

Salesforce users are having problems with Gen. The "Could not generate document [100] error
occurred reference ID" error displays in the following scenarios:

- Administrators upload new documents to a Gen template
- Users try to generate agreements
- Users try to preview agreements

title: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
heading_path: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
Resolution 1: Audit Field Changes in Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Cause: If you change or delete Salesforce fields that are present in
your Gen template, the template fails.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Use [Field History Tracking](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.tracking_field_history.htm&type=5) in your Salesforce organization to
   see if fields have been changed or deleted.
2. If fields have been changed or deleted, update your Gen template:
   1. [Remove all fields that no longer
      exist](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=vxs1586134339522.html&_LANG=enus) in your Salesforce organization.
   2. [Update all fields that changed](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=irc1586134297835.html&_LANG=enus) in your Salesforce organization.
   3. [Remove and update the fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ffk1586134297621.html&_LANG=enus) on your Gen template Word document.
   4. [Upload the new version of your Word
      document.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=gxl1586134297397.html&_LANG=enus).

   You have completed the process. You have updated your Gen template to
   reflect changes or deletions to your Salesforce fields.

title: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
heading_path: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
Resolution 2: Paste Your Template Content Into a New Word Document
text:
Cause: Gen templates sometimes have hidden formatting in their Word
documents, like macros or hidden XML data. These hidden elements can prevent the template
from generating agreements. Pasting all text from your Word document into a new,
unformatted Word document fixes issues with hidden formatting.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Edit the Gen template Upload Documents page as a Salesforce
   administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=nyj1621662912687.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Select a document name.

   The document preview page opens in a new tab or window.
3. Select Download and open the document in the Microsoft Word
   program.
4. Paste the template's text into a blank Word document:
   1. Copy all document content from your Gen template's Word document.
   2. Create a new blank Word document and paste the copied content.
   3. Save the new Word document to your device. Use the same file name as the
      original Gen template document.
5. Return to the Gen Upload Documents page and select
   X next to the old document version. ![A screenshot of the Upload Your Word Templates section displays the Generated File Name, File Suffix, and File Format fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/deletewordtemplate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.eUaKjJTpdfQIksVRtig-pf3P7omLler9pAGwSS-1uJY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload Your Word Templates section displays the Generated File Name, File Suffix, and File Format fields.")

   A message displays: File was successfully
   deleted.
6. Select Upload Files and follow the prompts to upload the new
   version from your device.

   A message displays: File was successfully uploaded.
7. Select Save and Close.
8. Select Finish and choose Save in the
   Do you want to save changes? dialog box.

   You have completed the process. Your old Gen template has been replaced with
   the new template, which has all the same content, but no hidden formatting.

title: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
heading_path: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
Resolution 3: Restore System Administrator Status to Your Integration User
text:
Cause: The Salesforce administrator credentials that you use to
connect Docusign and Salesforce is called the integration user. Follow
this link for more information: [Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.")

If you revoke the administrator status of your integration user, the integration fails
and Gen does not work.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. As an administrator, select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.eUaKjJTpdfQIksVRtig-pf3P7omLler9pAGwSS-1uJY&_LANG=enus) and choose
   Setup.
2. Enter and select Users in the Quick Find field.
3. Restore System Administrator status to the installing Salesforce user. Or use
   different Salesforce administrator credentials to [reconnect the accounts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.").

   You have completed the process. You have restored the access of the
   integration user.

title: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
heading_path: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
Resolution 4: Check Your Salesforce Storage
text:
Cause: Gen fails if you don't have enough storage available in your
Salesforce organization. When users generate agreements, document creation fails at the
Preview step. One of the following error messages display:

- There were some problems with your document generation. Unable to generate
  the following files: <file name>
- Unable to create a new ContentVersion for the file name : <file name>
  and SfContentDocumentId.

Use this procedure to check the issue:

1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.eUaKjJTpdfQIksVRtig-pf3P7omLler9pAGwSS-1uJY&_LANG=enus) and choose Setup.
2. Enter and select Storage Usage in the Quick Find field.
3. Check the Storage Type section.

   Note: If the Percent Used column adds up to
   100%, you are out of storage.

   You have completed the process. You have checked the storage capacity of
   your Salesforce organization. If there is not enough storage, delete data or contact
   your Salesforce representative to purchase additional storage.

title: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
heading_path: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
Resolution 5: Check Your Salesforce API Limits
text:
Cause: Gen fails if you have reached your organization's API limit.
When users generate agreements, document creation fails at the Preview step.

Use this procedure to check the issue:

1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.eUaKjJTpdfQIksVRtig-pf3P7omLler9pAGwSS-1uJY&_LANG=enus) and choose Setup.
2. Enter and select System Overview in the Quick Find field.
3. API Usage: Check to see if you have reached your limit.

   Note: If the column adds up to 100%, you have reached your
   limit.

   You have completed the process. You have checked the API limits of your
   Salesforce organization. If you have reached the limit, wait until your levels reset
   or contact your Salesforce representative to increase limits.

title: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
heading_path: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
Resolution 6: Save Your Word Document in the Correct Format
text:
Cause: You can only use .PDF or .DOCX file types in Gen templates.
If a Gen template document is saved as .DOC or another unsupported format, the template
fails.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Edit the Gen template Upload Documents page as a Salesforce
   administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=nyj1621662912687.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Select a document name.

   The document preview page opens in a new tab or window.
3. Select Download and open the document in the Microsoft Word
   program.
4. Open your device's Downloads folder and verify that the file extension is
   .DOCX.
5. If the file is in a different format, open it and save a copy in .DOCX
   format.
6. Return to the Gen Upload Documents page and select
   X next to the old document version.![A screenshot of the Upload Your Word Templates section displays the Generated File Name, File Suffix, and File Format fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/deletewordtemplate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.eUaKjJTpdfQIksVRtig-pf3P7omLler9pAGwSS-1uJY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the Upload Your Word Templates section displays the Generated File Name, File Suffix, and File Format fields.")

   A message displays: File was successfully
   deleted.
7. Select Upload Files and follow the prompts to upload the new
   version from your device.

   A message displays: File was successfully uploaded.
8. Select Save and Close.
9. Select Finish and choose Save in the
   Do you want to save changes? dialog box.

   You have completed the process. You have replaced your old Gen template with
   a .DOCX version.

title: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
heading_path: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
Resolution 7: Disconnect and Reconnect Docusign and Salesforce
text:
Cause: Your connection with Docusign might be
broken. If so, reset the connection and preserve your user settings.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.eUaKjJTpdfQIksVRtig-pf3P7omLler9pAGwSS-1uJY&_LANG=enus) and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/TroubleShooting-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.eUaKjJTpdfQIksVRtig-pf3P7omLler9pAGwSS-1uJY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.

   The TroubleShooting page opens.
4. Disconnect Account and Preserve User Settings: Select
   Execute.

   A message displays: Successfully logged out from Docusign
   account. The Troubleshooting page opens, displaying the message: No Docusign account connected.
5. Follow the instructions to [Connect Salesforce and Docusign](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=oks1610512362945.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disconnect Docusign from Salesforce while preserving user settings with the Docusign TroubleShooting tool."), using
   different Docusign administrator credentials.

   You have completed the process. You have updated the connection between your
   Salesforce organization and your Docusign account.

title: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
heading_path: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
Resolution 1: Check If the User Is Active
text:
Cause: If a Gen user has not activated their account, the document
generation process fails. To have Active status, users must create a Docusign password, and authorize the Gen app upon first use.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Open Docusign Apps Launcher as
   an administrator.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to open the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package in your organization.")
2. Select User Management in the left column.
3. Check the Status column.
4. Inform Pending users that they must [activate their user accounts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate your Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.").

   You have completed the process. When the user completes activation, they can
   generate agreements from Salesforce records.

title: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
heading_path: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
Resolution 2: Grant the Necessary Field-Level Security
text:
Cause: Docusign for Salesforce users require at
least Read access for all Gen field-level security entries.

Add Edit field-level security for users who must add, edit, and
delete Gen templates.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Open the user's Profile page as an administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.eUaKjJTpdfQIksVRtig-pf3P7omLler9pAGwSS-1uJY&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Profiles in the Quick Find field.
   3. Profile Name: Select the user's profile.

      ![A screenshot displays the Profiles page with the standard user link selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforceeditprofile.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.eUaKjJTpdfQIksVRtig-pf3P7omLler9pAGwSS-1uJY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Profiles page with the standard user link selected.")

   The Profiles page displays.
2. Field-Level Security: Select View next
   to a Salesforce object containing a Gen button.

   The Field-Level Security page displays.
3. Update field-level security for the profile:
   1. Select Edit.
   2. Select the Read Access check box next to all field types
      that Gen accesses.
   3. (Optional) Edit Access: Select these Gen field check
      boxes to enable add, edit, and delete access to Gen templates.
   4. Select Save.

      ![A screenshot displays the Field-Level Security page with the Save button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genreadaccess.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.eUaKjJTpdfQIksVRtig-pf3P7omLler9pAGwSS-1uJY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Field-Level Security page with the Save button highlighted.")

   You have completed the process. You have granted Read or Edit field-level
   access for the user. The user can generate agreements or add, edit, and delete Gen
   templates.

title: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
heading_path: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
Resolution 3: Grant "Enabled Custom Setting Definitions" Permissions to Generating Users
text:
Cause: Docusign for Salesforce users require
Enabled Custom Setting Definitions access to use Gen. If a user
cannot use Gen, add their profile to the Enabled Custom Setting
Definitions list.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Open the Permission Sets page as an administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.eUaKjJTpdfQIksVRtig-pf3P7omLler9pAGwSS-1uJY&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Permission Sets in the Quick Find
      field.

   The Permission Sets page displays.
2. Open **Docusign Document Generator** Permission Sets page:
   1. Select **Docusign Document Generator**.
   2. Select Custom Setting Definitions.
   3. Select Manage Assignments.

   The DocuSign Document Generator page
   opens.
3. Add the user to the permission set: Salesforce Profile to the **Enabled Custom
   Setting Definitions** list.
   1. Select Manage Assignments.
   2. Select Add Assignment.
   3. Select the check box next to the user.
   4. Select Next.
   5. Select Assign.
   6. Select Done.

   You have completed the process. You have granted Docusign
   Gen for Salesforce access to the user.

title: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
heading_path: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
Resolution 4: Have the User Change Their Locale
text:
Cause: Generation fails when Gen does not support your Salesforce
locale. Have the user switch to a known-supported locale to see if this resolves their
issue.

Have your affected user follow this procedure to fix their issue:

1. Access your Salesforce Language & Time Zone
   settings:
   1. Select the Salesforce user icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforceusericon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.eUaKjJTpdfQIksVRtig-pf3P7omLler9pAGwSS-1uJY&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Settings.
   2. Select Language & Time Zone.
2. Locale: Select English (United
   States) and select Save.

   A message displays: Your settings have been successfully
   saved.
3. Empty your browser cache and restart the browser.
4. If switching to English (United States) resolves the issue, switch to a
   commonly-used locale with the same language. For example, if your locale is French
   (Guinea) try French (France).
5. Select Save.

   The affected user has completed the process. They have updated their locale
   to one that Gen supports.

title: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
heading_path: "Could Not Generate Document [100] Error Occurred Reference ID" Displays When I Try to Generate or Preview a Document
Resolution 5: Remove and Re-Add the User
text:
Cause: If a Salesforce user has persistent issues accessing Docusign apps and features, try re-adding the user.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Open](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to open the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package in your organization.") Docusign Apps Launcher as
   an administrator.
2. [Remove the user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=tha1628110439784.html&_LANG=enus).
3. [Re-add the user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=uwf1586134275195.html&_LANG=enus).
4. Inform Pending users that they must [activate their user accounts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate your Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.").

   You have completed the process. When the user completes activation, they can
   generate agreements from Salesforce records.

title: Reset Folder Permissions Using the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Reset Folder Permissions Using the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn how to reset folder permissions for Docusign content with the
Docusign TroubleShooting tool.

Administrators can use the Docusign Apps Launcher
TroubleShooting tool to fix issues with Docusign apps. The TroubleShooting tool is available in Docusign Apps Launcher version 2.5 and higher. Salesforce
administrators with Docusign administrator permissions can use it.

You can reset folder permissions for Docusign content in your Salesforce
organization using the Folder Permissions feature of the Docusign TroubleShooting tool. Administrators can select one
button and quickly reset all Docusign folders to their default
permissions, removing all custom security changes.

Use this procedure to use the Folder Permissions feature:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MDEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.eboqK2i671lvrqsBdF8q_yEWvnmRhQi02L1Wjz4NRdg&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting in the Label
   column.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/oas1598330401772.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MDEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.eboqK2i671lvrqsBdF8q_yEWvnmRhQi02L1Wjz4NRdg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link highlighted on the Salesforce Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.
4. Disconnect Account and Reset Users: Select
   Execute.

   The page refreshes.

You have completed the process. You have cleared all custom folder
permissions for your Docusign content.

title: Upload a New Version of a Word Document in Your Gen Template
heading_path: Upload a New Version of a Word Document in Your Gen Template
text:
Learn to update your Gen template Word documents.

When you add, edit, or delete Gen template fields, you must update the associated Word
document.

Example: You add the Opportunity ID field to
your Gen template. You download the Gen template Word document and paste the
Opportunity ID anchor text. You remove the old Word document from
your Gen template and upload the new version.

Use this procedure to update your Word document to reflect changes to your Gen
template:

1. [Edit the Gen template Upload Documents page as a
   Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=nyj1621662912687.html&_LANG=enus):
2. Select a document name.

   The document preview page opens in a new tab or window.
3. Select Download and [update
   your Salesforce data fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ffk1586134297621.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to paste your Salesforce anchor text fields into a Microsoft Word document.").
4. Return to the Gen Upload Documents page and select
   X next to the old document version.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/deletewordtemplate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1NjYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.tCAyIEN64lHGIpD6f1LwD4ce7URAX1ZDPb8Ykj4hgoU&_LANG=enus)

   A message displays: File was successfully
   deleted.
5. Select Upload Files and follow the prompts to upload the new
   version from your device.

   A message displays: File was successfully uploaded.
6. Select Save and Close.
7. Select Finish and choose Save in the
   Do you want to save changes? dialog box.

You have completed the process. You have uploaded a new
version of your Gen Word document.

title: Change the Owner of a Gen Template
heading_path: Change the Owner of a Gen Template
text:
Learn to change the owner of a Gen template to a different Salesforce user.

You can change the owner of a Gen template to a different user in your Salesforce
organization.

Use this procedure to change Gen template ownership:

1. Select Docusign Gen Templates.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0NTAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.1aGPBjBezxaet69z2oowm57tv3A0IpFH9ap1YuZ0noQ&_LANG=enus) next to a Gen template and choose Change
   Owner from the dropdown menu.
3. Add a new owner from the Change Owner dialog box:
   1. Search Users: Enter the name of a Salesforce
      user.
   2. (Optional) Send notification email: Select this
      check box to notify the user that they are the new owner of the Gen
      template.
   3. Select Submit.

   A message displays: (New Salesforce user) now owns the record
   for (Gen template name).

You have completed the process. You have given ownership of
the Gen template to a new Salesforce user.

title: Use Hyperforce with Docusign Apps Launcher
heading_path: Use Hyperforce with Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Learn to add domains to your allowlist so that Docusign apps work with
your Hyperforce organization.

[Hyperforce](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=000388902&type=1) is a Salesforce infrastructure architecture
that is built for the public cloud. Hyperforce infrastructure is composed of code
rather than hardware. Hyperforce uses enhanced standards for compliance, security,
agility, scalability, and privacy.

You must update your Salesforce security settings to use Docusign apps in
your Hyperforce organization. Otherwise, Salesforce denies connections to your Docusign account and causes the integration to fail.

You can update your security settings by adding either IP ranges or
domains to your allowlist.

If you want to allow IP addresses, use the following ranges:

- [Salesforce](https://ip-ranges.salesforce.com/ip-ranges.json)
- [Docusign eSignature](https://www.docusign.com/trust/security/esignature)
- [Docusign CLM](https://www.docusign.com/trust/security/clm)

However, due to the dynamic nature of cloud environments, Salesforce recommends allowing
domains, rather than IP ranges.

Use this procedure to add domains to your allowlist:

1. Access the Trusted URLs page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYzMTQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.uGHQ4F0O7iwFq6mF88el_XImakXijwy2LfOW5sNmYX8&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Trusted URLs in the Quick Find
      field.

   The Trusted URLs page opens.
2. Add a trusted URL to the list:
   1. Select New Trusted URL.
   2. API Name: Enter a simple display name, like
      salesforce for salesforce.com.
   3. URL: Enter
      https://\*.salesforce.com.
   4. Select Save.![A screenshot displays the API Name and URL fields of the Trusted URLs page filled with Salesforce domain information.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-API-Name-and-URL-fields-of-the-Trusted-URLs-page-filled-with-salesforce-domain-information.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYzMTQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.uGHQ4F0O7iwFq6mF88el_XImakXijwy2LfOW5sNmYX8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the API Name and URL fields of the Trusted URLs page filled with Salesforce domain information.")
3. Repeat the process to add the following domains:

   - https://\*.force.com
   - https://\*.lightning.com
   - https://\*.visualforce.com
   - https://\*.docusign.com
   - https://\*.docusign.net
4. (Optional) [Add domains from this list](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=xcloud.setup_domains.htm&type=5) if you
   control user or server access to the internet through allowlists.
5. (Optional) For IP-restricted user profiles. To access Docusign
   Apps Launcher, Salesforce users must have valid authorization to the Docusign connected app.
   1. Copy all restricted Hyperforce IPs from your login history.
   2. Add the restricted Hyperforce IPs to your allowlist.

   You have added Hyperforce IPs to your allowlist. For the complete list of
   Hyperforce IPs, visit the [Salesforce Help site](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=release-notes.rn_hyperforce_inboud_ips.htm&release=260&type=5).

You have completed the process. You have added domains to
your allowlist. You can use Docusign Apps Launcher apps in your
Hyperforce organization.

title: "You Must Have Docusign Billing Admin Permissions to Perform This Operation" Displays When I Try to Create a Gen for Billing Template
heading_path: "You Must Have Docusign Billing Admin Permissions to Perform This Operation" Displays When I Try to Create a Gen for Billing Template
text:
Learn to fix the "You must have Docusign Billing Admin permissions to
perform this operation" error.

I am a Salesforce and Docusign administrator with a Gen for Salesforce
Billing license. An error displayed when I built a Gen template: You must
have Docusign Billing Admin permissions to perform this operation.
Why can't I build a Gen for Salesforce Billing template?

title: "You Must Have Docusign Billing Admin Permissions to Perform This Operation" Displays When I Try to Create a Gen for Billing Template
heading_path: "You Must Have Docusign Billing Admin Permissions to Perform This Operation" Displays When I Try to Create a Gen for Billing Template
Resolution: Assign Your Salesforce User to the Docusign Billing Admin Permission Set
text:
Cause: Your administrator user is not a member of the **Docusign Billing Admin** permission set.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Open the **Docusign Billing Admin** permission set
   page as an administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.vS573GKfL-3A3mjSjrviEnGb9q3sEkLQzPzhAvMpaKw&_LANG=enus) and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Permission Sets in the
      Quick Find field.
   3. Select a Salesforce object.
   4. Select Permission Sets.
   5. Select **Docusign Billing Admin** from the
      Permission Set Label column.

   The Docusign Billing Admin permission set page
   displays.
2. Select Manage Assignments.
3. Select Add Assignment.
4. Select the check box next to your user name and select
   Next.
5. Select Assign and select
   Done.

   You have completed the process. You have added yourself to the
   **Docusign Billing Admin** permission set.
   You can create a Gen for Billing template.

title: Create Document Rules to Add Conditional Logic to a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Create Document Rules to Add Conditional Logic to a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn about using document rules to add conditional logic to a Gen template.

Administrators can create document rules to add conditional logic to Gen templates. Conditional
logic uses document rules to customize generated agreements. You can use the following
types of logic in a document rule:

- Conditional document generation: Load different versions of a Gen
  template based on fields in a Salesforce record.
- Match: Generate content when fields are present on a Salesforce
  record.
- Does Not Match: Generate content when fields are not present on a
  Salesforce record.

![A screenshot displays the document rule dialog box with if, and if, and then rules.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-document-rule-dialog-box-with-if-and-if-and-then-rules.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1NzAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.wmXjPJgA4lK8xLv7IRQiXiKT00f-GU5Xhmf8iKIUhCE&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the document rule dialog box with if, and if, and then rules.")

- [Learn About Using Document Rules](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=itz1588421826562.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about using document rules to set conditional logic in Gen templates.")
- [Create a Document Rule](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=djo1614894478713.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create a document rule to enable conditional document generation in a Gen template:")
- [Learn About Adding Conditional Logic to a Gen Template
  Word Document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=nnl1586134232740.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about adding conditional logic blocks to Gen template Word documents.")
- [Use "Match" Conditional Logic](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=vvu1586134344292.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create \"match\" conditional logic to add or remove text when a Salesforce field is present.")
- [Use "Does Not Match" Conditional Logic](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=hvx1586134344563.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create \"does not match\" conditional logic to add or remove text when a Salesforce field is not present.")

title: Edit a Gen Button
heading_path: Edit a Gen Button
text:
Learn to edit the label or details of a Gen custom button.

Use this procedure to edit the label or details of a
Gen custom button:

1. Access the Buttons, Links, and Actions page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0NTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.uTxc2He8Txd72UrKsD87fvwWNhCfo5xioxmvYNoec30&_LANG=enus) icon and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Object Manager in the Quick Find
      field.
   3. Select an object (Account, Opportunity, and so on) in the left navigation
      bar.
   4. Select Buttons, Links, and Actions.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0NTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.uTxc2He8Txd72UrKsD87fvwWNhCfo5xioxmvYNoec30&_LANG=enus) next to a custom button and choose Edit from the
   dropdown menu.

   ![A screenshot displays the Buttons, Links, and Actions page with two custom button links.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/gendeletecustombutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0NTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.uTxc2He8Txd72UrKsD87fvwWNhCfo5xioxmvYNoec30&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Buttons, Links, and Actions page with two custom button links.")
3. Edit Label, Name, or other fields in the
   custom button detail page and select Save.

   ![A screenshot displays the custom button detail page with all available fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/geneditcustombutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0NTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.uTxc2He8Txd72UrKsD87fvwWNhCfo5xioxmvYNoec30&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the custom button detail page with all available fields.")

You have completed the process. You have edited the
custom button.

title: Remove Docusign App Access From a Salesforce User
heading_path: Remove Docusign App Access From a Salesforce User
text:
Learn to remove Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature access from Salesforce
users.

You can use the Remove command to remove Docusign app access from
Salesforce users.

- Remove removes a Salesforce user's access to Docusign apps. This command does not remove users from the connected Docusign account or
  delete Salesforce users.
- You can not remove and close your user while logged on.
- You can not remove and close the only eSignature administrator user.

Use this procedure to remove Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature access from
Salesforce users:

1. Select the check box next to each user you want to remove.
2. Select Remove from the Add
   Users
   ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/CLM-Files-Lightning-action-arrow.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyNTIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.mTdWzxS0m3sfINHzMdDteUDr7oHVBsaviXqDoUi6hA8&_LANG=enus) drop-down menu.
3. Select Remove.

You have completed the process. You removed a user's access to the
Gen, Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce apps. The user remains in the Salesforce
organization and the connected Docusign account.

title: Use the CLM Tab of the CLM Document Generation Page
heading_path: Use the CLM Tab of the CLM Document Generation Page
text:
Learn to use the CLM tab of the CLM Document Generation page.

When you go to Docusign Setup >  Document Generation, you see the following tabs:

- CLM: Links and information about CLM Document Generation
  features.
- Docusign Gen: Links and information about how to create, edit, and
  delete Docusign Gen templates.

This guide demonstrates how to access and use the CLM tab:

1. Select the CLM tab:![A screenshot displays the CLM tab of the CLM Document Generation page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/CLM-Document-Generation-tab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgyNzQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.4nDkz4I7KJjjE989zYonNuA2HUs5R5nqV5hTjNK1bz8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the CLM tab of the CLM Document Generation page.")
2. Access any of these links or buttons:

   - Learn how to generate documents in Docusign CLM: Select this link
     to open the Components page.
     - Folder Portal: Open the Salesforce Object
       Manager page to add the folder portal component to Salesforce
       layouts.
     - CLM for Salesforce Experience Cloud
       (Communities): Set up a Docusign integration user for your Experience Cloud sites.
   - Learn how to add a button to a Salesforce Object page: Open the
     Components page.
   - Open Docusign CLM: Open your connected Docusign
     CLM account.
   - Open Object Manager: Open the Salesforce Object Manager page to add
     CLM Document Generation buttons to Salesforce layouts.
   - Go to Document Generation: Open the Document Generation page of
     your connected Docusign CLM account.

You have completed the process. You have explored the
features of the CLM tab.

title: Gen Word Template Options: Add Fields Step
heading_path: Gen Word Template Options: Add Fields Step
text:
Learn about the options that you can use in the Add Fields step of the Gen template
creation process.

This guide demonstrates the options that you can use in the Add Fields step of the Gen template
creation process:

- [Access the Gen Template Add Fields Step](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=was1621662565775.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to access the Add Fields step in a Word-based Gen template.")
- [Delete Salesforce Fields from a Word-Based Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=vxs1586134339522.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove data fields from a Word-based Gen template.")
- [Edit Salesforce Data Fields in a Word-Based Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=rcq1586134340244.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to edit data fields in your Gen template configuration.")
- [Use Conditional Logic in a Word-Based Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=pwd1586134341196.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use Salesforce record fields to trigger conditional logic that displays or hides content in your document.")
- [Learn About Setting the Default Format of
  Currency, Date, and Time Data Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ysz1686866281515.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the default formatting that you can apply to data fields in a Gen template.")
- [Set the Default Currency Formatting of Gen Data
  Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=any1686940255489.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set the default formatting of currency data fields.")
- [Set the Default Date and Time (Locale) Formatting
  of Gen Data Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=gyv1686944628002.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to set the default formatting of date and time (locale) data fields.")
- [Edit the Currency and Locale Settings of Your Gen
  Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=kqf1686095608923.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a default currency and locale to your Gen template.")
- [Learn About Adding Child Objects to a Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=cfa1586134349276.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about adding up to four levels of child objects and two levels of related lists.")
