- [Learn About Using SOQL-Based Table Filtering and Sorting in Gen](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=mrr1588510303522.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about inserting Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) code into Gen templates.")
- [Use SOQL Code to Filter and Sort Gen Data](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=dra1737744175418.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add Salesforce Object Query Language (SOQL) code to filter and sort date in Gen templates.")
- [Exception for Gen Template SOQL Sorting Rules](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ndk1614892150271.html&_LANG=enus "Learn about the scenario where sorting works differently than the standard SOL sorting rules.")

title: "This Page Has an Error. You Might Just Need to Refresh It" Displays When I Open Docusign for Salesforce Apps
heading_path: "This Page Has an Error. You Might Just Need to Refresh It" Displays When I Open Docusign for Salesforce Apps
text:
Learn to fix the "This page has an error. You might just need to refresh it." error that
displays.

When I open Docusign apps in Salesforce, an error displays. The
error message reads: This page has an error. You might just need to
refresh it. Error in $A.getCallback() [TypeError: d is not a function] Failing
descriptor: {ltng:outApp}. I cannot open Docusign
apps in Salesforce.

title: "This Page Has an Error. You Might Just Need to Refresh It" Displays When I Open Docusign for Salesforce Apps
heading_path: "This Page Has an Error. You Might Just Need to Refresh It" Displays When I Open Docusign for Salesforce Apps
Resolution: Upgrade your version of Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Cause: You have Docusign Apps Launcher
version 5.4 or lower installed in your organization.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Check your Docusign Apps Launcher version number:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1MTQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.I86yHSzsqWi3UWpZIwoj3XlGnIToVx9k8_it-cYrJg8&_LANG=enus)
      and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Installed Packages in
      the Quick Find field.
   3. Check your Version Number.
2. If your version number is 5.4 or lower, [upgrade Apps Launcher in your
   Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=nok1698959626724.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to upgrade the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.").
3. Select Save.

   You have completed the process. You have upgraded the Apps
   Launcher package in your Salesforce organization. Docusign apps and services load normally.

title: Install and Configure the Docusign Connected App
heading_path: Install and Configure the Docusign Connected App
text:
Learn to install and configure the Docusign connected app for your Salesforce organization.

Important: You are not required to make any changes if you have taken the
following steps:

- Installed the Docusign connected app
- Set the OAuth Policy to Admin-approved users are
  pre-authorized

Starting in September 2025, Salesforce began enforcing [restrictions for uninstalled connected apps](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=005132365&type=1). These
restrictions impact some organizations using Docusign Apps
Launcher for Salesforce. If you have not installed the Docusign
connected app and assigned permissions, your users cannot access Docusign apps.

Use this procedure to install the Docusign connected app and grant
access to your users:

1. Access the Connected Apps OAuth Usage page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear icon ![A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside it.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyODQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.HwaDMHq1vWBWZrodaabF_C-BKmONSSK86QwHG4r3QY4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a six-point gear with a lightning bolt inside it.") and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Connected Apps OAuth Usage in the
      Quick Find field.

      The Connected Apps OAuth Usage page opens.
2. Install the Docusign connected app:
   1. Docusign: Select
      Install.

      The Install connected app? dialog box
      opens.
   2. Select Install.![A screenshot displays the install button highlighted on the Docusign connected app OAuth Usage page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A_screenshot_displays_the_install_button_highlighted_on_the_Docusign_connected_app_OAuth_Usage_page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyODQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.HwaDMHq1vWBWZrodaabF_C-BKmONSSK86QwHG4r3QY4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the install button highlighted on the Docusign connected app OAuth Usage page.")

      The Docusign Connected App Detail page
      displays.
3. Edit the connected app policies:

   Note: Salesforce and Docusign recommend
   selecting Admin-approved users are pre-authorized.
   Each user must enter credentials to gain Docusign for
   Salesforce app access if you select All users may
   self-authorize.

   1. Select Edit Policies.

      The Connected App Edit page displays.
   2. Permitted Users: Select
      Admin-approved users are
      pre-authorized.![A screenshot displays the permitted users field set to Admin approved users are pre-authorized.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A_screenshot_displays_the_permitted_users_field_set_to_Admin_approved_users_are_pre-authorized.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyODQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.HwaDMHq1vWBWZrodaabF_C-BKmONSSK86QwHG4r3QY4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the permitted users field set to Admin approved users are pre-authorized.")

      A dialog box opens: Enabling this option will result
      in all users currently using this app being denied access. Please
      reference the Connected Apps OAuth Usage Report if you are unsure
      who is using the app.
   3. Select OK.

      The dialog box closes.
   4. Select Save.

title: Install and Configure the Docusign Connected App
heading_path: Install and Configure the Docusign Connected App
text:
is app being denied access. Please
      reference the Connected Apps OAuth Usage Report if you are unsure
      who is using the app.
   3. Select OK.

      The dialog box closes.
   4. Select Save.

      The Docusign Connected App Detail page
      displays.
4. Grant Apps Launcher access to Salesforce users:
   1. Select Manage Profiles or Manage
      Permission Sets at the bottom of the Connected App
      Detail page.![A screenshot displays the OAuth Usage page with the Manage Profiles and Manage Permission Sets buttons highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A_screenshot_displays_the_OAuth_Usage_page_with_the_Manage_Profiles_and_Manage_Permission_Sets_buttons_highlighted_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyODQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.HwaDMHq1vWBWZrodaabF_C-BKmONSSK86QwHG4r3QY4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the OAuth Usage page with the Manage Profiles and Manage Permission Sets buttons highlighted.")
   2. Select the check box for each profile or permission set you want to
      include.
   3. Select Save.

   The Docusign Connected App Detail page
   displays.
5. If your organizations has API Access Control configured, take the following actions:
   1. Enable Allow Visualforce pages to access APIs.
   2. Go to Setup > Permission Sets > your Docusign permission set > Assigned Connected Apps and assign at least one permission set to the Docusign connected app.![A screenshot displays the API Access Control Docusign permission set page with the Assigned Connected Apps link highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/API_Access_Control_Assigned_Connected_Apps.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyODQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.HwaDMHq1vWBWZrodaabF_C-BKmONSSK86QwHG4r3QY4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the API Access Control Docusign permission set page with the Assigned Connected Apps link highlighted.")

You have completed the process. You have installed the Docusign connected
app and assigned permissions to your users. Users can access Docusign
apps in your Salesforce organization. It you selected All users may
self-authorize, your users must [re-authenticate to use Docusign apps](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html).

title: Gen for Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Gen for Salesforce Flow
text:
Learn to build Generate agreement with Docusign actions for
Salesforce Flow.

Important: You must meet the following requirements to trigger Gen from Salesforce flows:

- Install Docusign Apps Launcher v7.7.1 or higher.
- Purchase a Salesforce plan that supports flows and Invocable Apex.

Salesforce Flow is a tool that helps you automate your business processes.
Flow replaces Salesforce Process Builder. [Visit Salesforce for more
information](https://admin.salesforce.com/).

Flow can use any of the following events to generate an agreement with Docusign Gen:

Note: The current API limitation is 100 generations per 10 minutes. If
you want to increase your limit, [contact your Docusign representative](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/How-do-I-contact-DocuSign-Customer-Support) about options.

- Record created
- Record updated
- Platform event message received
- Flow triggered by another Flow
- Date and time from a Schedule-Triggered Flow

title: Gen for Salesforce Flow
heading_path: Gen for Salesforce Flow
Example
text:
You build a Record-Triggered Flow and link it to a Docusign Gen
template. You configure the Flow to trigger agreement generation when a record's stage changes
to Proposal/Price Quote. A Salesforce user changes the stage of the Burlington Textiles opportunity
to Proposal/Price Quote. Salesforce triggers the Docusign Gen template linked to your Flow. Docusign generates an agreement using data from the record.

The following topics demonstrate using Gen with Salesforce flows:

- [Add a Generate Agreement with Docusign Action to a Salesforce Flow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=fvj1713565839079.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a Generate agreement with Docusign action to your Salesforce Flow to trigger document generation.")
- [Change the Docusign Template Used in a Salesforce Flow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=jik1697735894052.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to change the Docusign template or Salesforce object connected to your Flow.")
- [Create a Generate and Send Experience for
  Salesforce Flow](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=kvs1727119250302.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create a Salesforce Flow action that generates an agreement and sends it for Docusign eSignature.")

title: I Cannot Connect Salesforce to Docusign
heading_path: I Cannot Connect Salesforce to Docusign
text:
Learn to fix integration errors that occur when you try to connect Docusign and Salesforce.

I cannot connect Salesforce to my Docusign account. I [followed the standard connection procedure](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.") but it
failed.

title: I Cannot Connect Salesforce to Docusign
heading_path: I Cannot Connect Salesforce to Docusign
Resolution 1: Enable Pop-Ups in Your Browser
text:
Cause: If your browser is set to block all pop-ups, you cannot
connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.

Without pop-ups enabled, administrators cannot enter their Docusign and
Salesforce administrator credentials.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Open your browser settings.
2. Enter exceptions for salesforce.com and
   docusign.com.

   You have completed the process. You have enabled exceptions for
   salesforce.com and docusign.com.

title: I Cannot Connect Salesforce to Docusign
heading_path: I Cannot Connect Salesforce to Docusign
Resolution 2: Confirm That Your Integration User Is an Administrator
text:
Cause: The Salesforce administrator user that you use to connect Docusign and Salesforce is called the integration user. Follow this link
for more information: [Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.")

If you revoke the administrator status of your integration user, the integration fails
and Gen does not work.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

Restore System Administrator status to the installing Salesforce user. Or use
different Salesforce administrator credentials to [reconnect the accounts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.").

You have completed the process. You have restored the access of the
integration user.

title: I Cannot Connect Salesforce to Docusign
heading_path: I Cannot Connect Salesforce to Docusign
Resolution 3: Log out of Any Active Docusign Sessions on Your Browser
text:
Cause: You are logged into two different Docusign
accounts on the same browser.

Example: You log on to **Docusign Account 1**
as an administrator. On another tab, you try to connect Salesforce to **Docusign Account 2** with different Docusign
administrator credentials.

Result: The login session to Docusign Account 1 is
still active. This session prevents the administrator from connecting Salesforce to Docusign Account 2.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Log out of any Docusign accounts.
2. [Connect Docusign and
   Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account.").

   You have completed the process. You have ended any active Docusign sessions and connected accounts.

title: I Cannot Connect Salesforce to Docusign
heading_path: I Cannot Connect Salesforce to Docusign
Resolution 4: Activate Your Expired Gen Trial as a Paid App
text:
Cause: Your 30-day Gen trial has expired.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

Contact your Docusign sales representative to activate Gen as a
paid application.

You have completed the process. You have activated your expired Gen
trial.

title: I Cannot Connect Salesforce to Docusign
heading_path: I Cannot Connect Salesforce to Docusign
Resolution 5: Reset Your Login Session
text:
Cause: Your login session gets stuck and you must reset it.

If you are unable to log out, manually delete Gen Setup Data in Salesforce.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Open the Salesforce Tabs page as an administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MjUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.4xFrTo1XPyuKDeWXJIE5PsDFbxeibnsE0QYL1dk4COY&_LANG=enus) and choose Setup.
   2. Enter and select Tabs in the Quick Find field.
   3. Select New.

      ![A screenshot displays the new button highlighted in the visualforce tabs page.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/gennewcustomtabs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MjUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.4xFrTo1XPyuKDeWXJIE5PsDFbxeibnsE0QYL1dk4COY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the new button highlighted in the visualforce tabs page.")
2. Create a new Docusign Setup tab:
   1. Visualforce Page: Select **Docusign
      Setup** from the dropdown menu.
   2. Tab Label: Enter **Docusign
      Setup**.
   3. Tab Name: Keep DocuSign\_Setup.
   4. Select Next.

      ![A screenshot displays the new visualforce tab page with Docusign Setup highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/gennewvisualforcetab.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MjUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.4xFrTo1XPyuKDeWXJIE5PsDFbxeibnsE0QYL1dk4COY&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the new visualforce tab page with Docusign Setup highlighted.")
3. Select Next on the Add to Profiles page, and
   Save on the Add to Custom Apps page.
4. Select the App Launcher ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MjUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.4xFrTo1XPyuKDeWXJIE5PsDFbxeibnsE0QYL1dk4COY&_LANG=enus) grid and enter **Docusign Setup** in the Search
   apps or items field.
5. Select Setup Data.
6. Delete the Default row.

   You have completed the process. You have deleted the Default row and reset
   the Gen login. This action ends your stuck session. You can log on and connect Docusign and Salesforce.

title: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
heading_path: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
text:
Learn to move Gen templates from one Salesforce organization to another.

I built a Gen template for my Salesforce sandbox organization. I want to use the template
in my production organization without completely rebuilding it.

Can I copy my sandbox Gen template and import it to production?

title: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
heading_path: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
Resolution 1: Create Sandbox Data Tags in Your Production Organization
text:
Cause: Gen templates created in a sandbox do not work in Production.
The sandbox template's Word document contains anchor text tags from the Sandbox
organization.

If sandbox anchor text tags don't exist in production, the Word template finds nothing to
merge and fails.

You can fix the issue by creating the template's sandbox fields in your production
organization.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Edit the Gen template Upload Documents page as a Salesforce
   administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?topicId=nyj1621662912687.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Select a document name.

   The document preview page opens in a new tab or window.
3. Select Download and open the document in the Microsoft Word
   program. Note the Salesforce data field tags that are present.
4. Log out of your sandbox session and log on to your Salesforce production
   organization as an administrator.
5. [Edit the Gen template Add Fields page as a
   Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=was1621662565775.html&_LANG=enus):
6. Add data fields that you are missing from your sandbox Gen template.
7. [Replace the Word document from your production
   Gen template with the sandbox document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sqp1586134339354.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to update your Gen template Word documents.").

   You have completed the process. You have added sandbox data fields to your
   production organization and uploaded the sandbox Word document. You can use the
   sandbox Gen template in your production organization.

title: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
heading_path: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
Resolution 2: Create an Edit Fields Button in Salesforce Production and Sandbox Organizations and Copy/Paste JSON
text:
Cause: You have not enabled Edit Fields buttons in your Salesforce
organizations. You can turn on an option to show Edit Fields buttons on your Gen
templates. You can use these buttons to copy the raw JSON data from sandbox Gen templates
and paste them into production templates.

You can also create a custom button that exposes the JSON code. You can paste that JSON
code from sandbox Gen templates into production templates.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

title: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
heading_path: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
Step 1: Create an Edit Fields Button in Your Sandbox Organization
text:
1. Open the Object Manager page as an administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NDgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.gBW5K8SLS3fLZzg2E0txTOlah6_BiyfkbyY1lSGjjk0&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Object Manager in the Quick Find field.

   The Object Manager page displays.
2. Select **Docusign Gen Template** in the Label column.
3. Select Buttons, Links, and Actions and select New
   Action.
4. Set up a New Action as follows:
   1. Action Type: Choose Update a Record
      from the dropdown menu.
   2. Standard Label Type: Keep
      --None--.
   3. Label: Enter Edit Fields.
   4. Name: Keep Edit\_Fields.
   5. Select Save.

      ![A screenshot displays the Gen template new action page with new action fields highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/gencreateeditfieldsbutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NDgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.gBW5K8SLS3fLZzg2E0txTOlah6_BiyfkbyY1lSGjjk0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Gen template new action page with new action fields highlighted.")

      The Action page opens.
5. Drag all fields to the palette.
6. Select Save.

   ![A screenshot displays Docusign Gen Template fields being dragged to the palette.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/gencreateeditfieldsdrag.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NDgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.gBW5K8SLS3fLZzg2E0txTOlah6_BiyfkbyY1lSGjjk0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Docusign Gen Template fields being dragged to the palette.")

   You have completed Step 1. You have created an Edit Fields button. Move to
   Step 2 to add the new button to your Docusign Gen Template layout.

title: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
heading_path: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
Step 2: Add the Edit Fields Button to Your Sandbox Docusign Gen Template Layout
text:
1. Select Page Layouts on the **Docusign Gen
   Template** page.
2. Select **Docusign Gen Template layout**.
3. Select the Quick Actions menu.
4. Quick Actions in the Salesforce Classic Publisher: Select the
   wrench button that displays.

   ![A screenshot displays Quick Actions in the Salesforce Classic Publisher with a wrench icon highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/geneditfieldswrench.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NDgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.gBW5K8SLS3fLZzg2E0txTOlah6_BiyfkbyY1lSGjjk0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays Quick Actions in the Salesforce Classic Publisher with a wrench icon highlighted.")

   The bar turns blue and exposes a button menu.
5. Drag the Edit Fields button to the Quick Actions
   in the Salesforce Classic Publisher bar.
6. Select Save.

   ![A screenshot displays the Edit Fields button being dragged to the quick actions in the Salesforce classic publisher menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/gencreateeditfieldspagelayout.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NDgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.gBW5K8SLS3fLZzg2E0txTOlah6_BiyfkbyY1lSGjjk0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Edit Fields button being dragged to the quick actions in the Salesforce classic publisher menu.")

   You have completed Step 2. Salesforce adds the Edit Fields button to the
   **Docusign Gen Template** layout. Users can see the button
   when they open the Docusign Gen Templates tab. Move to Step 3 to
   create an Edit Fields button in your Salesforce Production organization.

title: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
heading_path: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
Step 3: Add the Edit Fields Button in Your Production Organization
text:
1. Open another tab in your browser and sign into your Salesforce Production
   organization.
2. Follow the instructions from Step 1 and Step 2 to add the Edit Fields button to
   your production organization. You can paste sandbox Gen template data into these
   production Gen template fields.

   You have completed step 3. Move to Step 4 to expose the JSON in your Sandbox
   organization.

title: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
heading_path: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
Step 4: Expose Sandbox Gen Template Data
text:
1. [Open Docusign Apps Launcher as
   an administrator.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to open the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package in your organization.")
2. Select the **Docusign Gen Templates** tab and select the Gen
   template you want to copy for production.
3. Select Edit Fields. The Edit Fields dialog box opens:

   ![A screenshot displays the edit fields page with object mappings and signer mappings highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/geneditfieldsjson.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NDgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.gBW5K8SLS3fLZzg2E0txTOlah6_BiyfkbyY1lSGjjk0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the edit fields page with object mappings and signer mappings highlighted.")

   You have completed Step 4. This dialog box contains the raw JSON data from
   your sandbox Gen template. These are the Gen template anchor text tags that you can
   paste into your production Gen template. Keep this browser tab open and open a new tab
   for Step 5.

title: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
heading_path: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
Step 5: Create a New Blank Gen Template in Production
text:
1. Open a new browser tab and log on to your production Salesforce organization as an
   administrator.
2. Select the **Docusign Gen Templates** tab and select
   New.
3. Create a basic Gen template:
   1. Add Template Name: Enter a name for your template.
   2. Select Data Source: Select a Salesforce object to import
      data from.
   3. Select Template Editor: Select Microsoft
      Word.
   4. Select Create.
   5. Select Save and Close >  Save.
4. Select the **Docusign Gen Templates** tab and select your new
   Gen template.
5. Select Edit Fields.

   You have completed Step 5. A dialog box opens, displaying the fields from
   your new production Gen template. Keep this browser tab open. Move to Step 6 to paste
   the JSON data from your sandbox template to your production template.

title: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
heading_path: I Want to Clone My Sandbox Gen Template and Use It in Production
Step 6: Paste the Sandbox JSON Data Into Your Production Gen Template
text:
1. Copy the sandbox Object Mappings JSON data to your production
   Gen template:
   1. Select the sandbox Gen browser tab.
   2. Copy the data from the first Object Mappings
      field.
   3. Delete the data from the first production Object
      Mappings field.
   4. Paste the sandbox data into the empty production field.
   5. Repeat these steps for all Object Mappings fields.
2. Copy the sandbox Signer Mappings JSON data to your production
   Gen template:
   1. Select the sandbox Gen browser tab.
   2. Copy the data from the first Signer Mappings
      field.
   3. Delete the data from the first production Signer
      Mappings field.
   4. Paste the sandbox data into the empty production field.
   5. Repeat these steps for all Signer Mappings fields.

   Note: You can also copy and paste
   the sandbox Name, Source Object, and
   Steps Completed data.
3. Select Save.

   ![A screenshot displays the Edit Fields dialog box with sandbox Gen template JSON data highlighted in the production object and signer mappings fields.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/geneditfieldsjson.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NDgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.gBW5K8SLS3fLZzg2E0txTOlah6_BiyfkbyY1lSGjjk0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Edit Fields dialog box with sandbox Gen template JSON data highlighted in the production object and signer mappings fields.")

   You have completed the process. You have created a production Gen template
   with the data from a sandbox template. Now that you have exposed the Edit Fields
   button, you can copy any Gen template between Salesforce organizations.

title: Step 4: Uninstall Apps Launcher from Your organization
heading_path: Step 4: Uninstall Apps Launcher from Your organization
text:
Learn to uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package from your
Salesforce organization.

You have removed all Docusign Apps Launcher dependencies.

Use this procedure to uninstall the Apps Launcher package from your organization:

1. Open the Installed Packages page as an
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1MzEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.TWm9rgX5l3lg_kfijp_SxT2sH93ZSy885cpTO5H_ouw&_LANG=enus) and select
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Installed Packages in the Quick
      Find field.

      The Installed Packages page opens.
2. Select Uninstall next to **Docusign
   Apps Launcher**.

   The Uninstalling a Package page opens.
3. Scroll to the bottom and select Yes, I want to uninstall this
   package and permanently delete all associated components.
4. Select Uninstall. ![A screenshot displays the uninstall button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/daluninstall.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1MzEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.TWm9rgX5l3lg_kfijp_SxT2sH93ZSy885cpTO5H_ouw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the uninstall button highlighted.")

   Note: If an
   Unable to uninstall package message displays, you
   have remaining Docusign dependencies. Remove the listed
   dependencies and repeat the uninstall process.

You have completed the entire process. You have removed
all Docusign Apps Launcher dependencies, and have uninstalled the
package from your Salesforce organization. You can [install the
latest version from the Salesforce AppExchange](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.").

title: Create Docusign for Salesforce Users
heading_path: Create Docusign for Salesforce Users
text:
Learn to create Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature users on the user
management page.

Use this procedure to create Docusign
eSignature, CLM, Gen, or Negotiate users on the user management page. You can only add
permissions for Docusign products you have purchased.

Important: A new user displays a Pending
status [until activated by the user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=iqc1594156517921&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus). After activation, user status displays as Active.

1. Select Add Users.
2. Complete the following tasks in the Select Users dialog box:
   1. Value: Enter a Salesforce user name or email address.
   2. Select the check box next to each Salesforce user that you want to add.
   3. (Optional) Filtered by Filter your search by
      User, Profile, or
      Permission Set.
   4. Select Continue.![A screenshot displays the Select Users dialog box with filters added for User and Profile and a user check box selected.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Use-filters-to-add-a-user.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyMzMsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.CfUSISMade2uC2IoMtrXRDVkFAvx3Vi2TXIIZxeMO80&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Select Users dialog box with filters added for User and Profile and a user check box selected.")
3. Apply Permissions: Select permissions for your users:

   - eSignature:
     - Administrator: Administrators can create templates and
       manage all Docusign users in Salesforce.
     - User: Users can send documents for signature.
   - CLM:
     - Administrator: Administrators can access everything in
       CLM and manage connections with Salesforce. Administrators cannot manage Docusign users in Salesforce.
     - User: Users can access workflows and view, edit, and
       upload documents.
     - Viewer: Viewers can only preview and upload
       documents.
     - No Access: Users have no access to the CLM app.
   - Gen:
     - Generator: Generators can create new documents and
       templates.
     - No Access: Users have no access to the Gen app.
   - Negotiate:
     - Negotiator: Negotiators can collaborate with signers and
       viewers.
     - No Access: Users have no access to the Negotiate
       app.
   - (Optional) Update permissions for any existing users: Select
     to update a user's permissions in the connected Docusign
     account.
4. Select Apply.

   The Summary dialog box opens, displaying the message:
   (User name or number of users) was added.

You have completed the process. You have granted or updated Docusign permissions to Salesforce users.

title: Connect a Docusign Demo Account to Salesforce
heading_path: Connect a Docusign Demo Account to Salesforce
text:
Learn to connect your Salesforce org to your Docusign Demo
account.

title: Connect a Docusign Demo Account to Salesforce
heading_path: Connect a Docusign Demo Account to Salesforce
Before you begin
text:
- [Install Docusign Apps Launcher in Your Salesforce Organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ogj1586134254571.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to install the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.")
- [Create a Docusign Developer account](https://developers.docusign.com/)

To use Docusign applications, you must connect a Docusign account to your Salesforce organization. You create this
connection by entering Salesforce and Docusign administrator
credentials on the Docusign Setup page. The Docusign administrator used to connect accounts is called the
integration user.

Note:

- Docusign recommends that you create a generic integration
  user. For more information, see this topic: [Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.").
- Docusign does not support connecting multiple Salesforce
  organizations to a single Docusign account.

Use this procedure to connect your Salesforce organization to a Docusign
demo account:

1. Complete the following from the Authorize page:
   1. Select Allow Access and choose
      Allow on the dialog box that displays.
   2. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that
      displays and select Log In.

   The Log in to Docusign page displays.
2. Complete the following steps on the Log in to Docusign
   page:
   1. Select Log in to Docusign. The Docusign Login
      dialog box displays.
   2. Select Advanced Options and choose
      Log in to Demo Account.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-Docusign-login-page-with-Advanced-Options-and-Log-in-to-Demo-Account-selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYxNTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.1g6c29o5agJ9XcgZ0_sODT0gKSAiVuwyqYNDaRhJags&_LANG=enus)
   3. Enter Docusign
      *demo* administrator credentials in the dialog box that displays
      and select Log in.
   4. Re-enter Salesforce administrator credentials in the dialog box that
      displays and select Log In.

   The Select an account dialog box displays.
3. Select Account: Choose a Docusign
   account and select Next.

   The Docusign Setup page displays.

You have completed the process. You have connected
your Salesforce organization to your Docusign demo account. You can
set up your Docusign Apps Launcher applications.

title: Step 2: Remove Apps Launcher Components from Your Layouts
heading_path: Step 2: Remove Apps Launcher Components from Your Layouts
text:
Learn to remove all Docusign Apps Launcher components from Salesforce
layouts.

Before you can uninstall Apps Launcher, you must remove all Docusign
components that are present on your layouts.

Use this procedure to remove Docusign components from your layouts:

1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1MjQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.sPNqc1UIsS0w14aAVIOGOhGNxV-DRdFWdbE3A4ExeOg&_LANG=enus)and choose Edit Page from the dropdown
   menu.

   The Lightning App Builder page opens.
2. Remove all Docusign components from the layout:
   1. Select a Docusign component and select the trash icon
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210615-at-123257-AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1MjQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.sPNqc1UIsS0w14aAVIOGOhGNxV-DRdFWdbE3A4ExeOg&_LANG=enus) that displays. ![A screenshot displays the trash icon highlighted on the envelope status component.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-trash-icon-highlighted-for-the-Docusign-Files-component.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1MjQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.sPNqc1UIsS0w14aAVIOGOhGNxV-DRdFWdbE3A4ExeOg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the trash icon highlighted on the envelope status component.")
   2. Select Save.
   3. Repeat this procedure for all Docusign components on
      this layout.

   Salesforce removes the components from the layout.
3. Select the back arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Screen-Shot-20210615-at-13251-AM.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1MjQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.sPNqc1UIsS0w14aAVIOGOhGNxV-DRdFWdbE3A4ExeOg&_LANG=enus) when you have removed all components.
4. Repeat this procedure for each Salesforce layout that contains Docusign Apps Launcher components.

You have completed step two of the process. You have
removed all Docusign Apps Launcher components from your Salesforce
layouts. Move to [Step 3 (Optional): Remove Apps Launcher Custom Buttons from Your Layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=cif1623740064281.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove Docusign Apps Launcher custom buttons from your Salesforce layouts.").

title: Change the Salesforce Data Source of Your Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Change the Salesforce Data Source of Your Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn to change the data source of a Gen template.

Warning: Changing your Gen template data source deletes all
of your template's Salesforce fields. Docusign fields remain unchanged.Docusign recommends creating a new template, rather than changing the data
source of an existing template. If you do want to change the data source of a Gen template,
use this procedure:

1. Select the Select Source arrow. The arrow displays no text until
   selected.
2. Select Salesforce Object and select a new Salesforce object from
   the dropdown menu.
3. Select Next.

You have completed the first part of the process. You
have updated the main data source. Proceed to step two to [add Salesforce anchor text tags](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=lig1586134209279.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a Gen template using a Microsoft Word document.") to the Gen template.

title: Disable Docusign CLM Federated Security
heading_path: Disable Docusign CLM Federated Security
text:
Learn how to disable the Federated Security feature in your Salesforce
organization.

Federated Security can be disabled at any time. Here's how to disable Federated Security in
your Docusign CLM account:

1. Log in to your Docusign CLM account as an administrator.
2. Select the Admin tab.
3. Navigate to Integrations  >  Connected Apps.
4. Uncheck the Enable federated security box under the
   Security header.

   ![Disable Federated Security.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Disable-Federated-Security.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1NjIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.pjLoC5-eK_RQc34Y6vm2jg21R5fJMNfcpuCtvXuhmQw&_LANG=enus "Disable Federated Security.")

   A modal opens, asking: Are you sure you want to disable federated
   security?
5. Select Disable federated security.
6. Select the Save button on the bottom of the page.

The process is complete. Salesforce Federated Security is disabled
on your CLM account. Salesforce and CLM security settings are separate, and
administrators must maintain security models for each.

title: Delete a Word Document from Your Gen Template
heading_path: Delete a Word Document from Your Gen Template
text:
Learn to delete a Word document from your Gen template if you no longer need it.

Use this procedure to delete a Word document from your Gen
template if you no longer need it:

Select the X next to a template document.![A screenshot displays the X button highlighted on a Gen template document.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/deletewordtemplate.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1NDgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.uyFB0NoJEcpeJLJzyv8iwy7M_nOcLl56zVug467No9M&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the X button highlighted on a Gen template document.")

A message displays: File was successfully deleted.

You have completed the process. You have deleted the
template document.

title: Step 1: Add a Name, Data Source, and Template Editor
heading_path: Step 1: Add a Name, Data Source, and Template Editor
text:
Learn to set the main template details: template name, description, and Salesforce data
source (Invoice.)

Use this procedure to enter a template name and choose
Invoice as the Salesforce data source.

Important: To create a Gen for Billing
template, you must select Invoice as the Salesforce data
source.

1. Select Create a Template Configuration at the far right.
2. Complete the following tasks:
   1. Add Template Name: Enter a name for your
      template.
   2. (Optional) Add Template Description: Add details
      about the template.
   3. Select Data Source: Select
      Invoice from the drop-down menu.
   4. Select Template Editor: Select
      Microsoft Word.
   5. Select Create.

You have completed step one. Move to [Step 2: Create Docusign and Salesforce Anchor Text Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=irc1586134297835.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Docusign and Salesforce anchor text fields for your Gen template.").

title: Disconnect Docusign and Salesforce and Remove Users with the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
heading_path: Disconnect Docusign and Salesforce and Remove Users with the Apps Launcher TroubleShooting Tool
text:
Learn how to disconnect a Docusign account and remove users with the
Docusign TroubleShooting tool.

If you want to connect your Docusign account to a different
Salesforce organization, use Disconnect Account and Remove
Users. Docusign removes your connection to
Salesforce and deletes all Docusign for Salesforce users.

Important:
Disconnect Account and Remove
Users does not remove users from your connected Docusign account. This feature performs the following actions:

- Disconnects your Docusign account from your Salesforce
  organization
- Removes Docusign access from all Salesforce users
- Resets custom user settings
- Removes all permission set assignments
- Resets the integration user token stored in the connected CLM account

Follow this procedure to use the Disconnect Account and Remove
Users feature:

1. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzOTQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.A0dQC9_NGVoXrc8k-E2FqPjbY7vvMDHgDs1xfcWxG4Y&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
2. Select TroubleShooting.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/TroubleShooting-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzOTQsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.A0dQC9_NGVoXrc8k-E2FqPjbY7vvMDHgDs1xfcWxG4Y&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.")
3. Select Preview.

   The TroubleShooting page opens.
4. Disconnect Account and Remove Users: Select
   Execute.

   A message opens: Successfully logged out from Docusign account. User custom
   settings and Docusign Apps Launcher Permission set assignments should be
   cleared shortly. The Troubleshooting page displays the message:
   No Docusign Account Connected.

You have completed the process. You have disconnected your Docusign account from your Salesforce organization. You can [connect Salesforce to a different Docusign
account](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?language=en_US&bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=zfp1586134208988.html&_LANG=enus).

title: Learn About Using Conditional XML in the Test Attribute for Check Boxes, Picklists, and Fields
heading_path: Learn About Using Conditional XML in the Test Attribute for Check Boxes, Picklists, and Fields
text:
Learn about using conditional logic with check boxes, picklists, fields, and more
in the Test attribute.

You can add conditional logic blocks to Gen template Anchor Text Tags.
Conditional logic creates "if-then" statements that add or exclude
text from a generated agreement. You can also use conditional logic
with checkboxes, picklists, fields, and more.

Important: Content
is case-sensitive.

title: Learn About Using Conditional XML in the Test Attribute for Check Boxes, Picklists, and Fields
heading_path: Learn About Using Conditional XML in the Test Attribute for Check Boxes, Picklists, and Fields
Check box example:
text:
Only display content if a check box field is marked
True:

```
|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | <# <Conditional Test="//Opportunity/HasOverdueTask[text() = 'True']" /> |
|  | #>CONDITIONAL CONTENT HERE<# <EndConditional /> #> |
```

title: Learn About Using Conditional XML in the Test Attribute for Check Boxes, Picklists, and Fields
heading_path: Learn About Using Conditional XML in the Test Attribute for Check Boxes, Picklists, and Fields
Picklist examples
text:
- Option 1: Only display content if a multi-picklist
  contains certain
  values:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | <# <Conditional Test="//Opportunity/Preferred_Color__c[contains(., ‘Green;Blue’)]" /> #> |
  ```
- Option 2: Only display content if a multi-picklist
  contains certain
  values:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | <# <Conditional Test="//Opportunity/Preferred_Color__c[text() = ‘Green;Blue’]" /> #> |
  ```

title: Learn About Using Conditional XML in the Test Attribute for Check Boxes, Picklists, and Fields
heading_path: Learn About Using Conditional XML in the Test Attribute for Check Boxes, Picklists, and Fields
Field examples
text:
- Only display content if a field matches certain
  values:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | <# <Conditional Test="//Opportunity/Type[text() = 'New Customer' or text() = 'Existing Customer']" |
  |  | /> #>CONDITIONAL CONTENT HERE<# <EndConditional /> #> |
  ```
- Only display content if two fields match certain
  values:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | <# <Conditional Test="//Opportunity/Type[text() = 'New Customer'] and //Opportunity/Industry[text() = |
  |  | 'Technology']" /> #>CONDITIONAL CONTENT HERE<# <EndConditional /> #> |
  ```
- Only display content if at least one item matches
  some
  value:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | <# <Conditional Test="count(//Quote//QuoteLineItems/Base_Product__c[text() = 'Digital Display']) > 0" /> |
  |  | #>CONDITIONAL CONTENT HERE<# <EndConditional /> #> |
  ```
- Only display content if at least one item contains
  certain
  values:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | <# <Conditional Test=“count(//Opportunity//OpportunityLineItems/Name[contains(., ‘Product XYZ’) |
  |  | or contains(., ‘Product ABC’)]) > 0” /> #>CONDITIONAL CONTENT HERE<# <EndConditional /> #> |
  ```
- Only display content if one field (Field 1) is empty
  and another (Field 2) is not
  empty:

  ```
  |  |  |
  | --- | --- |
  |  | <# <Conditional Test="not(//Opportunity/Field_1__c/text()) and //Opportunity/Field_2__c/text()"/> |
  |  | #>CONDITIONAL CONTENT HERE<# <EndConditional /> #> |
  ```

title: I Created New Docusign Users, But They Cannot Use Gen
heading_path: I Created New Docusign Users, But They Cannot Use Gen
text:
Learn to fix issues that prevent new users from generating agreements.

I [added new Docusign users](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=dzl1586134206658.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add Docusign Gen, CLM, Negotiate, and eSignature users and manage permissions on the user management page.") to
our Salesforce organization.

In Docusign User Management, I see a green check mark with a status of
Successful next to both users.

One user has not received a Docusign activation email.

Another has activated their user, but cannot generate agreements.

title: I Created New Docusign Users, But They Cannot Use Gen
heading_path: I Created New Docusign Users, But They Cannot Use Gen
Resolution 1: Reset the Existing User's Password
text:
Cause: If a user has an invalid Docusign password,
Gen actions will fail.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Log in to the connected Docusign account](https://account.docusign.com/#/username) as an
   administrator.
2. Go to Admin >  Users.
3. Select Actions next to the user's name and choose
   Reset Password.

   ![A screenshot displays the reset password option selected in the actions menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genresetpassword.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MjgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.RYelE47L4z9TSin1YUYQUXuM0R9DILA_cpYf0HNrFZg&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the reset password option selected in the actions menu.")

   A message displays: A reset password email has been sent to (user
   name).

   You have completed the process. Docusign sends the user
   [a new activation email](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate your Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.").

title: I Created New Docusign Users, But They Cannot Use Gen
heading_path: I Created New Docusign Users, But They Cannot Use Gen
Resolution 2: Add Generator Permissions to the User
text:
Docusign does not grant Generator permissions to new users. Add
Generator permissions for new users who cannot generate agreements.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Open Docusign Apps Launcher as
   an administrator.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=etv1586134206174.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to open the Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce package in your organization.")
2. Select User Management in the left column.
3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MjgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.RYelE47L4z9TSin1YUYQUXuM0R9DILA_cpYf0HNrFZg&_LANG=enus) next
   to the user and choose Edit Permissions from the dropdown menu.
4. Select Generator from the Docusign Gen
   dropdown menu.
5. Select Apply.![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-the-edit-permissions-page-with-generator-selected.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0MjgsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.RYelE47L4z9TSin1YUYQUXuM0R9DILA_cpYf0HNrFZg&_LANG=enus)

   A message displays: Successfully edited permissions for (user
   name).

   You have completed the process. Docusign sends the user
   [a new activation email](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ymi1586134213877.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to activate your Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce user.").

title: Use Match Conditional Logic in a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Use Match Conditional Logic in a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn to create "match" conditional logic to add or remove text when a Salesforce field
is present.

Use this procedure to create "match" conditional logic to a
template. Match logic adds or removes text when a Salesforce field is present.

1. Select the Add Fields arrow. The arrow has no text until you
   select it.
2. Select Copy![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1ODksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.qL5vqNSzcsR9qDo7-2fVqsZkFL8DkdkpRINzoriSe3A&_LANG=enus)
    next to a field and choose
   Options. ![A screenshot displays the description field with the copy menu open to options and remove.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genconditionaloptionsdropdown.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1ODksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.qL5vqNSzcsR9qDo7-2fVqsZkFL8DkdkpRINzoriSe3A&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the description field with the copy menu open to options and remove.")

   The Table Options dialog box opens.
3. Create match logic:
   1. Select Include other content if the conditions set for this field are
      met.
   2. Select matches.
   3. Enter keywords to match. For example, use matches >  New Customer to only send a welcome letter to your new customers.![A screenshot displays the display options field with the values set to matches and new customer.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genconditionaltagmatch.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY1ODksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.qL5vqNSzcsR9qDo7-2fVqsZkFL8DkdkpRINzoriSe3A&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the display options field with the values set to matches and new customer.")
   4. Select Save.

   The Add Fields page opens.
4. Select Copy next to the anchor text tag you updated.
5. Add the conditional logic to your Gen template:
   1. Open the Word document that you are using in your Gen template.
   2. Paste the copied anchor text into the document.
   3. Add any custom text to the tag. If you use Welcome, new
      customer! as custom text, the following tag displays: `<#
      <Conditional Select="/Opportunity/Description" Match="New Customer" /> #>
      Welcome, new customer! <# <EndConditional/> #>`
6. Select Finish when you are done editing.

   Note: You can create more advanced
   conditional logic than match and does not match. See the Conditionals topics in [Gen Advanced Features](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=pui1586134216490.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to use advanced actions when configuring your Gen templates.") for more information.

You have completed the process. You added
match conditional logic to your Gen template.

title: Step 1: Choose a Name, Data Source, and Template Editor
heading_path: Step 1: Choose a Name, Data Source, and Template Editor
text:
Use this procedure to decide the name, Salesforce data source, and editing tool for the
template.

Use this procedure to decide the name, Salesforce
data source, and editing tool for the template.

1. Select Configure at the far right.
2. Select Create a Template Configuration.
3. Complete the following tasks:
   1. Add Template Name: Enter a name for your template.
   2. (Optional) Add Template Description: Add details about the
      template.
   3. Select Data Source: Select a Salesforce object to import
      data from.
   4. Select Template Editor: Select Microsoft
      Word.
   5. Select Create.

You have completed step one. Move to [Step 2: Create Docusign and Salesforce Anchor Text Fields](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=irc1586134297835.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Docusign and Salesforce anchor text fields for your Gen template.").

title: Delete Salesforce Fields from a Word-Based Gen Template
heading_path: Delete Salesforce Fields from a Word-Based Gen Template
text:
Learn to remove data fields from a Word-based Gen template.

Use this procedure to remove fields from a Word-based
Gen template:

1. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0OTAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Kb3auAyI3EHPWWs62sV5Dc1NP55jKc--JPlzeGn9ox4&_LANG=enus) next to
   Copy.
2. Select Remove from the dropdown menu.![A screenshot displays the Field and Anchor Text fields with the copy, options, and remove dropdown menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-an-anchor-text-field-with-the-copy-menu-open-to-options-and-removepng.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0OTAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Kb3auAyI3EHPWWs62sV5Dc1NP55jKc--JPlzeGn9ox4&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Field and Anchor Text fields with the copy, options, and remove dropdown menu.")
3. Select Next.

   Important: Remove all
   deleted tags from this template's Word document and re-upload the document.

You have completed the first step of the process. You
have deleted the tags. [Remove the tags from this template's
Word document](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sqp1586134339354.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to update your Gen template Word documents.")

title: Access the Gen Template Add Fields Step
heading_path: Access the Gen Template Add Fields Step
text:
Learn to access the Add Fields step in a Word-based Gen template.

Use this procedure to access the Add Fields step of a Word-based Gen template:

1. Edit the Gen template Add Fields page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0ODUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.ijsqTzJH4QqLYKahWp8XK514iloWfNbxHk_VUjN9Dsg&_LANG=enus) and choose View
      All.
   2. Select Docusign Apps Launcher.
   3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0ODUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.ijsqTzJH4QqLYKahWp8XK514iloWfNbxHk_VUjN9Dsg&_LANG=enus) next to a Gen template and choose
      Edit from the dropdown menu.

   The Gen template creation page opens.
2. Select the Add Fields arrow. The arrow displays no text
   until selected.

You have completed the process. You have accessed the
Add Fields step of a Gen template.

title: Access the Gen Word Template Create Button Step
heading_path: Access the Gen Word Template Create Button Step
text:
Learn to access the Create Button step of a Microsoft Word-based Gen template.

Follow this procedure to access the Create Button step of a Microsoft Word-based Gen
template:

Edit a Gen template as a Salesforce administrator:

1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgyNTcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XDT7lsl2Rik-_BVb3aOg7E0MHK0QUtnU1uNx8P86tqA&_LANG=enus) and select View
   All from the dropdown menu.
2. Select Docusign Apps Launcher.
3. Select Docusign Gen Templates.
4. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgyNTcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.XDT7lsl2Rik-_BVb3aOg7E0MHK0QUtnU1uNx8P86tqA&_LANG=enus) next to a Gen template and choose
   Edit from the dropdown menu. The Gen template
   creation page opens.
5. Select the Create Button arrow. The arrow has no
   text until selected.

You have completed the process. You have opened the
Create Button step of a Gen template.

title: Use Repeat Tags in Gen to Import Data from Multiple Salesforce Objects
heading_path: Use Repeat Tags in Gen to Import Data from Multiple Salesforce Objects
text:
Learn to use XML Repeat tags in a Word-based Gen template to import data from multiple
Salesforce objects.

You can use XML Repeat tags in a Word-based Gen template to import data from multiple
Salesforce objects. Repeat tags look for Salesforce field data that exists on more
than one object.

When users generate agreements, Docusign imports field data from each
Salesforce object.

Example: You want to generate agreements that insert a Salesforce
contact that exists on multiple objects. You add Repeat tags to your Gen template to
import data from all instances of certain fields.

A user generates an agreement from your Gen template. The record contains a contact
who is the founder and CEO of a number of companies. She is listed in one parent
account and two child opportunities. Docusign generates an
agreement containing her contact information from all three businesses.

Use this procedure to add Repeat tags to your Gen template:

1. Select a document name.

   The document preview page opens in a new tab or window.
2. Add a table with custom XML to your Word template:
   1. Select Download.
   2. Open the Word document and enter the following XML code: `<#
      <Repeat Select="/Opportunity/Account/Name" /> #> Name:
      <# <Content Select="/Name" /> #><# <EndRepeat/>
      #>`
   3. Add a Repeat tag to import product information along with the name:

      `<# <Repeat Select="/Opportunity/Account/Name" />
      #>`

      Name: `<#
      <Content Select="/Name" />
      #>`

      Age: `<#
      <Content Select= “/Age” />
      #>`

      Companies:

      - `<# <Repeat Select=
        “/Opportunity/Account/Companies” /> #>`
      - Name: `<# <Content Select=
        “/Opportunity/Account/Companies/Name” />
        #>`
      - Products:
        - `<# <Repeat
          Select=”/Opportunity/Account/Companies/CompanyProducts”
          /> #>`
        - Name: `<# <Content
          Select=”/Opportunity/Account/Companies/CompanyProducts/Name”
          /> #>`
        - Founder: `<# <Content
          Select=”/Opportunity/Account/Companies/CompanyProducts/CompanyProductFounder”
          /> #>`
        - `<# <EndRepeat /> #>`
        - `<# <EndRepeat /> #>`
      - `<# <EndRepeat /> #>``<# <EndRepeat/> #>`
   4. Save the changes in Word and return to the Gen Upload
      Documents page.
3. Add a Repeat tag to import product information along with the name:

   **`<# <Repeat Select="/Opportunity/Account/Name" />
   #>`**

   **Name: `<# <Content Select="/Name" />
   #>`**

   **Age: `<# <Content Select= “/Age” />
   #>`**

   **Companies:**
   - **`<# <Repeat Select= “/Opportunity/Account/Companies”
     /> #>`**
   - **Name: `<# <Content Select=
     “/Opportunity/Account/Companies/Name” />
     #>`**
   - **Products:**
     - **`<# <Repeat
       Select=”/Opportunity/Account/Companies/CompanyProducts”
       /> #>`**
     - **Name: `<# <Content
       Select=”/Opportunity/Account/Companies/CompanyProducts/Name”
       /> #>`**
     - **Founder: `<# <Content
       Select=”/Opportunity/Account/Companies/CompanyProducts/CompanyProductFounder”
       /> #>`**
     - **`<# <EndRepeat /> #>`**
     - **`<# <EndRepeat /> #>`**
   - **`<# <EndRepeat /> #>`****`<# <EndRepeat/> #>`**
4. Save the changes in Word and return to the Gen Upload
   Documents page.
5. [Re-upload the document to your Gen
   template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sqp1586134339354.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to update your Gen template Word documents.").
6. Select Save and Close and select
   Save on the Do you want to save
   changes? dialog box.

   You have added the Repeat tags to your Gen template. The generated content from
   these Repeat tags displays as follows:

title: Use Repeat Tags in Gen to Import Data from Multiple Salesforce Objects
heading_path: Use Repeat Tags in Gen to Import Data from Multiple Salesforce Objects
text:
lose and select
   Save on the Do you want to save
   changes? dialog box.

   You have added the Repeat tags to your Gen template. The generated content from
   these Repeat tags displays as follows:

   ![A screenshot displays the Word document output of the repeat tags used in this Gen template.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Repeat-tag-generated-output.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzMTksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.QvSJkUAVm6FFExZx0ho-KPSn5xFCRyatUzoMSedajog&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Word document output of the repeat tags used in this Gen template.")

You have completed the process. You have added group XML tags to
the Word document that you are using for your Gen template. Generated agreements add all
instances of the Salesforce accounts and opportunities related to a contact.

title: My Gen for Billing Template Doesn't Generate or Send an Invoice When the Record Status Changes to "Posted"
heading_path: My Gen for Billing Template Doesn't Generate or Send an Invoice When the Record Status Changes to "Posted"
text:
Learn to fix the issue of Gen failing to generate or send invoices when the record
status changes to Posted.

My Gen for Billing template does not generate and send invoices when a record status changes
to Posted. When the status changes to Posted, nothing
happens.

title: My Gen for Billing Template Doesn't Generate or Send an Invoice When the Record Status Changes to "Posted"
heading_path: My Gen for Billing Template Doesn't Generate or Send an Invoice When the Record Status Changes to "Posted"
Resolution: Change the Salesforce Locale to German (Germany)
text:
Cause: Your Salesforce locale is set to German
(Germany, EURO), rather than German
(Germany).

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Open your Salesforce user page:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0ODcsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.ileDeN8s8gCrLJto_-ABJ4MGa1LHx4fbZRNzJPGwpiw&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Users in the Quick Find
      field.
   3. Select Edit next to your user.
2. Locale: Select German
   (Germany) from the dropdown menu.
3. Select Save.

   You have completed the process. You have changed your Locale from German (Germany,
   EURO) to German (Germany). When a record status changes to
   Posted, Gen generates an invoice and sends it
   for signature.

title: A Spinning Wheel Icon Displays When I Try to Open Docusign Applications
heading_path: A Spinning Wheel Icon Displays When I Try to Open Docusign Applications
text:
Learn to fix the issue of a spinning wheel icon displaying when you try to open Docusign applications.

A spinning wheel icon displays when I try to open Docusign applications. Docusign apps and features never load.

title: A Spinning Wheel Icon Displays When I Try to Open Docusign Applications
heading_path: A Spinning Wheel Icon Displays When I Try to Open Docusign Applications
Resolution: Upgrade your version of Docusign Apps Launcher
text:
Cause: You have Docusign Apps Launcher
version 5.4 or lower installed in your organization.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Check your Docusign Apps Launcher version number:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0OTEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.ym1yFBro0jHpdSmlQ5z_da0cykB7n7Nn7WETgGPJbc0&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Installed Packages in
      the Quick Find field.
   3. Check your Version Number.
2. If your version number is 5.4 or lower, [upgrade Apps Launcher in your Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=nok1698959626724.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to upgrade the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps in your Salesforce organization.").
3. Select Save.

   You have completed the process. You have upgraded the Apps Launcher package in your Salesforce organization. Docusign
   apps and services load normally.

title: Add Conditional Logic to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
heading_path: Add Conditional Logic to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
text:
Learn to format Agreement Cloud Editor templates to display, hide, or change content
depending on conditions.

You can format Agreement Cloud Editor templates to display, hide, or change content depending on
conditions. The Add logic rule button ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genaceconditionalicon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.9iiEa-l9-2dvoZFVL1IuqxQQkvHmPkO-7Cn4Kqu656E&_LANG=enus)
displays when you highlight any amount of text in the template document. You can create
logic rules to display or hide this text.

For example, you can set a logic rule to display a paragraph of New York legal text if the
Billing State of a record equals New York.

You can also chain multiple rules together to display text if the record data matches a set
of conditions.

Example:

You create a rule to display text if the record has a Billing State
of New York. You add a second rule to display text if the
Employees field value is greater than 100. You
add a third rule to display text if the Annual Revenue field value is
greater than $1,000,000.

Result: Docusign only displays the text if a record
meets each of these conditions:

- The record has a billing state of New York
- The record has more than 100 employees
- The record has annual revenue exceeding $1,000,000

Docusign does not limit the number of rules you can apply to a block of
text.

Use this procedure to create a logic rule for your Agreement Cloud Editor template:

1. Use the cursor to highlight text and select the Add logic rule
   button ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genaceconditionalicon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.9iiEa-l9-2dvoZFVL1IuqxQQkvHmPkO-7Cn4Kqu656E&_LANG=enus) that displays to the right.

   ![A screen capture demonstrates applying an Add logic rule to a selection of text.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screen-capture-demonstrates-applying-an-Add-logic-rule-to-a-selection-of-text.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.9iiEa-l9-2dvoZFVL1IuqxQQkvHmPkO-7Cn4Kqu656E&_LANG=enus "A screen capture demonstrates applying an Add logic rule to a selection of text.")

   Rule Builder opens.
2. Create a name for your rule:
   1. Select the Edit
      ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genaceeditlogicname.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.9iiEa-l9-2dvoZFVL1IuqxQQkvHmPkO-7Cn4Kqu656E&_LANG=enus) icon next to
      Name Rule.
   2. Enter a name for your rule.
   3. Select the check mark to save the new name.

   ![A screenshot displays a logic rule name with a green check mark highlighted in green.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genacerenamerule.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.9iiEa-l9-2dvoZFVL1IuqxQQkvHmPkO-7Cn4Kqu656E&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a logic rule name with a green check mark highlighted in green.")
3. Choose the conditions for your rule:

title: Add Conditional Logic to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
heading_path: Add Conditional Logic to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
text:
NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.9iiEa-l9-2dvoZFVL1IuqxQQkvHmPkO-7Cn4Kqu656E&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a logic rule name with a green check mark highlighted in green.")
3. Choose the conditions for your rule:

   - Apply any of the following conditions: Trigger your rule if
     at least one condition is met.
   - Apply all of the following conditions: Trigger your rule if
     all conditions are met.
4. Create If logic. Here you create the conditions that a record must
   meet to trigger your rule:
   1. Select the Salesforce Fields icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genacesalesforcefieldsicon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.9iiEa-l9-2dvoZFVL1IuqxQQkvHmPkO-7Cn4Kqu656E&_LANG=enus)
      in the left column.
   2. Drag a Salesforce field to the If box.
   3. Equals: Keep this selection or select Does not
      equal from the dropdown menu.
   4. Enter text in the field next to Equals. For example, enter
      "New York" if you want a rule of Billing State/Province >  Equals >  New York.

      ![A screen capture demonstrates applying a Salesforce field to an if logic block.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screen-capture-demonstrates-applying-a-Salesforce-field-to-an-IF-logic-block.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.9iiEa-l9-2dvoZFVL1IuqxQQkvHmPkO-7Cn4Kqu656E&_LANG=enus "A screen capture demonstrates applying a Salesforce field to an if logic block.")
   5. (Optional) OR IF: Add more conditions to your rule.
   6. (Optional): Move the cursor to a condition and select ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genacex.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.9iiEa-l9-2dvoZFVL1IuqxQQkvHmPkO-7Cn4Kqu656E&_LANG=enus)
      to delete it.
   7. (Optional) + ADD RULE: Add another rule to this block of
      text.
5. Decide whether to display or hide text when your rule is matched:

   - Then: Choose an option from the dropdown menu:
     - Show the following content
     - Hide the following content
   - Otherwise...: Choose an option from the dropdown menu:
     - Hide the following content
     - Show the following content
6. Save and publish your rule:
   1. Select Save at the top right of the Rule Builder
      section.
   2. Select Next.
   3. Select Publish.

   A message displays: Template (your template name)
   updated.

You have completed the process. You added a logic rule to your
Agreement Cloud Editor template. When users generate documents, Docusign
displays, hides, or changes content based on record data.
