title: I Cannot Use the Gen Template Create Button Page to Remove a Button
heading_path: I Cannot Use the Gen Template Create Button Page to Remove a Button
text:
Learn to remove Gen buttons from layouts.

I created a Gen template and want to remove the button from one of my layouts. I open the
template and go to the Create Button page. I do not see an option to remove the button.

title: I Cannot Use the Gen Template Create Button Page to Remove a Button
heading_path: I Cannot Use the Gen Template Create Button Page to Remove a Button
Resolution 1: Use the Gen Create Button Page
text:
Cause: Docusign does not display a remove button
in the Gen template user interface.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. [Access the Gen template Create Button page
   as a Salesforce administrator](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=way1621663319299.html&_LANG=enus).
2. Uncheck the box for each layout that you want to update. Uncheck all boxes if you
   want to remove the button from all layouts listed.
3. Select Update Layouts. ![A screenshot displays all page layout boxes unchecked with the Update Layouts button highlighted.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-all-page-layout-boxes-unchecked-with-the-Update-Layouts-button-highlighted.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.JGYXy3cfauGMfF2DM-SHAtQq1Y_s_NXyIfwHMH_OFEs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays all page layout boxes unchecked with the Update Layouts button highlighted.")

   A message displays: Successfully modified
   layouts.
4. Select Save and Finish.

   You have completed the process. You have removed the Gen button from the
   layouts.

title: I Cannot Use the Gen Template Create Button Page to Remove a Button
heading_path: I Cannot Use the Gen Template Create Button Page to Remove a Button
Resolution 2: Remove Your Gen Button in Salesforce Setup
text:
Cause: I added the Gen button to more layouts than the Create Button
step displays.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Open the Buttons, Links, and Actions page as an
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.JGYXy3cfauGMfF2DM-SHAtQq1Y_s_NXyIfwHMH_OFEs&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Object Manager in the Quick Find field.
   3. Select a Salesforce object.
   4. Select Buttons, Links, and Actions.

   The Buttons, Links, and Actions page displays.
2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.JGYXy3cfauGMfF2DM-SHAtQq1Y_s_NXyIfwHMH_OFEs&_LANG=enus)a next to the custom button and
   choose Delete from the dropdown menu.![A screenshot displays the Buttons, Links, and Actions page with two custom button links.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/gendeletecustombutton.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.JGYXy3cfauGMfF2DM-SHAtQq1Y_s_NXyIfwHMH_OFEs&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Buttons, Links, and Actions page with two custom button links.")

   A dialog box displays: Are you sure?.
3. Select Delete. The page refreshes with the button no longer
   displayed.

   Note: Salesforce may display an
   error: Your request to delete this custom button or link can not be
   completed because this button or link is being used by another
   feature. Remove the button from all Salesforce layouts.

   The process is complete. You have removed the Gen button from your
   layout.

Use this procedure to edit a Gen
template:

2. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0NDIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.v6wUNq1pSFhNMm-0JISi4EP4Va6011WZAhxndcIbMzQ&_LANG=enus) next to a Gen template and choose

and Salesforce fields.

title: Add Split and Nested Conditional Tags to a Gen Template
heading_path: Add Split and Nested Conditional Tags to a Gen Template
text:
Learn how to add split and nested conditional tags to a Word-based Gen
template.

You can add conditional logic blocks to Gen template Anchor Text Tags. Conditional logic
creates "if-then" statements that add or exclude text from a generated agreement. For
example, you can add conditional logic that hides new customer language when generating
existing customer agreements.

You always start conditional logic blocks with a <Conditional>
tag and end with an <EndConditional> tag. You can place
<Conditional> and
<EndConditional> tags on the same line. You create split
conditionals by splitting conditional text over multiple lines of text.

You can also use nested conditional logic, which involves placing child conditional tags
inside of parent tags. For example, you have conditional text for new customers and add
child conditional text for all new California customers.

Use this procedure to add split and nested conditional tags to a Word-based Gen
template:

1. Select a document name.

   The document preview page opens in a new tab or window.
2. Add the conditional anchor text to your Word document. In this example, you split
   conditional tags over multiple lines and add a nested legal clause for California
   customers:
   1. Select Download and open the document in Microsoft
      Word.
   2. Where you want the conditional text to display, paste `<# <Conditional
      Select="Opportunity/Type" Match="New Customer"/ > #>Welcome, new
      customer.<# <Conditional Select="Opportunity/State" Match="California" />
      #>Custom California Legal clause< #<EndConditional/>
      #>`.
   3. Replace the Custom California Legal clause text with your
      California legal clause text.![A screenshot displays split and nested conditional text around California legal clause text.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-split-and-nested-conditional-text-around-California-legal-clause-text.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzMDksInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.o_iYB00t2aVSjhAoSsLFX37fZPuH0idsHBilwO6qqK0&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays split and nested conditional text around California legal clause text.")
   4. Save changes and go to the Gen Upload Documents page.
3. [Re-upload the document to your Gen
   template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sqp1586134339354.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to update your Gen template Word documents.")
4. Select Save and Close and select Save on
   the Do you want to save changes? dialog box.

You have completed the process. You have added split and nested
conditional logic for new customers. Generated agreements for California customers display the
California legal clause. Generated agreements for all other new customers do not display the
California clause.

title: Add Docusign Components to Salesforce Layouts
heading_path: Add Docusign Components to Salesforce Layouts
text:
You can add Docusign components to your Salesforce layouts. Components give your users a space to view and interact with Docusign content on Salesforce records.

You can add Docusign components to your Salesforce page layouts. Docusign components display as sections on your Salesforce records. In these sections, users can view and interact with Docusign content without logging on to their Docusign account.

You can add the following components to your Salesforce layouts:

[Docusign Agreement Requests](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=map8c30aa94-7e9f-4950-92fc-dd22c6d9f835&topicId=tsk151c1542-bdf4-4c8e-ba20-37affb7adc7e.html&_LANG=enus)
:   View Docusign Agreement Desk requests on your records. You can use this component with Docusign Agreement Desk. Use Agreement Desk to view and act on your Docusign requests.

[Docusign CLM Files](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=dny1626816139155.html&_LANG=enus)
:   View and act on files and folders from your connected Docusign CLM account. You can use this component with the CLM for Salesforce app.

[Docusign CLM Obligations](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=tvw1659667572631.html&_LANG=enus)
:   View and act on your CLM obligations from a Salesforce record. You can use this component with the CLM for Salesforce app.

[Docusign CLM Tasks](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=ysk1643667858834&topicId=lbi1628268219401.html&_LANG=enus)
:   View and act on tasks from your connected Docusign CLM account. You can use this component with the CLM for Salesforce app.

[Docusign Completed Agreements](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=pwq1747797355782&topicId=cmv1746652068556.html&_LANG=enus)
:   View Docusign agreements on your records. You can use this component with Docusign Agreement Manager. Use Agreement Manager to view and act on your Docusign agreements.

[Docusign Envelope Status](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=all1586134265721.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Monitor the status of Docusign envelopes sent from Salesforce records. You can use this component with these apps:

    - Gen for Salesforce
    - Negotiate for Salesforce
    - eSignature for Salesforce

[Docusign Files](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=isj1586134263707.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Add files to a record that can be sent for Docusign signature or review. You can use this component with the Negotiate for Salesforce app.

[Docusign IAM Launcher](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=qwr1706590641805&topicId=tsk590e18e7-9dbc-418f-9fb8-0169f182eb3a.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Start Docusign workflows from your records. You can use this component with Docusign Workflow Builder. Workflow Builder reads Salesforce data and writes changes back to records when workflows complete.

title: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
heading_path: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
text:
You can customize the fields you add in the template creation process.

You can customize the Docusign fields you add in the template creation
process. You can use the following options:

- Required or read-only
- Add additional signers
- Add tooltips
- Customize data labels
- Add validation types
- Add character limits

You can use the following fields and customizations in your Gen templates:

title: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
heading_path: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
Add Recipient Views
text:
Use this Docusign Fields menu to add more recipients and signature
fields. You can create a customized view for each recipient, giving each their own
signature fields.

title: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
heading_path: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template
Signature
text:
Place this field where the recipient adds their signature to the document. You can
configure the following options:

- Switch to a different recipient
- Required or optional
- Add a tooltip
- Add a custom data label

title: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template

Initial

- Add a custom data label

title: Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template

Date Signed
text:
Place this field where the signature date will be added. You can configure the
following options:

- Add a tooltip
- Add a custom data label

Name

following options:

- Add a custom data label

Email

- Add a custom data label

Company

Add this field where the organization name will be placed. You can configure the

- Add a custom data label

Title

title: Change the Currency Type of a Salesforce Record
heading_path: Change the Currency Type of a Salesforce Record
text:
Learn to change the type of currency Salesforce displays on a record.

Your Salesforce organization has a default currency format. However, your administrator can
add new currencies to your organization. Adding currencies lets you change the
default currency on a record.

Example: Your Salesforce organization uses U.S.
Dollar as the default currency, but your administrator has added
Euro. You change the Opportunity
Currency field of a French company to Euro.
The Amount field displays the value in Euros.

Note: If you want to [choose the currency format when generating documents](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=bpg1586134377111&topicId=hhz1686691127617.html&_LANG=enus), you must change a record's currency first.

Use this procedure to change the currency type of a Salesforce record:

1. Select the Details tab of a record.
2. Select the pencil icon ![A screenshot of the pencil icon used to edit fields on a Salesforce record.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-pencil-icon-used-to-edit-fields-on-a-Salesforce-record.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYzMTAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.5CBI5P8WM0rpOrCl0UFR34Gw637QwtO8YITIR8hEJao&_LANG=enus "A screenshot of the pencil icon used to edit fields on a Salesforce record.") on the (object) Currency field.
3. Select a new currency type from the dropdown menu.
4. Select Save.

   The Amount field displays the new
   currency.

You have completed the process. You have changed the default
currency of a record.

title: Learn About Formatting Gen Percentage, Currency, Date, Time, and Number Fields
heading_path: Learn About Formatting Gen Percentage, Currency, Date, Time, and Number Fields
text:
Learn about how you can enhance Gen field formatting to customize your
templates.

You can enhance Gen field formatting to customize your templates.

Gen templates automatically match the Salesforce settings for percentage, date, time,
number, and currency field formatting.

Use the following guidance when adding these fields in a Gen template:

title: Learn About Formatting Gen Percentage, Currency, Date, Time, and Number Fields
heading_path: Learn About Formatting Gen Percentage, Currency, Date, Time, and Number Fields
Percentage Field
text:
Gen template percentage fields have enhanced formatting options and requirements. These
include:

- Ability to include or exclude the percentage sign (%)
- Accepting as many decimal places as specified on the field, including
  .00
- Inheriting Salesforce locale format for decimal places separators: comma as
  opposed to period (123,00 as opposed to 123.00)
- Inheriting Salesforce locale format for thousand separators: comma as opposed to period
  (123,456,789.00 as opposed to 123.456.789,00)

title: Learn About Formatting Gen Percentage, Currency, Date, Time, and Number Fields
heading_path: Learn About Formatting Gen Percentage, Currency, Date, Time, and Number Fields
Date Field
text:
Date fields inherit the same format as specified in Salesforce Locale settings. Short Date
and Custom Date options can also be used.

- Short Date options: (using January18, 2020 as an example)
  - 18/1/2020
  - 18-01-20
  - 18-Jan-20
  - 18-01-2020
  - 18.01.2020
  - 18-Jan-2020
  - 18 January 2020
  - 1/18/2020
  - 01-18-2020
  - 01/18/2020
  - 01/18/20
  - Jan-18-2020
  - Jan 18, 2020
  - January 18, 2020
  - 2020-01-18
  - 2020-Jan-18
  - 2020/01/18
  - 2020 January 18
- Custom Date: Enable a text field where users can customize
  dates.
  - Example 1: (English)
    - **Date to display**: Wednesday, March 14, 2020
    - **User input**: dddd, MMMM D, YYYY
  - Example 2: (Spanish)
    - **Date to display**: Miercoles, 14 de Marzo de 2020
    - **User input**: dddd, D de MMMM de YYYY
- No validation is needed on custom input: Salesforce default
  preferences for Language and Date Format dictate the Date Format on a Gen template.
  Example: A user's Salesforce language is set to French. The Date
  Format is set to D MMMM YYYY. The Gen output format results in 6 janvier 2020, not 6
  January 2020.

title: Learn About Formatting Gen Percentage, Currency, Date, Time, and Number Fields
heading_path: Learn About Formatting Gen Percentage, Currency, Date, Time, and Number Fields
Date & Time Field
text:
Date & Time field formatting in Gen templates matches the formatting set in the
connected Salesforce organization. Users can customize dates when you enable a Date &
Time text field.

This field includes the following options:

- Locale Default: display the same format as specified in
  Salesforce Locale
- Short Date Options: (using January 18, 2019 as an example)
  - 18/1/2019
  - 18-01-19
  - 18-Jan-19
  - 18-01-2019
  - 18.01.2019
  - 18-Jan-2019
  - 18 January 2019
  - 1/18/2019
  - 01-18-2019
  - 01/18/2019
  - 01/18/19
  - Jan-18-2019
  - Jan 18, 2019
  - January 18, 2019
  - 2019-01-18
  - 2019-Jan-18
  - 2019/01/18
  - 2019 January 18
- Custom Date: Enable a Text Field where users can customize
  dates:
  - Example 1: (English)
    - Date to display: Wednesday, March 14, 2019
    - User input: dddd, MMMM D, YYYY
  - Example 2: (Spanish)
    - Date to display: Miercoles, 14 de Marzo de 2019
    - User input: dddd, D de MMMM de YYYY
    - - No validation is needed on custom input

title: Learn About Formatting Gen Percentage, Currency, Date, Time, and Number Fields
heading_path: Learn About Formatting Gen Percentage, Currency, Date, Time, and Number Fields
Number Field
text:
Number field formatting in Gen templates matches the formatting set in the connected
Salesforce organization. This includes: Gen, Negotiate, eSignature, and CLM for Salesforce
(Docusign Apps Launcher).

This includes:

- Accepting as many decimal places as specified on the field, including .00
- Inheriting Salesforce locale format for decimal places separators: comma as opposed to
  period (123,00 as opposed to 123.00)
- Inheriting Salesforce locale format for thousand separators: comma as opposed to period
  (123,456,789.00 as opposed to 123.456.789,00)

title: Learn About Formatting Gen Percentage, Currency, Date, Time, and Number Fields
heading_path: Learn About Formatting Gen Percentage, Currency, Date, Time, and Number Fields
Currency Field
text:
Gen uses the ASP.Net core-based defaultCurrencyIsoCode to determine currency formatting.

Currency field formatting in Gen templates matches the formatting set in the connected
Salesforce organization. This includes:

- Accepting as many decimal places as specified on the field, including .00
- Inheriting Salesforce locale format for decimal places separators: comma as opposed to
  period (123,00 as opposed to 123.00)
- Inheriting Salesforce locale format for thousand separators: comma as opposed to period
  (123,456,789.00 as opposed to 123.456.789,00)
- Inheriting Salesforce currency locale for currency symbol.
- An additional option is now provided for users to print a Currency value without
  Symbol/ISO code

However, the same currency code can be used across multiple countries and cultures. Due to
this overlap in use, there are 38 countries for which Gen formats currency values
differently than expected. Here are those countries and the formatting that is expected and
delivered by Gen:

Table 1. Countries with Different Currency Formatting

| **ISO Code** | **Culture** | **Display Name** | **Expected Formatting** | **Actual Formatting** |

title: Learn About Formatting Gen Percentage, Currency, Date, Time, and Number Fields
heading_path: Learn About Formatting Gen Percentage, Currency, Date, Time, and Number Fields
Other CurrencyIsocodes
text:
In addition, there are a number of CurrencyIsocodes which are only
specific to Salesforce and are not identified by .Net. Docusign has added
a dictionary to ensure the correct formatting.

The additional codes are as follows:

ANG, AOA, AWG, BBD, BIF, BMD, BSD, CSD, CVE, FJD, FKP, GHS, GIP, GMD, GNF, GYD, KMF, KPW,
KYD, LRD, MGA, MRU, MUR, MWK, MZN, NAD, PGK, SBD, SCR, SDG, SHP, SLL, SRD, STN, SZL, TOP,
TZS, UGX, VUV, WST, XPF

title: Gen Template Advanced Options: Agreement Cloud Editor
heading_path: Gen Template Advanced Options: Agreement Cloud Editor
text:
The Agreement Cloud Editor is an in-browser option for creating and editing Docusign Gen for Salesforce templates.

The Agreement Cloud Editor is an in-browser option for creating Gen templates without using
Microsoft Word. You can quickly add Docusign and Salesforce fields to a
template, without copying and pasting XML tags. You can also Download Agreement Cloud Editor
templates as Word documents.

Administrators can use these advanced features to customize their Agreement Cloud Editor Gen
templates:

- [Download Agreement Cloud Editor Documents as Word Files](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=crs1588512498923.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to download Agreement Document Format Gen contracts as Microsoft Word documents.")
- [Customize Agreement Cloud Editor Template Text](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=iat1586134315670.html&_LANG=enus "You can customize Agreement Cloud Editor template documents to change font size and style, add sections of conditional content and add tables.")
- [Preview an Agreement Cloud Editor Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=hht1586134306427.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to see what a document generated from your template looks like.")
- [Add Child Objects to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=qsk1586134322865.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add up to four levels of child objects and two levels of related lists.")
- [Add Conditional Logic to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=wqi1586134323385.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to format Agreement Cloud Editor templates to display, hide, or change content depending on conditions.")
- [Learn About Adding Tables to Agreement Cloud Editor Templates](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zeh1586134329665.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a table to an Agreement Cloud Editor template.")
- [Learn to Customize Docusign Fields in a Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=xna1586134322115.html&_LANG=enus "You can customize the fields you add in the template creation process.")

title: Understanding Docusign Permission Sets in Salesforce
heading_path: Understanding Docusign Permission Sets in Salesforce
text:
Learn about the types and functions of Docusign Apps Launcher permission sets.

Permission sets are a collection of Salesforce settings and permissions that give users access to various tools and functions. Permission sets extend Salesforce users’ functional access without changing their profiles.

Docusign adds a group of permission sets when you install the Docusign Apps Launcher package. Docusign permission sets display which Salesforce users have Docusign app access.

Important:

- Administrators grant most Docusign app access with  [Docusign User Management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=dzl1586134206658.html&_LANG=enus). Administrators can only use the Docusign Login permission set to help grant access.
- Administrators should not manually add or remove users from Docusign permissions sets except Docusign Login.

The following is a list of the Docusign permission sets and their functions:

| Permission Set Name | Permission Set Function |
| --- | --- |
| Docusign Login | Allows users to authorize Docusign Apps Launcher and auto-assign permission sets corresponding to existing Docusign user permissions |
| Docusign Administrator | Allows administrative access to Docusign Apps Launcher |
| Docusign CLM Admin | Allows administrative access to CLM in Docusign Apps Launcher |
| Docusign CLM Experience Cloud User | Allows access to CLM in Communities (Experience Cloud) |
| Docusign CLM User | Allows subscriber access to CLM in Docusign Apps Launcher |
| Docusign CLM Viewer | Allows guest access to CLM in Docusign Apps Launcher |
| Docusign Document Generator | Allows users to generate documents with Docusign Gen |
| Docusign Document Negotiator | Allows users to send files for internal approval and external review with Docusign Negotiate |
| Docusign Sender | Allows users to send envelopes as the system sender in Docusign Apps Launcher﻿ |
| Docusign User | Allows access to eSignature in Docusign Apps Launcher |

Users Send and Sign Envelopes With the Apex Toolkit

title: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
heading_path: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
text:
Learn to work around the duplicate files issue when sending Gen agreements for
signature.

I built a Gen template that is connected to an envelope template. When I send generated
agreements for signature, I see duplicate documents in the
Documents step.

title: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
heading_path: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
Resolution 1: Uncheck the Boxes of Duplicate Salesforce Files Before Sending
text:
Cause: You selected the Attach documents from
Salesforce record check box on the envelope template
Documents page. Selecting this option in an envelope
template that is linked to a Gen template causes file duplication.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Select a Gen button on a Salesforce record.

   Gen creates a new agreement, and opens a preview page.
2. Select Send for Signature.

   Your linked envelope template displays the
   Documents page.
3. De-select the check box for any duplicate Salesforce documents that
   display.
4. Proceed with the rest of the envelope creation process.

   You have completed the process. You have removed any duplicate Salesforce
   documents and sent the envelope.

title: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
heading_path: Duplicate Salesforce Documents Display When I Send Gen Agreements for Signature
Resolution 2: Uncheck the "Attach Documents from Salesforce Record" Box Before Sending
text:
Cause: You selected the Attach documents from
Salesforce record check box on the envelope template
Documents page.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Select a Gen button on a Salesforce record.

   Gen creates a new document, and opens a preview page.
2. Select Send for Signature.

   Your linked envelope template displays the
   Documents page.
3. De-select the Attach documents from Salesforce
   record check box.
4. Proceed with the rest of the envelope creation process.

   You have completed the process. You have removed any duplicate
   Salesforce documents and sent the envelope.

title: I Want to Remove a Data Field from My Gen Template
heading_path: I Want to Remove a Data Field from My Gen Template
text:
Learn to remove a data field from a Gen template.

Resolution: Edit Gen Template in the Data Fields Step
text:
Cause: Docusign hides the
Remove option in an arrow menu.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NTEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.omD-uGUbuPzWlWRPO7rGf8A-ApHxQ9DH98rvuDXerr8&_LANG=enus) next
   to Copy.
2. Select Remove from the dropdown menu.![A screenshot displays the Field and Anchor Text fields with the copy, options, and remove dropdown menu.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-displays-an-anchor-text-field-with-the-copy-menu-open-to-options-and-removepng.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg0NTEsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.omD-uGUbuPzWlWRPO7rGf8A-ApHxQ9DH98rvuDXerr8&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the Field and Anchor Text fields with the copy, options, and remove dropdown menu.")
3. Select Next.

   Important: Remove all

title: Activate Your Docusign for Salesforce User
heading_path: Activate Your Docusign for Salesforce User
text:
Learn how to activate your Docusign CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or
eSignature for Salesforce user.

Your administrator can create a Docusign for Salesforce user for you. You
can access approved Docusign apps in Salesforce with this user.
Administrators can create these types of Docusign for Salesforce
users:

- CLM: Access CLM files, folders, and features (workflow, document
  generation, review, and so on.)
- Gen: Generate contracts and send them for signature.
- Negotiate: Send Docusign files for external
  review, internal approval, or signature.
- eSignature: Create envelopes and send them for signature.

You receive an activation email from Docusign when your administrator
creates your user. Activate your user to access Docusign apps in
Salesforce.

Use this procedure to activate your Docusign user:

1. Open the Account Activation email from Docusign. Check your spam
   folder if you don't see the message in your inbox.
2. Select Activate.
3. Follow the activation prompts on the Docusign
   Log In page:

   - Existing
     Docusign
      users:
     Enter your Docusign credentials and select Accept
     Invitation.
   - New
     Docusign
      users:
     Create a Password and Security Question,
     and select Activate.
4. Complete your Docusign authentication in Salesforce:

   Important: Your Docusign user is not
   active until you complete these steps.

   1. Select a Docusign component or custom button on a record and
      select Authorize.

      The Docusign Log In dialog box opens.
   2. Enter your Docusign credentials or select Company Login to log in with SSO credentials.
   3. Select Log In.
   4. Select Accept.
   5. Select Allow Access.
5. Enter your Salesforce credentials.

   Note: Salesforce may require you to select
   Authorize and enter your credentials twice.

You have completed the process. Your Docusign user is active. You can use approved Docusign apps
in Salesforce.

title: Step 7: Remove the Person Account Custom Button from the Accounts Layout
heading_path: Step 7: Remove the Person Account Custom Button from the Accounts Layout
text:
Learn to to remove the custom button from the Salesforce Account layout.

   Account custom button.
2. Select Delete from the drop-down menu.
3. Select Delete from the drop-down menu.

   The page refreshes.

You have completed step seven. Move to [Step 8: Add Your Template Button to the Salesforce Person Account Layout](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=yxb1637170224921.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a custom button to the Salesforce Person Account layout.").

title: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your CLM or CLM Essentials Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
heading_path: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your CLM or CLM Essentials Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
text:
Learn to fix domain integration issues using the Sync Domains feature. Sync Domains
fixes issues with Enhanced Domains and any changes that you make to your Salesforce
domain.

title: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your CLM or CLM Essentials Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
heading_path: Use Sync Domains to Rebuild Your CLM or CLM Essentials Integration After a Salesforce Domain Name Change
Before you begin
text:
[Install Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce
v5.2](https://login.salesforce.com/packaging/installPackage.apexp?p0=04t3u000002wLcp)

You can change your Salesforce domain name to update your brand, adopt [Enhanced Domains](https://help.salesforce.com/s/articleView?id=sf.domain_name_enhanced.htm&type=5), or enable partitioned domains. When
your My Domain name changes, your Salesforce-hosted URLs also change. You must
rebuild the connection to your Docusign account.

The Sync Domains feature of the Docusign
TroubleShooting tool rebuilds your connection to Docusign after a
domain change.

Use this procedure to rebuild your CLM or CLM
Essentials integration with the Sync Domains
feature:

1. Access the My Domain page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1NTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.hnH8Yz6IWk6rBm3yadC2o2PBKKGCY3zIqQkHqGnQ0lU&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select My Domain in the Quick Find
      field.
2. Enable redirects for your domain:

   Note: The Domain Name System (DNS) server takes up to 72 hours to update
   the connection between Docusign and Salesforce. Keep the
   redirect setting enabled during this time.

   1. Routing: Select Edit.
   2. Select the Redirect previous My Domain URLs to your current
      My Domain check box.
   3. Select Save. The My Domain page opens.
3. Access the Visualforce Pages page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1NTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.hnH8Yz6IWk6rBm3yadC2o2PBKKGCY3zIqQkHqGnQ0lU&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter and select Visualforce Pages in the Quick
      Find field.
4. Select TroubleShooting.![A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/TroubleShooting-page.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1NTUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.hnH8Yz6IWk6rBm3yadC2o2PBKKGCY3zIqQkHqGnQ0lU&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays the TroubleShooting link on the Visualforce Pages screen.")
5. Select Preview.

   The TroubleShooting page opens.
6. Sync Domains: Select Execute.

   The page refreshes. A message displays: Successfully synced
   domains on Docusign account.
7. (Optional): After 72 hours, uncheck the Redirect previous My Domain
   URLs to your current My Domain box.

You have completed the process. You have rebuilt the CLM or CLM
Essentials integration with your Salesforce organization.

title: Learn About Setting the Default Format of Currency, Date, and Time Data Fields
heading_path: Learn About Setting the Default Format of Currency, Date, and Time Data Fields
text:
Learn about the default formatting that you can apply to data fields in a Gen
template.

When you build a Gen template, you can set the default formatting of currency, date,
and time data fields. You can set the default format of these data field types:

- Date fields: Set the date formatting of all
  documents generated from this template
- Currency fields: Set the currency formatting of
  all documents generated from this template
- Time fields: Set the time formatting of all
  documents generated from this template

Important: Set currency and date formats to Use
Template Format to use template-level [Gen date and currency formatting](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=kqf1686095608923.html&_LANG=enus).

You can use the following settings to format the currency, date, and time fields that display
in generated documents:

title: Learn About Setting the Default Format of Currency, Date, and Time Data Fields
heading_path: Learn About Setting the Default Format of Currency, Date, and Time Data Fields
Currency settings
text:
| Setting Name | Example |
| --- | --- |
| Use Template Format | Use the Date and Currency Format settings of the Gen template |
| Display as symbol | $123,456.78 |
| Display as symbol (no decimals) | $123,457 |
| Display as ISO code | 123,456.78 |
| Display as ISO code (no decimals) | USD 123,457 |
| Display without symbol and ISO code | 123,456.78 |

title: Learn About Setting the Default Format of Currency, Date, and Time Data Fields
heading_path: Learn About Setting the Default Format of Currency, Date, and Time Data Fields
Locale (Date) Settings
text:
| Setting Name | Description |
| --- | --- |
| Use Template Format | Use the Date and Currency Format settings of the Gen template |
| Generator's Locale | Use the generating user's Salesforce locale for date formatting |
| Locale Default | Use the Salesforce organization's locale for date formatting |
| Org Locale | Use the Salesforce organization's locale for date formatting |
| All Locales | Select a country or region to set date formatting |
| Custom | Enter a custom date format |

title: Learn About Setting the Default Format of Currency, Date, and Time Data Fields
heading_path: Learn About Setting the Default Format of Currency, Date, and Time Data Fields
Time settings
text:
| Setting type | Example |
| --- | --- |
| Locale Default | Use the formatting from the default locale |
| 12-hour formats | 2:59 |
|  | 2:59 pm |
|  | 2:59:23 |
|  | 2:59:23 pm |
| 24-hour formats | 14:59 |
|  | 14:59:23 |
| Custom | Enter a custom time format |

title: Refresh or Reconnect a Salesforce Organization With Docusign CLM Federated Security
heading_path: Refresh or Reconnect a Salesforce Organization With Docusign CLM Federated Security
text:
Learn about best practices for refreshing a Salesforce organization with Federated
Security enabled.

Salesforce organizations using CLM Federated Security should avoid taking these actions:

- Refreshing their organization
- Connecting their CLM account to a different Salesforce organization

If a Salesforce organization must be refreshed or re-connected, follow these steps
first (in order):

1. [Turn off Federated Security in your CLM
   account.](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=vbn1599207323944.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to disable the Federated Security feature in your Salesforce organization.")
2. Open the CLM **Other Sources** folder and rename the Salesforce folder:
   **Salesforce\_Old**.
3. Create a new **Salesforce** folder under Other Sources parent folder.

   ![Rename Salesforce folder.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Rename-Salesforce-folder.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1NjAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Cl5jX2ezkpBIUesqI50_DeG-y8hJJEP0Ty9JexFOikI&_LANG=enus "Rename Salesforce folder.")
4. [Perform a refresh of your Salesforce
   Sandbox](https://help.salesforce.com/articleView?id=data_sandbox_refresh.htm&type=5).
5. Add records to your Sandbox.
6. [Turn on CLM Federated Security](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=gqm1599194045432.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to enable standard Federated Security in your Docusign CLM account.").
7. Test your updates to ensure that all is working properly.

The process is complete. The Sandbox is refreshed and
Federated Security is properly applied to the new content. All archived content is found
in the **Salesforece\_Old** folder.

title: Activate Docusign Gen from a Salesforce CPQ Plus License
heading_path: Activate Docusign Gen from a Salesforce CPQ Plus License
text:
Salesforce CPQ Plus customers are given a Docusign Gen account that is activated by
following these instructions.

Docusign eSignature customers purchasing Salesforce CPQ Plus also receive a
Docusign Gen license. Your organization must meet the
following prerequisites to qualify:

- Your Salesforce organization has a Gen CPQ Plus license.
- Your Salesforce organization is connected to a paid, multi-user Docusign
  account (not a trial.)
- You do not have an existing paid Gen license.
- You have at least one Gen CPQ activation remaining.

Follow this link to [get started with your Gen CPQ Plus
license](https://support.docusign.com/s/articles/Getting-Started-with-DocuSign-Apps-Launcher-and-Gen-CPQ-Plus?language=en_US).

CPQ Plus customers matching the prerequisites can activate their Gen license by
following these steps:

1. Select Document Generation.
2. Select Activate.
3. Select Allow Access.

   The Salesforce login page opens.
4. Enter Salesforce administrator credentials.

   A message displays: Successfully connected and authorized to
   Docusign.

The process is complete. You have activated your Gen license. You
can create users, build templates, and add Gen buttons to your Salesforce page
layouts.

title: Edit or Delete a Gen Template or Custom Button
heading_path: Edit or Delete a Gen Template or Custom Button
text:
Learn how to edit, delete, or change ownership for a Gen template or custom
button.

You can edit, delete, or change ownership for a Gen template or custom button. If you edit
the name of a Gen template, also edit the custom button name. If you delete a Gen template,
remove that template's buttons from your Salesforce layouts.

Use the following links to edit, delete, or change ownership of a Gen template:

- [Edit the Name of a Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=gww1586134337518.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to change the name of a Gen template.")
- [Edit a Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=xcs1646713533209.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to edit a Gen template.")
- [Delete a Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=mbd1586134342525.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to delete a Gen template.")
- [Change the Owner of a Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sut1646711991124.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to change the owner of a Gen template to a different Salesforce user.")
- [Edit a Gen Custom Button](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=tet1586134342760.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to edit the label or details of a Gen custom button.")
- [Delete a Gen Custom Button](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=bzt1586134341821.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove a Gen custom button from your Salesforce page layouts.")

title: Step 8: Add Your Template Button to the Salesforce Person Account Layout
heading_path: Step 8: Add Your Template Button to the Salesforce Person Account Layout
text:
Learn to add a custom button to the Salesforce Person Account layout.

Use this procedure to add a custom button to the Salesforce Person
Account layout:

1. Open the Person Account Layout page as a Salesforce
   administrator:
   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforce-gear_.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0MjYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Xtin1bIw0thkO6zAF93prpMa_by7-NSiyNhTlZB9VI0&_LANG=enus) icon and choose
      Setup.
   2. Enter Object Manager in the Quick Find
      field.
   3. Select the Person Account link in the left
      column.
   4. Select the Page Layouts link in the left
      column.
   5. Select the Person Account Layout link.
2. Select the Mobile & Lightning Actions link in the
   left column of the Person Account layout menu.
3. Drag the Person Account template button to the Salesforce Mobile and
   Lightning Experience Actions section.
4. Select the Save button in the upper left.

   ![A screen capture displays the cursor dragging the Person Account button to Mobile and Lightning Actions and selecting Save.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Add-Person-Account-custom-button.gif?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTY0MjYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.Xtin1bIw0thkO6zAF93prpMa_by7-NSiyNhTlZB9VI0&_LANG=enus "A screen capture displays the cursor dragging the Person Account button to Mobile and Lightning Actions and selecting Save.")

   The page refreshes.

You have completed the entire process. You built a template for
the Person Account object, and added it to the Person Account page layout.

title: Uninstall Docusign Apps Launcher from Your Salesforce Organization
heading_path: Uninstall Docusign Apps Launcher from Your Salesforce Organization
text:
Learn to uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package of apps from
your Salesforce organization.

You may be required to uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package from
your Salesforce organization. The Apps Launcher package contains the CLM, Gen,
Negotiate, and eSignature for Salesforce applications.

You might uninstall the package for the following reasons:

- You want to revert to an earlier version of the Apps Launcher package.
- You installed the latest version for Admins Only, and must reinstall
  for All Users.
- You installed a Beta version of the package and must install the official release.

To uninstall the package, you must first remove all Apps Launcher dependencies from your
Salesforce organization. Dependencies are relationships between Docusign apps and Salesforce buttons, layouts, and objects. The
following examples demonstrate the dependencies between Salesforce and Docusign:

- You add the **Docusign CLM Files** component to the Account
  layout.
- You create a Gen template and add a Gen button to the Opportunities layout.
- You add Salesforce users to the Docusign Generator
  permission set.

Salesforce prompts you to remove these dependencies when you uninstall Apps
Launcher.

Use these procedures to remove all dependencies and uninstall Docusign Apps
Launcher from your organization:

1. [Remove users from Docusign
   Apps Launcher permission sets](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=pnw1623705527946.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove all users from Docusign permission sets.")
2. [Remove Docusign Apps
   Launcher components from Salesforce layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=uxu1623708462949.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove all Docusign Apps Launcher components from Salesforce layouts.")
3. [Remove Docusign Apps
   Launcher custom buttons from your Salesforce layouts](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=cif1623740064281.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to remove Docusign Apps Launcher custom buttons from your Salesforce layouts.")
4. [Uninstall the Docusign Apps
   Launcher package from your Salesforce organization](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ump1623741000284.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to uninstall the Docusign Apps Launcher package from your Salesforce organization.")

title: Enable Translations for Your Salesforce User
heading_path: Enable Translations for Your Salesforce User
text:
Learn how to change the display language of all Docusign Apps Launcher
apps.

You can change the display language of all Docusign Apps Launcher apps by changing the
language settings for your Salesforce user.

1. Access the Salesforce Language & Time Zone settings:
   1. Select the Salesforce user icon ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforceusericon.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYzMDUsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.d63SW4ttKu8tvPXpw3olagFtw53EY0XkBRJDS3lsBRE&_LANG=enus) and choose
      Settings.
   2. Select Language & Time Zone.
2. Language: Select a language from the dropdown menu.
3. Select Save.

   A message displays: Your settings have been successfully
   saved.
4. Empty your browser cache and restart the browser.

You have completed the process. Docusign Apps Launcher content displays in the language that you
chose.

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
text:
Learn how to grant Docusign app access to your Salesforce
users.

There are several ways that you can grant Docusign app access to your
Salesforce users:

[Docusign user management](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=uwf1586134275195.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Create Docusign users with the Docusign Apps Launcher
    user management page.

[Docusign Login permission set](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=xxm1623778416524.html&_LANG=enus)
:   Create Docusign users by adding Salesforce users to the Docusign
     Login permission set.

Single sign-on (SSO)
:   Create Docusign users with the Docusign Login
    permission set and your SSO identity provider.

When you grant Docusign access to a Salesforce user, Docusign searches for a matching Docusign name
and email address. If matching credentials exist, Docusign links
the Salesforce and Docusign users. If matching credentials do not
exist, Docusign creates a new user.

Important: Never change the name or email address of a
Salesforce or Docusign user after activation. If Docusign and Salesforce credentials don't match, the user may be
unable to send envelopes and access Docusign objects.

You can use the following methods to grant Docusign access to Salesforce
users:

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
A new Docusign user activates from an email link
text:
A Docusign administrator assigns CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature
for Salesforce app permissions to a Salesforce user.

The user receives an email from Docusign, selects the activation
link, and creates a password.

The user selects a Docusign
Authorize button on a record and enters their Docusign and Salesforce credentials.

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
A new Docusign user activates with SSO credentials
text:
A Docusign administrator assigns CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature
for Salesforce app permissions to a Salesforce user.

The user selects a Docusign
Authorize button on a record and enters their SSO
credentials.

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
An existing Docusign user activates with standard or SSO credentials
text:
You can grant app access to an existing Docusign user on the user management
page. Existing Docusign users can activate their Docusign app access from an email link or with SSO credentials.

Important: Make sure the Salesforce user's name and email
address match those of the existing Docusign user. If the
Salesforce user's credentials differ from the existing Docusign
user's, Docusign creates a new user.

A Docusign administrator assigns CLM, Gen, Negotiate, or eSignature
for Salesforce app permissions to a Salesforce user.

The user selects a Docusign
Authorize button on a record and enters their credentials:

- Standard authentication: The user enters Salesforce and Docusign credentials
- SSO authentication: The user enters SSO credentials

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
A user with Docusign Login permissions self-activates
text:
You can let existing Docusign users activate their Docusign app access by adding them to the Docusign Login permission
set. Existing users can self-activate using Docusign and
Salesforce credentials or with SSO credentials. This permission set lets you manage
user activation in Salesforce, not on the Docusign user management
page.

A Salesforce administrator adds a Salesforce user to the Docusign
Login permission set.

The user selects a Docusign
Authorize button on a record and enters their credentials:

- Standard authentication: The user enters Salesforce and Docusign credentials
- SSO authentication: The user enters SSO credentials

title: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
heading_path: Learn How to Create and Manage Docusign Users in Salesforce
A new Docusign user activates with SSO just-in-time provisioning
text:
You can create new Docusign users by enabling Docusign SSO
and adding Salesforce users to the Docusign Login permission set.
Users can self-activate Docusign app access using SSO credentials.

Important:

- You must enable just-in-time user provisioning for Docusign in your SSO
  identity provider.
- You can only use just-in-time provisioning to add new eSignature for Salesforce users. You
  can add Gen, Negotiate, or CLM permissions after you create eSignature
  users.

A Salesforce administrator adds a Salesforce user to the Docusign
Login permission set.

The user selects a Docusign
Authorize button on a record and enters their SSO
credentials.

title: Learn About Adding Tables to Agreement Cloud Editor Templates
heading_path: Learn About Adding Tables to Agreement Cloud Editor Templates
text:
Learn to add a table to an Agreement Cloud Editor template.

You can add the following table types to an Agreement Cloud Editor template:

title: Learn About Adding Tables to Agreement Cloud Editor Templates
heading_path: Learn About Adding Tables to Agreement Cloud Editor Templates
Standard Table
text:
You create a standard table by manually adding the information in your table. You decide
the number of rows and columns in your table, and you manually add data into the cells. You
do not add Salesforce merge fields to a standard table.

Example: You create a standard table in your Agreement Cloud Editor
template. You add one column and enter New England as the header. You
add six rows and enter the name of a New England state in each row. A user generates a
document from a record. The standard table that you created displays in the generated
document.

[Add a Standard Table to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=zqz1586134333505.html&_LANG=enus "You use the table button to add a standard table to an Agreement Cloud Editor template.")

title: Learn About Adding Tables to Agreement Cloud Editor Templates
heading_path: Learn About Adding Tables to Agreement Cloud Editor Templates
Dynamic Table
text:
You create a dynamic table by adding Salesforce line item fields to columns. Docusign uses the line item fields to import Salesforce data to your table.
When you generate a document, Docusign creates a table that adds rows and
columns to match the record's data.

Example: You create a dynamic table in your Agreement Cloud Editor
template containing the Salesforce Region field. A user generates a
document from a record with the Region value of New England. Docusign creates a table with one column and six rows. The column is titled
New England and each row displays the name of a New England
state.

[Add a Dynamic Table to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=pzt1586134333317.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a dynamic table to an Agreement Cloud Editor template.")

title: Learn About Connecting Salesforce and Docusign
heading_path: Learn About Connecting Salesforce and Docusign
text:
Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign
account.

Docusign Apps Launcher is a unified package that includes these
applications:

- CLM for Salesforce
- CLM Essentials for Salesforce
- Gen for Salesforce
- eSignature for Salesforce
- eSignature for Salesforce Apex Toolkit

You must connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign account to
use these apps. To create the connection, you must enter Docusign and
Salesforce administrator credentials on the Docusign Setup page. The
Docusign administrator credentials that you use to connect accounts is
called the integration user.

The integration user must have Salesforce System Administrator access.
The integration user's Salesforce profile must have the Enable MFA for API
logins value set to False.

You can also follow these instructions to [make a
standard Salesforce user your integration user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=srl1586134368658&topicId=qig1698704091391.html&_LANG=enus).

Note:

- Docusign does not support connecting multiple Salesforce
  organizations to a single Docusign account.
- Docusign recommends that you create a generic integration user. For
  more information, see this topic: [Apps Launcher Integration User Best Practices](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=rhi1657226496755.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how best to manage the integration user that connects your Salesforce and Docusign accounts.").

Example: An administrator at Fontara creates a generic Docusign administrator named
docusign-admin@fontara.com. No person at Fontara uses these
credentials to access their Docusign account. The Fontara administrator
connects Docusign and Salesforce by entering the
`docusign-admin@fontara.com` credentials on the Docusign
Setup page. Since no person uses these credentials, the integration is
protected from administrators leaving or changing roles.

Use a procedure to connect your Docusign account to your Salesforce
organization:

- [Connect a Docusign Production Account to Salesforce](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=qnq1614667628308.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to connect your Salesforce organization to your Docusign production account.")

title: Use Object Key Prefixes to Identify an Envelope's Salesforce Object
heading_path: Use Object Key Prefixes to Identify an Envelope's Salesforce Object
text:
Learn how to find the Salesforce object from which users sent a Docusign envelope.

The Docusign Status page displays the status of Docusign envelopes sent from Salesforce records. You can find the page by opening
Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce and selecting the
Docusign Status tab.

However, Docusign Status does not display the name of the Salesforce object
from which you sent the envelope. You can use Salesforce object key
prefixes to identify the Salesforce object type.

Object key prefixes are the first three characters in a Docusign envelope's
Salesforce Source ID. Each three-character prefix
represents a different Salesforce object.

title: Use Object Key Prefixes to Identify an Envelope's Salesforce Object
heading_path: Use Object Key Prefixes to Identify an Envelope's Salesforce Object
Example
text:
You want to know from which objects (Accounts, Opportunities, and so on) you sent an envelope.
You follow these steps:

1. Open Docusign Apps Launcher for Salesforce.
2. Select the Docusign Status tab.
3. Check the first three digits of the envelope IDs in the Salesforce Source
   ID column.![A screenshot of the Docusign Status page highlights the first three digits of envelopes' Salesforce Source IDs.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/A-screenshot-of-the-Docusign-Status-page-highlights-the-first-three-digits-of-envelopes-Salesforce-Source-IDs.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTYyMjAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.-UU5oGvS4TUtJ6fLHCTwvp9L3eZVYUJ4jRMosBsSYCI&_LANG=enus)

The following table displays the list of Salesforce object key prefixes:

title: Create Gen Templates to Automate Your Contract Process
heading_path: Create Gen Templates to Automate Your Contract Process
text:
Learn to build Docusign Gen templates to automate your contract
generation process.

You can build Docusign Gen templates to automate your contract
generation process. Gen templates create agreements by merging Docusign and Salesforce fields to a Word document that you upload.
When users select the Gen button, Docusign generates an agreement
based on the record data.

Important: To create a template, [grant Generator status to your
Docusign user](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=doi1628107496987.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to edit the permissions of Gen, CLM, Negotiate, or eSignature for Salesforce users.").

You can create the following types of Gen templates:

[Microsoft Word Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=lig1586134209279.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a Gen template using a Microsoft Word document.")
:   Build a Gen template using Microsoft Word. Copy Salesforce and Docusign fields from Gen into a Word document. Then upload
    the document to your Gen template.

    Benefit: More
    flexibility. This method gives you the most flexibility
    when building a template. You can use custom XML code to add custom
    tables, filters, and conditional logic to your template.

    Drawback: More manual work. You must
    copy and paste fields between Salesforce and Word and upload your Word
    document to the Gen template.

[Agreement Cloud Editor Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=bcp1586134298132.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create a Gen template with the Docusign Agreement Cloud Editor.")
:   Build a Gen template using the Docusign Agreement Cloud
    Editor. Drag Salesforce and Docusign fields directly to an
    editable in-browser document.

    Benefit: Ease of use.
    You create templates in your browser without leaving Salesforce. You do
    not need to copy and paste anchor text into a Word
    document.

    Drawback: Less flexibility. You
    can not use some of the more advanced XML options in Word. You can still
    create tables, filters, and conditional logic with some limitations.

[Gen for Billing Word Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ldc1610646513281.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to create Gen for Billing templates to generate invoices from a Salesforce record automatically or from a custom button.")
:   Build a Word Gen template based on the Salesforce
    Invoice object. You can choose to have invoices
    generate automatically when a record changes status to
    Posted. You can also create a custom button to let
    users generate an invoice manually.

    Note: Only Gen for
    Salesforce Billing customers can use this option.

[Person Account Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=ird1636867371186.html&_LANG=enus "Learn how to build a Gen template using the Salesforce Person Account object.")
:   Build a Word Gen template based on the Salesforce Person
    Account object.

    Docusign cannot
    display the Person Account object when you select a Salesforce data
    source at the start of the template creation process. You must select
    Account as the data source and manually add a custom button to the
    Person Account layout.

[Clone a Gen Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=rwn1586134208474.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to clone a Gen template to create a new template that is similar to an existing one.")
:   Copy or "clone" a Docusign Gen template. Cloning is
    helpful if you want to create a new template similar to an existing
    template.

title: Create Gen Templates to Automate Your Contract Process
heading_path: Create Gen Templates to Automate Your Contract Process
text:
reate a new template that is similar to an existing one.")
:   Copy or "clone" a Docusign Gen template. Cloning is
    helpful if you want to create a new template similar to an existing
    template.

[One-Click Generation and Sending Template](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=sjm1686076461925.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to build a one-click generation and sending experience for generating a contract and sending it for signature.")
:   Link a send now envelope template to a Gen template.
    When users select the button on a record, Docusign creates
    a document and sends it for signature. Docusign does not
    allow input from the sender in this experience.

title: Docusign Does Not Email Recipients When I Generate Invoices from a Gen for Billing Template
heading_path: Docusign Does Not Email Recipients When I Generate Invoices from a Gen for Billing Template
text:
Learn the workaround for invoices failing to send from a Gen for Billing
template.

I can generate invoices from a Gen for Billing template, but Docusign does
not email invoices to recipients.

title: Docusign Does Not Email Recipients When I Generate Invoices from a Gen for Billing Template
heading_path: Docusign Does Not Email Recipients When I Generate Invoices from a Gen for Billing Template
Resolution: Switch Your Salesforce Currency Locale to FR-Euro
text:
Cause: Your Salesforce Currency Locale
field is set to fr-FR-Euro.

Use this procedure to fix the issue:

1. Edit the Company Information page as a
   Salesforce administrator:

   1. Select the gear ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/salesforcegear.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTg1MDAsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.UfWeBSO--jTpGf3kGz9W8K79yYNzKVZ5p_hEsZq3uIE&_LANG=enus)
      and select Setup.
   2. Enter and select Company Information in
      the Quick Find field.
   3. Select Edit.
2. Currency Locale: Select French (France)
   - EUR.
3. Select Save.

   You have completed the process. You switched your Currency
   Locale settings from fr-FR-Euro to FR-Euro. Gen for Billing generates
   invoices and sends them to template recipients.

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
text:
Learn about using table row XML tags to import Salesforce data into a generated
agreement.

You can use table row XML tags in Word-based Gen templates to import Salesforce data into a
generated agreement.

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
Table Row tag
text:
The Table Row tag is represented by the <TableRow> XML tag. You
use a Table Row tag in a table that you add to your Word-based Gen template. When
users generate agreements, Table Row tags dynamically add a row for each unique
piece of record data.

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
Table Row tag example
text:
You add a Table Row tag to your Gen template. A user generates an agreement from a Salesforce
record that has five product fields. Docusign adds five table rows
to the generated agreement; one for each product.

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
Using Table Row tags in Gen templates
text:
You create custom tables in Word by copying tags from a Gen template and pasting them
after a table row header. Use the following guidelines when creating Table Row
tags:

- Place Table Row tags at the beginning of the first cell of the table.
- The Select attribute adds a new row in the table for each match that it
  finds.
- The Select attribute adds rows in order of the values that it finds on a
  record.
- If you place all merge tags inside of a table row, Docusign
  adds them based on their rules.
- You can place any merge tag in a table row.
- You can place multiple Table Row tags in a single table.
- You can nest Table Row tags. Copy a table that contains a Table Row tag and
  paste it inside the cell of another table.
- You can use an unlimited number of rows in a Microsoft Word table.
- <# <TableRow … /> #> must always be the first element on
  a table row, with no leading spaces.
- A single table cannot contain both Table Row and Table Column tags.

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
Table Row attributes
text:
You can use the following attributes in <TableRow> tags:

| Attribute | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Select | XPath to select data from an XML document |
| Tag | Can be any String value |
| TagRef | Used with a Tag attribute placed on a Repeat, Table Row, or Table Column tag |
| TrackName | Specific to Term Sync |

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
Suppress Table Row tag
text:
The Suppress Table Row tag is represented by the
<SuppressTableRow> XML tag. You use a Suppress
Table Row tag in a table you add to your Word-based Gen template. When users
generate agreements, Suppress Table Row tags dynamically hide rows based on record
data. Suppress Table Row tags are useful when a record has no data in a field. You
can also use Suppress Table Row tags to hide data from certain recipients.

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
Suppress Table Row example
text:
You add a Suppress Table Row tag to your Gen template. A user generates an agreement from a
Salesforce record with a row for a Service Add-On. The record's Service Add-On field
contains no data. Docusign does not add the Service Add-On row to
the generated agreement.

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
Using Suppress Table Row tags in Gen templates
text:
You create custom tables in Word by copying tags from a Gen template and pasting them
after a table column header. Use the following guidelines when creating Suppress
Table Row tags:

- <SuppressTableRow> removes an entire row from a table.
- <SuppressTableRow> attributes are identical to Conditional tag attributes.
- Docusign removes the entire table if the row to be suppressed is the only
  one on the record.

title: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
heading_path: Learn About Using Table Row XML Tags in Gen to Import Salesforce Data
Suppress Table Row attributes
text:
You can use the following attributes in <SuppressTableRow> tags:

| Attribute | Function |
| --- | --- |
| Select | XPath used to select data from an XML document |
| Match | Table row is suppressed (removed) if the data returned matches this value |
| NotMatch | Table row is suppressed (removed) if the data returned does not match this value |
| Test | Conditional option based on Boolean logic |
| TagRef | Used with a Tag attribute placed on a Repeat, Table Row, or Table Column tag |

title: Add a Standard Table to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
heading_path: Add a Standard Table to an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
text:
You use the table button to add a standard table to an Agreement Cloud Editor template.

You can add a standard or dynamic table to your Agreement
Cloud Editor template. Use this procedure to add a standard table to an Agreement Cloud Editor
Gen template using the table button.

Note: You cannot add Salesforce data tags to a standard table. Follow this link to [add Salesforce data tags to dynamic
tables](https://support.docusign.com/s/document-item?bundleId=izj1586134369853&topicId=pzt1586134333317.html&_LANG=enus "Learn to add a dynamic table to an Agreement Cloud Editor template.").

1. Open the Gen template Create tab as a Salesforce administrator:
   1. Select the App Launcher grid ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/app-launcher-grid.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNTIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.jpEzhYP_h1Rz6Robxbzh3QH4YX6hSwPu-FtMVWz4CWw&_LANG=enus) and go to View All >  Docusign Apps Launcher.
   2. Select the Docusign Gen Templates tab.
   3. Select the arrow ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/Arrowhead.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNTIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.jpEzhYP_h1Rz6Robxbzh3QH4YX6hSwPu-FtMVWz4CWw&_LANG=enus) next to a template and choose
      Edit.

   The Gen template page opens to the Create tab.
2. Place the cursor where you want to add a table and select the
   Table button ![](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genacetable.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNTIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.jpEzhYP_h1Rz6Robxbzh3QH4YX6hSwPu-FtMVWz4CWw&_LANG=enus).

   A dropdown menu displays a table grid.
3. Move the cursor to the right to add columns, and down to add rows. The cells change
   from white to blue.

   Note: This example creates a table with three columns and four
   rows.![A screenshot displays a table grid with three columns and four rows of blue squares.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genacetableadd.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNTIsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.jpEzhYP_h1Rz6Robxbzh3QH4YX6hSwPu-FtMVWz4CWw&_LANG=enus "A screenshot displays a table grid with three columns and four rows of blue squares.")
4. Enter text to the rows and columns of the inserted table.
5. Select Next and Publish.

   A message displays: Template (your template name)
   updated.

You have completed the process. You have added a
standard table to a Gen template.

title: Customize a Standard Table in an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
heading_path: Customize a Standard Table in an Agreement Cloud Editor Template
text:
Learn to customize content in a standard Agreement Cloud Editor table.

You can customize content in a standard Agreement Cloud Editor table. You can use the
following actions:

- Change font size and style
- Add rows and columns
- Add fill colors to cells
- Delete rows, columns, or cells
- Merge data in multiple cells

When you create a new table with the Agreement Cloud Editor, the table toolbar displays
below your table. The merge cells button displays when you select multiple cells in the
table.![A screenshot displays the Agreement Cloud Editor table toolbar, containing 7 icons.](https://docusign-be-prod.zoominsoftware.io/api/bundle/izj1586134369853/page/images/genacetabletoolbar.png?token=eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJjdXN0b21lciI6ImRvY3VzaWduX3Byb2R1Y3Rpb24iLCJleHAiOjE3ODIxNTgzNTYsInNoZWFmIjoiaXpqMTU4NjEzNDM2OTg1MyJ9.DCH8rm__QwklrGd4lUm3uvMz6rGUrBIMvGAP1tF7c-g&_LANG=enus)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | Add a row above |
|  | Add a row below |
|  | Add a column to the left |
|  | Add a column to the right |
|  | Add color to a cell |
|  | Delete a selected row, column, or cell |
|  | Merge content from selected cells |

title: Add a Document to a Gen Template
heading_path: Add a Document to a Gen Template
text:
Learn to add a document to your Gen template.

You can add multiple documents to your Gen template. When you upload several versions of
the same contract, your users can decide which version they want to generate. You can upload
Word documents or PDF files.

Use this procedure to add a document to your Gen template:

1. Select Upload Files and follow the prompts to upload a document
   from your device.

   A message displays: File was successfully
   uploaded.
2. Select Save and Close.

You have completed the process. You have added a
document to your Gen template.

title: Gen Template Recommended Limits
heading_path: Gen Template Recommended Limits
text:
Learn the best practices of uploading files and adding Salesforce fields when building a
Gen template.

There are no definitive limitations on the documents and lists you can add to Gen templates.
But your template can fail if you add large files, too many files, or too many
lists.

Your Gen templates will not fail if you stay within the following recommended limits:

| Gen template aspect | Limit |
| --- | --- |
| Recommended maximum Word document template size | 3MB |
| Recommended maximum size of a generated Word document | 30MB |
| Recommended maximum number of Word templates used to generate a document | 10 |
| Recommended maximum number of lists connected per object | 20 |
| Maximum levels of related list per field | 2 |
| Maximum levels of parent object per field | 4 |
| Maximum number of triggered invoices (Gen for Salesforce Billing) | 1,000 per hour |
